###Chapter 1

The black limousine was hidden deep within the shadows of the trees.

Vivian Bennett was pulled into the car without warning.

The man's scorching body pressed her down into the leather back seat.

"Ah!" she cried out in shock.

Liam Sullivan's eyes burned with dark fire. "You said you wanted to help me?"

Vivian blinked in panic. "I just wanted to help bandage your wound..."

Before she could finish speaking, her skirt was torn apart.

Her fingertips touched the abnormally high heat of his chest.

Vivian froze instantly.

She finally understood what he meant by "help."

"Let me go!" She struggled desperately.

Liam leaned down and captured her lips.

The kiss was predatory, meant to destroy everything.

Vivian's mind buzzed.

She pushed uselessly against the mountainous weight of his body.

Liam was always calm and controlled.

If he hadn't been tricked, he would never have lost control like this.

But the moment this woman approached him, she had ignited his suppressed desire.

He kissed down her slender neck.

"No!" Vivian jolted awake.

She broke free and lunged for the car door.

Her fingers just touched the handle when a powerful force dragged her back.

Bang— The car door slammed shut.

Liam pulled her tightly into his arms.

"Help me..." His scorching breath burned her ear.

"No!" Vivian writhed violently in his embrace.

The man's eyes darkened abruptly.

"I'll marry you."

Three words, spoken with absolute certainty.

Vivian stood frozen in place.

Marry her?

Absurd!

As sharp pain assaulted her, she vaguely remembered a few hours earlier—

She should have realized it was a trap when Brandon White fell to the ground, clutching his arm and screaming in fake agony.

William White's slap struck her face hard.

"Who said you could touch your brother?"

One side of her cheek swelled rapidly.

"He started it..." she tried to explain.

William didn't even bother to listen.

In his heart, his son was a treasure. His daughter wasn't even worth a blade of grass.

Stepmother Linda pretended to intervene. "Vivian must have heard about being married off to the Qin family for the dowry. She took her anger out on Brandon..."

Those words completely ignited William's fury.

"Lock her in the storage room! No one lets her out without my permission!"

The dilapidated storage room reeked of mildew.

Cold wind poured in through a broken window pane.

Vivian hugged her arms tightly.

Ever since her mother died and her father remarried, she had no place in the White family.

Her stepmother, proud of bearing a son, found endless ways to torment her.

They finally dumped her with her grandmother to raise.

For over a decade, they showed no concern.

Now they suddenly brought her back, only to sell her to an old man for a dowry!

Even worse, Brandon dared to have wicked intentions toward her.

She was only defending herself when that scum performed his fake injury act.

Bang—

Vivian kicked the storage room door open.

As she climbed over the wall to escape, shouts came from the servants behind her.

"The young mistress is running away!"

"Quick, catch her!"

She fled blindly into the trees.

She vaguely saw a black car.

She had just pulled the door open when she smelled the strong metallic scent of blood.

Before she could retreat, an iron-like hand gripped her throat.

"Who are you?" The man's voice was low and dangerous.

Vivian gasped with difficulty. "I'm... being chased..."

The sounds of the White family servants searching came from outside the car.

The man's grip loosened slightly. "They're after you?"

She nodded urgently.

She thought she would be shown mercy, but instead heard his cold order. "Get out."

Vivian froze.

Getting out now meant walking right into their hands.

"Mister... let's make a deal..." She moved closer.

The man suddenly roared. "Get out!"

The menace radiating from him was almost scalding.

Vivian gritted her teeth. "I'll help bandage your wound. You let me hide here for a while?"

A hoarse question came from the darkness. "Are you sure you want to help?"

She couldn't see his expression, but she could feel his burning gaze.

"Yes!"
###Chapter 2

Vivian Bennett would never forget that night.

The man in the limousine demanded tirelessly.

He was clearly drugged.

The madness lasted until dawn.

She couldn't remember how she passed out.

Every bone in her body felt disassembled and reassembled.

Finally, even the strength to curse was exhausted. Darkness swallowed her vision, and she lost consciousness.

When she opened her eyes again, it was already noon the next day.

"Hmm..."

She tried to sit up, but a wave of sore weakness hit her.

Her arms felt too heavy to lift even an inch.

The images from last night crashed into her mind.

Vivian jolted upright, a sharp pain making her gasp.

She frantically looked around—

She was lying in an unfamiliar, large bed.

A dazzling crystal chandelier hung overhead. A 180-degree floor-to-ceiling window offered a panoramic view of the city's skyline.

Where was this?

Did that man bring her here?

Judging by the decor, it was at least a six-star hotel suite.

But the room was empty.

That beast had his fill and then ran off!

Bastard!

She angrily clenched the sheets.

She had only wanted to borrow the luxury car to escape the White family's pursuit. Who knew she would accidentally become someone else's antidote.

She hadn't even gotten a clear look at his face.

The only consolation was that she had finally escaped the White family's clutches.

She would consider this a blessing in disguise.

She forced herself out of bed, preparing to leave.

Out of the corner of her eye, she noticed a dark glint on the pillow.

It was a rare black diamond ring.

The ink-black diamond was cut into countless facets, each angle refracting a dazzling light.

An exquisite dragon pattern coiled around the band. It was clearly priceless.

Was this compensation left by that man?

Vivian bit her lip.

Why shouldn't she take it?

She had paid for it with her innocence.

She decisively stuffed the ring into her pocket.

"Vivian Bennett! The big boss is arriving any moment, and you dare space out?"

The sharp reprimand from PR manager Lily Evans snapped her back to reality.

"Sorry, Manager Evans!" she hurriedly apologized.

To escape her father and stepmother, she had fled to Seattle to rely on her cousin.

She had pulled strings to get into this company. She couldn't afford to make a mistake.

Lily Evans gave her a cold look. "The big boss is inspecting for the first time today. Anyone who messes up is fired!"

She turned to the other reception staff. "Everyone, be alert! Not a single misstep is allowed!"

"Yes!" everyone chorused.

Ten o'clock sharp.

Over a dozen luxury cars, surrounding a Rolls-Royce Phantom, drove through the company gates.

"The big boss is here!" Lily Evans's tense voice came through the walkie-talkie.

Everyone immediately straightened their backs and formed a welcoming line.

Vivian stood at the end of the line, unable to resist peeking—

A tall, imposing figure stepped into the lobby, surrounded by bodyguards.

A pure black bespoke suit outlined his broad shoulders and narrow waist, a perfect proportion.

His height of 6'2" brought an intense sense of oppressive feeling.
The man's features were deep and sculpted. Every step carried an aura of complete control.

The reception line fell completely silent.

All the prepared flattering words stuck in their throats.

It wasn't until the man passed by that everyone hurriedly greeted in unison, "President!"

He didn't stop, heading straight for the elevators.

Just as everyone thought the inspection was over—

The man suddenly stopped at the end of the line.

"Your name."

A deep voice landed beside Vivian's ear.

She looked up in a daze, finding his face somewhat familiar.

Impossible...

How could she know someone so far above her station?

"Vivian Bennett! What are you staring at!" Lily Evans rushed over and snapped sharply.

Vivian snapped back to reality. "Sorry! What did you just ask?"

Liam Sullivan narrowed his eyes, a dark light swirling in their depths. "Vivian Bennett?"

"Yes!" she stiffened her body.

Lily Evans hurriedly tried to smooth things over. "Mr. President, please forgive her, she's new..."

The man's gaze was like a blade, fixed on her face for a long time.

The air in the entire lobby froze.

Everyone held their breath, waiting for the storm to break.
###Chapter 3

Liam Sullivan withdrew his gaze, his expression returning to its usual cold severity.

It was as if that brief moment of eye contact had never happened.

He turned and stepped into his private elevator.

Everyone's tense nerves finally relaxed.

Manager Lily Evans immediately confronted her. "Vivian Bennett, what was that about?"

How dare she space out when the big boss was speaking.

Vivian tried to explain. "Manager, I—"

Lily cut her off sharply. "Don't think you can slack off just because your brother-in-law got promoted! In the PR department, it doesn't matter who brought you here. Perform poorly, and you're out!"

Vivian bit her lip in frustration.

She had really lost her mind just now.

How could she think the big boss looked familiar?

No one would believe that if she said it out loud.

Lily walked a few steps before turning back to snap. "Keep up!"

She hadn't originally wanted Vivian involved in the reception.

But she changed her mind after noticing the boss seemed to pay her some attention.

Vivian quickly lowered her head and followed.

They took another elevator upstairs.

They saw the company president, Daniel Lopez, leading the executives as they bowed and scraped around Liam Sullivan.

Her brother-in-law, Jason Harper, was among them.

"Boss, right this way!"

Daniel bent at the waist to guide the way, his posture humble.

Vivian followed quietly behind Lily.

She suddenly felt an intense gaze land on her.

But when she looked up, she saw nothing.

President Wang was smiling obsequiously while explaining the company's situation.

Liam looked straight ahead, his face expressionless.

Someone pushed her from behind.

Vivian gasped as she stumbled forward.

She fell heavily to the ground, wincing in pain.

Gasps echoed around her.

A pair of polished leather shoes appeared in her line of sight.

Vivian looked up dazedly.

Her eyes traveled up the crisp suit trousers—

And crashed into a pair of deep, intense eyes.

Sharp, cold, and locked firmly on her.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

That sense of familiarity washed over her again.

Where had she seen these eyes before?

President Wang hurriedly apologized. "My apologies, President Sullivan! This was an accident!"

Liam ignored him, his gaze still fixed on Vivian.

She felt her scalp prickle under his stare.

"Which department are you from? Get out of—" President Wang began to scold.

But he stopped when Liam suddenly bent down and offered Vivian a hand up.

The entire room was stunned.

Vivian was frozen in shock.

President Wang stood speechless.

Rumors said the big boss avoided women. Yet he personally helped someone up?

Liam studied her for a moment before issuing a cold command. "You are dismissed."

His tone brooked no argument.

Vivian looked toward Lily.

After receiving a nod, she acknowledged the order.

She breathed a sigh of relief as she watched Liam lead the entourage away.

Back in the PR department, colleagues were excitedly discussing the event.

"Finally saw the big boss in person!"

"So handsome. I almost got a nosebleed!"

"I'd be his mistress in a heartbeat!"

Jiang Meiqi suddenly turned the topic toward Vivian. "Why did the boss single you out earlier?"

Vivian replied calmly. "I don't know."

Jiang Meiqi said jealously, "Maybe he fancies you?"

A colleague chimed in. "Vivian is so pretty. It wouldn't be strange if he did."

Jiang Meiqi stamped her foot in anger.

Ever since Vivian joined the PR department, she had lost her title as the top beauty.

Now Vivian had caught the big boss's attention. She felt even more resentful.

Seeing Manager Lily return, Jiang Meiqi immediately tattled. "Manager, Vivian has only been here a few days and is already trying to seduce the boss!"

Manager Lily said coldly, "If you had the ability, you could be restless too!"

Jiang Meiqi was taken aback. "Manager..."

Manager Lily warned, "Do your job. Stop stirring up trouble."

Jiang Meiqi gritted her teeth in frustration.

Manager Lily walked over to Vivian. "President Wang is hosting a welcome dinner for the boss tonight. He specifically requested your attendance."

Vivian shook her head in refusal. "Manager, I have a blind date tonight..."

Manager Lily's face hardened. "Vivian Bennett, get your priorities straight! This is a great opportunity for the leadership to promote you!"

She emphasized sternly, "Make sure you dress beautifully tonight!"
###Chapter 4

The sea breeze gently brushed against the six-star hotel standing along the coastline.

Vivian Bennett arrived in the company car and called her cousin Grace Harper. "I can't make it to the blind date tonight."

Grace laughed. "Jason just told me. Focus on your work event. We've rescheduled the blind date for tomorrow."

After hanging up, Vivian entered the hotel.

The private dining room was already full.

Her arrival instantly drew everyone's attention.

Including Liam Sullivan at the head of the table.

His gaze was too sharp to ignore.

The CEO had arrived early.

Everyone was present except her.

Vivian anxiously searched for an empty seat.

Every table was completely occupied.

She was new and didn't know her colleagues well. She felt uncomfortable asking to squeeze in.

Just as panic set in, a waitress approached gracefully. "Miss, right this way. There's a seat available at the front."

Vivian sighed in relief. "Thank you."

Another moment under everyone's stare would have suffocated her.

The waitress led her toward the main table.

Recognizing the guests, Vivian froze.

They were all senior executives!

She whispered to the waitress, "Are there any other seats?"

Her eyes scanned the room for her cousin's husband, Jason Harper.

A cold voice cut through the air. "Sit down."

Vivian turned abruptly.

Her heart skipped a beat.

Did Liam Sullivan just speak to her?

Why did the waitress bring her to the CEO's table?

The empty seat was right beside him. She dared not sit there.

Vivian stood frozen.

Sitting or not sitting both felt wrong.

Daniel Lopez, the General Manager, smoothed things over. "If Mr. Sullivan says to sit, then please sit."

Vivian had no choice but to take the seat.

She felt like she was on pins and needles.

Countless eyes were fixed on her.

Some held jealousy. Others held contempt. Some were mocking. Others were waiting for drama.

A newcomer, sitting with executives, right next to the CEO.

The whole company would be talking tomorrow.

Liam Sullivan gave a quiet order. "Begin the service."

Vivian's fingers turned cold.

Had everyone been waiting for her?

This was a huge mistake.

She kept her eyes down, avoiding the executives' gazes.

Liam's presence beside her was overwhelmingly intense.

She had barely started working and was already dining with the CEO.

She even breathed lightly.

Exquisite dishes began arriving.

Steamed grouper. Lobster thermidor. All were top-tier seafood.

Vivian's mouth watered, but she didn't dare touch her utensils first.

The leaders hadn't started eating yet. It wasn't her place.

A soft clink of silverware. A piece of tender grouper landed in her bowl.

Vivian looked up, stunned.

Liam Sullivan served her himself?

How did he know she wanted fish?

The executives looked shocked.

Before she could react, a lobster claw joined the fish in her bowl.

Vivian could hardly believe it.

She wouldn't even dream something this unreal.

"Eat." His voice was deep.

When the CEO serves you, you don't refuse.

Vivian ate under pressure, chewing slowly and carefully.

The six-star cuisine was truly exceptional.

The executives quickly recovered, competing to toast Liam Sullivan.

He remained seated at the head of the table, unmoving.

His tailored black suit emphasized his tall, commanding figure. His noble aura dominated the room.

Even amidst the clinking glasses, he seemed to look down on everyone.

Liam Sullivan left early.

Mr. Lopez led the executives to see him off at the hotel entrance.

Vivian lingered at the back of the group.

A black Rolls-Royce Phantom pulled up.

Before getting in, Liam Sullivan suddenly turned back.

His gaze swept in her direction.

Vivian's heart tightened.

Was she imagining things?

There's no way the CEO was looking at her.

The Phantom drove away. The crowd dispersed.

Mr. Lopez walked over to her, squinting. "Vivian Bennett. Did you know Mr. Sullivan before?"

Vivian looked up abruptly. "No!"

How could she possibly know the CEO?

Mr. Lopez studied her for a moment, then turned and left.

Back at her cousin's house.

Jason Harper greeted her. "Vivian, did you know Mr. Sullivan previously?"

Vivian was adamant. "Of course not!"

Jason frowned. "Then why did he show you special attention?"

Vivian was speechless.

She wondered the same thing.

Even her cousin's husband noticed something was off.

Tomorrow at the company would be chaotic.

Jason smiled at her cousin. "Grace, your little sister is quite something."

Since Vivian had come to live with them, Jason had been dissatisfied.

He thought she was a burden and often took it out on Grace.

He pressured Grace to mind her own business.

This was the first time he had ever praised her.
###Chapter 5

Morning sunlight filtered through the blinds, casting patterns across the desk.

Vivian Bennett had just stepped into the office when Daniel Lopez summoned her to his executive suite.

Mr. Lopez beamed, personally pouring her a cup of tea.
"Vivian, you've truly brought honor to our company!"

She stood frozen, completely bewildered.
What had she done?

Mr. Lopez grinned from ear to ear.
"Boss Liam personally requested to see you. Tonight at nine, penthouse suite of The Ritz-Carlton!"

Vivian's pupils contracted sharply.
Liam Sullivan?
The legendary CEO known for his indifference toward women and ruthless demeanor?

She instinctively took half a step back.
"Mr. Lopez, tonight I..."

His expression darkened immediately.
"Don't be ungrateful! Countless people would kill for this opportunity!"

She clenched her fists.
"But my cousin arranged a blind date for me..."

Mr. Lopez sneered.
"A blind date? How could that compare to meeting Mr. Sullivan?"
He gave her a meaningful look.
"Seize this chance. It will benefit you greatly."

Vivian bit her lip.
She had joined this company simply to prove herself to her brother-in-law.
She never intended to climb the social ladder.

But his next words offered slight relief.
"The meeting is at nine. It won't conflict with your date."
Vivian reluctantly nodded.
However, Mr. Lopez's suggestive gaze left her uneasy.

Seven PM, Le Bernardin.
Crystal chandeliers cast dazzling reflections while the air carried notes of expensive perfume.
Vivian sat by the window, absently tapping her fingers on the table.

Her blind date arrived twenty minutes late.
A man in a gray suit lounged casually into the seat opposite her.
"Vivian Bennett?" He raised an eyebrow, his gaze tinged with arrogance.
"That's me." She nodded politely.

As her brother-in-law had described, he was strikingly handsome with a roguish charm.
But his tardiness for a first meeting was unacceptable.

"Zachary Hudson." He delivered his name with indifference.
"I assume you know my background?"

Before she could respond, he interrupted.
"I have no intention of marriage." He crossed his legs.
"But my family keeps pressuring me."

Vivian frowned.

"If you're willing to behave after marriage, ignore my affairs, and focus on bearing children—"
"Mr. Hudson!" Vivian stood abruptly.
"We might not be compatible."

Zachary's expression darkened.
"You dare reject me?"
No woman had ever refused him to his face.

She maintained a polite smile.
"We're truly not suitable."

Zachary narrowed his eyes, radiating danger.
"You'll regret this." He delivered the cold statement before storming off.

Vivian massaged her temples wearily.
As she prepared to leave, someone else sat across from her.
She looked up and froze instantly.

Liam Sullivan?
Why was he here?
She pinched herself sharply, gasping at the pain.

"Vivian Bennett." His low voice resonated.

She forced a professional smile.
"Mr. Sullivan."

His gaze deepened.
"Blind date?"

She nodded hesitantly.
"Yes..."

The air solidified instantly.
Liam radiated intimidating pressure.

Vivian's palms grew damp.
She couldn't comprehend what she had said wrong.

"Mr. Sullivan, if there's nothing else..." She attempted to rise.

"Sit." His commanding tone brooked no argument.

Vivian reluctantly resumed her seat.

He locked eyes with her, enunciating each word clearly.
"Now, I'm your blind date."
###Chapter 6

Vivian Bennett froze instantly.

She stared at the man across from her in disbelief.

"What...did you just say?"

She almost thought she was hallucinating.

Liam Sullivan's expression remained cold and stern as he repeated, "A blind date with me."

Vivian felt as if struck by lightning.

She stood completely rigid on the spot.

It took her a long moment to find her voice again. "Mr. Sullivan, are you joking?"

Liam's gaze deepened. "Do I look like I'm joking?"

Vivian's heart tightened.

His expression was serious, his eyes sharp.

He truly didn't seem to be joking.

But why would this lofty group president suddenly appear at a blind date?

And with her, of all people?

Cold sweat seeped through the back of her blouse.

Her voice trembled slightly. "Mr. Sullivan, with your qualifications..."

Liam cut her off. "Are you dissatisfied with me?"

Vivian quickly shook her head. "Not dissatisfied, but with your status, why would you need a blind date?"

Liam's eyes darkened. "I've never been in a relationship."

Vivian's eyes widened.

How was that possible?

A man like him with no romantic experience?

Liam continued, "But I own numerous properties and have substantial assets. Are these conditions satisfactory to you?"

Vivian inhaled quietly.

Did she dare say no?

Liam's gaze burned intensely. "What did you say?"

Vivian's fingers trembled with nervousness. "I said...very satisfactory."

She hurriedly picked up her water glass and took several quick sips.

This wasn't a blind date.

It felt more like a high-pressure interview.

Liam's expression softened slightly.

Just as Vivian began to relax, she heard him state decisively, "Then let's get married."

"Pfft—"

Vivian spat out her water.

It landed directly on Liam's face.

His expression instantly darkened.

"I'm sorry..."

Vivian scrambled to her feet, apologizing.

She grabbed napkins and handed them to him.

But her nerves made her cough again.

She sprayed his face once more.

Liam's face turned extremely grim.

Vivian was flustered and at a loss.

She hadn't meant to do it.

Liam stood up abruptly.

He strode quickly toward the restroom.

His brows were furrowed tightly.

Dark currents churned in his eyes.

He had severe mysophobia.

Anyone else would have faced consequences.

But with her...

He forced down his anger.

He told himself she was just overly excited.

But when he returned to the table after cleaning up, Vivian was already gone.

She had slipped away while he was gone.

No way was she staying.

Remaining any longer might cost her her life.

She hastily hailed a taxi and returned to her cousin's house.

The moment she stepped inside, she sensed the tense atmosphere.

Her cousin Grace Harper had red-rimmed eyes.

Her cousin's husband Jason Harper looked furious.

"Cousin, what's wrong?"

Vivian hurried forward with concern.

Grace forced a weak smile. "It's nothing. You should go rest in your room."

She tried to steer Vivian toward the bedroom.

Jason suddenly shouted sharply, "Stop! You think you can just leave?"

Grace glared at her husband. "What more do you want?"

Jason fumed with anger. "She offended Young Master Hudson! I went through great effort to make the connection, and she dared to reject him! How am I supposed to explain this to the family now?"

Grace protected her cousin. "Explain what? If Vivian wasn't willing, she must have had her reasons."

Jason scoffed dismissively. "What reasons could she possibly have? She clearly doesn't want to get married and is just mooching off us!"

Grace raised her voice. "Jason Harper, that's enough!"

Jason smashed a teacup. "I've had enough! You don't earn money and rely on me to support you! Now I have to support your cousin too! My mother wants to come live here, and you make excuses, but you let an outsider stay long-term!"

He poured out all his pent-up resentment.

After finishing, he slammed the door violently on his way out.

Grace swayed unsteadily.

She almost lost her balance.
###Chapter 7

Vivian Bennett rushed to her cousin Grace Harper's side.

Her voice was tight with concern. "Cousin Grace, are you alright?"

Grace's face was pale. She shook her head weakly. "I'm fine..."

Vivian immediately poured a glass of warm water and handed it to her.

She kept her eyes downcast. "I should move out," she said quietly.

She didn't want to be a burden to her cousin anymore.
Since she moved in, her cousin's husband Jason Harper had never stopped expressing his displeasure.
Tonight's argument was just the eruption of long-held resentment.

Grace frowned. "You just arrived in Seattle. Where would you go?"

Vivian opened her mouth to speak.

Grace cut her off directly. "Don't listen to Jason's nonsense. Just stay here peacefully."

Vivian's gaze fell on her cousin's tired, worn face.
She asked softly, "If I keep staying, how will you two ever try for a baby?"

Half a year ago, Grace had lost a child.
Her in-laws had pressured her to quit her job to focus on the pregnancy.
She lost the baby, and then lost her job too.
Now her mother-in-law nagged her daily about having a child, while her husband resented her for not earning money.
Grace was stuck in the middle, torn and conflicted.

Grace's expression froze. A flash of embarrassment crossed her eyes.
How could she not want to get pregnant soon?
But her husband hadn't touched her in a very long time.
Without intimacy, how could there be a child?

Vivian held her hand. "I'll rent a place nearby. You can visit me anytime."

Grace sighed. "Vivian, I'm sorry I haven't taken good care of you."

Vivian shook her head. "I'm already so grateful you took me in."
If not for her cousin, she would have had nowhere to go after leaving the White family.

Grace thought for a moment. "Let me help you look for a place first. If we can't find a suitable one, you'll continue staying with me."
Vivian was her only cousin on her mother's side. She lost her mother young, her father didn't care for her, and her stepmother was unkind.
If Grace didn't help her, who would?
Besides, her husband never hesitated to financially support his own relatives. What was wrong with her helping her cousin?

Vivian felt deeply grateful and nodded gently.

The next day at work, all her colleagues congratulated her with smiles.
Vivian was completely confused.

Lily Evans, the PR manager, walked over in her high heels.
She gave a tight smile. "Congratulations on your transfer to the headquarters."

Vivian stood frozen.
"Why me?"

Lily's eyes held mockery. "You should ask yourself what methods you used."
Her words were heavy with implication.

Vivian wanted to explain.
Lily cut her off coldly. "Report to headquarters tomorrow."
She turned and walked away.

Colleagues instantly swarmed around her.
"Vivian, you're so lucky! The benefits at headquarters are much better than here!"
"You're the fastest promoted employee in the company, right?"
"Boss Liam only promoted you during his last inspection. He must really value you!"

Amid the clamor of compliments, Vivian only felt her scalp prickling.
She simply couldn't understand why she was suddenly transferred to headquarters.
The only possibility was Liam Sullivan.

Running into him during the blind date last night was bizarre enough.
He even proposed marriage.
She was so shocked she sprayed water in his face and fled while he was in the restroom.
She thought she would be fired today, but instead, she got a promotion.

This was clearly setting her up for a fall.
Liam transferred her to headquarters purely to make it easier to deal with her.
On the surface, it was a promotion. In reality, it was a poison pill.
One that could explode at any moment.

Heavy-hearted, Vivian didn't return to her cousin's after work. She went directly to the club where her best friend Sophia Miller worked.
She needed to discuss with her best friend: should she go to headquarters, or simply resign?

She had just sat down when a server approached.
"Miss Bennett, Mr. Sullivan requests your presence upstairs for a drink."
###Chapter 8

Vivian Bennett’s heart clenched violently.

Liam Sullivan?

Why was he here?

Her fingertips trembled slightly as she forced a smile. "I'm sorry, but can I decline?"

If she had known he was here, she would never have set foot in this place.

The waiter looked uncomfortable, but Sophia Miller spoke first. "She's joking. She'll go right away!"

The waiter retreated with visible relief.

Vivian tugged at her friend's sleeve. "I really don't want to go."

Sophia raised an eyebrow. "What are you afraid of? I'm here."

Vivian pressed her lips together.

Sophia appeared to be a cocktail waitress, but she was actually a top-tier operative.

With her protection, Vivian did feel considerably safer.

"Alright." Vivian took a deep breath and followed the waiter toward the private elevator.

The top-floor VIP area was silent.

It was a stark contrast to the noise downstairs.

Only the wealthy and powerful could access this area.

The waiter pushed open the ornate gilded door at the very end.

The private room was filled with the scent of alcohol and pulsating music.

A group of young socialites were laughing and chatting around a card table.

Oliver Winston noticed her first.

His eyes lit up. "Did Sophia send you?"

Vivian shook her head. "I'm looking for someone."

Her gaze swept across the room, finally settling on a corner.

Liam Sullivan sat alone in the shadows.

The tip of his cigar glowed, wreathed in smoke.

His sharp profile looked particularly aloof under the dim lighting.

No one in the entire room dared to approach him.

Vivian walked straight toward him.

Oliver moved to intercept her, but he was a step too late.

Under the astonished stares of everyone, she stopped in front of Liam.

"Mr. Sullivan."

Liam looked up, his dark eyes unfathomable.

He flicked the ash from his cigar, silent as a mountain.

Vivian clenched her fingers.

She picked up a glass and filled it with strong whiskey.

"I apologize for last night's incident."

She downed it in one gulp.

A burning sensation churned in her stomach.

He remained unmoved.

Vivian gritted her teeth and poured another glass.

Just as she was about to force it down, she heard his cold voice.

"Have you made your decision?"
###Chapter 9

Vivian Bennett stared blankly.
"Consider what?"

She looked at Liam Sullivan in confusion.

His deep, dark eyes swept over her.
He spoke in a low, clear voice.
"Marry me."

Vivian's eyes widened instantly.
Her pretty face was filled with shock.

She never expected him to bring this up again.
Last night, when he suddenly proposed marriage, she thought it was a joke.

The memory of spitting in his face still lingered in her mind.
The idea of marriage was so absurd she didn't dare take it seriously.

Hearing him say it again now.
The impact was just as staggering.

Her hand jerked violently.
The red wine in her glass splashed all over his trousers.

Vivian panicked.
"I'm sorry... I didn't mean to..."

She frantically used her sleeve to wipe it, trying to fix her mistake.

But she overlooked how sensitive the spot she was touching was.
Liam's face darkened instantly.

"Get out!"
He snapped coldly.

The temperature in the private dining room plummeted.
Everyone held their breath, waiting for the drama to unfold.

Vivian nodded repeatedly.
"I'm leaving right now."

She just wanted to escape this dangerous man as quickly as possible.

Just as she turned to leave, her wrist was seized abruptly.
Liam pulled her back in front of him.

"Trying to run?"
He leaned in close, his presence dangerous and oppressive.

Vivian's voice trembled.
"Mr. Sullivan, please let me go..."

She felt the hostility radiating from him, her body shaking uncontrollably.

Liam's knuckles tightened.
Red marks immediately appeared on her slender, pale wrist.

Vivian winced in pain.
"I... I already wiped it..."

She tried to defend herself.

Liam's gaze turned even colder.
"Shut up!"

The air almost froze solid.

All eyes were fixed on the unfolding drama.
Alexander Lowell put down his mahjong tiles and strode over.

He had a burly build, his eyes sharp as knives.
Vivian's heart clenched.

There were rumors Liam preferred men.
Could this be his...

Seeing Alexander get closer.
Vivian had a quick idea and turned to Oliver Winston.

"Mr. Winston, didn't you say you wanted to ask Sophia out for dinner?"
She forced a calm smile. "How about having supper together tonight?"

Oliver's eyes lit up.
"Great!"

He only realized the inappropriateness of the situation after speaking, awkwardly rubbing his nose.

Alexander indeed stopped in his tracks.
His gaze shifted between the two.

Vivian secretly breathed a sigh of relief.
Unaware that Liam saw everything.

He tightened his grip abruptly...
###Chapter 10

Vivian Bennett was caught completely off guard.

She lost her balance and stumbled forward.

Her hands landed unexpectedly on his firm chest.

The private dining room fell into dead silence.

Everyone held their breath.

Was this woman insane?

She dared to take advantage of Mr. Sullivan in public?

She actually touched his chest.

Oliver Winston stared in stunned disbelief.

He had intended to help Vivian out of this situation.

He never expected her to make such a bold move toward Liam Sullivan.

Everyone knew Liam despised women getting close to him.

Now Vivian had not only thrown herself into his arms but openly touched his chest.

Oliver could already imagine her tragic fate.

This is bad.

His date with Sophia tonight is definitely ruined.

Vivian wore a thin dress.

She suddenly fell into Liam's embrace.

Their bodies pressed tightly together.

An intense masculine scent enveloped her.

Her blood felt like it was flowing backward.

An unfamiliar tingling sensation spread throughout her body.

The well-defined muscles under her palm felt scorching hot, making her flustered.

Vivian quickly withdrew her hand.

She urgently tried to stand up.

But due to nervousness, she fell back into his arms again.

This is terrible.

Vivian wished she could disappear into the ground.

Her cheeks burned bright red.

She frantically tried to get up once more.

Her slender waist was suddenly held firmly in place.

Liam pressed her down onto his lap.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

She glanced secretly at Alexander Lowell, feeling utterly desperate.

She really hadn't meant to seduce Liam.

Now Liam wasn't letting her get up.

"Mr. Sullivan, what exactly do you want?" Vivian asked with forced courage.

She knew she had thoroughly angered the big boss.

She'd rather face punishment directly than endure this tension.

"You don't want to marry me?" A cold question came from above her head.

Vivian froze.

She looked up sharply at the man holding her waist.

His sharp features were shrouded in shadow.

Dangerous yet captivating.

His black dress shirt was impeccably tailored, with three buttons undone at the collar.

He exuded a restrained, sensual aura.

Vivian stared at him blankly, momentarily mesmerized.

She shook her head unconsciously.

Who wouldn't want to marry him?

Even with rumors about him avoiding women.

His handsome looks, immense wealth, and prominent family background...

Still drove countless women wild.

Vivian was just an ordinary person.

She had no reason to reject such a man.

Even though she knew it was impossible.

The next second, Liam suddenly stood up.

He swept her up into his arms.

He strode toward the private dining room door.

Vivian's mind went completely blank.

Her entire body stiffened.

Everyone in the room was utterly petrified.

My God, what were they witnessing?
###Chapter 11

All eyes were fixed on him in shock.

He was carrying this woman in his arms?

Did Liam Sullivan have feelings for her?

This was unbelievable!

Everyone knew Liam was an eternal iceberg.

How could he show such special treatment to a woman now?

Under everyone's disbelieving gazes, Liam carried Vivian Bennett straight toward his limousine.

He shoved her into the car.

Vivian felt the vehicle looked vaguely familiar.

Before she could think further, Liam leaned over her.

His solid arms trapped her between the car seat and his chest.

"Ah!" Vivian gasped, pressing her hands against his firm chest.

"What do you want?"

Liam's deep dark eyes locked onto her, swirling with complex emotions.

"Why did you run away?" His low voice held accusation.

He had searched for her endlessly after that night.

He had practically turned the entire city upside down.

He never expected her to vanish silently to Seattle.

She stammered, "I... was too scared..."

She forced an ingratiating smile. "You're magnanimous, Boss Liam. You wouldn't hold a grudge against someone like me, right?"

She secretly studied his expression, noticing his face growing darker.

Steeling herself, she blurted, "Do you want to spit back?"

Liam frowned deeply.

This woman clearly didn't understand what he was asking.

An icy chill radiated from him, making her shiver.

Vivian's scalp prickled under his intense stare.

"What do you want? Just tell me directly," she mustered her courage to ask.

Liam gazed at her, then suddenly spoke. "Thirty million."

Vivian was stunned. "What thirty million?"

He pressed his forehead against hers. "Marry me. I'll give you thirty million."

Vivian was completely shocked.

He not only wanted to marry her but would also pay thirty million?

What kind of unbelievable deal was this?

She quickly collected herself.

This had to be a trap.

"Why me?" she asked warily.

Liam narrowed his eyes. "Didn't you throw yourself at me first?"

Their first time happened because she climbed into his car.

Vivian opened her mouth, wanting to say more.

Her confused, defenseless expression was utterly captivating.

Liam's desire, suppressed all night, erupted instantly.

He gripped her chin and kissed her fiercely.

The kiss was aggressive, overwhelming her senses.

Vivian's eyes widened, her entire body stiffening.

He pinned her against the back seat, kissing her forcefully.

His dominant breath invaded her, tangling wildly with hers.

His large hand slid along her curves, slipping beneath her dress...
###Chapter 12

Vivian's mind buzzed.

An electric current shot through her entire body.

She raised her hands to push against his hard chest.

"Don't touch me..."

Liam Sullivan released her lips, his breathing heavy.

His large palm roamed freely over her body.

He had become addicted after tasting her just once.

These past days, that night constantly replayed in his mind.

He remembered the sensation damn well.

He wanted more.

His gaze was dark and intense, displeased by her resistance.

"Don't want me? Or don't want the thirty million?"

Vivian really wanted to tell him to get lost.

Who cared about his money.

But remembering he was her ultimate boss, she held back.

A shadow passed through his eyes.

Vivian gave a bitter smile. "Even a hundred million is too much for me."

Liam fell silent.

She was smiling, but her eyes were cold.

She was truly refusing him.

His gaze deepened as he pulled her tighter.

His thin lips brushed her ear. "Two hundred million. Can you handle that now?"

His voice was low and husky, his warm breath flooding her ear.

Vivian trembled slightly.

She looked at his perfect face up close.

He was actually trying to seduce her with his charm.

Fortunately, her willpower was strong.

Vivian mimicked him, bringing her red lips close to his ear. "When I want it, I'll come find you. How about that?"

It was still a refusal.

Anger flared in Liam's eyes.

This was the first time he had been rejected.

He had offered his utmost sincerity, and she didn't appreciate it.

He lowered his head and bit her neck.

He left a mark as Vivian cried out in pain.

Vivian shivered from the pain.

She feared he would force himself on her.

She didn't understand what he saw in her, why he insisted on marriage.

But she could clearly feel his rage.

Liam gripped the back of her head, forcing her to look directly at him.

"Report to headquarters tomorrow. I'll be waiting!"
###Chapter 13

His husky voice carried a command that brooked no refusal.

Vivian Bennett's heart raced wildly.

She could only nod, her mind completely blank.

When Liam Sullivan helped her out of the car, her legs felt weak and unsteady.

Everything that had just happened felt like a dream.

The powerful boss, Liam Sullivan, wanted to marry her.

He had almost taken her right there in the car.

It all felt too surreal.

He was a completely different person from the furious man who had slammed the door last night.

Vivian was shocked. "Apologized?"

She had lived with her cousin for months and knew Jason's temperament well.

He was extremely proud and stubbornly chauvinistic. He never easily admitted he was wrong.

Last night he had been hurling insults. Today he was apologizing. It was very strange.

Grace squeezed her hand, smiling. "Your brother-in-law said you're welcome to stay as long as you want."

That stingy brother-in-law who resented her for freeloading had suddenly changed his attitude.

Before she could process it, Jason brought over a fruit platter.

"Have some fruit." He politely offered her an apple.

Vivian was stunned. "Corporate headquarters?"

Jason Harper's sudden change in attitude puzzled her.

Liam Sullivan's unexpected proposal left her heart in turmoil.

She wanted to earn her place at Sullivan Group through her own merits.

Over a dozen bodyguards in black escorted a woman in white into view.

The woman's skin was fairer than snow, and her demeanor was elegant.

Oversized sunglasses could not hide her delicate features.

She exuded the noble aura of an heiress from a prestigious family.

"She tested into the gifted program at sixteen and is fluent in six languages."

Liam's urgency to arrange blind dates.

She truly couldn't understand why he insisted on marrying her.

They were clearly from different worlds.

"You think I'm joking?" Liam frowned.

Vivian mustered her courage to retort. "Aren't you? Someone of your status should marry a socialite from a suitable background."

She muttered under her breath, "A mistress would be more appropriate..."
###Chapter 20

Liam Sullivan’s gaze darkened as it locked onto her.
“You’d rather be my mistress?”

Vivian Bennett’s cheeks flushed with warmth.
She quickly shook her head.
“That’s not what I meant.”

Before she could finish, the man suddenly leaned in closer.

His sharply defined, handsome face abruptly filled her vision.

Without warning, his warm lips covered hers.

“Mmm…”

Vivian’s pupils contracted sharply.
She froze completely.

Liam Sullivan was kissing her?

His intense masculine presence overwhelmed her senses.

Her breath caught.
Her mind went blank.

The kiss was both domineering and lingering.

He only pulled back slowly when she was nearly breathless.

Vivian gasped for air, her chest rising and falling rapidly.

“You don’t even know how to breathe properly during a kiss.”
A mocking curve touched Liam’s lips.
“You’re not qualified to be my mistress yet.”

She steadied her breathing, a slight frown forming between her brows.

When had she ever said she wanted to be his mistress?

She had merely been objectively analyzing the situation.
The probability of becoming his mistress seemed higher than becoming Mrs. Sullivan.

She never agreed to be his mistress.

Vivian shot him an annoyed look.
“Mr. Sullivan, what exactly is the purpose of your late-night visit?”

It couldn’t possibly be just to test her suitability as a mistress.

Liam’s gaze remained calm yet authoritative.
“Report to headquarters tomorrow. I need a secretary.”

Vivian’s heart skipped a beat.

The CEO himself came to personally offer her a secretary position?

Being transferred to headquarters was already an exceptional honor.

Being promoted directly to the CEO’s secretary was like skipping several levels at once.

She cautiously pressed further.
“What responsibilities does the secretary position entail?”

Liam’s expression was cool and detached.
“What a secretary normally does.”

Vivian bit her lip, deciding to ask directly.

“Does it require sleeping with you?”

Some secretaries did have to fulfill their boss’s special needs.

She needed to confirm exactly what kind of secretary he wanted.

A dark glint flashed in Liam’s eyes.
“Are you looking forward to sleeping with me?”

Vivian immediately shook her head.
“No!”

The man’s expression instantly darkened.

The air grew heavy and still.

An oppressive atmosphere pressed down on her.

Vivian gathered her courage and spoke.
“I may be single, but I’m not a virgin anymore.”

Liam’s gaze turned icy.
“So?”

She forced a smile.
“So I don’t meet your standards for a partner.”

She heard Liam Sullivan had a cleanliness obsession.
He only touched pure women.

She lost her virginity in a luxury car that night.
She was no longer pure.

A cold light flashed in Liam’s eyes.
“Who told you being a secretary requires sleeping with me?”

Vivian was stunned.

Had she misunderstood?

“But you must be competent.” Liam looked down at her with a scrutinizing gaze.
“I don’t need a vase.”

Vivian’s cheeks burned instantly.
###Chapter 21

If he thought she was all looks and no talent, why insist on having her?

He came to her apartment late at night just to ask her to be his secretary.

…

After Liam Sullivan left, Vivian Bennett tossed and turned in bed, unable to sleep.

On her first night living alone, the CEO himself had shown up at her door.

He specifically requested to transfer her to headquarters as his secretary.

What was his real motive?

She couldn't figure it out.

But working close to Liam Sullivan was a rare opportunity for anyone.

She was no exception.

Vivian didn't remember how she finally fell asleep.

She only recalled the nightmare returning once she drifted off.

She was back in that luxurious car.

The man was tirelessly taking what he wanted.

No matter how she cried or struggled, he remained unmoved.

His stamina was shocking, like a wild beast.

Vivian woke up drenched in cold sweat again.

Morning light streamed through the window.

She looked around, realizing she had that nightmare again.

It had been over half a year since that night, but the shadow still haunted her.

For the past six months, the same nightmare had jolted her awake repeatedly.

She still didn't know who that man was.

If she ever found out, she would make him pay.

She had only accidentally entered his car and kindly bandaged his wound.

Yet he used her as an antidote, unleashing his desires without restraint.

Vivian was gritting her teeth when her phone suddenly rang.

She answered the call. "Hello?"

"Is this Vivian Bennett?"

"Yes, speaking."

"This is the PR department of Sullivan Group. Congratulations on passing the interview. Please come to headquarters this morning to complete your onboarding."

Vivian was stunned.

She had actually been hired by the PR department.

This should have been good news.

But then she remembered Liam's personal visit last night, asking her to be his secretary.

She felt torn.

Should she go to PR or become the CEO's secretary?

The secretary position was clearly higher ranking with better pay.

It also meant working closely with the CEO.

But Vivian had no special feelings toward Liam Sullivan.

Every time they met, his demands left her feeling uneasy.

Was this some kind of under-the-table deal?

If she had a choice, she would prefer to stay as far away from him as possible.

After careful consideration, Vivian decided to join the PR department.

She got up, washed up, and applied professional makeup.

She chose a white business suit from her closet.

After changing, she had a quick breakfast and took the subway to Sullivan Group.

The Sullivan Group tower stood against the sky like a pyramid, soaring into the clouds.
###Chapter 22

Vivian Bennett completed her onboarding procedures.

She walked briskly toward the public relations department.

She pushed open the conference room door.

Manager Thomas Carter was leading a meeting.

Vivian entered with Emily Davis.

Emily was the girl who had gossiped about Liam Sullivan during the interview.

They exchanged smiles.

They swapped contact information.

As newcomers, they needed to look out for each other.

Thomas had someone distribute meeting materials.

Vivian opened to the first page.

Her gaze froze.

The man in the photo looked extremely familiar.

Wasn't he that outrageous rich second-generation from the last blind date?

She couldn't remember his name.

But she remembered his words vividly.

"I don't plan to marry, but my family is eager for a grandson. If you're willing to be a housewife after marriage, ignore my affairs outside, and only focus on having children and caring for my parents, I might consider it..."

Vivian still found it laughable now.

The man practically treated his blind date as a breeding tool.

She had never met a man so disrespectful toward women.

She had rejected him on the spot.

He had left with a vicious threat. "You'll pay for today's rejection!"

Later, her cousin's husband, Jason Harper, had indeed flown into a rage.

He had stormed out, slamming the door.

If Liam Sullivan hadn't taken an interest in her, Jason would never have returned to apologize.

She and her cousin Grace had almost been ruined by this man.

The more Vivian thought about it, the angrier she became.

She continued reviewing the materials.

She was surprised to discover the man had significant connections.

He was actually the only son of the Hudson Group.

Notorious for his philandering ways.

His reputation with women was well-known.

Vivian understood immediately.

As the only son, his family was pressuring him to marry and have children.

But he hadn't had his fill of fun yet.

He wanted to continue his playboy lifestyle.

So his requirement for a marriage partner was that she couldn't interfere with his outside affairs.

Such an outrageous demand.

No wonder he couldn't find a suitable socialite from a similar background.

He had been reduced to going on a blind date with someone like her.

But she hadn't agreed either.

So what if he was a rich second-generation?

Marry her and still play around outside? In his dreams!

She could never accept a man like that.

Thomas's serious voice rang out. "Our next public relations target is Zachary Hudson! He has always been at odds with Mr. Sullivan. This contract has been stalled. Anyone who can handle Zachary Hudson and secure the contract will get double the commission and year-end bonus!"

Vivian's eyes lit up.

Handling this outrageous man meant double the money?

She was immediately interested.

The conference room buzzed with discussion.

"To handle Zachary Hudson, we need to send a beautiful woman. Everyone knows he loves beauties. He changes women faster than he changes clothes."

The public relations department did have quite a few beautiful women.
###Chapter 23

The women in the PR department all lit up at the chance to get close to Zachary Hudson.

Who wouldn't want to catch the eye of the dashing young heir?

It could change their lives overnight.

Vivian Bennett was different.

She was only interested in the money.

As for Zachary, she desperately hoped he wouldn't recognize her.

She didn't want him remembering their disastrous blind date and seeking revenge.

Manager Thomas Carter scanned the room.

He selected the most outstanding beauties in the department to join him that evening to meet Zachary.

"Vivian Bennett, you're coming too."

Thomas finally called her name.

Vivian paused for a moment, then nodded.

Her mind was already calculating the substantial bonus.

Emily Davis, sitting beside her, nudged her arm. "Congratulations! You get to be up close with the famous Zachary Hudson."

Vivian forced a weak smile. "I'm only interested in the money."

Emily's eyes sparkled as if she had found a kindred spirit.

"I can't believe we think alike!"

She warmly linked arms with Vivian.

Thrilled to find a colleague who shared her pragmatic outlook, Emily was especially excited.

Vivian smiled in response. "It seems we get along well."

Emily scoffed dismissively. "I really don't get those women. Going crazy over a playboy like Zachary Hudson. If I were to be infatuated with anyone, it would be our big boss."

Vivian nearly choked on her own saliva.

"So you're a fan of Mr. Sullivan."

Emily raised an eyebrow. "Isn't everyone? Mr. Sullivan is far superior to Zachary Hudson. At least he keeps to himself and has never been involved in any messy relationships. The only person he's ever been linked to romantically is his adopted sister, Vice President Isabella Sullivan."

Vivian's heart clenched tightly.

Liam Sullivan had been involved in rumors with Isabella Sullivan.

That time she saw them with a little boy at the restaurant...

Could that child really be their secret love child?

After the meeting, Vivian returned to her workstation, lost in troubled thoughts.

Just then, her phone suddenly rang.

Other colleagues in the office turned to look.

Sullivan Group strictly prohibited taking personal calls during work hours.

Without a second thought, Vivian declined the call.

Liam Sullivan's expression darkened instantly.

How dare she hang up on him?

He had waited all morning for her to come to his office.

Now he called her personally, and she hung up?

Did she not want to work at Sullivan Group anymore?

Frowning, Liam dialed again.

Vivian hung up once more.

He stubbornly continued to call.

Vivian continued to decline.

After several rounds, Liam's patience finally wore thin.

Vivian had also reached her limit.

She gritted her teeth and answered the phone, her tone sharp. "Haven't you had enough? When someone hangs up on you, it means they can't talk. Don't you understand that? If this isn't important, I'll call you non-stop tonight. I'll make sure you don't sleep a wink..."
###Chapter 24

Vivian Bennett was speaking animatedly.

A cold, authoritative voice abruptly cut her off. "What?"

She froze instantly.

Her mind went completely blank.

That voice...

Why did it sound so familiar?

It sounded exactly like the CEO, Liam Sullivan.

The next second.

Assistant Ryan's respectful voice came through the receiver. "Boss, we've found the information."

Vivian's hand trembled.

Her phone nearly slipped from her grasp.

She forced herself to remain calm.

Thinking quickly, she said, "I think you have the wrong number? I'm quite busy right now..."

Playing dumb was her only option.

He hadn't identified himself when he called.

She could easily claim she didn't know who it was.

"Stop right there!"

Liam's cold voice commanded.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

Her fingertips trembled slightly.

"Why didn't you report to headquarters?" His voice carried clear displeasure.

Vivian struggled to explain. "I already reported..."

She just hadn't gone to the CEO's office.

"Where are you?" Liam's tone turned icy.

How dare this woman lie to his face?

Vivian answered with difficulty. "I've joined the Public Relations Department."

Liam's eyes darkened. "Who gave you permission to join Public Relations?"

They had agreed she would become his secretary.

"The Public Relations Department hired me first." Vivian quickly fabricated an excuse.

"..."

"I need to get back to work now."

Before he could speak again.

She quickly ended the call.

"Vivian Bennett!"

Hannah Green suddenly appeared before her.

Her face was terrifyingly dark. "Company policy prohibits personal calls during work hours. Are you unaware of this?"

Vivian immediately lowered her head in apology. "I'm sorry. I'll be more careful next time."

She thought to herself: Does answering the big boss's call count as personal business?

"Since it's your first offense, I'll let it slide this time." Hannah said coldly. "Copy the employee handbook one thousand times tonight!"

Vivian was shocked. "One thousand times?"

Hannah warned sharply. "The next offense will result in direct salary deduction!"

"..."

Vivian was speechless.

She had long heard about Sullivan Group's strict management.

Now she finally experienced it firsthand.

Was this extraordinary promotion a blessing or a curse?

Hannah shot her a fierce glare.

Then she turned and left.

Emily Davis quietly approached. "Are you okay?"

Vivian shook her head. "I'm fine."

After years in the workplace.

She had developed a thick skin.

This level of criticism was nothing.

"Be careful." Emily lowered her voice. "Deputy Manager Hannah is famously known as the old maid. She's particularly difficult to deal with."

Vivian was surprised. "Old maid?"

She completely couldn't tell.

The deputy manager of Public Relations should theoretically be very sociable.

How could she still be a virgin?
###Chapter 25

Su Mu gently touched the surface of the Moonheart Stone.

The medicinal formulas from his master's teachings surfaced in his mind.

A glint of sharp insight flashed in his eyes.

Li Chunyang watched Su Mu's expression closely.

This young miracle doctor's reaction was highly unusual.

He couldn't help but speak up. "Young friend Su, do you recognize this object?"

Ren Qingxin's beautiful eyes shifted.

Her slender fingers lightly tapped the table.

"Mr. Su, what exactly is this treasure?"

A slight smile touched Su Mu's lips.

"For you, this is a deadly poison."

His voice remained calm and even.

"Divine Doctor Li, have you heard of the Moonheart Stone?"

Li Chunyang's brow furrowed deeply.

He pondered for a long moment before shaking his head.

"I know of Moonheart Grass and Crescentwood."

"But this Moonheart Stone—I've never heard of it."

Su Mu's fingertips gently brushed the stone's surface.

The formation of this object was nothing short of miraculous.

The symbiotic growth of Moonheart Grass and Crescentwood was already rare.

Let alone their transformation into this amber-like marvel.

"This object is precisely their combined form."

His voice remained composed.

"They grew together through a fortunate coincidence."

"Years of weathering and erosion followed."

"Eventually, it took this form."

Li Chunyang's beard trembled slightly.

"Impossible!"

His voice held disbelief.

"How could two common medicinal herbs become this?"

Su Mu suddenly exerted force in his palm.

The earthy yellow stone shell cracked audibly.

A bluish-black core was revealed.

An interwoven scent of pungency and rich fragrance filled the air.

Li Chunyang's expression changed drastically.

"A highly toxic substance!"

He exclaimed urgently.

Ren Qingxin hurriedly covered her mouth and nose.

Su Mu handed over the shell fragments.

"Smell this instead."

His gaze fell upon the core.

Swirling colors shifted unpredictably across its surface.

Without hesitation, he picked up the core.

Under Li Chunyang's horrified stare, he swallowed it.

A warm sensation instantly spread through his limbs.

A hidden pain somewhere within his body quietly dissipated.

His mind achieved unprecedented clarity.

Ren Qingxin's beautiful eyes widened.

She watched the changes in Su Mu's aura.

She found herself unable to look away.

Su Mu let out a long breath.

The hidden ailment in his spirit finally showed improvement.

He turned to Ren Qingxin with a smile.

"Miss Ren, I will remember your kindness today."

"Ten million will be sent to you shortly."

"Let us each take half of these shell fragments."

"They may save lives in the future."

Ren Qingxin smiled gracefully.

"To receive Mr. Su's guidance."

"Qingxin has already gained invaluable knowledge."
###Chapter 26

The Ritz-Carlton stood as a luxurious six-star resort between the blue sea and sky.

Its gilded dome shimmered under the setting sun.

The nation's most renowned seafood feast was held here.

The Harper Group's PR department had booked the finest private dining room tonight.

Manager Thomas Carter and Assistant Manager Hannah Green led the team personally.

They were to finalize an important collaboration with the Hudson Group.

No one expected Zachary Hudson, the heir of the Hudson Group, to grace them with his presence.

His tall frame was accentuated by a custom silver-gray suit.

The floral shirt beneath was casually unbuttoned, revealing well-defined pectorals.

His mischievous phoenix eyes swept lazily across the room.

His gaze finally settled on the figure huddled in the corner, head bowed.

Thomas hurried forward. "Mr. Hudson, your presence is an honor."

A playful smile touched Zachary's lips.

Of course he had come personally.

He intended to discover who that audacious woman truly was.

The man at the head of the table lazily crossed his long legs.

Only then did the others dare to take their seats.

Vivian Bennett shrank into the most remote corner.

Her icy fingers clutched her skirt.

She prayed Zachary had already forgotten that night's incident.

An array of exquisite seafood dishes was presented.

A giant lobster exuded a tantalizing aroma.

But Vivian tasted nothing.

She was merely a newcomer in the PR department.

Her task tonight was to maintain a pleasant smile.

The negotiations were best left to the managers.

She kept her head down.

Minimizing her presence.

Zachary held court, laughing and chatting surrounded by everyone.

He seemed completely unaware of her.

Vivian began to relax.

Hannah's voice suddenly cut through the air.

"Vivian Bennett, aren't you going to propose a toast to Mr. Hudson?"

All eyes instantly focused on her.

Vivian's fingers trembled violently.

Her wine glass nearly slipped.

She stiffly raised her glass.

Looking up, she met Zachary's amused, knowing eyes.

He remembered her.
###Chapter 27

Vivian Bennett clenched her fists.

She prepared to force herself to stand and offer a toast to Zachary Hudson.

But unexpectedly, Zachary stood up first.

Under everyone's astonished gaze, he strode toward her with long steps.

"How could I let the beauty make the first move?"

His thin lips curved into a wicked smile.

His phoenix eyes glinted with dangerous light.

The smile seemed captivating, but was actually icy cold.

Vivian's spine stiffened.

Her expression froze instantly.

She couldn't even force a fake smile.

Did... did he recognize her?

While all the women present were jealous that Vivian had caught Zachary's attention, he stopped right in front of her.

His gaze was playful, filled with the interest of a hunter.

He clinked his glass with hers.

Anyone else would have been overjoyed.

But Vivian turned deathly pale, as if facing a great enemy.

After clinking glasses, Zachary suddenly leaned in close.

His scorching breath brushed her ear. "We meet again?"

Vivian's mind exploded.

All the blood in her body froze.

He remembered!

She was screaming internally, but had to maintain a professional smile on her face.

Today she represented the Sullivan Group's PR department. She absolutely could not lose her composure.

Zachary examined her smile, which was uglier than a cry, up close.

He frowned displeased.

His usually irresistible charm repeatedly failed with this woman.

Last time, she rejected him during their blind date.

This time, she looked at him as if she'd seen a ghost.

Zachary straightened up coldly and returned to his seat.

The usual flirtatious smile on his lips completely disappeared.

His supposedly perfect charm was utterly useless in front of Vivian.

This ungrateful woman dared to ignore him again and again?

Zachary felt publicly challenged by Vivian once more.

This feeling of losing control made him extremely unhappy.

Naturally, this meal was impossible to continue.

Zachary stood up abruptly and left without looking back.

The Hudson Group staff hurried after him.

Thomas Carter and the PR department beauties tried to salvage the situation.

But they didn't even get a glance from Zachary.

In the private dining room, Hannah Green immediately seized the opportunity to attack.

"Vivian Bennett, who do you think you are?" she scolded sharply, her face ugly. "How dare you make Young Master Hudson come toast you personally?"

Fiona Clark nearby was also full of jealousy.

She had had a crush on Zachary for a long time.

Today she finally had a chance to get close to him, but he completely ignored her.

Instead, this insignificant Vivian stole the spotlight.

She immediately chimed in. "Exactly! She has no manners at all! She made Young Master Hudson so angry he left! All our efforts are wasted now!"

Everyone else in the PR department also shot blaming looks at Vivian.
###Chapter 28

Vivian Bennett instantly became the target of everyone's hostility.

All eyes in the private dining room turned to her with accusation.

Only Emily Davis stepped forward.

"Didn't you all see Zachary Hudson make the first move? He got angry and ran off. Why blame Vivian?"

Fiona Clark let out a derisive laugh.

"It was an honor for Mr. Hudson to show interest in her. Who is she trying to fool with this act of purity?"

The surrounding colleagues nodded in agreement.

Vivian and Emily stared at them in disbelief.

Were they supposed to feel grateful for being harassed?

Hannah Green, the deputy manager, looked coldly at Vivian.

"If you're unwilling to make such small sacrifices, Vivian, you should leave the PR department now."

She turned and led everyone away, leaving Vivian behind.

Vivian stood frozen in place.

Was Hannah suggesting she should accept an under-the-table deal?

Was this how the PR department secured contracts?

She finally understood why the manager only selected young, attractive employees for these dinners.

They were essentially being offered up for Zachary Hudson's selection.

But Zachary wasn't truly interested in her.

This was his revenge for her rejecting him during their blind date.

He deliberately humiliated her in public.

And he succeeded.

Now she felt not only humiliated but had become the department's enemy.

Vivian and Emily walked out of the private dining room.

They stopped at the hotel entrance, stunned.

The company shuttle bus was already gone.

"They actually left us behind?" Emily exclaimed.

Vivian's expression turned grim. "It appears so."

Emily stamped her foot in frustration. "It's so far from the city center. How do we get back?"

Vivian felt guilty. "I'm sorry for getting you involved in this."

Emily immediately shook her head. "Don't say that. We're both newcomers. We should look out for each other."

Vivian felt deeply moved.

People as principled as Emily were rare these days.

"Let's ask the hotel if they have any cars available," Vivian suggested.

They turned toward the front desk.

They didn't notice Liam Sullivan standing just a few steps away.

He had come to the hotel tonight for a business meeting.

His business partner was respectfully seeing him out.

Liam's stern profile revealed no emotion.

But his peripheral vision had already settled on Vivian.

His hearing was exceptionally sharp.

He had caught every word of their conversation.

Especially Emily's statement about "rejecting Zachary Hudson's advances."

Liam's brow furrowed abruptly.

His handsome face instantly chilled with frosty intensity.
###Chapter 29

The business partner watched Liam Sullivan’s departing figure with growing unease.

He couldn't help but wonder which of his remarks had offended the powerful man.

Inside the Rolls-Royce, Liam Sullivan settled into his seat.

He immediately ordered his driver and assistant, Ryan Johnson, to contact Thomas Carter, the PR manager.

He needed to understand everything that had happened tonight.

When Thomas revealed that Vivian Bennett had been assigned to entertain Zachary Hudson alongside other PR beauties, and that Zachary had publicly shown interest in her, Liam’s handsome face instantly darkened.

The temperature inside the car seemed to drop sharply.

Ryan, who was driving, shivered involuntarily.

At that moment, he spotted Vivian Bennett and Emily Davis waiting for a taxi outside the hotel.

Ryan quickly suggested, "Sir, it’s late. It might not be safe for two women to wait here alone. Should we offer them a ride back to the city?"

Ryan’s ability to read his boss’s moods had kept him in his position for years.

He had already sensed that Liam treated Vivian differently ever since he was asked to report on her daily activities in the PR department.

Now, seeing Liam’s stormy expression after learning about Zachary’s advances, Ryan was certain his boss had more than professional concern for Vivian.

It was no coincidence they had run into her waiting outside.

He knew Liam wouldn’t want her taking a taxi alone at night.

So he made the suggestion naturally.

To his surprise, Liam pressed his lips together and said coldly, "Do what you want. It has nothing to do with me."

Ryan was taken aback.

Nothing to do with him?

In the past, when other women tried to hitch a ride or flirt with Liam, Ryan had been sternly rebuked for even asking.

But now, when it came to Vivian, it was suddenly Ryan’s decision?

Clearly, Liam was giving silent permission without admitting it.

Ryan knew better than to point it out.

He pulled the car over and stepped out to extend the invitation.

"Vivian, please get in."

Vivian and Emily were still waiting for the hotel shuttle when they heard a male voice.

They turned in surprise.

"Assistant Ryan?" Emily blinked several times, hardly believing that Liam Sullivan’s personal assistant was standing before them.

"Get in?" Vivian was stunned, not quite processing the offer.

"I’ll drive you back to the city," Ryan said with a friendly smile.

Vivian was speechless.

Assistant Ryan himself driving them back?

Since when did they deserve such VIP treatment?

Emily pinched her own arm. "Am I dreaming?"

The company shuttle had left without them, stranding them at the hotel.

She thought the company had abandoned them.

Instead, Assistant Ryan was personally offering them a ride?

"Please, the boss is waiting in the car," Ryan gestured again, urging them to enter.
###Chapter 30

His words only made Vivian Bennett more reluctant to get into the car.

She stood frozen in place, her fingers unconsciously clutching the hem of her shirt.

"Really... there's no need to trouble you," she said, her voice tight, the words almost forced through her teeth.

Ride in the big boss's car? She didn't have the nerve.

Who knew if the news of her messing up the Zachary Hudson deal had already reached Liam Sullivan's ears?

Him asking her to get in the car now was practically an invitation into a trap.

She had no intention of walking right into it.

However, before her words even faded, Emily Davis beside her had already darted toward the black Rolls-Royce like a happy little bird.

Vivian sighed inwardly, forced to steel herself and follow.

How could she have forgotten? Emily was Liam Sullivan's number one fan.

How could Emily possibly pass up such a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity?

Liam Sullivan sat upright in the back seat, his impeccably tailored suit accentuating his straight posture.

An undeniable, powerful aura radiated from him, like an emperor in control of everything.

Emily excitedly greeted him, but he only responded with a faint "Hmm," his expression cold and stern, intimidating to behold.

Vivian originally wanted to sit next to Emily.

But Liam showed no intention of moving over.

They naturally didn't dare ask the big boss to make room for them.

Vivian had no choice but to walk around to the other side, open the door, and get in.

She deliberately pressed herself close to the window, leaving an empty seat between her and Liam, drawing a clear line.

In her peripheral vision, the lines of his profile were sharp and cold, his thin lips pressed tightly together.

She simply remained silent, skipping the greeting altogether.

Ryan Johnson, the driver, started the engine, and the car smoothly merged into the night.

Dead silence filled the cabin.

Both Vivian and Emily sat ramrod straight, even breathing lightly.

Afraid that any slight misstep might anger the important figure in the back seat.

Suddenly, Vivian's phone vibrated.

It was a message from Emily.

"Ahhh! I'm actually in the big boss's car! It's like a dream!"

Vivian pressed her lips together helplessly.

Another message popped up.

"The boss is even more handsome up close! I could admire this face for a lifetime!"

"Tonight, a disaster turned into a blessing! This is something I can boast about for ten years!"

Vivian didn't know whether to laugh or cry.

Ten years? Wasn't that a bit exaggerated?

Perhaps the cabin was too quiet, or perhaps the driver Ryan couldn't contain his curiosity.

He drove while occasionally stealing glances back through the rearview mirror.

An old woman suddenly darted across the road ahead.

Ryan slammed on the brakes in a panic.

Caught off guard, Vivian was thrown forward.

Just as her forehead was about to hit the front seat, a large hand suddenly wrapped around her waist—
###Chapter 31

Vivian Bennett collided abruptly with a scorching chest.

The scent of tobacco mixed with cedar instantly enveloped her.

She trembled.

Looking up, she met Liam Sullivan’s lowered gaze.

His eyes were deep like whirlpools.

Her heart skipped a beat.

Panic flashed and vanished.

She forced a calm smile. “Thank you.”

She tried to pull away.

Liam tightened his arm around her.

“Did you fall?” His voice was husky.

His breath brushed her ear.

Her ears burned. “I’m fine.”

She pushed subtly.

His palm remained seared against her waist.

The fabric was too thin.

The heat of his fingertips nearly scorched her skin.

Liam’s gaze darkened.

“Watch your step.” He finally released her.

Vivian murmured in agreement, her cheeks flushed.

The car stopped at that moment.

Ryan Johnson had driven to Emily Davis’s apartment first.

The rear door opened.

Emily was about to say goodbye when Vivian climbed out too.

“Why are you getting out?” Emily asked, surprised.

“I’ll come up for some water.” Vivian gripped her bag strap.

Emily looked uncomfortable. “My mom’s already asleep. Another time?”

“Is it inconvenient?” Vivian pressed.

“She’s a light sleeper. I’ll get scolded if I bring someone home.” Emily smiled wryly.

Vivian realized she lived with family.

It really wasn’t appropriate to disturb them so late.

“See you tomorrow.” Vivian waved.

Emily hurried away.

Vivian turned and froze.

The black Rolls-Royce was still there.

Ryan leaned out the window. “Miss Bennett, please get in.”

She hesitated, glancing at the back seat.

Liam’s profile was hidden in shadow, his expression unreadable.

The night breeze was cool.

Vivian finally opened the door.

Only the two of them remained inside.

She noticed immediately upon sitting.

Liam had moved closer without her realizing.

Their legs were almost touching.

Her spine stiffened.

The tips of her ears turned crimson.

She fanned herself nervously. “It’s so hot tonight...”

No one replied.

Only his heavy gaze pressed down on her.
###Chapter 32

Ryan restarted the car.

He glanced back at her with surprise.

"The weather forecast said it's cooling down today. Only nineteen degrees. How could you be hot?"

Vivian Bennett remained silent.

The luxury car slowly merged into traffic.

Ryan learned from his earlier mistake.

He did not dare to get distracted again.

He focused on gripping the steering wheel.

His eyes remained fixed on the road ahead.

Vivian felt Liam Sullivan subtly moving closer.

His presence faintly surrounded her.

She wanted to tell him to move away several times.

His proximity made her extremely uncomfortable.

But the words died on her lips each time.

Liam suddenly leaned in closer.

His husky voice whispered near her ear. "Your heart is beating very fast."

His warm breath brushed against her neck.

A tingling sensation instantly spread through her.

Damn it!

Could he please move away?

Vivian forced a stiff smile. "Maybe... I'm still shaken from the scare earlier."

Liam narrowed his deep, dark eyes. "Earlier?"

She wished she could bite her tongue off.

That excuse was terribly weak.

It had been over half an hour since the accident.

Even with the slowest reflexes, her reaction was delayed.

She desperately tried to calm her racing heart.

She nodded bravely. "Yes."
