Liam's deep voice held suppressed anger.

Vivian blinked in confusion.

"What calls?"

His eyes turned icy cold.

He leaned in, his thin lips almost brushing her ear.

"Playing dumb?"

Vivian instinctively turned her head away.

"I really don't know..."

Before she could finish, Liam suddenly captured her lips.

The kiss came without warning.

Vivian's eyes widened in shock.

She struggled fiercely, but he pinned her hands above her head.

Liam's kiss carried a punishing intensity.

He forced her lips apart, plundering ruthlessly.

Vivian felt like she couldn't breathe.

A mix of pain and unfamiliar tingling spread from her lips.

She let out a helpless whimper.

Liam pulled back slightly.

Just as Vivian thought it was over, he deepened the kiss again.

More fierce, more intense than before.

Her legs grew weak, barely able to support her.

Liam swiftly wrapped an arm around her waist, holding her firmly against him.

After what felt like an eternity, he finally released her.

Both were breathing heavily.

Liam rested his forehead against hers, his voice hoarse.

"Give me your phone."

Vivian stared at him dazedly.

She instinctively pulled out her phone and handed it over.

Liam took it and unlocked it with practiced ease.

His expression darkened instantly when he saw his number saved as "Harassment Call" in her contacts.
###Chapter 85

Liam Sullivan's long fingers swiftly swiped across her phone screen.

He saved his own number into her contacts.

"Do not delete this."

He shoved the phone back into her hand, gripping her wrist firmly.

His low, husky voice held an undeniable command.

Vivian Bennett blinked, nodding obediently.

Her docile demeanor stirred something within Liam.

"Next time you dare not answer my call, I'll assume you're playing hard to get."

He narrowed his eyes, gazing at her swollen lips.

His large hand slipped beneath her nightgown.

"Stop this!" Vivian panicked.

They were still outside.

What if someone saw them?

Liam ignored her resistance.

His fingertips traced patterns across her smooth skin.

Vivian's entire body grew feverish.

A blush spread across her cheeks.

"Let me go, let go now!" She struggled fiercely.

Terrified he might do something even more reckless outdoors.

Liam buried his face in her neck.

Complex emotions churned in his eyes.

He slowly calmed his breathing.

Finally, he released her.

Vivian's heart hammered like a drum.

She stared at the man's tense, handsome face.

The desire in his eyes hadn't faded.

She felt both flustered and furious.

She shoved Liam away violently.

Turning, she fled back toward the house.

Watching her panicked retreat, Liam's lips curved into a faint smile.

He pulled out a cigarette and placed it between his lips.

Lighting it, he took a deep drag.

Blue smoke wreathed his sharp features.

It made him appear especially enigmatic in the night.

Vivian ran all the way home without stopping.

She collapsed onto her bed, tossing and turning sleeplessly.

Liam's forceful kisses and entanglement haunted her thoughts.

Images of Isabella Sullivan directing Hannah Green to harass her also surfaced.

She felt trapped in a web woven by the siblings.

There seemed no escape.

Morning sunlight streamed through the floor-to-ceiling windows into the living room.

Liam had just descended the stairs when he spotted two figures on the sofa.

Isabella Sullivan was chatting cheerfully with Eleanor Sullivan.

The elderly woman looked radiant and in high spirits.

"Grandma? Isabella? What brings you here?"

Liam's brow furrowed slightly as he approached.

"Brother..." Isabella called to him softly.

Eleanor shot him a displeased glare. "You refused to bring Isabella to visit me at the estate."

"So I had to come see you myself."

Liam's voice was low. "The company has been extremely busy lately."

"I truly haven't had any free time."

As he spoke, a cold glint flashed in Isabella's eyes.

Was her brother really that busy?

Or was he busy in Vivian Bennett's bed?

Before, she thought although her brother was cold toward her, at least he had no other women.

He always maintained his integrity.

He never entangled himself with the opposite sex.

Now, for Vivian's sake, he even stood up his grandparents.

It was unprecedentedly absurd.

"Busy, busy, busy. That's all you ever are," Eleanor said unhappily.

"Next month is your grandfather's seventieth birthday."

"Let's see if you're still too busy then."

Liam finally remembered his grandfather's birthday was next month.

How could he have forgotten such an important date?
###Chapter 86

Liam Sullivan met her gaze steadily. "Don't worry, Grandma. I will be there on time for Grandfather's seventieth birthday."

Eleanor Sullivan's expression softened slightly. "That's more like it."

She suddenly remembered something and pulled a stack of carefully prepared photos from her pocket.

"These are pictures of several young ladies from good families. See if any catch your eye? If you find one you like, you can bring her to meet your grandfather at the banquet."

The old woman's eyes shone with anticipation, eager to arrange her grandson's marriage immediately.

Liam remained unmoved.

He didn't even glance at the photos and walked directly toward the foyer.

"I'm going to the office."

"Stop right there!" Eleanor's anger flared at his dismissive attitude.

Liam didn't pause, his figure quickly disappearing outside the door.

Eleanor stamped her cane in frustration.

"This child, is he really determined to remain alone forever?"

He was the only heir to the Sullivan family, yet he showed no interest in women.

She had gone through great efforts to introduce so many society ladies and famous actresses, but he hadn't favored a single one.

Eleanor turned her sharp gaze toward Isabella Sullivan beside her.

Was this adopted daughter still scheming for her grandson?

"Isabella, the Sullivan family has given you everything you deserve all these years we've raised you. Don't entertain thoughts about things that aren't meant for you." Eleanor's warning was low and pointed.

Isabella seethed inwardly.

She knew the old woman looked down on her orphan background, fearing she would taint the Sullivan family's noble bloodline.

Moreover, as an adopted daughter of the Sullivans, being with Liam would create a huge scandal.

The Sullivan family couldn't afford such embarrassment.

Was her love destined to be sacrificed?

"You misunderstand, Grandma. I've always regarded my brother as my real sibling." Isabella forced a smile, quickly defending herself.

"It better be that way." Eleanor's authority remained undiminished.

Isabella's eyes shifted cunningly as she lowered her voice with feigned concern. "But I've heard there's a woman at the company who has designs on my brother?"

Eleanor's eyes turned icy. "Who?"

Isabella leaned closer. "I think her name is Vivian Bennett. She's always trying to cling to my brother, dreaming of rising to the top overnight."

"Vivian Bennett?" Eleanor frowned thoughtfully.

She had never heard that name before.

"What's her background?"

Isabella curled her lip disdainfully. "She's just a low-level employee."

Eleanor's brow furrowed deeply. "Isabella, your grandfather and I placed you in Sullivan Group so you could keep a close watch on Liam..."

Isabella immediately understood the implication.

"Don't worry, Grandma. I won't let any of those unworthy women get close to my brother." She pledged confidently.

With Grandma's support, she felt even more emboldened to deal with Vivian.

Her brother could only belong to her.

If she couldn't have him, no one else would either.

Eleanor nodded in satisfaction. "At least I haven't spoiled you all these years for nothing."

"Grandma, shall I make you something else to eat?" Isabella asked solicitously.

"No need. I'm not accustomed to your cooking." Eleanor refused coldly.

Isabella's smile froze instantly.

Grandma only treated her kindly when Liam was present.

The moment he left, she became nothing.

If she couldn't marry Liam, she would always remain an outsider in the Sullivan family.
###Chapter 87

Liam Sullivan returned to the company.

His grandmother's sudden visit and her insistence on arranging blind dates left him deeply unsettled.

He massaged his temples, his thoughts inevitably drifting to Vivian Bennett.

If she were his chosen partner, the endless stream of arranged meetings would become bearable.

A faint smile touched his lips at the thought of her.

He took out his phone and dialed her number directly.

He knew she would avoid him after last night, but he couldn't resist hearing her voice.

Emily Davis noticed Vivian's phone ringing incessantly on her desk.

The screen displayed a contact saved simply as "B."

Hesitating for a moment, Emily answered it. "Hello?"

An unfamiliar female voice made Liam frown.

His tone turned cold. "Where is Vivian?"

"She went to get coffee beans. She hasn't returned yet," Emily replied truthfully.

"Coffee beans?" Liam's voice dropped several degrees. "That is not part of her duties."

Emily grew nervous. "Manager Hannah Green assigned the task. We had no choice..."

Liam's expression darkened.

Manager Hannah, the one he had transferred to a neighboring city, had returned already?

And she was targeting Vivian again. She clearly hadn't learned her lesson.

His face instantly clouded with anger.

...

Vivian had been sent to fetch coffee beans first thing in the morning by Manager Hannah.

She went to the cafeteria, only to be told the coffee beans were out of stock and she needed to retrieve them from the underground warehouse.

Reluctant but wanting to avoid further trouble, she headed to the warehouse.

"Hello? I'm here for the coffee beans!"

She called out several times outside the warehouse door.

Receiving no response, she ventured inside alone.

The moment she stepped in, the lights abruptly cut out.

The heavy door slammed shut behind her.

Vivian was trapped inside.

"Open the door! I'm still in here!" She rushed to the door, pounding on it with her fists.

Muffled voices echoed from outside, but no one responded to her pleas.

"Let me out! There's someone in here!"

She screamed until her voice grew hoarse, her palms stinging from the impact.

Complete darkness enveloped the warehouse.

Vivian was terrified of the dark.

Her claustrophobia began to trigger.

Her breathing became labored and ragged.

She collapsed weakly onto the cold floor.

"Help... me..."

Her cries grew fainter and fainter.

...

Back in the CEO's office.

Liam had just returned from a meeting.

His secretary approached him quietly. "Boss, shall I arrange lunch for you?"
###Chapter 88

Liam Sullivan tapped his fingers lightly on the desk.

His voice was deep as he ordered, "Order two portions."

His secretary was momentarily stunned.

She didn't dare ask any questions.

She nodded respectfully and withdrew.

Liam sat back in his leather chair.

He dialed Vivian Bennett's number again.

He had originally intended to invite her to lunch.

Unexpectedly, Emily Davis was the one who answered again.

"Is she still not back?" Liam's voice was low.

It carried an unquestionable authority.

"No..." Emily's voice was uneasy. "It's so strange. She should have been back long ago... Could something have happened?"

The word "happened" made Liam's eyes darken instantly.

What kind of accident could happen while fetching coffee beans?

Unless someone had planned this—

He stood up abruptly.

He strode out of his office.

"Good afternoon, Mr. Sullivan!"

The cafeteria staff bowed hurriedly upon seeing the CEO appear unexpectedly.

Liam's voice was cold. "Where is Vivian Bennett?"

An employee's eyes darted nervously. "Who?"

Liam's gaze was sharp as a blade. "Vivian Bennett. Where is she?"

Cold sweat broke out on the employee's forehead.

He braced himself and said, "I... I don't know..."

A cold glint flashed in Liam's eyes. "Do I need to review the security footage?"

The employee trembled from head to toe.

His face instantly turned ashen.

How could he have forgotten about the surveillance cameras.

He might have been able to bluff his way through with anyone else.

But this was Liam Sullivan.

The entire group belonged to him.

"I remember now!" the employee quickly changed his tune. "The new lady from Public Relations, Miss Bennett, right? The coffee beans ran out earlier. She went to the warehouse to get some herself..."

Liam turned and rushed toward the warehouse.

The basement warehouse.

Vivian was curled up in a corner.

Her sobs had become hoarse.

The darkness swallowed her like a tidal wave.

She was terrified of the dark.

Ever since her mother passed away, abuse from her father and stepmother became routine.

As a young child, she was often locked in a dark storage room.

Those painful memories were carved deep into her bones.

"Daddy... save me..." She whimpered helplessly.

It was as if she had become that trembling little girl again.

But William White never appeared.

He allowed her stepmother's torment to grow increasingly cruel.

She thought growing up would allow her to escape all this.

She believed independent living would free her from the nightmares.

But when darkness fell, she realized she had never truly left that storage room.
###Chapter 89

A suffocating sensation washed over her.

Vivian Bennett struggled to breathe.

Her brain felt dizzy from lack of oxygen.

"Don't hit me..."

"Daddy, I'll be obedient..."

"Mommy, where are you..."

She was transported back to those unbearable years.

William White's cold expression.

Linda White's hypocritical smile.

Brandon White's arrogant face.

These memories clung to her like shadows.

"You eat the White family's food, you do the White family's work!"

"I, William White, have no daughter like you!"

"You're nothing but our servant!"

"No..."

"Stop!"

Vivian struggled desperately.

Tears blurred her vision.

She couldn't go on like this.

She had to break free from this cage.

She had to escape these nightmares.

She had to become strong.

She had to take control of her own destiny.

She had to survive.

She had to live with dignity.

The warehouse door suddenly burst open.

Blinding sunlight poured in.

"Vivian!"

Liam Sullivan's anxious voice called out.

He quickly spotted the figure in the corner.

Vivian curled on the ground, trembling.

"Vivian!"

Liam rushed forward.

He gathered her into his arms.

Her face was pale as paper.

Her body shook uncontrollably.

"Don't be afraid, I'm here."

He whispered comforting words in her ear.

Vivian raised her tear-filled eyes.

She met his worried gaze.

It was really him?

He came to save her?

"Breathe deeply, take it slow."

Liam keenly felt her distress.

He immediately carried her out of the warehouse.

He comforted her softly throughout the journey.

Fresh air filled her lungs.

Sunlight warmed her skin.

Vivian gradually calmed down.

"I thought... I was going to die."

Her voice choked with emotion.

When she was locked in the storage room before.

No one had ever come to save her.

This was the first time.

Someone had rescued her from the darkness.

"I'm here, don't be afraid."

Liam's eyes filled with worries.

He regret himself for arriving too late.

He let her get hurt right in the company.

He tightened his arms, holding her securely.

Vivian nestled against his chest.

She finally closed her eyes in peace.

This was her safest harbor.
###Chapter 90

Liam Sullivan carried the unconscious Vivian Bennett, swiftly heading toward his limousine.

He carefully placed her in the back seat.

"To the hospital," he said, his voice low and urgent.

The driver immediately started the engine.

The car sped off like an arrow.

Liam took out his phone and dialed Ryan Johnson's number.

"Find out who is behind the attack on Vivian Bennett," he said coldly.

"Yes, boss," Ryan responded promptly.

Liam ended the call.

His gaze fell on Vivian's pale face.

She suddenly woke up.

She found herself held in Liam's arms.

Instinctively, she struggled to break free.

"Don't move," Liam restrained her. "We're going to the hospital."

His tone brooked no argument.

Vivian softly refused. "I'm fine now."

She didn't want to trouble him further.

Liam's eyes darkened.

He held her firmly in place.

"What happened just now?" he asked gravely.

Vivian lowered her eyelids.

Her voice trembled slightly. "I went to the storage room to get coffee beans. The door suddenly closed. The lights went out too..."

Recalling the scene, she shivered uncontrollably.

Liam watched her frightened expression.

His brows furrowed tightly.

"Do you have claustrophobia?" he asked perceptively.

Vivian nodded gently, admitting the fact.

Liam's face instantly turned grim.

He couldn't imagine the torment for someone with claustrophobia locked in a dark storage room.

"Who told you to go to the storage room?" he pressed further.

"The staff at the cafeteria," Vivian answered. "He said the coffee beans were out. He told me to get them myself."

Liam's eyes turned sharp. "And who told you to get the coffee beans?"

"Manager Hannah," Vivian said softly.

Hannah Green had just returned from a business trip this morning.

She immediately started ordering Vivian around.

It wasn't even her job, but she was forced to accept.

Liam's eyes turned icy cold.

Hannah Green. He remembered that name.

This wasn't the first time.

"Why didn't you say anything?" His voice suppressed anger. "Why didn't you tell me you were being bullied?"

Vivian pressed her lips together.

She didn't know how to answer.

There was always an invisible chasm between them.

"You must tell me in the future," Liam said sternly. "Do you hear me?"

Vivian looked into his determined eyes.

She finally gave a slight nod.
###Chapter 91

Vivian Bennett felt completely drained.

Her recent claustrophobia attack had sapped all her strength.

She didn't even have the energy to argue.

Her eyelids grew heavy.

She soon fell back into unconsciousness in Liam Sullivan's arms.

Rapid footsteps echoed through the hospital corridor.

A doctor hurried in to give Vivian a full examination.

Liam stood outside the hospital room, his expression terrifyingly dark.

The doctor finished the examination and reported respectfully.

"Mr. Sullivan, Miss Bennett is physically fine. She's just suffered from shock."

Liam's gaze was sharp. "Are you certain?"

The doctor lowered his head nervously. "If you're concerned, we can keep her for observation for a few days."

Liam's voice was cold. "Admit her."

His phone suddenly rang.

Ryan Johnson's voice came through the line.

"Boss, the cafeteria staff has confessed."

Liam's eyes turned icy. "Go on."

"He admitted to taking money to deliberately lead Miss Bennett to the storage room."

"Who ordered it?"

"Contact was made through an anonymous number. The bank transfer was also from an anonymous account."

A cold glint flashed in Liam's eyes.

Someone was deliberately targeting Vivian.

They dared to make a move right under his nose.

"Focus the investigation on Hannah Green."

Liam's voice was frigid.

She was the one in the PR department with the deepest conflict with Vivian.

She was always causing trouble for Vivian.

Sending her to pick up coffee beans right after returning from the neighboring city was too much of a coincidence.

"Yes, sir!"

Ryan responded immediately.

Liam ended the call and walked toward the VIP room.

Vivian lay quietly on the hospital bed, her face pale.

He sat by the bed and held her cold hand.

Vivian remained unconscious throughout the night.

She slowly woke the next morning.

Opening her eyes, she saw Liam leaning on the sofa.

He wore the same suit from yesterday. Stubble shaded his jaw.

He had clearly stayed with her all night.

Vivian was surprised.

Everyone said Liam had severe mysophobia.

Yet he had stayed overnight for her.

She shifted slightly.

Liam's eyes opened immediately.

"How do you feel?"

His voice was low and husky.

"Much better. Thank you for saving me."

Vivian expressed her gratitude sincerely.

Liam gave a slight nod. He called to have breakfast delivered.

"Boss, you should go attend to your work."

Vivian felt bad for taking up his time.

Liam's expression darkened. "I'm not busy."

Vivian was instantly speechless.

She excused herself to wash up and retreated to the bathroom.

Her reflection in the mirror showed a deathly pale face.

The terror from the storage room was still vivid.

She had nearly suffocated to death.

Liam was truly her savior.

When she emerged from the bathroom, breakfast had arrived.

Exquisite dishes covered the table.

Liam stood by the window waiting for her.

Sunlight fell on him, outlining his tall, straight figure.
###Chapter 92

He motioned for her to come closer.

Vivian Bennett hesitated before returning to the hospital bedside.

Liam Sullivan efficiently set up the small table tray and arranged the breakfast neatly.

Vivian looked down at the spread.

The meal was exquisitely prepared and abundant.

She picked up the glass of milk and took a small sip.

"Take the next few days off to rest at home. Don't come to the office," Liam stated abruptly.

Vivian's fingers stilled slightly.

Taking consecutive days off would be difficult to explain to the manager.

Hannah Green was just looking for an opportunity to cause trouble for her.

Liam saw right through her concern. "I'll personally approve your leave request."

Vivian was stunned.

With the CEO's special approval, the manager naturally wouldn't dare say anything.

But did this count as special privilege?

Was she essentially using backdoor connections?

"You were injured on duty. You deserve proper recovery time," Liam's gaze was intense, his tone leaving no room for argument.

"Thank you, Boss Liam," Vivian replied softly.

Noticing her deliberate distance, Liam's brow furrowed slightly.

"If you really want to thank me, why not consider my previous proposal?"

His eyes were deep and penetrating, as if trying to draw her in.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

"Do you really need a girlfriend to ward off admirers?"

With his qualifications, why would he need to go to such lengths?

Unless it was for Isabella Sullivan?

"I genuinely do need one," Liam's gaze was burning, impossible to avoid.

The pressure from his family was increasing daily.

If he didn't take some action soon, his parents would likely intervene in his marriage plans.

Vivian opened her mouth to speak but stopped herself.

In the end, she just silently lowered her head and continued eating.

Agree to be his girlfriend?

It sounded like something out of a fantasy.

"Silence means agreement," Liam's voice was low, carrying a magnetic, almost hypnotic quality.

Vivian sighed in resignation. "So saving my life means I have to offer myself in return?"

A hint of amusement flickered in Liam's eyes. "Shouldn't it?"

"Are you seriously doing this?" Vivian found it absurd.

What would Isabella do then?

"Yes," Liam stated decisively.

Vivian was speechless.

Yet doubts still lingered in her heart.

She quickly finished her breakfast.

Liam called for the doctor to reexamine her.

After confirming she was fine, he personally handled her discharge procedures.

The black limousine came to a smooth stop at the entrance of Lakeside Manor.

"Boss Liam, I'm here," Vivian unbuckled her seatbelt and turned to say goodbye.

Her fingertips had just touched the door handle when his hand suddenly closed around her wrist.
###Chapter 93

Vivian Bennett turned around, blinking in confusion. "Boss Liam, is there something else?"

A dark shadow suddenly closed in.

Liam Sullivan moved closer without warning.

She instinctively stepped back.

His deep, intense eyes were now right in front of her.

Vivian wore no makeup.

Yet her features were strikingly exquisite.

Her skin was fair and luminous.

Even without cosmetics, her beauty was dazzling.

Liam's gaze swept over her brows and eyes.

It finally settled on her rosy lips.

He wanted to taste them.

His scorching presence enveloped her completely.

Vivian swallowed lightly.

Her heartbeat suddenly lost its rhythm.

"You—"

Her unfinished words were swallowed.

Liam's handsome face suddenly loomed large.

A warmth pressed against her lips.

His arm locked firmly around her waist.

Vivian froze.

Her pupils contracted sharply.

He was kissing her again?

The kiss was fierce and consuming.

Her mind went completely blank.

She didn't know how much time had passed.

Liam finally released her.

He pulled a black card from his pocket.

He pressed it into her palm. "Take it."

Vivian looked down.

The diamond black card glittered brilliantly.

"What does this mean?"

"As my girlfriend for three months, I will cover all your expenses."

He stared at her pale, delicate face.

His thin lips parted slightly.

Vivian was stunned.

When had she agreed to this?

Did silence count as consent?

What was this black card supposed to mean?

Was she his girlfriend or his kept woman?

"I have my own money," she said, trying to refuse.

Liam's expression darkened.

"Take it."

His voice was low and brooked no argument.

Refusal seemed like a direct challenge.

Vivian didn't want to anger him.

But...

This card was far from ordinary.

"Aren't you afraid I'll run off with the money?" she joked, looking up.

Liam's eyes deepened.

"If you dare run, you'll be responsible for a lifetime."

The corner of his mouth lifted in a warning curve.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

Responsible for a lifetime?

She absolutely did not want that.

She hurriedly tucked the card into her bag.

"Boss Liam, I will keep it safe. I'll return it exactly as it is after three months."
###Chapter 94

Liam Sullivan felt a sudden weight in his chest.

Was she really this eager to cut ties with him?

"Mr. Sullivan, I'll be leaving now."

Vivian Bennett curved her lips into a polite smile.

Her tone was respectful yet distant.

Without waiting for his response, she quickly pushed the door open and got out of the car.

She was afraid that if she hesitated for even a second, he would pin her against the car and kiss her until she couldn't breathe.

She rushed into the gated community without looking back.

She ran all the way home.

The big boss had personally approved her leave, so she hadn't gone to the company these past few days.

But she wasn't idle.

She went to the Seattle Sanitarium to visit her grandmother, Margaret Bennett.

Her grandmother was advanced in years and suffered from a rare disease, requiring long-term care.

Vivian usually had to work to earn money, so she could only entrust her grandmother to the sanitarium.

She visited whenever she had free time.

These days off were a perfect opportunity to spend more time with her.

The doctors said her condition had been stable recently.

She hadn't had any episodes.

Seeing the fine weather, Vivian wanted to take her grandmother out for some fresh air.

"Grandma, how about we go to Le Bernardin?"

It was her grandmother's favorite restaurant from the old days.

The old woman's eyes lit up, but then she shook her head.

"It's too expensive. Let's not go."

"I'm earning money now. Let me treat you just this once?" Vivian coaxed gently.

"You work hard for your money. Don't waste it." The old woman still couldn't bring herself to agree.

Vivian linked her arm through her grandmother's. "My performance has been good lately. The boss gave me a bonus. Let's go celebrate with me?"

The old woman looked skeptical. "Really?"

Vivian nodded emphatically. "Of course it's true. If we don't go soon, their signature dishes will be sold out."

She finally managed to persuade her grandmother.

She hailed a taxi and helped her grandmother inside.

But shortly after they set off, clear skies suddenly turned overcast with dark clouds.

It soon began to rain.

The driver slammed on the brakes and pulled over to the side of the road.

"Driver, what's wrong?"

Vivian shielded her grandmother and asked urgently.

"Sorry, miss. Car trouble. Let me get out and take a look."

The driver got out and popped the hood.

Vivian thought it would be fixed quickly.

But after waiting over half an hour, the driver still hadn't returned.

The rain grew heavier.

After a few words to her grandmother, Vivian grabbed the spare umbrella and got out of the car.

Inside the Rolls-Royce.

Isabella Sullivan had managed to get into Liam Sullivan's car.

She spent the whole ride trying to ingratiate herself.

"Brother, how about having dinner together tonight? It's been so long since you spent time with me and Brandon."

She made the suggestion softly, deliberately using their younger brother as an excuse.

Liam still refused.

"I have plans tonight."

His voice was cold, commanding authority without any hint of anger.

Isabella wasn't ready to give up. She leaned closer to him.

"Brother, when will you have time to take Brandon and me abroad for a vacation?" She batted her eyelashes, her face full of expectation.
###Chapter 95

Liam Sullivan's expression was indifferent.
"My schedule is completely full lately."

Isabella Sullivan's smile instantly froze.
Again.
He always used work as an excuse to reject her.
She clenched her fingers in frustration.
A stubborn glint flashed in her eyes.

"Grandfather's birthday banquet is coming soon," she softened her tone.
"I haven't chosen a gift yet. Will you come with me to pick one out?"
She deliberately invoked the family patriarch.
She thought this time she would surely succeed.

Liam suddenly fell silent.
His gaze traveled through the car window.
It settled on a specific point in the pouring rain.
"Pull over," he abruptly ordered the driver.

Isabella was stunned.
"Brother?"
They were on an overpass in a torrential downpour.
Why stop now?

Liam had already grabbed a black umbrella.
He pushed the car door open and stepped out.
Isabella followed his line of sight.
Through the rain, she saw a familiar figure.
It stood beside a taxi with its hazard lights on.

It was Vivian Bennett.
Isabella's pupils constricted sharply.

Liam strode through the curtain of rain.
The slender figure trembled slightly in the wind.
The umbrella was useless against the driving rain.
She was completely drenched.

Vivian seemed to sense something.
She looked up.
Their eyes met.
Her breath caught.
"Mr. Sullivan?"

She never expected to see him here.
Liam frowned deeply.
"What happened?"
His low voice carried through the sound of the rain.

Vivian explained awkwardly.
"I took Grandma out for dinner and got caught in the storm.
The taxi broke down..."

Everything had gone wrong today.
Liam stared at her intently.
"Get in the car."
His tone brooked no argument.

Vivian instinctively refused.
"The driver will have it fixed soon..."

Even though he had demanded she be his girlfriend for three months.
Even though he had given her a black card.
An unbridgeable chasm lay between them.
She didn't dare take it seriously.

The air around Liam grew icy.
The taxi driver's hand shook.
His wrench dropped into a puddle.
This man's presence was too intimidating.
The pressure was overwhelming.

"Miss, you should just go," the driver urged hastily.
"This car won't be fixed anytime soon."
###Chapter 96

Vivian Bennett frowned at the driver's words.

The rain grew heavier.

Liam Sullivan's deep gaze settled on her.

"Get in."

His voice was low, leaving no room for argument.

Vivian hesitated briefly.

She didn't mind getting wet herself.

But her grandmother was elderly and couldn't stay in the taxi.

She looked up at Liam. "My grandmother is still in the car."

She couldn't leave her grandmother alone.

Liam turned his attention to the taxi's back seat.

An elderly woman bundled in warm clothing sat there.

He walked over.

"I'll carry her to the car."

Vivian froze.

She hadn't expected him to offer to carry her grandmother.

Liam opened the car door and greeted Grandma Margaret politely.

He bent down and carefully lifted her onto his back.

Vivian quickly followed with an umbrella.

Rain soaked through his suit.

A ripple of emotion stirred within her.

Since her grandmother fell ill, even relatives had kept their distance.

Only Vivian had persisted in caring for her.

A man of Liam's status didn't hesitate to carry her grandmother.

The Rolls-Royce driver stared in astonishment.

He hurried out of the car.

"Boss Liam, let me do it."

Liam shook his head.

He personally carried Grandma Margaret into the luxury vehicle.

Vivian closed the umbrella and got in.

She looked up and met Isabella Sullivan's scrutinizing gaze.

The wealthy socialite wore impeccable makeup and maintained an elegant posture.

Vivian prepared to greet her.

But Isabella focused solely on Liam.

She took out a tissue to wipe his face.

Liam raised his hand to stop her.

"Not necessary."

Isabella bit her lip, clutching the tissue with visible hurt.

He had rejected her in front of others.

Liam instructed the driver to bring towels.

He handed them to Vivian and her grandmother.

"Dry off."

Vivian accepted them with thanks.

She helped her grandmother first, then tended to herself.

Grandma Margaret was barely damp.

But Liam's hair still dripped with water.

Vivian finished drying and looked up.

She met Isabella's icy stare.

The gaze felt sharp as a blade.
###Chapter 97

Isabella Sullivan’s gaze drifted subtly toward Vivian Bennett before she turned to Liam Sullivan as if nothing had happened.

Her voice was dripping with sweetness. “Brother, who is this?”

Liam’s thin lips parted.

Vivian cut him off. “Mr. Sullivan, I am Vivian Bennett, an employee of Sullivan Group. This is my grandmother.”

Her tone was calm and detached, as if stating the most ordinary fact.

The guarded look in Isabella’s eyes softened slightly, her lips curving into a reserved smile.

Liam’s expression darkened abruptly, the air around him turning icy and oppressive.

Isabella put on an obedient act, addressing the grandmother with a sweet and innocent smile.

Grandma Margaret praised her for her outstanding appearance.

Vivian kept her eyes downcast, sitting upright, but she could clearly feel the scorching gaze from across the car.

That gaze felt tangible, sweeping over her skin inch by inch, carrying unmistakable anger.

She clenched her fingertips, forcing herself to remain calm.

But the gaze was too intense, almost burning through her.

She truly couldn’t understand what she had done to anger him.

Was it because she introduced herself before he could?

Did he actually want her to confess to Isabella that she was his three-month girlfriend?

Given his ambiguous relationship with Isabella, how could Isabella possibly tolerate her?

The car fell into dead silence, the air stagnant and suffocating.

Isabella sensed something unusual.

Her brother had always been cold and distant toward women. When had he ever shown such overt emotion?

Right now, he was clearly angry, and the target was this woman named Vivian Bennett.

There was definitely something going on between them.

She had thought that with Vivian’s background, she wouldn’t even catch her brother’s eye.

But now it seemed her brother cared about her.

Isabella re-examined Vivian.

Her clothes were plain, without a single piece of jewelry.

Only her face and figure were somewhat eye-catching.

Had her brother been bewitched by this temptress?

A trace of coldness flashed in Isabella’s eyes as she quietly took a bottle of red wine from the wine cabinet.

She skillfully filled four glasses and handed them out one by one.

Grandma Margaret declined due to illness, and Vivian thanked her on her behalf.

Isabella smiled brightly, but her eyes were nailed to Vivian’s face. “Miss Bennett, this is a collected wine I brought back from a French vineyard. You probably haven’t tasted such fine wine before. Have a few more glasses. After all, ordinary people rarely get the chance to enjoy...”

Her tone was gentle, but every word carried a sharp sting.

She was clearly scorning Vivian for her lowly status, unworthy of climbing into high society.

Liam watched coldly from the sidelines without saying a word to stop her.

Isabella felt secretly pleased.

Her brother’s consent of her behaviors showed he had no true feelings for this woman.
###Chapter 98

Isabella Sullivan curled her lips in a mocking smile.

She pushed the wine glass forward again.

"Miss Bennett, have another drink."

Vivian Bennett lowered her gaze.

Her fingertips lightly supported the base of the glass.

She swirled the wine slowly.

The deep red color rippled under the light.

She bent her head and inhaled the aroma with graceful composure.

Isabella nearly snorted with laughter.

What an act.

How dare this low-class woman pretend to appreciate fine wine?

"Château Pétrus from Bordeaux's Right Bank?"

Vivian took a small sip and softly identified the vineyard.

Isabella's fingers twitched.

She almost dropped her glass.

"You've had this before?"

She suppressed her shock and quickly lifted her chin.

"Then tell me the vintage."

Vivian took another sip.

She closed her eyes to savor the taste.

"Nineteen eighty-three."

Isabella's pupils constricted sharply.

Impossible.

How could this woman get everything right?

She clenched her skirt, her voice turning cold.

"Did you specifically study wine tasting, Miss Bennett?"

She must have worked hard to climb the social ladder.

Vivian shook her head.

"No."

The chill of the cellar seemed to creep up her spine again.

When she was dizzy with hunger, she could only steal her father's hidden wine to fill her stomach.

One sip of wine.

One moment of clarity.

Isabella snorted in disbelief.

Stop pretending to be innocent.

Vivian ignored her and turned to look out the window.

The rain had stopped unnoticed.

Sunlight broke through the clouds, falling on the wet streets.

"Mr. Sullivan, we'll get off here."

She helped her grandmother up and nodded politely.

"Thank you for today."

She offered Isabella a faint smile.

"And thank you for the wine."

Isabella forced a smile.

"Stop the car."

Liam Sullivan's voice cut through the air.

The vehicle pulled over smoothly.

Vivian supported her grandmother as they exited.

Their figures gradually disappeared into the distance.

Isabella was in high spirits.

She smiled and leaned closer to her brother.

"Brother, shall we have dinner together tonight?"

Liam turned his head.

His gaze was cold and heavy.

"Isabella, when did you become so narrow-minded?"

Isabella's smile froze.

"I wasn't..."

"That bottle of wine." His voice turned icy. "You were deliberately making things difficult for her."

Isabella opened her mouth to speak.

"She is mine."

Liam lowered his voice.

Each word was laced with frost.
###Chapter 99

Isabella Sullivan’s pupils contracted sharply.

Disbelief was written all over her face.

She never expected her brother to openly admit his relationship with Vivian Bennett.

The phrase “my woman” struck her like thunder, ringing in her ears.

“Brother?”

Her voice trembled as she sought confirmation, afraid she had misheard.

Liam Sullivan offered no further explanation.

His declaration seemed entirely natural to him.

Isabella bit her lower lip hard.

Tears welled in her eyes.

Just then, a phone ringtone broke the silence.

The caller ID showed Eleanor Sullivan.

The matriarch invited her to dinner at Le Bernardin.

Isabella immediately felt emboldened.

“Grandma wants us to join her at Le Bernardin.”

She emphasized the word “us” deliberately.

Liam gave her a brief glance.

He signaled the driver to change routes.

Le Bernardin was Seattle’s top restaurant.

Reservations were notoriously difficult to secure.

Vivian had booked a private dining room in advance.

She wanted to ensure her grandmother wouldn’t be disturbed by the main hall’s noise.

She guided the elderly woman toward their reserved room.

As she reached for the door, the manager hurried over.

“Are you Miss Vivian Bennett?”

“Yes, that’s me.”

The manager smiled apologetically.

“I’m very sorry, but this room has been temporarily allocated to a VIP guest.”

“Please wait in the main hall instead.”

Vivian frowned.

“You didn’t mention this when I made the reservation.”

The manager wrung his hands.

“VIP guests have priority. I hope you understand.”

Vivian laughed in frustration.

“Your restaurant’s policies are certainly unique.”

She recalled the day’s misfortunes.

Her frustration grew.

Her taxi had broken down halfway.

A heavy downpour had soaked her.

She had endured Isabella’s unpleasantness.

Now, even a simple meal was being disrupted.

The manager continued apologizing.

“We made a mistake due to today’s rush.”

“How about a twenty percent discount as compensation?”

Vivian had no energy to argue.

She turned to leave.

Suddenly, she encountered Liam’s group.

Isabella was clinging affectionately to an elegantly dressed older woman.

They were walking directly toward her.

The elderly woman appeared vigorous and exuded an air of grandeur.
###Chapter 100

She looked every inch a wealthy matriarch.

Vivian Bennett watched as the manager, who had just been apologizing to her, suddenly brightened at the sight of the three newcomers.

He eagerly hurried over to greet them.

"Mrs. Eleanor Sullivan, Mr. Liam Sullivan, Miss Isabella Sullivan, welcome."

"Your private dining room is ready. Right this way, please..."

Vivian's expression darkened.

So the elderly lady beside Liam Sullivan was his grandmother, Eleanor Sullivan.

The private dining room she had reserved had been taken by these three.

No wonder the manager called them VIP guests.

How could ordinary people afford to offend them?

They could only obediently give up the room.

Vivian felt wronged, but she had to swallow her frustration.

Who asked her and her grandmother to lack the wealth and influence of the Sullivan family?

Vivian helped her grandmother step aside, waiting for them to enter the private room before leaving.

Liam first helped his grandmother into the private room and entrusted her to Isabella's care.

He turned and came back out, just as Vivian was about to lead her grandmother away.

He immediately asked the manager, "Are there any other private rooms available?"

The manager shook his head. "I'm truly sorry, there are none."

Liam walked over to Vivian and her grandmother.

After greeting them politely, he said to her, "Since there are no other rooms, why not join us?"

Seeing that they were acquainted, the manager quickly suggested, "Since you know Mr. Sullivan, miss, perhaps you would care to dine together?"

Vivian's expression was cool and detached. "Mr. Sullivan and I may be acquainted, but we move in different worlds."

"How could we possibly share a table?"

She made to leave after saying this.

Liam raised his hand as if to stop her, but upon seeing Vivian's grandmother, he ultimately refrained from insisting.

His gaze was somber as he watched them leave, his brow furrowed tightly.

"Arrange a private room for them. Immediately," Liam commanded the manager suddenly, his handsome face stern.

The manager looked troubled. "Mr. Sullivan, this..."

But under Liam's authoritative gaze, he had no choice but to agree. "Yes, sir. I'll see to it right away."

He had seriously misjudged the situation today.

He never imagined that Miss Bennett had this powerful man's backing.

Now he had to find a way to invite her back.

Liam withdrew his gaze and elegantly returned to the private room.

Isabella had already ordered, selecting all of Liam's favorite dishes according to their grandmother's wishes.

"Brother, would you like to order anything else?" Isabella obediently handed him the menu.

Liam's expression was indifferent. "No need."

Isabella awkwardly took back the menu and asked the server to bring the food.

"Liam, what kept you outside for so long just now?" Mrs. Eleanor Sullivan asked curiously.

Liam glanced at his grandmother and shook his head. "It was nothing."

He had originally intended to take this opportunity to introduce Vivian to his grandmother.

He wanted his grandmother to know he already had a girlfriend, so she would stop worrying about his marriage plans.

But Vivian seemed determined to distance herself from him today.

Both in the car and outside the private room.

She didn't treat him like a boyfriend at all.

Instead, she was as respectful as if he were her boss.

An indescribable frustration settled in Liam's heart.
###Chapter 101

Liam Sullivan pulled out his cigarette case.

He selected a cigarette with his fingertips.

As his lighter sparked, he noticed his grandmother sitting upright.

He froze mid-motion.

He put the cigarette case away and stood up.

"I'm going to the restroom."

Eleanor Sullivan watched her grandson's retreating figure with a frown.

She turned to Isabella Sullivan. "What's going on with your brother lately?"

Isabella lowered her eyes to hide her satisfaction.

She knew exactly why her brother was upset.

She had nearly laughed out loud earlier when she saw Vivian Bennett being chased away by the manager in the hallway.

How could a woman that poor even think about dining in a place like this?

"Brother might be tired from work," she replied softly, maintaining her obedient facade.

In front of the Sullivan elders, she always played the role of the dutiful daughter.

Some things didn't need to be said outright.

She just needed the old lady to see Vivian's true background.

Her brother was only temporarily infatuated.

Once he grew bored, he would naturally discard such a low-class woman.

Vivian was helping Grandma Margaret leave.

The restaurant manager hurried over to them.

"Miss Bennett, a private dining room just became available downstairs."

Vivian confirmed it was far from where Liam was seated before nodding.

She ordered a full table of delicacies for her grandmother.

Grandma Margaret looked at the spread with moist eyes.

"Vivian, you're the only one who remembers what I like to eat."

Vivian placed a piece of fish in her grandmother's bowl.

"I remember all your favorites, Grandma."

Grandma Margaret sighed. "If only Amelia were half as filial as you..."

Vivian's hand stilled slightly.

She knew her grandmother was referring to her aunt, Amelia Sullivan.

Grandma Margaret only had two daughters in her lifetime.

After Vivian's mother passed away, only Amelia remained.

But Amelia considered Grandma Margaret old and sickly, never bothering to care for her properly.

Even her visits were perfunctory.

She only doted on her youngest son, showing no concern for her own daughter, Grace Harper.

"Grandma, you still have me and Cousin Grace," Vivian said gently, holding her grandmother's hand.

Grandma Margaret shook her head with a bitter smile. "Having you is enough. I don't expect anything from the others..."

If not for Vivian's frequent visits to the sanitarium, she would have lost all hope in this world.

Vivian felt a pang of sadness and was about to speak—

Grandma Margaret suddenly lowered her voice. "Vivian, that young man from the limousine today... does he like you?"
###Chapter 102

Vivian Bennett froze.

The young man her grandmother mentioned was Liam Sullivan.

She quickly explained, "Grandma, he's my boss."

Grandma Margaret smiled knowingly. "Your boss? He clearly has feelings for you."

Vivian's cheeks flushed instantly.

Grandma Margaret held her hand, her gaze tender. "Vivian, no one has truly cared for you since your mother passed."

Vivian reassured her softly, "Grandma, I can take care of myself."

Life had been difficult right after her mother's death.

Her stepmother Linda was harsh. Her half-brother Brandon bullied her. Even outsiders felt free to mistreat her.

No one ever stood up for her.

Over the years, she learned to face everything alone.

She grew accustomed to bearing burdens herself. To supporting herself.

Now she was strong enough. She no longer needed anyone's protection.

Grandma Margaret still worried. "It's good that you can take care of yourself. But if someone truly cherishes and protects you, I could rest easy even after I'm gone."

Vivian's eyes welled up. "Grandma, you will live to be a hundred!"

Grandma Margaret chuckled. "Silly child, no one actually becomes an old monster. My only concern now is you... still without a partner..."

"Being single isn't so bad. I can live a fulfilling life on my own," Vivian gently persuaded.

Grandma Margaret winked at her. "I think your boss is quite good. Handsome and clearly interested in you. Why not consider him?"

Vivian hadn't expected her grandmother to speak in Liam's favor.

No matter how suitable he seemed, anything between them was impossible.

They came from different worlds. How could they ever be together?

"Grandma, don't joke about that. He really is just my boss..." Vivian awkwardly explained again.

The private dining room door opened just then.

Waiters began serving dishes.

Vivian quickly served her grandmother, seizing the chance to change the subject.

The elderly lady enjoyed her meal, soon chatting happily about food and amusing stories.

A phone suddenly rang.

It was her best friend Sophia Miller calling.

After explaining to her grandmother, Vivian stepped out to take the call.

Sophia had just returned from abroad. She was injured during a mission and wanted to see Vivian.

Vivian promised to visit after finishing dinner with her grandmother.

Hanging up, she hurried back to the private room.

Worried about her friend's injury, she quickened her pace.

A tall figure abruptly blocked her path.

Her wrist was seized forcefully.

Liam Sullivan dragged her all the way to the garden outside the restaurant.

"Boss Liam?"

Vivian struggled in shock.

His grip tightened, leaving no room for escape.

He trapped her between a tree trunk and his chest.

One hand pressed against the tree near her ear.

He looked down at her with a cold glare.
###Chapter 103

"Mr. Sullivan!"

Vivian Bennett instinctively stepped back.

Liam Sullivan's gaze turned icy cold.

"Do not call me that."

She was startled by his sudden anger.

Her long lashes fluttered slightly.

She didn't understand what she had said wrong.

The rough tree bark pressed against her back.

His tall figure loomed over her, radiating intense pressure.

Vivian felt her breath catch.

She forced herself to appear calm and managed a faint smile.

"Did you need something?"

"Why did you say you were my subordinate earlier?" Liam leaned closer, his dark eyes unfathomable.

Vivian found it almost amusing.

"Am I not?"

Liam's expression darkened.

"We agreed you would be my girlfriend for three months."

Vivian sighed helplessly.

"That was a private arrangement. Did you want everyone to know?"

Especially his sister with her taboo inclinations.

He probably wouldn't want that.

She was completely considering his position.

"Why not?" Liam stared at her fiercely, an inexplicable fury rising in his chest.

She didn't want to go public?

Was he that embarrassing?

Or had she always planned to leave after three months?

Vivian froze, looking at him in disbelief.

"You..."

Liam gripped her chin, his gaze sharp.

"You thought I only wanted three months?"

Vivian blinked in confusion.

"Didn't you say you wanted me to ward off your admirers?"

Was there another meaning?

Liam's jaw tightened, suppressing his anger.

"Listen. I don't need to ward off admirers. I just want you to be my girlfriend."

His forever girlfriend.

He wanted her.

Vivian met his burning gaze, her heart suddenly racing.

Did he mean it had been an excuse from the very beginning?

Vivian remained silent for a moment before speaking softly.

"Then I won't do it..."

Those words completely enraged Liam.

Before she could finish, he closed the distance between them.

Sensing his dangerous aura, Vivian panicked.

"What are you doing? Let me go..."

Liam focused on her lush lips and suddenly dipped his head to bite them.

"Ah..."

Vivian gasped in pain, her brows furrowing tightly.

Liam didn't continue.

He lifted her chin, forcing her to look directly at him.

Seeing her reddened eyes, his tone unconsciously softened.

"Did it hurt?"

Vivian's eyes widened, her cheeks flushed with indignation.

"Let me bite you and see how it feels?"

Liam looked at her flushed cheeks, a slight smile touching his lips.

"Fine. Bite me anywhere you want."

He moved his entire face closer to hers.
###Chapter 104

Vivian Bennett froze instantly.

She finally realized what she had just said.

Her heart pounded with unease.

She lifted her clear eyes and looked at him seriously. "We truly are not from the same world..."

Liam Sullivan's face darkened abruptly.

He stared at her tempting red lips. They looked so soft, yet the words that came out were so hurtful.

He suddenly lowered his head and captured her lips again.

This kiss was as fierce as a storm.

Vivian had no time to react. She could only widen her eyes.

Liam kissed her with urgency and madness.

One hand braced beside her ear. The other gripped her waist tightly, locking her firmly in his embrace.

Vivian was forced to tilt her head back and endure this dominant kiss.

She clenched her hands into fists and pushed against him with all her strength.

But she couldn't move him an inch.

Instead, his kiss made her whole body go weak. She could barely stand.

Even worse, Liam's hand actually slipped inside her clothes...

Vivian's mind exploded with shock.

Humiliated and angry, she bit down hard.

The taste of blood instantly spread between their lips.

Liam released her, fury burning in his eyes. "You dare bite me?"

Vivian glared at him, flushed with anger. "You forced me first! How could you do this? You're a jerk!"

This was the garden outside the restaurant. Someone could pass by at any moment.

How was she supposed to face anyone after this?

Liam looked at her aggrieved little face and listened to her accusations. His brows furrowed tightly.

His thin lips pressed together. A sense of helplessness surged within him.

Finally, he carefully cupped her face and said in a low voice, "I am a jerk. But I'm only a jerk to you."

Vivian was completely stunned.

What did that mean?

He was jerk to her, and she was supposed to be grateful?

She turned her face away. "Please let me go. I'm really not the kind of woman you think I am."

"What kind of woman do you think I want?" Liam's gaze was deep.

He felt both helpless and amused. This little woman had indeed misunderstood him.

"I..." Vivian was about to explain.

He kissed her again.

This kiss was no longer rough. It held a rare tenderness and lingering affection.

Liam savored her sweetness carefully, his tongue tangling with hers.

His unique scent enveloped Vivian's senses.

She trembled all over, almost drowning in this kiss.

Liam's hoarse voice sounded in her ear. "No matter what you think, I only want you."

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

He only wanted her? What did that mean?

Just because the big boss fancied her, she had to comply?

Seeing her still confused expression, Liam didn't explain further. He just held her hand tightly and pulled her into his embrace.

He buried his face in the crook of her neck, calming his breathing.

Vivian could clearly feel his desire for her...
###Chapter 105

Vivian Bennett expected Liam Sullivan to not let her off easily.

To her surprise, he only breathed heavily for a moment before releasing her.

"If you dare deny that I'm your boyfriend again, I'll teach you a lesson in bed." His eyes were deep and burning, his voice husky with warning.

Vivian's heart trembled.

"Grandma Margaret is waiting for me. I should go."

She quickly found an excuse and turned to escape.

Liam watched her hurried retreat and sighed helplessly.

......

In a corner of the garden.

Isabella Sullivan had witnessed the entire wild kiss between Liam and Vivian. Her face was filled with hatred.

Her brother had always been reserved and disciplined. When had he ever kissed a woman so passionately?

What kind of spell had Vivian cast?

How could she make her calm and controlled brother lose control to this extent?

Isabella shot a jealous glare in Vivian's direction before gritting her teeth and returning to the private dining room.

......

After returning to the private dining room, Vivian was visibly unsettled.

Grandma Margaret noticed something was troubling her. Since she had already finished eating, she suggested they leave.

Vivian called for the waiter to settle the bill.

The waiter informed her that their private dining room's bill had already been paid.

Vivian was surprised. "That's impossible. I haven't paid yet."

The waiter gave a knowing smile. "It's already been charged to Mr. Sullivan's account."

Vivian froze again.

Liam had quietly paid the bill without her knowledge.

But she didn't want to owe him this favor.

"Please process the payment again," Vivian told the waiter.

Despite Liam repeatedly insisting she was his girlfriend, Vivian still found it unreal.

All the hardships and suffering she had endured since childhood reminded her.

One shouldn't live in fantasies.

Being down-to-earth was the safest approach.

She didn't believe Liam truly wanted her as his girlfriend.

Who knew why this powerful boss had taken a sudden interest in her.

If his interest in her faded, she would eventually be cast aside.

Vivian couldn't allow herself to become overly dependent on him.

"I'm afraid that won't be possible. Your private dining room has already been settled..." The waiter looked uncomfortable.

They wouldn't dare offend Liam Sullivan.

Vivian didn't press the waiter further.

She helped Grandma Margaret leave the private dining room.

As they passed through the main hall, they happened to see Liam assisting his grandmother down the stairs.

"Vivian, isn't that your boss?" Grandma Margaret whispered.

"Yes." Vivian glanced quickly at Liam before immediately looking away.

She kept her eyes downcast, not daring to look up at him or greet him.

"Grandma, let's go." Vivian kept her head down and hurried Grandma Margaret away.
###Chapter 106

Liam Sullivan watched Vivian Bennett flee in panic.

Her avoidance of him, as if he were a venomous snake, made his brow furrow deeply.

Isabella Sullivan cast a cold glance at Vivian's retreating figure.

A trace of icy hatred flashed in her eyes.

After settling Grandma Margaret back at the sanitarium, Vivian drove straight to her best friend Sophia Miller's villa.

She stopped at a pharmacy along the way to pick up some common medications.

Sophia was often away on missions, and getting injured was routine for her.

Vivian had long grown accustomed to tending her wounds.

She pushed open the villa door, medical bag in hand.

The first floor was empty.

Vivian hurried upstairs to the second floor.

The bedroom door was slightly ajar, with blood-stained clothes and bandages scattered on the floor.

"Sophia!"

Vivian rushed anxiously to the bedside.

Sophia slowly opened her eyes and reached for the cigarette pack on the nightstand.

"It's fine. I won't die."

She lit a cigarette and took a deep drag.

"Smoking while injured?" Vivian frowned in disapproval.

Sophia blew out a smoke ring. "For the pain."

Vivian opened the medical kit and carefully examined her injuries.

Fresh wounds covered her body, some still oozing blood.

"Quit this line of work." Vivian's voice trembled.

Every time she saw her best friend covered in scars, her heart ached unbearably.

Sophia stubbed out her cigarette and shook her head with a bitter smile. "It's not that simple."

Vivian gently began redressing her wounds.

"But..."

Sophia suddenly sucked in a sharp breath.

Vivian deliberately applied more pressure. "You know it hurts, yet you keep going?"

Sophia stared blankly at the ceiling. "Some paths, once taken, can't be turned back from."

Vivian silently tied the final bandage.

Just then, Sophia abruptly sat up.

"Wait. Something's up with you?"

Vivian looked up, confused. "What?"

Sophia narrowed her eyes, studying her closely.

"You smell like a man."
###Chapter 107

Sophia Miller leaned in, her gaze sharp as a blade.

"What happened to your lips?"

Vivian Bennett instinctively pressed her lips together, avoiding eye contact.

"The weather has been dry lately. They're chapped."

Sophia scoffed.

"Cut the crap. That's clearly a bite mark."

She grabbed Vivian's wrist firmly.

"Tell me the truth. Are you seeing someone behind my back?"

Vivian's heart raced. She shook her head in panic.

"Nothing like that!"

Sophia narrowed her eyes.

"Still lying? That's clearly a hickey!"

Vivian's cheeks burned. She stammered, unable to form a response.

Sophia's expression instantly turned excited.

"You actually slept with him? Must have been intense!"

Vivian glared at her, both embarrassed and annoyed.

"Stop talking nonsense. We haven't gone that far."

Sophia raised an eyebrow.

"So something did happen?"

Vivian bit her lip in frustration, realizing she had slipped up.

Sophia laughed heartily.

"Seems like he couldn't wait!"

Vivian sighed and massaged her temples.

It seemed she really needed to be more careful around Liam Sullivan from now on.

Sophia leaned closer, pressing for details.

"Who is it? Do I know him?"

Vivian glanced at her.

This didn't seem like someone who was injured. She was a full-blown gossip enthusiast.

"Liam Sullivan. My boss." Vivian decided to come clean.

Sophia stroked her chin thoughtfully.

"Is he trying to proposition you?"

Vivian frowned, looking troubled.

"I'm trying to figure out how to turn him down."

Sophia slammed the table abruptly.

"Turn him down? Why would you reject a top-tier man falling right into your lap?"

Vivian was stunned.

She never expected this reaction from her best friend.

"But what if he's just playing with me?"

Sophia rolled her eyes.

"If he plays you, you play him back! This kind of opportunity is something others beg for!"

Vivian was left speechless.

Sophia continued fanning the flames.

"If you ask me, you should invite him to a hotel tonight. Sleep with him first and figure out the rest later!"

Vivian stared, completely dumbfounded.

Sophia grew more enthusiastic.

"You'll regret missing out on a man like this. You need to make the first move!"

Listening to her best friend's bold words, Vivian felt for the first time that she was utterly unreliable.

Late at night, Vivian tossed and turned in bed.

Liam Sullivan's image lingered persistently in her mind.

Should she really pursue this with him?

Her phone suddenly rang.

A familiar letter "B" flashed on the screen.

Her heart instantly began to race.
###Chapter 108

Vivian Bennett stared at her phone screen, her fingertips trembling slightly.

That name flashed on the display like an invisible hook, unsettling her mind.

She took a deep breath and finally pressed the answer button.

Dead silence filled the receiver.

Only heavy breathing could be heard, mixed with distant, muffled noise.

She frowned and spoke tentatively. "Boss Liam?"

The response was a sudden blast of electronic music that made her eardrums buzz.

A woman's coquettish voice pierced through the beat, clinging to the air with sticky sweetness.

"Boss Liam, you're so amazing~"

"Boss Liam, have another drink~"

"Oh my god, you finished a whole bottle of whiskey without flinching! You're incredible!"

Vivian's fingers turned icy cold.

The first two suggestive exclamations made her stomach churn.

It wasn't until the flattering words followed that she realized—he was probably drowning his sorrows at some entertainment venue.

But why did he specifically call her?

Did he accidentally dial her number while drunk?

She abruptly ended the call, her chest heaving violently.

Sophia Miller's words today had almost swayed her, but now they felt like a bucket of cold water poured over her head.

The diversions of wealthy young masters were nothing but wine, women, song, and money.

Why should she become just another decoration in his garden of flowers?

Even pretending to be his girlfriend to ward off admirers seemed utterly ridiculous now.

She powered off her phone and threw it into the drawer.

In the darkness, she clutched the corner of her blanket tightly.

But she couldn't shake off those soft, seductive voices echoing in her ears.

...

Inside the private dining room, colored lights swirled.

Liam Sullivan shook off the woman's clinging hand, his gaze frighteningly cold.

"Don't touch me."

The woman staggered back, her face filled with resentment. "Boss Liam, I just wanted to..."

"Get out."

That single word made her face turn pale.

She gritted her teeth and stepped away, but her eyes remained glued to the man's sharp profile.

Since entering the room, he had been sitting alone in the corner drinking heavily.

His phone screen lit up and went dark repeatedly.

She had leaned closer earlier and caught a glimpse of his screensaver—a blurry image of a woman's back.

She thought she could seize the opportunity to please him by dialing the number and saying a few good words for him.

Maybe she could gain his favor.

But just as the call connected, people started urging him to drink.

She had no choice but to rush to flatter him.

Unexpectedly, the other end hung up directly.

Even more surprising was that she couldn't even touch him.

The rumors about him avoiding women were actually true.

Oliver Winston swayed over with a glass in hand, nudging him with his elbow. "What's wrong? Who upset our young master Liam? Should I find someone more understanding?"
###Chapter 109

Liam Sullivan's gaze turned icy cold.

He shot a sharp look at Oliver Winston.

"Don't arrange women for me anymore."

Oliver shrugged awkwardly.

"I was just worried you'd hurt yourself holding back for too long."

The moment he finished speaking, Liam's eyes became terrifying.

Oliver shuddered in fear and immediately shut his mouth.

He didn't dare say another word.

Liam sat alone in the corner of the private dining room.

He drank one glass of whiskey after another.

A cold, unapproachable aura radiated from him.

No one dared to disturb him again.

When the midnight chime sounded, he stood up and left directly.

"Oliver, Liam's drunk. Go see him out?" Alexander Lowell frowned.

"You go if you want. I don't dare provoke him again," Oliver said, waving his hands repeatedly.

Alexander hurried out to support him. "Liam, let me drive you."

Liam pushed his hand away, his voice low. "No need."

Alexander could only watch him leave alone.

Liam got into the car as the alcohol began to take effect.

He leaned back against the leather seat, eyes closed, silent for a long time.

"Boss, back to the Sullivan Family Estate?" the driver asked cautiously.

Liam opened his eyes and named a residential area.

Lakeside Manor?

The driver was stunned.

Going to Miss Vivian's place this late?

He didn't dare ask more and immediately started the car.

Upon arrival, the driver was about to report when he noticed the man in the back seat had fallen asleep.

He didn't dare disturb him and waited silently.

Over an hour passed, and Liam still hadn't woken.

The night was deep and quiet. The driver couldn't fight off his drowsiness either.

When he woke again, the sky was beginning to lighten.

The driver checked the time. Six in the morning.

The back seat was empty.

He quickly got out to look.

Turning around, he saw Liam standing under a streetlight.

Several cigarette butts were scattered at his feet.

He was gazing intently at Vivian Bennett's window.

His profile was sharp and cold, his eyes deep.

It was impossible to tell what he was thinking.

A few days later, Vivian Bennett returned to Sullivan Group after her leave.

She noticed her colleagues were looking at her differently.

Especially Hannah Green, the deputy manager of the PR department.
###Chapter 110

Vivian Bennett stepped into the PR department and sensed an immediate shift in the atmosphere.

Colleagues who used to order her around now offered ingratiating smiles.

Even Hannah Green toned down her arrogance upon seeing her.

Vivian felt puzzled but showed no outward reaction.

During lunch, she pulled Emily Davis to a secluded corner of the cafeteria.

Emily couldn't wait to speak. "Vivian, you're finally back!"

Vivian lowered her voice. "What happened these past few days?"

Emily's eyes lit up. "You don't know? Manager Hannah got called into the boss's office for a reprimand!"

Vivian raised an eyebrow. "Why?"

Emily leaned closer, whispering near her ear. "Because of you! The boss personally ordered an investigation into the warehouse incident. That employee from the cafeteria who set you up has been fired!"

Vivian froze.

She hadn't expected Liam Sullivan to act so swiftly.

Emily continued. "Manager Hannah had six months' salary deducted. If the VP hadn't protected her, she would have been fired already!"

Vivian pressed her lips together.

This outcome surprised her.

Emily nudged her shoulder. "Why didn't you tell us you had the boss backing you? We were worried for nothing!"

Vivian forced a weak smile. "I don't—"

Emily cut her off. "Don't hide it from me! The whole company knows the boss is protecting you now!"

Vivian's heart sank.

Exactly as she feared.

No wonder her colleagues' attitudes had done a complete one-eighty.

The workplace's harsh reality felt chilling.

Emily lowered her voice further. "Come clean. What's really going on between you and the boss?"

Vivian lowered her gaze. "There's really nothing."

Emily refused to believe it. "Nothing? Then why would he go to such lengths for you?"

Vivian sighed helplessly. "Maybe he's just been bored lately?"

Emily rolled her eyes. "Don't lie! The boss is incredibly busy. Why would he care about something so trivial?"

Vivian remained silent for a moment.

She spoke softly. "Actually, it's not completely nothing..."

Emily's eyes brightened. "I knew there was more to it!"

Vivian sighed. "But it really doesn't count as any kind of relationship."

Emily's eyes suddenly widened. "Did he sleep with you and then refuse to take responsibility?"

Vivian nearly choked on her saliva.

"Stop making wild guesses!"

Emily grew indignant. "Even if he's the boss, he can't treat people like that!"

Vivian massaged her temples. "It's really not what you think..."
###Chapter 111

Under Liam Sullivan's protection, Vivian Bennett finally enjoyed a few peaceful days.

As her shift ended, she walked out of the company's main entrance with Emily Davis.

The two had made plans to have hot pot together.

Ryan Johnson suddenly appeared before them.

"Miss Bennett, Mr. Sullivan is waiting for you in the car." The assistant pointed toward a black limousine parked nearby.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

Why would Liam Sullivan suddenly come looking for her?

And why did he have to choose such a conspicuous spot right at the company entrance?

If her colleagues saw her getting into the CEO's car...

"Could you wait a moment?" She forced a smile.

At least until most colleagues had left.

"I'm afraid Mr. Sullivan can't wait." Ryan lowered his voice. "Unless you want him to come get you himself."

Vivian's scalp prickled.

If Liam actually stepped out of that car and walked toward her in public...

There would be no clearing up the rumors about her and the CEO.

Gritting her teeth, she glanced around to ensure no one was watching before hurrying toward the luxury vehicle.

The moment she opened the door, a strong force pulled her inside.

"Too slow." Liam's deep voice carried clear dissatisfaction.

Vivian couldn't help retorting, "I came as quickly as I could."

What more did he want?

Before she could finish speaking, he pulled her tightly into his embrace.

She instinctively struggled.

"Don't move." His husky voice brushed past her ear. "Let me hold you for a while."

Vivian froze in his arms, her heart pounding like a drum.

She couldn't begin to guess what he was thinking.

"Mr. Sullivan..."

"Call me by my name." He interrupted her.

Vivian's cheeks flushed.

Liam?

Such an intimate address felt impossible to utter.

"Liam Sullivan... did you need something?" She forced the words out.

Liam didn't answer, only buried his face deeper into the curve of her neck.

He breathed in her scent greedily.

After what felt like an eternity, he slowly released her.

His long fingers cupped her face.

"I'm going abroad on a business trip for a few days." His gaze was intense. "I wanted to see you before leaving."

Vivian stared blankly at his handsome features.

Her heart rippled with unfamiliar emotions.

Why would he specifically come to tell her about his business trip?

Did he really consider her his girlfriend?

She bit her lip uncertainly. "Have a safe trip."

Liam chuckled softly, his voice so tender it made her heart flutter. "Nothing else to say to me?"

Vivian frowned in confusion.

What else was she supposed to say?

After thinking hard for a moment, she hesitantly offered, "Don't... overwork yourself?"

Liam pinched her cheek helplessly. "That's not what I wanted to hear."

Vivian was completely lost.

Not that?

Then what did he want to hear?

She stared at him with wide eyes, unable to form any words.
###Chapter 112

Liam Sullivan gazed at her bewildered little face and sighed helplessly once more.

"Be good. Tell me you'll miss me, and ask me to come back early?"

Vivian Bennett's fair cheeks instantly flushed crimson.

She bit her lip, unable to speak for a long time.

Such intimate whispers between lovers were truly difficult for her to utter.

Her relationship with Liam Sullivan was far from that deeply attached stage.

Liam watched her for a long moment, confirming she wouldn't volunteer her feelings.

He suddenly leaned down and captured her lips.

Vivian's eyes widened in shock.

He was kissing her like this in the car?

The car window wasn't even fully closed!

It was the peak of rush hour.

If a colleague saw her kissing the CEO in a luxury car, how could she ever hold her head up at Sullivan Group again?

Vivian struggled fiercely.

But Liam's kiss was aggressive and allowed no retreat.

It only grew deeper and more lingering.

Until she was almost breathless, going soft in his arms.

Liam's husky voice was dark with desire. "Say you'll miss me. Tell me to come back early."

Vivian leaned against his chest, panting, still too shy to speak.

Liam kissed the corner of her mouth again until she whimpered in protest.

Under the man's forceful pressure, she finally whispered, blushing, "Liam Sullivan, I will miss you... Come back early, okay?"

Only then did Liam release her, satisfied.

"I'll miss you too." His expression suddenly turned serious. "But while I'm away, don't attract any other men. Understood?"

His eyes seemed to say betrayal would not be taken lightly.

Vivian felt helpless inside.

Who else could she possibly attract?

They weren't even officially in a relationship yet.

This man was overthinking things.

But to avoid being kissed again, she could only nod obediently.

She just hoped he would let her out of the car soon.

Unexpectedly, Liam ordered the front, "Drive."

Vivian was shocked to realize there was a driver in the front seat.

So all those intimate words and kisses had been witnessed by a third person?

She was instantly overwhelmed with shame.

The car had already merged into traffic.

By the time she collected herself, they were long gone from the company.

"Where are you taking me?" she asked urgently.

Wasn't he going on a business trip?

Was he taking her with him?

"Dinner first, then the airport." Liam held her slender hand tightly, his tone gentle.

Vivian was stunned.

So he was taking her to dinner.

"We've been dating for a while, but we haven't had a proper date yet." His sharp brows lifted slightly, his dark eyes profound. "Will you blame me for neglecting you lately?"

Hearing the word "dating," Vivian felt a headache coming on.

A date with the CEO was beyond imagination.

She hurriedly shook her head. "How could I..."
###Chapter 113

She thought to herself that it didn't matter even if he neglected her every day.

After all, three months would pass in the blink of an eye.

She never expected that being his nominal girlfriend for three months meant he would actually treat her like his real girlfriend.

If she really had to date the big boss, that would be incredibly stressful.

Soon, the driver stopped the car at the entrance of a six-star hotel in the bustling city center.

Liam Sullivan held her hand as they got out of the car and entered the hotel together.

Vivian Bennett had never imagined that a dinner could be so luxurious.

They were dining at the rooftop restaurant of the six-star hotel.

The restaurant was elegantly and opulently decorated, clearly catering only to the elite of high society.

Guided by a waiter, Vivian sat down uneasily at a table by the window.

The distance between their table and the next one was over five meters.

In such a vast space, there were only the two of them?

Looking around, the distance and space between each table on this rooftop restaurant were astonishingly large.
