It was truly a six-star hotel's rooftop restaurant, completely prioritizing the privacy of every distinguished guest dining here.

Liam was clearly a regular here.

He didn't need to say much; the attentive restaurant manager already knew all his preferences by heart.

However, considering Vivian's taste, Liam still signaled for the manager to bring the menu.

The manager respectfully handed the menu to Vivian with both hands.

She was the first woman Mr. Sullivan had ever brought here to dine.

She must be someone important to him, so they naturally had to serve her with the utmost care.

Vivian glanced at the delicacies on the menu.

This six-star hotel restaurant offered many top-tier gourmet dishes unavailable elsewhere.

Of course, the prices were exorbitantly high.

A single glass of water cost thousands.

Vivian was so startled she almost spilled the water she was holding.

She confirmed once again that this restaurant was absolutely not affordable for someone of her ordinary working-class means.

But since the big boss had brought her here, refusing to eat would be disrespectful to him.

If she had to order herself, she couldn't bring herself to do it.

Every dish here cost a month of her salary.

She truly couldn't bring herself to eat a single dish.

After struggling for a long time, Vivian finally spoke. "I'm fine with anything. You decide."

She forced a smile at Liam sitting across from her.

Liam immediately signaled the manager to serve the dishes, probably sensing her indecision.

Soon, the lavish table was filled with an entire spread of delicious dishes.

Vivian's eyes widened in shock.

Why so much food?

Did he ask the manager to bring every single dish on the menu?

There were only two of them. How could they possibly finish all this?

"What's wrong? Nothing you like?" Liam asked, raising an eyebrow at Vivian's lack of movement.

Vivian quickly shook her head. "No... it's just... there's too much..."

She genuinely didn't know which dish to start with.

"Choose what you like," Liam said, a slight curve on his lips.

Vivian broke out in a cold sweat.

This man was truly wasteful.

She reluctantly picked a few items in front of her and tasted them.

The flavor was surprisingly excellent.

As expected of a six-star hotel restaurant; the food was truly exceptional.

When she looked up again, Liam was walking toward her.
###Chapter 114

Vivian Bennett stared in shock. "What are you doing?"

Liam Sullivan didn't answer. He simply gestured to a waiter standing nearby.

The waiter immediately understood and approached.

He neatly arranged Liam's custom gold-rimmed place setting at the seat next to Vivian before silently withdrawing.

"I want to be closer to you." Liam settled beside her, a gentle smile warming his eyes.

Vivian froze in place.

What kind of reason was that?

Before she could process it, Liam leaned in close.

His familiar masculine scent enveloped her instantly.

She instinctively tried to pull back.

But Liam wrapped an arm around her waist, closing the distance between them.

"You..."

Vivian stared at him, a mix of shock and embarrassment flooding her.

This was a six-star hotel restaurant.

How could he be so brazen?

What if someone saw them?

Her thoughts were in turmoil when Liam suddenly used his silver fork to pick up a piece of steak from his own plate.

He held it to her lips.

"Thank you, I can manage myself." Vivian turned her head away, her ears turning red.

"Do you need me to feed you personally?" Liam's eyes darkened, a meaningful curve lifting the corner of his mouth.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat. Her cheeks burned instantly.

She understood exactly what he meant.

Did he want to feed her... that way again?

In the middle of a crowded six-star restaurant? Did she have no dignity left?

Vivian wished she could vanish on the spot.

Left with no choice, she reluctantly opened her mouth and accepted the steak.

She chewed awkwardly as Liam's deep voice murmured near her ear. "You're too thin. It's uncomfortable to touch. You should eat more."

Vivian was mortified. Every part of her felt uneasy.

The beef in her mouth seemed impossible to chew through.

Liam watched her flushed face, his Adam's apple moving unconsciously.

He was lying.

Though Vivian was slender, her curves were exquisite.

He adored her body exactly as it was.

He could never forget their night of passion.

Just the memory made his body tense and his breath quicken.

He wanted to pull her into his arms right then.

"But the way you are now... it enthralls me just the same." Liam's heated gaze locked onto her, his lips curving upward again.

If he didn't have to catch a flight for a business trip, he would take her upstairs immediately to relive that dream.

His gaze was too intense. Vivian could barely withstand it.

She swallowed the food in a fluster. Her cheeks were burning red, her eyelashes fluttering nervously.

"I... I need to use the restroom."

She seized the excuse and rose to escape.

Liam's low laughter followed her, echoing in her ears like a spell.

She quickened her pace.

Inside the luxurious restroom, Vivian stared at her reflection.

Her face was as flushed as a peach blossom.

She silently scolded herself for being so useless, for fleeing in such a panic.

She fanned her face with her hand, trying to cool her burning cheeks.

If she had stayed a moment longer, she didn't know how much more embarrassed she would have become.
###Chapter 115

Cold water rushed over her face.

Vivian Bennett closed her eyes, trying hard to calm the turmoil inside her.

A few seconds later, she took a deep breath and turned to leave the restroom.

She had just stepped out when she collided with a solid chest.

"I'm sorry..." she apologized hastily, her head lowered.

A familiar, teasing voice came from above her.

"In such a hurry to throw yourself into my arms?"

Vivian froze completely.

That voice...

Her head snapped up, meeting a pair of smiling, mischievous eyes.

Zachary Hudson?

How could it be him?

"You..."

Her eyes widened as she stared at his handsome, roguish face.

To run into him here?

"Never thought you had such skills," Zachary said, narrowing his eyes with a mocking tone.

"What?" Vivian frowned in confusion.

The ridicule in his eyes made her deeply uncomfortable.

"You actually managed to get into Liam Sullivan's bed?" Zachary glanced at her sideways, a sarcastic curve on his lips.

So she had repeatedly rejected him because she had set her sights on a bigger target.

Her goal was actually Liam Sullivan.

"I don't know what you're talking about," Vivian said, her face turning cold.

He was clearly implying she had slept with Liam for professional favors.

Her relationship with Liam was nothing like what he imagined.

But she had no obligation to explain herself to him.

Vivian turned to leave.

Zachary grabbed her wrist.

"In such a hurry to find Liam Sullivan?" he scrutinized her, his voice tinged with anger.

He had never failed to win over a woman.

Vivian was the only exception.

He had been wondering where he had fallen short.

Only today did he discover she had rejected him because of Liam Sullivan.

He and Liam had been rivals for so many years.

He couldn't claim total victory in every aspect.

But in matters of love, he had never lost.

How could Vivian choose that iceberg Liam Sullivan over him?

"Let go!"

Vivian glared at him.

"No."

Not only did Zachary not release her, he moved closer.

His warm breath brushed against her face.

"If you don't let go, I'll call for help!" Vivian said urgently.

"Go ahead, I don't mind." Zachary's eyes sparkled with amusement.

Vivian had never met someone so shameless.

"What do you want?" she demanded through gritted teeth.

"I want you..." Zachary gazed at her intently, deliberately drawing out his words.

Vivian gasped, looking at him in shock.

Zachary appreciated her changed expression, a slight smile playing on his lips.

He leisurely added, "I want you to come to my office tomorrow."

"What?"
###Chapter 116

Vivian Bennett froze at Zachary Hudson's words.

He actually wanted her to go to his office?

What was this man plotting now?

Did he plan to make her wait in his office like last time?

Zachary's captivating eyes locked onto her. "If you don't come, I won't let go."

Cornered, Vivian reluctantly nodded.

Only then did Zachary release her.

Vivian immediately turned and hurried back to Liam Sullivan's table.

She suddenly realized that staying with Liam felt safer than dealing with Zachary's advances.

"Why were you gone so long?" Liam's deep gaze settled on her face as he asked quietly.

"I..." Vivian twisted her fingers nervously, stammering incoherently.

Just then, Zachary's voice suddenly rang out.

"Mr. Sullivan, long time no see." Zachary walked over with a smile, initiating the greeting.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat when she saw him follow her.

"What are you doing here?" Liam's expression instantly turned cold, clearly displeased.

"Not happy to see me?" Zachary raised an eyebrow with an amused smile, his tone teasing.

Liam's eyes turned icy. "Should I be?"

"Mind if I join you for dinner?" Zachary squinted his peach blossom eyes, a smile playing on his lips.

Without waiting for an answer, he pulled out a chair and sat down.

Vivian was stunned.

How could Zachary be so presumptuous?

He was probably the only person in the world who dared treat Liam this way.

Liam didn't immediately throw him out. Instead, he studied him deeply for a moment before asking, "What do you want?"

Zachary's gaze fell on Vivian, filled with amusement.

"When did Mr. Sullivan start bringing women out?" he drawled lazily.

Liam frowned and pulled Vivian closer by her waist. "She's my girlfriend."

Zachary visibly paused, his movements stilling.

He looked at Vivian with renewed interest.

He had assumed she was just Liam's mistress, never expecting she was his official girlfriend.

This woman's methods were indeed extraordinary.

Her ambitions were clearly substantial.

She seemed to be aiming for the position of Mrs. Sullivan.

No wonder she had rejected his previous proposal.

"Girlfriend? Are you serious, Mr. Sullivan?" Zachary's eyes filled with playful curiosity.

Liam narrowed his cold eyes. "I'm not like you. I don't play games."

For him, women were either strangers or lovers.

He never toyed with emotions.

A dark glint flashed in Zachary's eyes, though his face maintained its carefree smile.

"I never thought this iceberg would actually fall in love. Congratulations are in order."

"Unnecessary." Liam's tone remained cold, radiating an aura that kept others at a distance.

Most people would have taken the hint and left by now.

But Zachary was not most people.

Not only did he stay, he even called a waiter over to order.

He genuinely seemed intent on joining their meal.

Vivian internally panicked at the sight.

She absolutely did not want to have dinner with Zachary.

Especially not in front of Liam.

The three of them sitting together at one table felt utterly bizarre.

She quickly spoke up. "Um... I just remembered I have something to do..."
###Chapter 117

Vivian Bennett found an excuse to leave the event early.

Liam Sullivan stood up as well, bidding farewell to Zachary Hudson.

He wrapped his arm around her waist and led her out directly.

Inside the car, the driver reminded him quietly, "Mr. Sullivan, it's time to head to the airport."

Vivian immediately spoke up. "You should go catch your flight. I can get back on my own."

She reached for the door handle to get out.

Liam's voice was firm. "Head to Lakeside Manor first."

Vivian was taken aback.

He wanted to take her home first?

Wasn't he worried about missing his flight?

The driver started the car as instructed.

Vivian turned to look out the window, her thoughts drifting away.

"What are you thinking about?"

Liam suddenly tightened his grip on her hand.

Vivian snapped back to reality and forced a smile. "Nothing."

"How do you know Zachary Hudson?" Liam narrowed his eyes, his tone scrutinizing.

Vivian stiffened.

Of course he had noticed.

She lowered her gaze and replied softly, "The PR department was negotiating a collaboration with him before. I attended some meetings."

Liam seemed to recall the incident after a moment of thought and didn't press further.

"Stay away from him." His voice was low, betraying no emotion.

Vivian had already intended to keep her distance.

"Okay," she agreed quietly.

The car arrived at the entrance of her community.

"I'm here." Vivian held the door handle and turned to say goodbye.

The person beside her remained silent.

Vivian looked at him, puzzled.

Liam suddenly pulled her closer by the waist, leaning in.

Before she could react, his lips were on hers.

Vivian's eyes widened.

Liam savored her sweetness greedily.

The kiss deepened.

Vivian felt her body go weak, surrendering to his demands.

A sudden chill touched her chest.

She looked down and saw her blouse had been pulled open.

"No!"

She pushed against him in panic.

Liam stopped abruptly.

He buried his face in her neck, breathing heavily.

He had almost lost control.

"Wait for me to return." He adjusted her blouse, his voice husky. "Stop avoiding me."

Vivian's mind was in chaos. She nodded hastily.

She just wanted to escape quickly.

She stumbled out of the car and ran home. Only after closing the door did she fully process what happened.

His last words…

What did they mean?

Was he saying she shouldn't refuse him when he returned from his business trip?

Did he want to…
###Chapter 118

Vivian Bennett's heart suddenly raced.

She tossed and turned all night, unable to sleep.

Her promise to Zachary Hudson at the restaurant about visiting his office was completely forgotten.

The next day, she walked into the office with heavy dark circles under her eyes.

Colleagues cast surprised glances her way.

She lowered her head to avoid their gazes.

Around noon, her phone suddenly vibrated.

Liam Sullivan's name flashed on the screen.

Her fingers trembled slightly as she answered the call.

The man's deep voice came through the receiver. "Did you sleep well last night?"

Vivian's ears burned.

She gripped the phone tightly and whispered, "It was okay..."

Liam chuckled softly. "I didn't sleep well."

Vivian instinctively asked, "Why?"

His voice turned magnetic. "What do you think?"

Her heart skipped a beat.

Liam spoke directly. "I couldn't sleep because I was thinking of you."

Vivian's cheeks instantly flushed.

She flustered and interrupted. "We can't make personal calls during work hours."

The call was hastily ended.

Liam frowned at his phone.

He pressed the intercom. "Is there a company rule against taking calls during work hours?"

Ryan Johnson answered respectfully. "It's a rule you personally implemented years ago, sir."

Liam's expression darkened.

He issued a cold order. "Cancel that rule immediately."

Ryan paused. "Cancel it now?"

Liam's tone brooked no argument. "Right now."

An hour later, a new announcement was posted on the company's internal system.

Taking phone calls during work hours was no longer restricted.

The entire Sullivan Group was abuzz.

Employees everywhere discussed the CEO's sudden humane reform.

Vivian stared at the announcement, her fingertips turning cold.

She could hardly believe her eyes.

The timing of this rule cancellation was too coincidental.

Ripples of emotion stirred within her heart.
###Chapter 119

Her heart suddenly began to race.

Vivian Bennett held her phone, her fingertips trembling slightly.

Liam Sullivan had just ended the call when a new notification popped up on the company's internal system.

Employees were strictly prohibited from taking personal calls during work hours.

The timing of this new rule was too perfect.

It was so perfect it frightened her.

She took a deep breath, trying to calm her pounding heart.

It was impossible.

Someone as lofty as Liam Sullivan wouldn't change company policy because of something she said.

She shook her head, dismissing the absurd thought.

It had to be a coincidence.

"Vivian, your face is so red."

Emily Davis approached with her lunch tray, her eyes sparkling with gossip.

Vivian instinctively touched her warm cheeks.

"Is it?"

Emily lowered her voice. "That call just now... was it the big boss?"

Vivian immediately denied it. "No!"

Emily raised an eyebrow with a light laugh. "If it wasn't him, do you have someone else on the side?"

Vivian was speechless, unsure how to respond.

Emily nudged her shoulder. "Admit it. Was the big boss checking up on you?"

Vivian pressed her lips together and remained silent.

It was a silent admission.

Emily instantly grew excited. "I heard the big boss went abroad? He's still thinking about you even on a business trip. You're something, Vivian!"

Vivian's ears burned. "Stop talking nonsense."

Ever since Ryan Johnson had openly invited her into the car in front of Emily, her relationship with Liam Sullivan could no longer be hidden.

But the intimacy Emily imagined was far from reality.

At the very least... they hadn't reached that point.

"Vivian, hold tight to the big boss's golden thigh. Your future will be limitless."

Emily winked at her.

Vivian forced a smile.

She would rather not have such a future.

But saying it out loud would only sound hypocritical.

She lowered her head and ate in silence.

A sudden commotion arose at the cafeteria entrance.

Everyone looked up.

Isabella Sullivan walked into the cafeteria wearing a simple professional outfit and a warm smile.

She wore no makeup today, radiating a gentle and approachable aura.

Praise followed wherever she went.

"The vice president is so down-to-earth."

"She doesn't put on airs at all. It's rare."

"This is the true demeanor of a socialite."

Vivian listened quietly, admitting that Isabella Sullivan was very skilled at winning people over.

Even Emily couldn't help but sigh. "Who wouldn't like a leader like Vice President Sullivan?"

Vivian looked up.

Isabella Sullivan was waiting patiently at the end of the line to get her food.

She could have enjoyed privileges but chose to be like everyone else.

It was a clever posture indeed.

Vivian was about to look away when she unexpectedly met a pair of icy eyes.

Isabella Sullivan was looking directly at her.

Those always-smiling eyes now held a terrifying coldness.

It lasted only a moment before returning to gentleness.

It was so fast one might think it was an illusion.

But Vivian saw it clearly.

The murderous intent in that gaze sent a chill down her spine.
###Chapter 120

After Liam Sullivan left on his business trip, phone calls became a daily necessity.

Vivian Bennett's phone would always ring when she least expected it.

Sometimes it was during a busy morning at the office. Other times it was late at night when she was preparing for sleep.

She had once used company policy as an excuse, prohibiting personal calls during work hours.

But Liam changed the rules for her.

He personally tore up that prohibition.

Just so he could hear her voice whenever he missed her.

Vivian could no longer find any reason to refuse.

Daytime calls were still bearable.

But the nightly calls left her restless and unsettled.

These past few days, after each call ended, she would toss and turn for hours.

If this continued, her dark circles would become impossible to hide.

Tonight, she decided to be smarter.

After her bath, she directly set her phone to silent.

This way she could sleep peacefully without being disturbed by his calls.

She indeed fell into deep sleep quickly.

Until midnight, when she suddenly woke up.

Her phone screen was silently flashing in the darkness.

Liam Sullivan's name kept appearing.

She had actually developed a conditioned reflex.

Every day around this time, she would automatically wake up, waiting for his call.

Since she was already awake, she simply pressed the answer button.

Heavy breathing came from the other end of the line.

Liam had drunk too much at tonight's business dinner.

He stood before the floor-to-ceiling window of his hotel room, gazing at the unfamiliar city nightscape.

Alcohol made his thoughts drift to distant places.

Vivian's smiling face lingered in his mind, impossible to shake.

Longing washed over him like a tidal wave.

He desperately wanted to hear her voice.

He had dialed over a dozen times before she finally answered.

Her receiver carried his slightly intoxicated breathing.

Vivian's heartbeat skipped a beat.

"Is something wrong?" She tried to keep her voice steady.

"Why did it take so long to answer?" His voice was low and hoarse.

"I was sleeping earlier," she explained softly.

There was a brief silence on the other end.

"Did I wake you?"

"No, I just happened to wake up," she lied instinctively.

"Are you home alone?" he pressed.

"Yes."

"Talk to me," he suddenly requested.

Vivian froze.

Weren't they already talking?

"About what?" she asked, confused.

"Anything."

"I don't know what to say..."

He had been leading these past days' conversations.

He was naturally sparing with words, never saying much.

Their calls often ended in silence.

But tonight he actively wanted her to speak.

Vivian truly didn't know what to say.

Sweet nothings wouldn't leave her lips.

Confessions were even more impossible.

If she reported work matters, it would seem too unromantic.

"Then sing a song," his voice grew even huskier.

"Sing?" Vivian's cheeks burned.
###Chapter 121

Vivian Bennett had never sung for a man alone before.

"Sing for me." Liam Sullivan's voice was low and husky, laced with undeniable pressure. "Otherwise, don't even think about hanging up tonight."

His threat worked.

Vivian just wanted to end the call quickly.

She had to get up early for work tomorrow.

She began to sing softly, her voice still hazy with sleep:

"If the hero never comes to you, if you need someone you......"

It was a soft English song.

Vivian repeated the melody over and over.

Her voice was sweet and pleasant to the ear.

In the silent depth of the night, it was particularly stirring.

Liam closed his eyes, his Adam's apple bobbing violently.

A suppressed groan escaped his throat......

Vivian abruptly stopped singing.

She gripped her phone tightly, alert. "What are you doing?"

Liam didn't answer.

But heavy, ragged breathing came clearly through the receiver.

Vivian froze instantly.

That sound was all too familiar......

Her mind went blank.

When she realized what was happening, she abruptly ended the call.

That bastard!

He was listening to her sing while he......

Her phone rang again, stubbornly persistent.

Vivian bit her lip, hesitating for a moment before answering.

"What else do you want?" she asked, struggling to contain her anger.

"Vivian, I really want to..." Liam's voice was terribly hoarse, his breathing growing more rapid.

"Shameless!" Vivian spat out angrily.

Liam chuckled lowly. "I hadn't even finished my sentence. Why the name-calling?"

Normally, she would never dare speak to him like this.

But tonight, he had gone too far.

"Don't you know exactly what you were doing?" Vivian accused, humiliated and furious.

Liam's laughter deepened.

He actually liked it when she lost her temper like this.

It meant she was finally seeing him as a man, not just her boss.

"Since you know," his voice was tempting, "why not sing it again? I was almost there......"

Vivian could hardly believe her ears.

How could someone be so utterly without shame.

Imagining his tense body, his rolling Adam's apple, the beads of sweat...

For some reason, she began to feel hot and flustered herself.

Bad company corrupts good morals.

He was rubbing off on her.

"If you need a woman, you can find someone else," Vivian suggested irritably.

Liam frowned. "I don't want anyone else. I only want you."

He was fastidious. He never touched those kinds of women.

So far, she was the only one who could stir his desire.

If he weren't on a business trip abroad, he would fly back immediately and crush her into his embrace.

"Get lost!"
###Chapter 122

Vivian Bennett ended the call with force.

She powered off her phone immediately and tossed it aside.

Let's see how Liam Sullivan could bother her now.

But sleep still eluded her that night.

Liam's voice seemed to echo in her ears.

She tossed and turned until the first light of dawn.

Morning.

Vivian walked into the company with heavy dark circles under her eyes.

She had just settled at her desk when the internal line rang.

Hannah Green summoned her to the office immediately.

Vivian's heart tightened.

Was Hannah looking for trouble again?

Hannah had been much quieter since the warehouse incident and Liam's punishment.

But Liam had been out of the office for days.

There was no guarantee Hannah wouldn't seize this chance for revenge.

Vivian pushed open the door to the assistant manager's office.

Zachary Hudson lounged lazily in a chair.

A playful smile tugged at his lips, his eyes gleaming with mischief.

Hannah stood beside him, her expression icy.

"Come here and apologize to Mr. Hudson now!" Hannah snapped sharply.

Vivian froze.

Apologize?

What had she done wrong?

She hadn't provoked Zachary recently.

Suppressing her confusion, Vivian stepped forward with a professional smile.

"Mr. Hudson, how have I offended you?"

Her attitude was respectful and polite, flawless.

"Vivian Bennett, what is this attitude!" Hannah scolded immediately. "How dare you act so arrogantly after making a mistake!"

Vivian frowned.

Hannah was clearly biased toward Zachary.

She was an employee of Sullivan Group.

Yet Hannah was openly helping an outsider target her.

Zachary chuckled and waved a hand.

"Don't be angry, Ms. Green." His gaze turned to Vivian, full of amusement. "Ms. Bennett only stood me up once. It's a small matter."

Vivian suddenly remembered.

Outside the hotel restroom last time, she had promised to visit him at Hudson Group.

She had completely forgotten about it afterward.

Hannah immediately switched to a fawning expression.

"You are so magnanimous, Mr. Hudson." She shot a cold look at Vivian. "Hurry up and thank him!"

Vivian laughed coldly inside.

Hannah was obviously using this as an excuse.

But she was outnumbered right now. A direct confrontation would only bring more trouble.

"Thank you for your generosity, Mr. Hudson." Vivian offered a smooth apology.
###Chapter 123

Zachary Hudson had come in person.

He had no intention of letting her off easily.

A shrewd glint flashed in his flirtatious eyes.

Vivian Bennett’s heart tightened.

She had a bad feeling.

Zachary was clearly here to cause trouble for her.

Hannah Green was also waiting to catch her making a mistake.

These two were playing off each other.

It seemed difficult for her to escape today.

“No need for thanks. It’s my honor to be stood up by Miss Bennett.”

Zachary curled his lips into a wicked smile.

His tone suddenly shifted.

“But I have a business trip to a neighboring city soon. My assistant is sick. You seem capable. Could you fill in temporarily?”

“I refuse.”

Vivian answered without hesitation.

Be his assistant? And go out of town?

Who knew what he was really planning.

“No problem! Of course it’s no problem!”

Hannah Green cut her off and agreed immediately.

“Vivian has no other arrangements lately. You can use her for as long as you need, Mr. Hudson.”

Vivian looked at Hannah in disbelief.

Was she really being sold out like this?

She was an employee of Sullivan Group.

“Manager Green is certainly decisive.” Zachary narrowed his eyes with satisfaction.

Vivian glared at him angrily. “Don’t even think about it…”

Her words were cut off by Hannah’s sharp interruption.

“It’s your good fortune that Mr. Hudson recognizes your talent!”

Hannah lowered her voice, threatening in Vivian’s ear.

“If you keep refusing, don’t expect your bonus this month.”

Her own bonus had already been deducted because of Vivian.

This was a perfect chance to deduct Vivian’s in return.

Vivian bit her lower lip.

Zachary in front, Hannah behind.

There was no way out.

The thought of being Zachary’s assistant made her scalp prickle.

Who knew how he would torment her.

Zachary watched her furious but silenced expression.

The corners of his lips lifted in pleasure.

The gloom of recent days vanished instantly.

He exchanged a few more pleasantries with Hannah.

Then he turned to leave.

Hannah immediately ordered, “Vivian, see Mr. Hudson out.”

Vivian looked up reluctantly.

She met Zachary’s teasing smile.

Her mood grew even heavier.

But she couldn’t disobey the order.

She had no choice but to escort Zachary to the company entrance.

His limousine was already waiting at the door.

“Take care, Mr. Hudson.”

She said expressionlessly.
###Chapter 124

Vivian Bennett waved weakly and turned to leave.

Her wrist was suddenly grabbed from behind.

Before she could struggle, Zachary Hudson pulled her into his luxurious limousine.

Vivian stared at him in shock. "What are you doing?"

Zachary removed his sunglasses. He gazed at her with an amused smile. "Do you know why I insisted on having you as my assistant?"

"Isn't it to get back at me for standing you up last time?" She shot him an annoyed glance.

She wasn't stupid. She could see right through his intentions.

Zachary's lips curved into a smirk. "That's only part of the reason."

Part of the reason?

Was there something else?

Vivian paused. "What else?"

Zachary suddenly leaned in close. His warm breath brushed against her ear. "My real goal is to make you fall in love with me."

His eyes sparkled with a charming, starry light.

Vivian blinked in disbelief. "You... want to seduce me? Why?"

Why would a playboy like him suddenly be interested in her?

A teasing smile flickered across Zachary's lips. "Of course, to steal Liam Sullivan's woman."

That guy Liam, who never showed interest in women, had actually claimed Vivian as his girlfriend.

This meant he must have real feelings for her.

The more that was true, the more determined Zachary was to take Vivian away.

Just thinking about Liam's beloved woman choosing him instead filled Zachary with immense satisfaction.

It was the perfect revenge against Liam.

Plus, he could teach this little woman a lesson for repeatedly offending him.

It was killing two birds with one stone!

As Zachary was lost in his perfect plan, Vivian suddenly let out a cold laugh.

"What makes you think I would fall for you?" Her tone was dripping with sarcasm.

So what if Zachary was the dream man for countless girls in Seattle?

She had zero interest in a man with his promiscuous reputation.

If she really had to choose between Liam and Zachary.

She would definitely choose Liam.

After all, Liam was disciplined and never messed around with women.

A man like that was worth entrusting her life to.

Zachary, on the other hand, changed women like changing clothes.

A playboy like him would never give his heart to anyone.

How could she be foolish enough to fall for him? Why would she ask for trouble?

Zachary was taken aback for a moment. Then, a flirtatious smile bloomed in his peach-blossom eyes. He leaned in close again.

"With my charm, I believe you'll soon ditch Liam and jump into my arms."

He spoke with full confidence.

Then, he lightly licked her lips.

Vivian shuddered.

She was so disgusted that goosebumps covered her skin.

Satisfied with her embarrassed and angry expression, Zachary put his sunglasses back on. He finally released her.

Before leaving, he blew her a flirtatious whistle. He instructed the driver to depart.

Vivian stomped her feet in fury right where she stood.
###Chapter 125

Vivian Bennett vigorously wiped her lips where Zachary Hudson had touched them.

She walked quickly back to the company.

Her high heels clicked sharply against the floor.

The public relations department office was just ahead.

Deputy Manager Hannah Green called out to her again.

Vivian frowned.

She had just gotten rid of Zachary Hudson.

Was Hannah Green already looking for trouble again?

She pushed open the office door and entered.

"Manager Green, is there something you need?" Vivian asked, maintaining the last of her patience.

Hannah raised an eyebrow at her. "The big boss will be back soon. You know what to say and what not to say, right?"

Vivian was taken aback. "What do you mean?"

Hannah decided to be blunt. "I personally quite admire you. It's a pity you offended the wrong person."

Vivian's heart sank.

Hannah was indeed acting on someone else's orders.

Who had enough power to force her out of Sullivan Group?

She could only think of one person: Isabella Sullivan.

"Being with Young Master Hudson will only benefit you," Hannah said persuasively. "If he values you, wouldn't it be better to develop your career at Hudson Group in the future?"

Vivian coldly refused. "I have no intention of changing jobs."

Why should she leave just because they wanted her gone?

Especially to be with someone like Zachary Hudson.

Hannah sneered. "Stop dreaming. Do you really think you can latch onto the boss? I've seen plenty of women like you."

Vivian's expression turned even colder. "If there's no official business to discuss, I'll take my leave."

She turned to leave.

Hannah issued a warning from behind her. "If the boss asks, you'd better say you volunteered to be Young Master Hudson's assistant."

Vivian found it ridiculous. "Are you saying it was really my choice?"

Hannah's eyes turned icy. "Otherwise, I'll make sure you're thrown out of the public relations department."

Vivian felt a tremor in her heart.

She strode out of the office and returned to her workstation.

Her mind was in chaos.

She knew she had been set up.

Her phone suddenly vibrated.

Liam Sullivan's name appeared on the screen.

He was on a business trip abroad.

He called at this time every day.

But today, Vivian did not want to answer.
###Chapter 126

Vivian Bennett never expected that Zachary Hudson’s destination would be City A.

This city filled her with both familiarity and dread.

She was born and raised in City A.

When her mother was still alive, she had spent a carefree childhood there.

Back then, the White family was at the peak of their glory.

Her parents were prominent figures in City A.

She had been the cherished little princess of the city's high society.

But everything changed after her mother passed away.

Her father remarried.

Her stepmother gave birth to a son.

Her status plummeted.

The White family declined steadily.

Their company suffered continuous losses.

Without her mother's protection, she became her stepmother's punching bag.

She endured endless abuse.

For her, City A was a place of love and hate intertwined.

She never thought she would return so soon after escaping.

She certainly never imagined returning with a playboy like Zachary Hudson.

Zachary's secretary had already booked the most luxurious hotel in the city.

After settling in, he took Vivian directly to a nearby high-end shopping mall.

"Vivian, 'assistant' is just a polite term," he said with a mocking smile.

She responded coldly, "And what's the impolite term?"

He smirked maliciously. "The impolite term is that you're my maid and slave!"

Vivian's face turned pale with anger.

She should have known he had ill intentions.

But she never expected he would treat her like a slave.

Zachary Hudson was rotten to the core.

Seeing her furious expression, Zachary felt immensely pleased.

"I'm going shopping now. You carry the bags. Understood?"

"What if I refuse?" she glared at him.

Did he really think she would become his slave? Never.

"Refuse? I'll immediately file a complaint with your company," he narrowed his eyes arrogantly. "I'll say you deliberately disobeyed orders and offended a client in City A."

"You!" Vivian's eyes widened in rage.

This was outright slander.

But her supervisor Hannah Green was already in cahoots with him.

Hannah wouldn't listen to her explanation.

Hannah, under Isabella Sullivan's orders, had been looking for an excuse to fire her.

She couldn't let them succeed.

Even if she left Sullivan Group, it would be by her own resignation.

Why should she fall victim to their schemes?

Besides, if she lost her job, her cousin's husband would make life difficult for her cousin.

"You got me!"

She shot him a furious glare and reluctantly agreed.

Carrying bags? Fine.

She would do it.

She refused to believe a grown man could buy that much.

But Vivian was wrong.

Zachary was no ordinary man.

He was more particular than any woman.

The moment they entered the mall, he headed straight for the luxury men's section.
###Chapter 127

Zachary Hudson's slender fingers glided over a row of men's clothing.

His gaze was critical, but his movements were swift and decisive.

Any style that caught his eye was immediately yanked from the rack and tossed into Vivian Bennett's arms.

Fabric hit her one piece after another. She could barely hold it all.

The price tags were glaring. Each item cost several months of her salary.

Vivian's arms ached. She cursed Zachary a thousand times in her mind.

"Pick something." He suddenly leaned in close. His breath brushed her ear. "Whatever you like, it's on me."

Vivian gritted her teeth. "I don't need it."

She would rather stay poor than owe him a favor.

She would be grateful if he just stopped finding new ways to torment her.

Unfortunately, Zachary clearly had no intention of letting her off.

At the checkout counter, the clothes formed a small mountain.

The store clerk shot her a sympathetic glance. She silently handed over two of the largest shopping bags.

Dozens of men's outfits were stuffed inside. The weight made her wrists tremble.

Vivian carried two heavy bags of spoils. She stumbled along behind Zachary.

He walked at a leisurely pace. She felt like a laborer hauling a heavy load.

Her high heels dug into her feet. Every step was agony.

Zachary, however, was in high spirits. He turned and strode into the next boutique.

Three hours passed.

Vivian was nearly buried under shopping bags.

Red marks lined her hands. Her shoulders and back ached from the pressure. Her heels were already raw and bleeding.

She panted for breath, watching Zachary walk further away.

"What's taking so long?" He stopped by his sports car. He tapped the window impatiently.

Vivian simply stood still. "I can't walk anymore."

Zachary raised an eyebrow. A wicked smile touched his eyes. "What, you want me to carry you?"

She felt like throwing all the bags right in his face.

Suddenly, a familiar voice cut through the noise.

"Vivian Bennett?"

She froze. Her head whipped around.

Brandon White had just stepped out of a car with a glamorous woman. His sunglasses were pushed up on his forehead. He squinted, studying her.

Vivian's heart stopped.

She could not let him recognize her.

She gripped the shopping bags tightly. A surge of adrenaline gave her shocking strength. She stumbled and rushed into Zachary's car.

"Drive! Hurry, drive!" she urged the driver urgently.

Zachary slid into the car leisurely. His gaze swept over her pale face with amusement.

"Run into an old flame?" he sneered.

Vivian pressed her lips together. She said nothing.

"None of your business."

Zachary leaned closer. He lifted a strand of her hair with his fingertip.

"You're quite something. Even Liam Sullivan broke his rules for you... and now some other man? Just how many have you hooked?"

In the rearview mirror, Brandon was approaching with his female companion.

His eyes were locked intently on this car.

Vivian curled her fingers tightly. Her nails dug into her palms.

She absolutely could not... be captured again.
###Chapter 128

"Are you going to drive or not?" Vivian Bennett stomped her foot in frustration.

Zachary Hudson's lips curved into a wicked smile. "So eager to hide from your old flame?"

Vivian had no patience for his nonsense.

In the rearview mirror, Brandon White's figure drew closer.

She grabbed the door handle, ready to jump out.

"Where do you think you're going?" Zachary suddenly seized her wrist.

"I have urgent business. If you're not leaving, I'll go first." Her expression was icy.

Zachary narrowed his captivating eyes. "You're my assistant now. You go nowhere without my permission."

"I quit!" She shoved him away forcefully.

She could always find another job. But if the White family caught her, it would be over.

Zachary yanked her back, his gaze turning dangerous. "Dare to play me? You'll regret it."

"Let me go!" Vivian struggled desperately.

Brandon had reached the car.

He bent down, about to knock on the window.

With a single glance from Zachary, the driver immediately hit the gas.

The luxury car sped away.

Brandon was left empty-handed, but he memorized the license plate.

That figure had looked exactly like Vivian.

Such a top-tier luxury car was rare even in all of Seattle.

The owner had to be incredibly wealthy or powerful.

How could a man like that be interested in Vivian?

Had he been seeing things?

"Brandon, what are you looking at?" the woman in his arms asked sweetly.

"Nothing. Let's go eat." He withdrew his gaze, casually patting her rear.

She let out a playful yelp. Suddenly, she asked, "Do you know the young master Hudson?"

Brandon frowned. "Which young master Hudson?"

Her eyes sparkled. "Zachary Hudson, the son of Seattle's richest man."

Brandon's face darkened. "How would I know him?"

He dreamed of being a playboy like Zachary, surrounded by wealth, power, and beautiful women.

But the White family had long fallen from grace. It could no longer support his ambitions.

The women he played with were nowhere near Zachary's league.

Their wealth and status were worlds apart.

Brandon felt both envy and jealousy toward Zachary.

"But wasn't that luxury car Mr. Hudson's?" the woman asked curiously. "I thought you were going to say hello to him."

Brandon halted abruptly. "What did you say? That was Zachary Hudson's car?"

"Yes, I even saw Mr. Hudson standing by the car earlier. He's much handsomer in person than on TV." She looked lovesick.

Brandon immediately shook off her hand.

"If you like Mr. Hudson so much, why don't you go find him?"

He turned and strode toward his own car.

"What's wrong with you?" the woman hurried after him.

She had only complimented Zachary a few times. Was he really going to throw a fit over that?

How petty.

"Get lost. Don't come looking for me again!" Brandon snapped angrily.

But he couldn't help wondering: If that really was Vivian earlier, had she actually hooked up with Zachary Hudson?
###Chapter 129

Vivian Bennett rushed back to her hotel room.

She slammed the door shut.

Zachary Hudson was completely locked out.

Zachary stood outside the closed door.

He stared at it with an amused glint in his eyes.

This woman had accompanied him to the city, yet she was constantly on guard against him.

When she saw that man at the mall today, she was scared out of her wits.

What history did they share?

Interesting.

A intrigued smile curled at the corner of Zachary's lips.

Vivian leaned against the door, her heart pounding like a drum.

She never expected to run into Brandon White so soon after arriving in the city.

If he discovered her and dragged her back, her life would be over.

She collapsed onto the bed, her entire body cold.

She had worn high heels and carried numerous shopping bags while accompanying Zachary.

Now, every part of her ached.

Overwhelming exhaustion washed over her.

Vivian closed her eyes and quickly fell into a deep sleep.

In her drowsy state, she felt something wriggling on her body.

It was slippery, tickling her as it moved.

Vivian was too tired to care.

She swatted it away and turned over, continuing to sleep.

Not long after, the wriggling sensation returned.

This time, it was more distinct.

Vivian could bear it no longer and opened her eyes.

"Who's there?" she shouted angrily.

The next second, her pupils constricted violently.

Vivian screamed in terror.

A snake?

There was actually a snake on her bed!

The snake was only about twenty centimeters long, with vibrant, colorful patterns.

It was flicking its tongue, slowly slithering.

Vivian was terrified out of her mind.

She had heard that the more brightly colored a snake, the more venomous it was.

This snake's patterns were so vivid, it must be extremely poisonous.

Cold sweat dripped from her forehead. She didn't dare move a muscle.

"What's going on?" Zachary's knocking came from outside the door.

Vivian cried for help urgently.

"Save me! There's a snake in my room!"

Even though she disliked this man, he was her only hope now.

"I don't have a key card. How do I get in?" Zachary asked leisurely.

"Go get the hotel manager! Hurry!" Vivian shouted, her voice choked with tears.

The snake suddenly began crawling up her leg.

The icy sensation made her blood run cold.

If it crawled into her clothes...

Vivian didn't even dare to imagine.

"Wait, I'll go get the key card." Zachary's voice faded as he walked away.

Just before he left, Vivian thought she heard him chuckle softly.
###Chapter 130

Bitterness churned inside her.

But currently trapped by this snake.

She dared not move a muscle.

A standoff between human and serpent.

Time crawled by.

Zachary Hudson had not returned.

He had been gone so long.

Had something happened?

Or was he deliberately stalling?

Hoping the venomous snake would bite her?

Vivian grew more panicked with each thought.

The door suddenly swung open.

Zachary finally appeared.

He wore a white bathrobe.

Water droplets still clung to his hair.

He carried a pet carrier.

His peach blossom eyes glanced at the snake coiled around her.

"Baby, so this is where you've been."

"I've been looking everywhere for you."

Vivian froze instantly.

Her mind went completely blank.

He was calling the snake baby?

This snake definitely belonged to him.

No wonder it appeared in her room.

He must have released it intentionally.

"Zachary Hudson, you set me up?"

Vivian's rage erupted.

She shouted his full name directly.

"How could I?"

"It's in heat recently."

"Loves sneaking into beautiful women's rooms."

Zachary flashed a roguish smile.

His expression both innocent and teasing.

Vivian glared at him fiercely.

"There are so many rooms in this hotel."

"Why specifically target me?"

She didn't believe his nonsense for a second.

This was clearly his doing.

"Because you're the most beautiful."

"Its taste has always been excellent."

Zachary's lips curved slightly.

His smile exceptionally punchable.

"Get out!"

Vivian snapped sharply.

Zachary pretended to leave.

"Wait!"

She urgently called him back.

"Do you want me to stay or go?"

Zachary raised an eyebrow at her.

"Take the snake with you!"

"Then get out!"

Vivian trembled with anger.

She shifted slightly.

The small snake immediately flicked its tongue.

It started slithering into her collar.

Vivian screamed in terror.

Every hair on her body stood on end.

She wished she could faint on the spot.

"That area is off limits."

"That's my territory."

Zachary reached out and grabbed the snake.

He lectured with complete seriousness.

Vivian wiped away cold sweat.

Finally breathing a sigh of relief.

"You seem quite afraid of it?"

Zachary narrowed his peach blossom eyes.

Studying her with great interest.
###Chapter 131

"Who said I'm scared?" Vivian straightened her back, forcing a fake smile at him.

She wouldn't give Zachary Hudson any leverage.

If this playboy knew she was afraid of snakes, who knew how he would torment her in the future.

She absolutely couldn't admit it.

"Really not scared?" Zachary raised an eyebrow, a mocking smile playing on his lips. "Who was it that turned pale just now?"

"You saw wrong." Vivian feigned composure, glaring at him fiercely.

Zachary stared at her for a few seconds, then suddenly grinned mischievously. "Since you're not scared, you can take care of it for me tonight."

He placed the snake back on the bed as he spoke.

Vivian instantly jumped up, her face ashen.

"Take it away! Now!" she shrieked, her voice commanding.

Zachary laughed triumphantly.

"Keep pretending." He exposed her lie with glee.

Vivian gritted her teeth in anger. "Are you taking it away or not?"

Zachary sat down leisurely, crossing his legs. "Tell me, who was that man earlier?"

"None of your business!" Vivian shot back immediately.

Why should she explain her relationship with Brandon White to him?

"Fine, don't tell me." Zachary narrowed his eyes, his smile turning wicked. "It can keep you company tonight."

"You..." Vivian trembled with rage.

This man was utterly shameless!

The small snake suddenly slithered toward her.

Vivian screamed and dashed into the bathroom.

She slammed the door shut with a loud bang.

Frantically, she stuffed towels and tissues into the gaps under the door.

Only after confirming the snake couldn't get in did she collapse onto the floor.

Zachary actually came to knock on the door.

"Won't talk? Then I'm leaving."

"Don't you dare leave!" Vivian roared.

"Goodnight." Zachary bid farewell cheerfully.

His footsteps faded away, and the door clicked shut.

Vivian was about to explode.

This man was the absolute worst.

Using a snake to threaten her.

Was he even human?

She had been at his beck and call all day at the mall and hadn't eaten anything yet.

Exhausted and starving, she was now trapped in the bathroom.

Her phone was still outside.

She couldn't even call for help.

A long night began.

Because of that snake, Vivian didn't dare close her eyes all night.

Early the next morning, Zachary finally came to collect his pet.

"Ten minutes to get ready. Come downstairs for breakfast." He glanced at his diamond watch. "We're going to a business meeting in twenty minutes."

Vivian's mouth twitched.

This demon didn't even give her a moment to catch her breath.

But she was his "assistant" now.

Suppressing her anger, Vivian walked into the bedroom to change.
###Chapter 132

Vivian Bennett dragged her exhausted body back to Seattle.

She stood before the mirror, barely recognizing herself.

Her reflection showed sallow skin with dark circles under her eyes.

Her hair was disheveled, her entire appearance haggard.

These past few days had been a complete nightmare.

Zachary Hudson was absolutely the worst man in the world.

She swore she never wanted to see him again in her lifetime.

Vivian planned to take a hot shower first.

Then get some proper sleep.

Just as she reached the bedroom door, the doorbell suddenly rang.

Puzzled, she opened the door.

No one was outside.

She closed the door and headed toward the bathroom.

She had just taken off her clothes when the doorbell rang again.

Vivian grabbed a bathrobe and wrapped it around herself.

She opened the door once more.

The hallway remained empty.

Was she hearing things?

Or was someone playing a prank?

She was about to close the door.

A large hand suddenly pressed against the doorframe.

Vivian stumbled back in alarm.

The door was pushed open.

A tall figure loomed over her.

He radiated intense pressure.

"Who's there?" she cried out.

"It's me."

A deep, familiar voice spoke.

Vivian looked up.

Liam Sullivan stood at her door in a crisp suit.

"Why are you here?" she asked in surprise.

Liam frowned, his expression stern.

"Who were you expecting?"

Her heart raced. "I thought someone was playing a joke..."

She suddenly remembered something.

"Weren't you on a business trip abroad?"

Liam gazed at her with profound intensity.

"I just returned."

"What a coincidence," Vivian murmured.

She had just escaped from A City.

And now he appeared.

Liam suddenly scooped her up into his arms.

He turned and kicked the door shut.

"What are you doing?" Vivian panicked.

She remembered what he had said before leaving.

Was he really going to...

Liam placed her on the sofa.

He studied her face carefully.

"You've lost weight."

After a week apart, she had clearly grown thinner.

She exhaustion from every pore.

Vivian smiled bitterly to herself.

It was all because of Zachary Hudson's torment.

That man had treated her like a servant.

He constantly terrified her with snakes.

She was on the verge of a mental breakdown.

She lowered her eyes and asked softly, "Did you need something?"

Right now, she just wanted to rest.

Liam stared at her pale face.

"I came to see you."

Vivian looked at him helplessly.

"What's so interesting about me?"

Liam's gaze deepened.

"You're my woman. Who else would I look at?"
###Chapter 133

"Who is your woman?" Vivian Bennett retorted instinctively.

Liam Sullivan's handsome face tightened instantly.

She immediately realized her mistake.

His dark brows rose. His eyes turned intense. "Not my woman?" he said in a low voice. "Then I'll make you mine right now."

His large hand gripped her slender waist. He lowered his head and captured her lips.

Vivian was caught completely off guard.

She pushed against him, but he held her firmly.

Liam kissed her with fierce possessiveness.

His hand slid beneath her thin dress. It caressed the smooth skin of her waist.

The scorching heat of his palm set her skin on fire.

Vivian quickly grabbed his wrist.

"Just kiss me. Stop touching me."

"So you like it when I kiss you..." Liam's eyes lit up.

He pinned both her hands above her head. This gave him better access to her mouth.

Vivian felt like she was suffocating from his kiss.

His potent masculine scent overwhelmed her senses.

"Stop..." she gasped breathlessly.

Liam showed no signs of stopping.

His hand continued exploring her skin.

Suddenly, he paused. His gaze deepened intensely.

Vivian's face flushed bright red.

She glared at him in embarrassment. "I was about to take a shower..."

She had already undressed for her shower.

Hearing the doorbell, she had only hastily wrapped a robe around herself.

Liam's breathing grew noticeably heavier.

His blood roared with restless energy.

Vivian pushed at him frantically. "Let me go."

Liam looked at her deeply. "You need to shower?"

Vivian desperately wanted to ask him to leave.

But he showed no intention of going anywhere.

If she admitted she needed to shower, would he think she was inviting him?

What if he wanted to join her? That would be disastrous.

"No," she said, making an excuse. "I just got back from a business trip. I'm very tired. I want to rest."

Liam narrowed his eyes. "You were on a business trip too?"

He had called her several times these past few days. She hardly ever answered.

It wasn't that Vivian was ignoring him on purpose.

Zachary Hudson had simply exhausted her.

She hadn't had the energy to answer his calls.

At first, Liam thought she was angry about their last flirtatious phone call.

Later, he learned she had gone to City A on business.

"Yes," Vivian nodded wearily.

Seeing how tired she was, surely he would let her go now?

Instead, Liam held her even tighter.

"Why didn't you tell me about your business trip?" he asked softly.

Vivian raised a brow. "You know now, don't you?"

She hadn't intended to hide it from him.

Working at Sullivan Group made it impossible to keep secrets from him.

"Are you tired?" Liam looked down at her.

"Very," she answered without hesitation.

Trapped in his embrace, her hands had nowhere to go.

Her fingertips accidentally brushed against his firm abs. She pulled back as if electrocuted.

"I'll stay with you tonight." Liam kissed her forehead. He said it suddenly.

Vivian immediately felt exasperated.

What was going on?

She had just admitted how exhausted she was.
###Chapter 134

Vivian Bennett closed her eyes wearily.

She couldn't hold on any longer.

Liam Sullivan gazed at her with profound intensity.

"I know," he said in a low voice.

He scooped her up into his arms.

He turned and carried her into the bedroom.

He gently laid her on the soft bed.

"Sleep," he whispered hoarsely.

Vivian blinked in confusion.

Liam showed no intention of leaving.

He remained seated by the bed, watching over her.

But he seemed unwilling to touch her again.

Vivian was simply too exhausted.

She had no energy left to decipher his thoughts.

Her heavy eyelids drifted shut.

She soon fell into a deep slumber.

Vivian had a nightmare.

In her dream, Zachary Hudson chased her with a snake.

Brandon White laughed maniacally beside them.

She woke up with a start, terrified.

The room was pitch black.

Her forehead was damp with cold sweat.

Vivian took several shaky breaths.

She realized it had only been a dream.

Those two despicable men had haunted her again.

Perhaps returning to the city had triggered it.

Unpleasant memories came flooding back.

After her mother passed, her status in the White family plummeted.

Once Brandon was born, she was treated worse than the servants.

Brandon had always enjoyed tormenting her since childhood.

He took pleasure in bullying her.

Zachary's recent behavior reminded her of those painful days.

Vivian's throat felt parched.

Her head throbbed dully.

Her entire body felt weak.

She pushed the covers aside and got out of bed.

She wanted to get some water from the kitchen.

As she passed through the living room, she suddenly spotted a figure in the darkness.

"Who's there?" she cried out in alarm.

"Awake?" a man's low, raspy voice responded.

Vivian froze.

It was Liam.

She remembered him carrying her to bed before she fell asleep.

She thought he had left long ago.

She hadn't expected him to still be here.

Her throat ached and her body felt unwell.

She didn't want to speak with him.

She turned and walked into the kitchen.

She had forgotten to boil water before bed.

Now she was desperately thirsty.

She opened the refrigerator and took out a bottle of cold water.

Just as she twisted the cap open.

A long, elegant hand suddenly reached over.

It snatched the water bottle from her grasp.

Vivian stared in shock.

She turned to look at the man behind her.

Liam stepped closer.

His solid chest pressed against her back.

"Don't drink cold water at night," he murmured softly.

His warm breath brushed against her ear.

Vivian shivered slightly.

She forced herself to remain calm. "I didn't boil any water."

"I already did," Liam said gently.

He picked up the thermal pot from the counter.

Vivian looked at him in surprise.

She hadn't expected him to be so thoughtful.

Liam had removed his suit jacket.

He wore only a dress shirt and trousers.

The thin fabric outlined his powerful physique.

The lines of his straight, broad back were strikingly defined.
###Chapter 135

The kitchen was cramped.

The air was filled with Liam Sullivan's distinct masculine scent.

Vivian Bennett's eyelashes fluttered slightly.

She subtly shifted her body toward the counter.

Liam Sullivan retrieved a thermal flask.

He then took an empty cup.

He poured half a cup of hot water first.

Then he added half a cup of mineral water.

The temperature was just right.

He directly brought the cup to her lips.

Vivian Bennett was taken aback.

She reached out and took the cup.

She tilted her head back and drank the warm water in one gulp.

The dryness in her throat was finally soothed.

Her head still felt heavy.

She turned to return to the bedroom.

After taking just two steps.

Everything suddenly went black.

Her body was abruptly suspended in the air.

Her consciousness completely faded.

When she woke again, the sunlight was harsh.

Vivian Bennett struggled to open her eyes.

Her entire body felt scorching hot, as if burning.

Her limbs were weak and limp.

A deep voice sounded in her ear. "You're awake?"

Liam Sullivan was sitting by the bed.

She frowned. "Why are you still here?"

He had stayed from last night until this morning.

Liam Sullivan's gaze was profound. "If I left, who would take care of you?"

Vivian Bennett was stunned.

He needed to take care of her?

"Your fever reached 39 degrees," he said gravely.
Vivian Bennett finally realized she had a fever.

No wonder she felt so awful.

She had rarely gotten sick since leaving the White family.

This time, Zachary Hudson had really worn her out.
"Did I faint last night?"

Liam Sullivan nodded.

He handed her a glass of warm water.

His palm pressed against her forehead. "The fever has broken."
His tone carried a hint of imperceptible tenderness.

Vivian Bennett took a small sip of water.
"You should go attend to your work."

She knew his duties were demanding.

He must have many matters to handle after just returning to the country.

"Sleep a little longer," he coaxed in a low voice.
Vivian Bennett was indeed utterly exhausted.

Her eyelids were too heavy to keep open.

She stopped overthinking and sank into a deep sleep.
###Chapter 136

Sunlight filtered through the gap in the curtains.

Vivian Bennett slowly opened her eyes.

The bedroom was quiet.

She instinctively reached for the space beside her.

The sheets were cold.

Liam Sullivan must have gone to the office long ago.

She propped herself up on her elbows, trying to sit.

The door opened at that moment.

Liam entered carrying a tray.

It held plain congee and small side dishes.

"Awake?" His deep voice held concern. "Still feeling unwell?"

Vivian was stunned. "You didn't go to work?"

Liam placed the tray on the nightstand.

The corner of his mouth lifted slightly. "You're sick. How could I leave?"

Vivian turned her face away. "It's just a fever."

Liam sat on the edge of the bed.

His gaze was intense. "A fever is still an illness."

He reached out to feel her forehead.

His palm was warm.

Vivian instinctively flinched away.

"Don't move." His tone was commanding. "Let me check if the fever is gone."

His fingertips gently brushed her skin.

Vivian held her breath.

"Good. The fever broke." Liam sighed in relief.

He picked up the bowl of congee. "Eat something."

Vivian reached for the bowl. "I can do it myself."

Liam moved it out of her reach. "I'll feed you."

"No need..." Vivian frowned. "I'm not incapacitated."

Liam looked at her intently. "I want to take care of you."

His gaze was too focused.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

She averted her eyes. "Suit yourself."

Liam gently blew on a spoonful of congee.

He brought it to her lips.

Vivian hesitated for a moment.

She finally opened her mouth.

The warm congee slid down her throat.

Her stomach immediately felt more comfortable.

Liam picked up some side dishes with chopsticks. "Eat slowly."

The branch plan had launched two weeks ago with five billion in funding.

This was Chengjing Beauty's crucial step into the Nanhe market.

A slow start was understandable, but no progress after this long was unacceptable.

Her voice turned cold. "Get Manager Liu on the line. I want answers."

"Funding is secured. Production lines are ready. Why hasn't the market been penetrated?"

The secretary nervously dialed and handed her the receiver.

"President Gu, hello."

Gu Qingcheng went straight to the point. "Manager Liu, why has the provincial branch made zero progress in two weeks?"

His voice wavered. "President Gu, just a little more time. I'll secure the market!"

Her tone sharpened. "That's what you said last time!"

"Give me the real reason, or I'll assume incompetence."

Manager Liu gritted his teeth. "President Gu, it's not for lack of effort!"

"The top three industry giants here have ordered a blockade. No company dares defy them!"

Gu Qingcheng paused, glancing at her secretary.

The secretary explained quietly. "Lankou Company has aristocratic backing. They lead Nanhe's beauty industry."

"All outside companies need their approval to enter."

"Our company is new. Being targeted was inevitable."

Gu Qingcheng considered this.

If true, Manager Liu couldn't bear full blame.

He added urgently. "President Gu, Lankou is hosting a beauty product competition in three days. They've invited us."

"I heard they've enlisted two divine physicians from the Medical Alliance and Western medicine experts as judges. This is a prime opportunity for exposure."

Her eyes lit up.

A product effectiveness competition? She had never feared such challenges!

The two products developed by Su Mu had passed rigorous testing and received exceptional market feedback.
###Chapter 148

Another deliberate obstacle.

Ready to deliver a fatal blow.
###Chapter 150

###Chapter 151

###Chapter 152
