She couldn't stay in the CEO's office forever.

Liam slowly looked up.

"You still want to go back?" His voice was low.

"What?" Vivian was confused.

Liam's gaze was profound. "Was Mr. Richardson's lesson not enough?"

Hearing that name, Vivian trembled.

Fear flashed in her eyes.

She nodded and then shook her head.

She naturally wanted to avoid such clients.

But as an employee, did she have the right to choose clients?
###Chapter 169

Liam Sullivan's deep eyes suddenly tightened.

He saw she still looked completely clueless.

He decided to be direct. "You could consider transferring to the executive office. You would report directly to me."

He leaned back lazily, his gaze sharp and locked on her.

He deliberately emphasized the words "report directly to me."

It meant he would personally shield her from now on.

No one would dare to give her a hard time again.

"What?" Vivian Bennett's heart jumped. She felt inexplicably uneasy.

It would have been better if Liam hadn't mentioned the transfer.

Now that he brought it up, all her alarm bells started ringing.

What did he mean by this?

Did he want to keep her under his direct supervision?

So he could…

Vivian shuddered violently.

She quickly shook her head. "No, that's not necessary. I'm better off staying in the PR department."

Even though staying in PR meant she would still face Hannah Green's response.

But it would mostly be psychological torment.

If she transferred to work near Liam, it would exhaust her both physically and mentally.

Comparing the two, she would rather go back and face Hannah Green.

At least Hannah wouldn't actually devour her whole.

Liam's expression darkened instantly when he saw her refuse without hesitation.

An inexplicable irritation surged in his heart.

He was genuinely trying to look out for her, yet she showed no appreciation at all.

The atmosphere plummeted to freezing point.

Even from a distance, Vivian could feel the dangerous aura emanating from him.

"I'm still inexperienced. I want more training from the ground up."

She forced a smile, trying to explain. "If I suddenly jump several levels to work directly under you, it will inevitably cause gossip."

She laughed dryly, struggling to find a plausible reason to prevent him from losing his temper on the spot.

"Who would dare to gossip?" Liam frowned, his handsome face instantly covered with a frosty layer.

Vivian was speechless.

The air in the office almost solidified.

Just then, a hurried voice came from outside the door. "Vice President, you can't go in…"

Vivian stiffened all over.

Vice President?

Was Isabella Sullivan here?

She looked at Liam in panic, her voice trembling. "I… I'll go hide inside."

As she spoke, she stood up and hurried towards the direction of the restroom.

If Isabella saw her in Liam's office, she would definitely suspect Vivian of seducing her brother.

Vivian had experienced Isabella's methods before.

To avoid causing trouble for Liam, and to protect herself, it was better to hide.

But before she could take two steps, Liam's deep and firm voice came from behind. "Stay right here. Don't go anywhere."

Vivian froze. She looked at the man's suddenly gloomy face, feeling confused.

Wasn't he afraid Isabella would see them and get the wrong idea?

She couldn't help but worry. "But what if she sees me when she comes in?"

Given her rank, she shouldn't be in the CEO's office at all.

If it was for business, Isabella definitely wouldn't believe it.

But if it was personal, it would confirm that she and Liam had an unusual relationship.
###Chapter 170

Liam Sullivan narrowed his eyes, watching her flustered state.

She resembled a startled doe, her gaze shifting nervously.

A wave of displeasure rose within him.

Was he so disgraceful to be seen with?

The man snorted coldly, his voice deep and authoritative. "Is the CEO's office a place she can just enter whenever she wishes?"

Vivian Bennett felt the tense string in her heart loosen slightly.

She sat back down on the sofa, but her ears remained sharply perked.

Isabella Sullivan's dissatisfied questioning echoed from outside the door.

Ryan Johnson's tall and straight figure blocked the entrance like an immovable gatekeeper.

Isabella had come today because of Hannah Green.

Hannah was someone she had personally promoted. How could she just be fired without reason?

She had come twice before, but Liam wasn't in his office.

This was utterly unbelievable.

Her brother was always a workaholic. It was the norm for him to be punctually present in the CEO's office.

Upon investigation, she discovered Liam had come to the company with Vivian today.

Not only were they late, but they had also stepped out of the same luxury car.

All signs indicated that Liam had been with Vivian last night.

Something must have happened between them.

Isabella trembled with rage.

Her brother had always been indifferent to women. Why make an exception for Vivian?

What was even more infuriating was that this wretched woman, Vivian, dared to linger in the CEO's office without leaving.

Isabella was determined to confront her.

Unexpectedly, she was stopped by Ryan as soon as she reached the door.

No matter how she threatened or cajoled, the man remained unmoved.

Isabella was practically driven mad with anger.

She was Liam's own sister, the company's vice president.

Did Ryan really show her no respect at all?

What did this imply?

It implied her brother was definitely doing something unspeakable with Vivian inside that office.

The thought made her burn with jealousy.

That heart-wrenching feeling threatened to consume her completely.

She shot Ryan a venomous glare, grinding her teeth. "You just wait!"

The sound of her high heels gradually faded into the distance.

Ryan continued to stand expressionlessly, as if nothing had happened.

Only when complete silence fell outside did Vivian finally let out a sigh of relief.

Thankfully, Isabella had left.

If she had been discovered, no amount of explanation would have cleared her name.

She looked toward Liam, who sat composedly behind his desk, and said with concern, "If she had seen me here, she would definitely suspect our relationship."

Liam lifted his head, his deep gaze locking onto her.

He raised an eyebrow and retorted, "What relationship?"

Vivian was immediately stumped.

She opened her mouth, struggling for a long moment before finally blurting out, "No relationship at all!"

Liam's eyes darkened, a faint, almost mocking smile tugging at the corner of his lips. "Save that line for Isabella. It doesn't work on me. We've slept together. How can there be no relationship?"

Vivian instantly refuted, "Who slept with you?"

Liam chuckled lowly, his voice laced with amusement. "Didn't we sleep together last night? And before that, at your place, we slept together too."

Vivian was rendered speechless.

They had indeed slept together.

Although nothing had happened...

They had definitely shared a bed.
###Chapter 171

Vivian Bennett stayed in Liam Sullivan's office until noon.

After lunch, she found an excuse to return to the Public Relations Department.

Her heart raced the entire way.

She was fully prepared to face a harsh scolding from Hannah Green.

Pushing open the door to the PR department, she was surprised to find Hannah absent.

Several colleagues were gathered together, chatting.

"Hannah was so arrogant before. Who knew she'd end up like this?" Female Colleague A's voice was sharp, dripping with undisguised schadenfreude.

"Wasn't she just relying on the Vice President's support? Even the VP couldn't save her this time. Serves her right!" Female Colleague B's tone was sarcastic.

"Who did she offend anyway? I thought she was the VP's person?"

Everyone chattered away, their curiosity thoroughly piqued.

"Who knows? Karma's a bitch!"

Laughter echoed through the office.

Vivian stood frozen in place.

From the sound of it, had Hannah been fired?

She suddenly remembered being assigned by Hannah to entertain Mr. Richardson.

Could it be because of that?

"Vivian!"

Emily Davis's voice came from behind her.

Vivian turned and saw the concern in Emily's eyes.

"Are you sick?" Emily looked her over.

Vivian was momentarily confused. "Sick?"

"Didn't you take a half-day sick leave?" Emily asked, puzzled.

Vivian then remembered the morning's events.

"Yeah, I wasn't feeling well. I'm better now." She forced a smile.

Emily studied her face carefully.

Confirming she was indeed okay, Emily breathed a sigh of relief.

"Did you hear? Hannah Green was fired." Emily lowered her voice.

Vivian had been wanting to ask about this.

"Why was she fired?"

Emily shrugged. "The notice came directly from the President's office. Even the Vice President couldn't protect her."

Vivian was stunned. "The President's office?"

So it was Liam himself who ordered the dismissal.

"Yeah." Emily raised an eyebrow. "But this is good for you. No one will trouble you anymore."

Vivian fell silent.

Did Liam fire Hannah for her sake?

In the Vice President's office, Hannah Green was sobbing to Isabella Sullivan.

"VP, on what grounds did the President fire me? I've worked so hard for the company all these years. Even if I haven't done great work, I've done my share..."

Isabella was agitated.

She had just learned Liam spent the entire morning alone with Vivian in his office.

Seeing Hannah in this state now only increased her irritation.

"Stop crying!" Isabella snapped sharply.

Hannah was startled into silence, though tears continued to stream down her face.

"VP, please plead my case to the President. It's all a misunderstanding. I'll work hard from now on..." Hannah hurriedly promised.

Isabella gave her a cold glance.

Hannah was her person. She had originally intended to keep her to deal with Vivian.

She never expected her brother to be so decisive this time.
###Chapter 172

Isabella Sullivan’s fingertips turned cold.

She never expected her brother to go this far for Vivian Bennett.

He didn’t even spare his own sister any face.

Hannah Green was her person, after all.

Yet he fired her without hesitation.

It seemed Vivian held significant weight in his heart.

Isabella’s gaze darkened as she scanned Hannah’s anxious face.

Confronting her brother head-on was unwise.

She took a deep breath, suppressing her rising anger.

“Go to the branch office for now,” she ordered coldly. “I’ll transfer you back when the time is right.”

Hannah’s face paled instantly.

She didn’t want to leave headquarters.

The salary and prospects here were unmatched by any branch.

“Vice President, when will the time be right?” she pressed urgently.

A ruthless glint flashed in Isabella’s eyes.

“When my brother grows tired of that bitch.”

She nearly spat the words through clenched teeth.

Hannah’s heart sank.

That could take forever.

She couldn’t wait that long.

She needed to make the CEO discard Vivian soon.

“Vice President, can you bear to see the CEO bewitched by that kind of woman?” she carefully fanned the flames.

Isabella shot her a cold glance. “What do you mean?”

Hannah leaned in, lowering her voice. “We can make the CEO see her true colors sooner...”

Isabella narrowed her eyes. “You have an idea?”

A sinister smile curled Hannah’s lips as she whispered a plan into Isabella’s ear.

Before leaving work, Vivian received a call from her cousin Grace Harper.

Hearing her cousin’s gentle voice, she immediately agreed to dinner that night.

She hadn’t seen Grace since returning from Seattle.

She wondered how Grace and her husband Jason were doing now.

After hanging up, she suddenly remembered Liam Sullivan’s words from the morning.

He had told her to wait for him after work.

But the boss was incredibly busy. He might have already forgotten.

Vivian shook her head, dismissing the thought.

She left the office promptly at the end of the workday.

Grace was frugal and didn’t want Vivian to spend money.

She had already bought groceries and was waiting at Vivian’s doorstep.

When Vivian returned to Lakeside Manor, she saw her cousin sitting alone by the door.

“Grace!” she called out, quickening her pace.

Grace looked up with a gentle smile. “Vivian, you’re back.”
###Chapter 173

Vivian Bennett took out her keys and unlocked the front door.

She took the shopping bags from her cousin and stepped aside to let Grace Harper enter first.

"You've been busy all day. Go rest on the sofa. I'll handle dinner," Grace said while rolling up her sleeves.

Vivian felt too embarrassed to let her cousin handle everything alone.

A light smile touched her lips. "It's fine. I'll help you."

She had just picked up an apron when her phone rang.

In a hurry to get to the kitchen, Vivian answered without looking.

"Where are you?"

Liam Sullivan's deep voice came through the receiver.

Vivian froze. "What?"

"Weren't you supposed to wait for me after work?" His tone carried clear displeasure.

He had specifically made time to go to the PR department to find her.

Yet he had come up empty again.

"I'm already home..." Vivian answered softly.

She hadn't actually expected him to come find her.

Was the CEO always this free?

Did he have time to meet women after work?

No business engagements?

"Vivian Bennett, how many times is this now?" Liam's voice sounded almost gritted out.

"How many times for what?" She was confused.

"This is the second time you've stood me up," he reminded her coldly.

This woman was getting bolder.

"Sorry."

Thinking quickly, Vivian softened her voice into a coaxing tone. "I'll make it up to you next time we meet."

Her voice was soft and sweet.

Liam's anger inexplicably faded by half.

"Make it up to me? How?"

Vivian immediately blushed.

She regretted her careless words.

"Grace is calling me to wash the vegetables. I have to go!"

She frantically found an excuse to end the call.

Liam's previously dark expression actually softened.

Ryan Johnson, standing nearby, watched in stunned silence.

What was wrong with the Boss?

One moment he was demanding answers, the next he looked positively pleased?

Of course, he only dared to keep these thoughts to himself.

"Who was that?" Grace asked casually.

"Just a colleague," Vivian feigned calm.

She didn't want her cousin to suspect anything.

"Why did you suddenly want to come have dinner here today?" She changed the subject.

"Your brother-in-law is on a business trip. I was free and thought I'd come improve your meals," Grace said with a laugh.

"Jason is on a business trip?" Vivian was taken aback.

She suddenly remembered what she had seen at the mall that day.

Her cousin's husband laughing and chatting animatedly with another woman.

She must have seen wrong.

"What's wrong?" Grace noticed her strange reaction.

"Nothing." Vivian lowered her eyes and shook her head gently.

Without evidence, she couldn't say anything.
###Chapter 174

Vivian Bennett stepped through the doors of Sullivan Group and immediately sensed a strange atmosphere.

Employees were huddled together, whispering.

They scattered the moment they saw her.

Their evasive glances held a scrutinizing quality.

There was even a hint of barely concealed disdain.

Suspicion rose within Vivian.

Had something happened at the company?

Was it related to her?

She took a deep breath and walked straight to the Public Relations department.

The murmurs followed her like a shadow.

She had just sat down at her workstation when Emily Davis approached.

"Vivian, have you checked Twitter?"

Vivian shook her head.

She had overslept and nearly been late. There was no time to check her phone.

"You really don't know?" Emily's eyes widened.

Seeing Vivian's confused expression, she stamped her foot in frustration.

"What is it?" Vivian pressed.

The company's vibe was too strange.

Hannah Green had already been fired. Was someone else causing trouble?

Or had Liam Sullivan gone public with their relationship?

He wouldn't be that impulsive.

Vivian's heart began to race.

A faint sense of unease crept over her.

"Just look for yourself." Emily lowered her voice.

Vivian's fingertips turned cold.

Her luck had been terrible lately. Had something else happened?

Her computer screen lit up, displaying a trending headline.

"Zachary Hudson's New Flame Exposed, Spent the Night Together"

The accompanying photo was clearly of her and Zachary Hudson at the A-city mall.

Her face was captured perfectly.

"What is this?" Vivian exclaimed without thinking.

It was a business trip. How had it turned into a scandal?

Zachary had given her a hard time during that trip.

Now it was being reported like this?

Which media outlet was spreading this nonsense?

They were clearly using Zachary's fame for clicks.
###Chapter 175

The top floor, Imperial Club.

Beneath dazzling crystal chandeliers, girls swayed their slender waists in the center of the dance floor.

Their sheer skirts fluttered with the rhythm of the music.

Ye Xiaofeng's gaze swept across the room.

Money and beauty were always inseparable.

"Mr. Li, please take the seat of honor!"

Hong Qigong pointed to the gilded dragon throne.

The pure gold armrests shimmered under the lights.

Ye Xiaofeng gave a slight bow. "You are the host, Qigong. The main seat is rightfully yours."

A glint of satisfaction flashed in Hong Qigong's eyes.

He laughed heartily and pulled Ye Xiaofeng to sit beside him.

The merchants took their seats one by one, their eyes burning with intensity.

Song Yazhi leaned in and whispered, "We're at the next table."

She gave a subtle wink.

Ye Xiaofeng nodded in acknowledgment and said no more.

This woman simply couldn't contain herself.

"A toast to welcome Mr. Li!"

Hong Qigong was the first to rise.

The clinking of glasses filled the air.

"To Mr. Li!"

Amid the boisterous flattery, Ye Xiaofeng took a small sip.

Delicacies were served in succession, and the song and dance grew more alluring.

Hong Qigong shot a meaningful glance to the man beside him.

The middle-aged man immediately raised his glass and stood.

"Mr. Li, I am Yu Gui of the Federation."

He offered his glass with both hands. "I heard you plan to invest in Qushui City. I am truly honored!"

Ye Xiaofeng raised his glass in return.

Yu Gui seized the moment to sit down. "Last year, investors from Hong Kong put in two hundred million, but it was all in the service industry."

He changed tack. "Do you plan to build factories or engage in trade?"

"My brothers are all in real estate."

Ye Xiaofeng swirled his glass. "I have a preference for the snack industry."

The corner of his mouth lifted. "Making two billion is not a difficult task."

The entire room fell silent.

Yu Gui downed his entire glass in one gulp. "Mr. Li has remarkable ambition!"

Hong Xianzhang immediately chimed in. "President Yu's annual output is sixty million!"

He looked eagerly at Ye Xiaofeng. "Absolutely reliable!"

"I have a capacity of ten million!"

"I can handle twenty million!"

The merchants rushed to shout out their numbers.

Hong Qigong narrowed his eyes as he studied Ye Xiaofeng.

The dance music suddenly became more intense.
###Chapter 176

The headline prominently featured the scandal between Vivian Bennett and Zachary Hudson.

Several clear photos accompanied the article.

The first showed Vivian helping Zachary select a wristwatch at a luxury boutique.

The second captured them walking side by side into the lobby of a five-star hotel.

The final shot was a candid photo of Zachary holding Vivian's waist as they exited a private club.

Liam Sullivan stared at his phone screen, his expression darkening instantly.

The oppressive atmosphere around him made Ryan Johnson hold his breath.

Suddenly, a loud crash echoed as the phone was violently smashed against the floor.

The screen instantly shattered into a spiderweb pattern.

"When did this happen?"

Liam's eyes churned with a terrifying storm. His voice was piercingly cold.

He added with difficulty, "Because Miss Bennett is so recognizable, many people at the company have already identified her."

"Suppress it."

Liam's command was absolute. "I don't want to see any related keywords."

Ryan answered cautiously. "There's discussion in every department... Should we issue a gag order?"

"Release an internal announcement immediately."

Liam's gaze was icy. "Anyone caught discussing the scandal will have their entire annual bonus revoked."

Ryan quickly noted the requirements.

Zachary Hudson's scandalous news usually flared up and died down quickly.

Suppressing the heat would work. Attention would naturally shift once new gossip emerged.

But this time was clearly different.

A dark glint flashed in Liam's eyes. "Find out who is pushing this from behind the scenes."

The photo angles were too precise.

It resembled premeditated stalking and secret photography.

As Ryan turned to leave, he hesitated. "Should we investigate the relationship between Miss Bennett and Mr. Hudson?"

The photos were far too intimate.

Anyone seeing them would draw conclusions.

Liam's chest tightened abruptly.

His knuckles turned white from clenching.
###Chapter 177

Vivian Bennett paced anxiously downstairs.

What a mess.

Her scandal with Zachary Hudson was spreading everywhere.

How could she ever show her face again?

Would her cousin Grace Harper and best friend Sophia Miller see the news?

Even if they didn't, everyone at the company already knew.

How could she continue working at Sullivan Group?

Would she really have to bear the label of Zachary Hudson's rumored girlfriend?

She believed Vivian wouldn't actively pursue the young master of the Hudson family.

But others wouldn't think the same.

How many judgmental stares would Vivian have to endure?

"Of course it's not true." Vivian stamped her foot in frustration. "I did go to Seattle with Zachary Hudson, but it was nothing like what the reports say."

That business trip had been a complete disaster.

Zachary had put her through so much trouble.

How could she possibly have tried to seduce him?

She wasn't insane.

Vivian's face remained clouded with worry.

How could others' opinions not matter?

Wherever she went now, people pointed and whispered.

Everyone had already labeled her—

Zachary Hudson's rumored girlfriend.

Her relationship with Liam Sullivan hadn't even been made public yet.

But this scandal had broken out first.

How could she maintain her position at Sullivan Group?

What would Liam think?

Vivian's head throbbed with pain.

She felt completely restless and agitated.

Just then, the building's intercom suddenly crackled to life: Discussing non-work related matters during company time is strictly prohibited. Violators will have their year-end bonus withheld.

Had he seen the news too?

He was here to confront her.

She immediately fell silent.

He trapped her against the railing.

"What happened?" His phoenix eyes narrowed, glinting with a cold light.

Liam stood tall and imposing, his presence overwhelming.

Even in high heels, Vivian stood a head shorter than him.

She was at a disadvantage in terms of presence.

Vivian's eyelashes fluttered slightly as she hesitated to speak.

The sharp intensity radiating from Liam was suffocating.
###Chapter 178

Qin Jiu Nian stood atop the towering palace walls, his gaze sweeping over the prosperous capital below.

He understood better than anyone that if the root cause of chaos was not completely eradicated, rebellion would inevitably return.

The noble clans and cultivators of the Eastern Cang Domain, who once harbored rebellious ambitions, now all knelt in submission to the imperial authority.

The strategy of combining benevolence with power had solidified the dynasty's fortune. The people's hearts were aligned, and heaven and earth worked in unison.

"While my elder brother wages war against powerful enemies on the front lines, I must hold this kingdom secure from the rear," Qin Jiu Nian murmured, his gaze fixed on the distant horizon, resolute and firm.

He knew deeply that the stronger the Great Xia Dynasty became, the more advantageous it was for Qin Feng.

…

One month later, the Great Xia imperial capital had long since regained its former bustle and prosperity.

The vast void was completely stable, with even the tiniest fissures having vanished without a trace.

The laws of heaven and earth had subtly changed. The reshaped space was more robust than before.

The concentration of spiritual energy had increased significantly. The entire imperial capital was shrouded in a dense, misty spiritual fog.

Elder Chen and the others manipulated the imperial capital's grand formation, directing the excess spiritual energy to the four directions.

Previously barren lands were revitalized. Spiritual herbs broke through the soil, and ancient trees towered toward the sky.

Birds and beasts migrated from distant lands, creating a vibrant scene full of life.

Within the capital, the streets were bustling with traffic. Merchant ships crowded the waterways, and caravans flowed in an endless stream.

Initially, some had worried that the Flame God Palace would return seeking revenge with their Emperor Weapon. But now, a month had passed, and all such doubts had dissipated.

Cultivators traveling across the Boundless Sea preferred to pass through the Great Xia Dynasty.

Here, no one dared to commit murder and robbery. Safety was guaranteed to the utmost.

Cultivators, alchemists, and artifact forgers from various domains came to settle here one after another.

The dynasty's fortune grew with each passing day. Its prosperous momentum was unstoppable.

In the early morning, the rising sun dispelled the night's chill.

"Boom—"

Array patterns within the imperial mausoleum suddenly glowed brightly. Mysterious and profound diagrams appeared in the air.

A surge of immense formation power raged forth, shaking the entire imperial palace.

But this force came and went quickly, subsiding in the blink of an eye.

The glow of the array patterns gradually dimmed, eventually disappearing entirely.

Qin Feng, who had been guarding and setting up the formation in the mausoleum all this time, finally revealed a satisfied smile.

"The ancient teleportation array is complete," he said softly to himself. "Summoning the Great Xia Dragon Sparrow will be effortless from now on."

Qin Jiu Nian, who had returned to the court some time ago, sensed the formation's fluctuations and hurried to the mausoleum.

He passed through layer after layer of restrictions, quickly walking up to Qin Feng.

"Elder Brother!" The usually dignified emperor rarely showed such excitement.

"Are all matters in the Eastern Cang Domain settled?" Qin Feng turned and asked with a light smile.

"I returned over two months ago," Qin Jiu Nian replied. "I saw you were focused on setting up the formation and did not dare to disturb you."

He sighed with deep emotion. "So many years have passed since we last parted."

Streaks of white marked his temples. Without the support of the dynasty's fortune, he would likely have aged considerably.

"I returned once," Qin Feng explained. "You were not in the court at that time, so I did not stay long."

"Did you go to the Boundless Sea, Elder Brother?" Qin Jiu Nian asked urgently. "Did you see Zi Ning?"

His voice could not conceal his loneliness.

The older generation of the royal family had passed away one after another. The previous era had truly ended.

His contemporaries, Qin Feng and his beloved daughter Qin Zi Ning, were far away in distant lands.

Every time he thought of them, he missed them dearly and worried for their safety.

"I saw her," Qin Feng said with a smile. "She is in secluded training at the Jade Capital Academy. She is safe and has even obtained a great opportunity."

"When she emerges next time, she might even be stronger than me."

"I don't care about her cultivation level," Qin Jiu Nian said, finally relieved. "I just want her to be safe."

Qin Feng nodded silently.

Though he had no children of his own, he understood this profound paternal love.

"You have governed the dynasty well these years," Qin Feng said, changing the subject. "The strength of its fortune exceeds my imagination."

Without such a prosperous national fortune, the Fusang Divine Tree would not have grown so rapidly.

"It was mostly due to favorable circumstances," Qin Jiu Nian said modestly.

"It is all thanks to you, Elder Brother, for unifying the Cang Lan Domain by force back then. Now, in this golden age, interactions between all sides are frequent. That is why the dynasty grows more prosperous each day."

Previously, the Cang Lan Domain was almost isolated from other major domains. The distances were simply too vast.

The nearest major domain was the Nine Mystic Domain, which didn't even have an Ancient Holy Land.

The golden age brought frequent opportunities and the emergence of prodigies. The connection between the continent and the sea grew increasingly close.

This was what created the prosperous scene of the Great Xia Dynasty.

"Regardless, this is the Great Xia Dynasty's opportunity," Qin Feng encouraged. "Maintain stability. Keep improving. One day, it can rival the ancient holy dynasties."

He had a faint feeling that the changes in the Cang Lan Domain's heavenly laws might be the very opportunity for the dynasty's rise.

…

P.S.: We are about to leave the Great Xia Dynasty. This transitional plot might not be very exciting, but it is all necessary setup. Thank you for your understanding, dear readers.
###Chapter 179

"Why should I? Why should I listen to you?"

Vivian Bennett stubbornly lifted her chin, her eyes filled with defiance.

Liam Sullivan suddenly leaned down and bit her lip hard.

It wasn't a kiss. It was punishment.

He was like an enraged beast, tearing at her softness.

Every ounce of his strength carried destructive intent.

Vivian winced in pain, yet she could clearly sense the fury raging within him.

But what was he so angry about?

Even if she really had something with Zachary Hudson, why should he be so enraged?

She was just a plaything he fancied on a whim.

When he grew tired of her, he could simply discard her.

Vivian hardened her heart and bit back with force.

The taste of blood instantly spread between their lips.

Liam released her, his eyes dark and terrifying.

"Mr. Sullivan, why bother?" Vivian lowered her lashes, a mocking curve forming at the corner of her mouth. "Getting angry over a woman like me isn't worth it."

"Yes, why bother?"

Liam stared at her, a trace of self-mockery flashing in his eyes.

Countless women longed to climb into his bed.

Why was he so fixated on her?

If he suspected her of being unfaithful, he could just discard her.

Why force an explanation? Why demand a guarantee?

It was meaningless.

"Mr. Sullivan, you're never short of women around you." Vivian's voice grew softer. "And I... have never been from your world."

She had held these words back for too long.

These past days felt like an unreal dream.

How could the big boss genuinely pursue her?

She couldn't afford to play these emotional games.

It was time to call it quits.

Now that he misunderstood, it was perfect.

This was the chance to make a clean break.

Liam's gaze swept over her coldly. "You're right."

He abruptly released her and took a step back.

He had returned to being that aloof, untouchable Mr. Sullivan.

As if they had never crossed paths.

"The three-month agreement is terminated." His tone was icy. "We're even from now on."

He turned and walked away without looking back.

He didn't spare her another glance.

Vivian stood frozen, leaning against the railing.

The sting on her lip reminded her this wasn't a dream.

The dream was over now.

Everyone was back in their rightful place.

It ended faster than she expected.

She thought he would at least grow bored after having her.

She never imagined he would lose interest without even touching her.

Was this a breakup?

No, she had been dumped by the big boss.

Maybe it was for the best.

She should have returned to her own track long ago.

Yet her chest felt inexplicably tight.

There was still some disappointment after all.

But for them, this was the best possible ending.
###Chapter 180

Silence filled the car.

Finn Carter focused on driving.

Aria Stewart gazed out the window.

An invisible wall seemed to stand between them.

Finn Carter took out his phone and sent a quick message.

He then closed his eyes to rest.

The images of those assassins lingered in his mind.

Letting them go was a risky move.

Could Moon Shadow follow this lead to find the mastermind?

Those thirteen were skilled, but their leader was inferior to Moon Shadow.

There should be no danger...

Aria Stewart quietly received Finn Carter's thoughts.

Who was Moon Shadow?

She didn't know.

But she understood Finn Carter's intention in releasing the assassins.

It was to uncover who wanted her dead.

He considered her in every way.

His meticulousness was astonishing.

The car entered the villa district.

It had just parked when a figure rushed over.

"Brother-in-law!"

Luna Stewart was like a joyful bird, throwing her arms around Finn Carter.

She pressed close to him.

Her softness made his heart flutter.

"Why are you here?" Finn Carter was surprised.

"I was afraid you wouldn't come back!" She pouted, her body squirming restlessly.

The friction and her faint fragrance improved his mood.

This girl truly cared about him.

Warmth spread through Finn Carter's chest.

He reached out and ruffled her hair.

"My life is tough. It's not that easy to die."

Luna Stewart's tears turned to laughter.

Her eyes shimmered with tenderness.

"You can leave now."

Aria Stewart's icy voice came from behind.

Seeing her sister's intimacy with Finn Carter left a bitter taste.

A sour emotion spread through her.

It felt like her most important possession was being stolen.

When Finn Carter affectionately ruffled Luna Stewart's hair, a sudden anger flared within her.

"I won't!" Luna Stewart clung tightly to Finn Carter. "What if you make him leave again!"

"None of your business."

Aria Stewart's gaze was cold. "Know your place."

"You!"

Finn Carter felt a headache coming.

"Enough." He interrupted them, looking at Aria Stewart. "It's late. Let her stay the night."

Seeing Finn Carter speak up for Luna Stewart made Aria Stewart's chest feel tighter.

"This is my home. It's not your decision!"

She regretted the words as soon as they left her mouth.

Finn Carter's expression shifted slightly before returning to normal.

He turned to Luna Stewart with a gentle smile.

"Come on. I'll take you home."

Luna Stewart's eyes had reddened with sad.

She had rushed over after learning the takeout was poisoned.

She hoped they would reconcile.

After waiting for so long, this was the treatment she received.

But Finn Carter's smile warmed her heart.

All her sad vanished instantly.

"Okay!" She nodded firmly, her smile radiant.

"Enough!"

Aria Stewart took a deep breath, suppressing her anger.

"Just one night."

Her heart ached with sourness.

A trace of panic crept in.

Her angry words seemed to push Finn Carter further away.

But she felt so wronged.

She was his fiancée.

Why was he so gentle with Luna Stewart yet always distant with her?

Aria Stewart turned.

Her long legs carried her up the stairs.

"President Lin."

Finn Carter suddenly called out to her.

The title made her steps falter.

"I may not be suited for the secretary position. Please transfer me back to the security department."
###Chapter 181

Vivian Bennett snorted disdainfully.
"How utterly boring!"

Zachary Hudson's face darkened instantly.

Most women would scream with excitement upon receiving such a large bouquet of blue roses from him.
Yet she showed no reaction at all.

"Free tonight? Have dinner with me?" Zachary asked lazily.

"Busy," Vivian stated firmly.

"Tomorrow night?"

"Also busy."

"The night after?"

"Busy all month."

"..."

Vivian hung up directly.

The elevator stopped at the PR department floor.

She stepped out, intending to find a trash can to discard the annoying blue roses.

Emily Davis's voice suddenly called from behind.
"Vivian!"

Emily hurried over, immediately noticing the flowers in Vivian's hand.

"Such beautiful blue roses! Who sent them?"

"Like them? They're yours." Vivian shoved the bouquet into Emily's arms.

Emily stood frozen, holding the flowers.

She quickly caught up to Vivian.

"Vivian, who sent these? Since when do you have such a generous admirer?"

"They were delivered to the wrong person."

"..."

That night, Liam Sullivan played mahjong with his childhood friends at the club.

Liam, who usually won steadily, lost completely tonight.

The other three players unexpectedly won big.

Ethan Roscente leaned close to Oliver Winston.
"Something's off with Liam tonight? Losing so easily to us? Doesn't seem like him?"

Oliver glanced covertly at Liam's cold, somber expression.

He also found it unusual.

He felt Liam was in a bad mood tonight and intentionally let them win.

That was the only reason the other three had a chance to beat him.

"Alexander, what do you think?" Oliver turned to Alexander Lowell on his other side.

Alexander, a straightforward man, noticed nothing different about Liam.

"Don't know."

Oliver scoffed softly; he had expected asking him would be pointless.

He continued discussing in a low voice with Ethan.

"Do you think Liam's been having work troubles lately?" Ethan guessed.

Oliver stroked his chin.
"Doesn't seem like it."

"Then what does it seem like?" Ethan asked casually.

While they were discussing, Liam had already ordered several drinks for himself and started drinking alone.

Seeing him like this, Oliver had a sudden insight.

"Heartbreak. Liam's condition clearly points to heartbreak."

Hearing this, neither Ethan nor Alexander responded.

In their minds, Liam was like a deity.

What woman could he possibly not have? How could he experience heartbreak?

Oliver couldn't figure it out either.

But based on his years of experience in romance, Liam's current state was unmistakably heartbreak.

"I'm leaving first!"
###Chapter 182

Liam Sullivan played several rounds of mahjong.

He drank several bottles of strong whiskey.

A deep sense of apathy settled over him.

He stood up to leave.

Oliver Winston noticed his drunken state and hurried after him. "Let me drive you."

"No need." Liam's voice was cold and detached.

He walked away without looking back.

The black limousine waited silently outside the club.

The driver saw his unsteady steps and rushed to support him.

Liam sank into the back seat and closed his eyes.

The engine hummed softly.

"Sir, where to?" the driver asked cautiously.

Liam pressed his fingers against his temples.

His thin lips parted. He uttered the name of a residential area.

Lakeside Manor.

The driver's heart skipped a beat.

Wasn't that Miss Vivian's address?

The boss still couldn't let her go.

When the car arrived, Liam was already asleep.

The driver didn't dare disturb him. He waited silently.

Late into the night, the smell of smoke woke him.

He turned to look.

Liam held a cigarette between his fingers. The glowing ember flickered in the darkness.

His sharp profile looked especially stern in the night.

His deep gaze remained fixed on a particular window.

The driver thought it was just a one-time occurrence.

But for an entire week after that.

Liam appeared outside the building every night.

He always smoked in silence.

His eyes never leaving that window.

The driver couldn't understand his thoughts.

He only knew to drive here punctually each evening.

The weekend arrived quietly.

Sophia Miller burst into Vivian Bennett's apartment.

She insisted on dragging Vivian out for shopping.

Vivian was exhausted. Her eyelids felt heavy.

She only wanted to stay home and catch up on sleep.

But Sophia wouldn't take no for an answer.

"A woman must treat herself well."

She pulled Vivian to a spa.

Then they headed straight to a high-end cosmetics counter.

Vivian was shocked by the prices.

Sophia swiped her card without hesitation.

"Money is meant to be spent. We can always earn more."

Next, they stormed into a luxury boutique.

"Try this one. And this one too."

Sophia shoved an armful of clothes into Vivian's arms.

Vivian gave in and entered the fitting room.

Each gorgeous dress enhanced her beauty.

The mirror reflected her increasingly radiant appearance.

Her mood gradually brightened.

But just as she put on the last gown.

The moment she opened the fitting room curtain.

Isabella Sullivan's figure appeared in the store.

She wore a custom-made outfit. Sunglasses hid her eyes.

Four bodyguards followed her closely.

The store manager recognized her and hurried over with staff.

The entire shop fell into complete silence.
###Chapter 183

Isabella Sullivan entered the store in her high heels.

The store manager immediately approached her, face covered in an ingratiating smile. "Miss Sullivan, what brings you here today? We just received new arrivals. Would you like to see them?"

Several sales associates behind her displayed similarly fawning expressions.

This Sullivan heiress was a VIP customer.

A single casual purchase from her could fulfill their monthly sales targets.

Isabella didn't even glance at the staff.

She had locked onto Vivian Bennett's location the moment she entered.

Her heels clicked sharply against the floor as she strode directly toward Vivian.

Vivian hadn't expected to encounter her here.

Her mood instantly sank.

This woman was a Sullivan heiress and the company's vice president.

From any perspective, offending her would be unwise.

But since they had crossed paths, she couldn't just pretend not to see her.

Vivian forced a faint smile.

Isabella accurately called out her name. "Vivian Bennett, what a coincidence?"

She actually remembered her.

Vivian didn't consider this a good thing.

Isabella had noticed her solely because of Liam Sullivan.

But she and Liam had already broken up.

There would be no further connection between them.

Isabella shouldn't have any reason to trouble her now, right?

Vivian responded flatly, "Yes, quite a coincidence."

Isabella's critical gaze swept over her.

"Nice outfit."

Her tone dripped with obvious sarcasm.

It seemed to question whether Vivian could even afford the clothes in this store.

Vivian suppressed her displeasure.

Starting a conflict with Isabella here would only disadvantage her.

Since she had already broken up with Liam, there was no need to provoke his sister.

She pretended not to catch Isabella's mockery.

Isabella mistook her restraint for fear and pressed further.

She turned to the sales associate. "How many pieces has this young lady tried on?"

The associate hurriedly replied, "Six sets, plus the one she's wearing makes seven. Each one suits her perfectly."

Isabella raised her voice. "Excellent. I'll take everything she tried on."

Sophia Miller immediately stepped forward. "What do you mean? We saw them first. There should be some order to this."

Vivian quickly pulled her best friend back.

Sophia looked at her, confused.

Vivian whispered a reminder. "She's the company's vice president."

Sophia froze for a moment.

So this was Vivian's superior.

But even a superior shouldn't bully people like this.
###Chapter 184

Sophia Miller seethed with silent fury.

Seeing Vivian Bennett unwilling to pursue the matter, she had no choice but to suppress her anger.

Vivian’s face was grim as she walked straight to the dressing room.

She quickly took off the designer dress and changed back into her own clothes.

Right now, she just wanted to leave as soon as possible. She couldn’t bear to look at Isabella Sullivan a moment longer.

“Why aren’t you wearing it? It seemed to fit perfectly a moment ago. Could it be you can’t afford it?” Isabella raised an eyebrow, stepping closer with a mocking tone.

Sophia’s rage boiled over. She clenched her fists, ready to charge forward.

Vivian stopped her once again.

“How would I dare to compete with the young Miss Sullivan? Since you’ve taken a liking to it, I naturally must yield. Besides—” Vivian gave a cold laugh, “I never wear the same clothes as anyone else.”

Her tone was calm, yet it carried an undeniable pride.

The words stung Isabella, igniting her anger.

Her brother wasn’t paying any attention to her now, so why was this woman still putting on airs?

Anyway, no matter how she bullied her now, her brother wouldn’t blame her.

“Oh? I thought vixens loved snatching other people’s things?” Isabella let out a scornful laugh.

Vivian took a deep breath.

Arguing with such a person was pointless.

“Miss Sullivan, please mind your language,” Vivian said, trying her best to remain calm.

A vixen?

How dare Isabella insult her so publicly?

“Hmph! Why should someone like you deserve any respect? Do you really think you can climb onto a high branch and become a phoenix? My brother is just momentarily amused by you. Don’t overestimate your importance!”

Isabella narrowed her eyes, her tone venomous.

In the end, it was still about Liam Sullivan.

“You’ve misunderstood, Miss Sullivan. Mr. Sullivan is merely my superior. Ours is a purely professional relationship. What you think is your own business. Now, if you’ll excuse me.”

Vivian finished coolly and turned to leave.

But Isabella suddenly grabbed her arm.

Sharp nails dug in, leaving two bloody scratches.

Vivian winced in pain.

Sophia could no longer hold back.

How dare this wretched woman lay a hand on her friend right in front of her?

Just as Sophia was about to step forward, Isabella raised her hand and swung it directly towards Vivian’s face.

The slap was about to land—

Smack!

Isabella’s wrist was caught in a fierce grip and violently shoved away.

She staggered back several steps, nearly falling.

“Miss Sullivan, what has my girlfriend done to offend you?”

A deep, magnetic voice rang out, filled with intimidating anger.

Vivian was dizzy from the earlier struggle, completely unprepared for Isabella’s brazen physical attack.

It wasn’t until she fell into a warm, solid embrace that she regained her senses.

“Zachary Hudson?” Isabella’s eyes widened in shock.

Sophia and Vivian were also stunned.

Zachary Hudson?

What was he doing here?

“Vivian, are you alright?” Zachary held her tightly, planting a light kiss on her forehead.
###Chapter 185

Vivian Bennett froze in place, caught off guard as Zachary Hudson wrapped an arm around her waist.

Her eyes widened in disbelief.

Why was Zachary here?

And why was he helping her?

It would have made more sense if he had teamed up with Isabella Sullivan to torment her.

This playboy who always enjoyed teasing her was unexpectedly coming to her rescue.

It felt utterly surreal to Vivian.

"How... how could she possibly be your girlfriend?" Isabella's voice trembled, her face pale.

Although she had orchestrated those online rumors herself.

She had fabricated them entirely to drive a wedge between Vivian and her brother.

She never imagined that Vivian and Zachary actually had something going on.

This woman not only seduced her brother but also dared to mess with Zachary?

"Got a problem with that?" Zachary lazily lifted his gaze, his peach-blossom eyes icy cold.

Isabella shivered under his stare and immediately fell silent.

Of course she had a problem.

But Zachary wasn't her brother Liam Sullivan.

She didn't dare act out in front of him.

"Does it hurt?"

Zachary looked down, his fingertips gently tracing the bloody scratches Isabella had left on Vivian's arm.

His touch was unusually tender.

"It's fine." Vivian steadied herself and shook her head.

But internally, she was reeling.

Had Zachary lost his mind today?

He seemed like a completely different person.

Playing the hero to rescue her?

"What would you like for dinner? I've already reserved a table at your favorite restaurant. Shall we go now?"

Zachary smirked, his tone intimate as if they were long-time lovers.

Without waiting for her answer, he wrapped his arm around her shoulder and guided her toward the exit.

As they passed Isabella, he paused.

"Please tell your brother to stop harassing my woman."

His words struck like thunder, leaving Isabella speechless.

His woman?

Was Vivian really involved with Zachary?

Sophia Miller was equally bewildered.

Forgetting her confrontation with Isabella, she hurried after them.

Just outside the boutique, Vivian pulled away from Zachary's embrace.

She knew she owed him for getting her out of that situation.

Otherwise, Isabella would never have let her off easily.

But his final words—"my woman"—didn't sound like just a ploy to help her.

It almost felt like he meant it.

"Thank you," Vivian said, stepping back with distant politeness.

"Is that all?" Zachary raised an eyebrow, a playful smile curling his lips.

"What else?" she countered warily.

She studied him carefully. His expression was inscrutable, his entire demeanor radiating a dangerous charm she couldn't decipher.

"At least have dinner with me?" A dark glint flashed in Zachary's peach-blossom eyes as he smiled enticingly.
###Chapter 186

Vivian Bennett froze.

Zachary Hudson's invitation came without warning.

"Tonight," he stated firmly, leaving no room for refusal.

His arm wrapped around her, pulling her into the car.

"My friend is still..." Vivian turned back urgently.

Sophia Miller stood on the street corner, giving her a sly wink.

She waved, signaling Vivian to go without worry.

Vivian pressed her lips together in resignation.

Her best friend had clearly misunderstood.

Zachary stepped on the gas, the sports car heading toward the most expensive French restaurant in the city.

A waiter bowed deeply as he led the way, his attitude respectful to the point of deference.

Even the folded corner of the napkin bore his exclusive mark.

"Has Liam Sullivan let you go?" Zachary suddenly asked while cutting his steak.

The silver fork gleamed coldly under the light.

Vivian's fingers trembled. "How did you know?"

"Isabella Sullivan dared to move against you precisely because..." He chuckled lightly. "You no longer have anyone backing you."

A chill ran down her spine.

Zachary swirled his red wine, his eyes darkening. "She often uses such methods."

"You seem to know her well?"

"Well enough." A shadow passed through his gaze. "After all, she destroyed someone I cared about."

Dinner ended in silence.

The night was thick as ink when the sports car stopped outside her apartment building.

"Thank you," Vivian reached to push the car door open.

Her wrist was suddenly grasped—right where Isabella had left that red cut.

His fingertips gently brushed over the wound. "Does it hurt?"

"I'm used to it by now," she shook her head faintly.

She had endured far worse pain than this.

Zachary suddenly laughed softly, a bewitching curve lifting the corner of his mouth.
###Chapter 187

Zachary Hudson abruptly pulled her into his embrace.

His deep, captivating eyes locked onto hers.

His gaze was so intense it felt like it could devour her whole.

"Be my woman."

His tone was commanding and left no room for argument.

Vivian Bennett's heart skipped a beat.

She couldn't believe he would suddenly make such a demand.

"No." She refused point-blank.

She would never become just another one of his playthings.

Zachary's expression darkened instantly.

"You don't have the right to refuse," he ordered coldly.

"I'm not like those other women." Vivian met his gaze directly.

Zachary raised an eyebrow, a flicker of amusement in his eyes.

"Wanting more?" He sneered, the corner of his mouth curling.

Vivian was utterly speechless.

She was clearly rejecting him.

Yet he thought she was negotiating.

"No amount of money will make me agree." She laid her cards on the table.

"Not money? Then what do you want?" Zachary's eyes glinted as he pressed further.

…

She didn't want anything at all.

She simply wanted no involvement with him.

Was he not understanding her?

Before she could refuse again, Zachary spoke.

"Three days. Name your terms."

He suddenly grasped her arm and lightly kissed the faint scratch mark.

Vivian shuddered violently and yanked her hand back.

She frantically pushed the car door open and rushed into the residential complex without looking back.

She ran all the way to her building but came to an abrupt halt.

Liam Sullivan's tall, imposing figure stood silently beside a black Bentley.

Her heart missed a beat.

Why was he here?

After not seeing him for days, he seemed to have lost some weight.

The lines of his face appeared even more severe and sharp.

But the powerful, overwhelming aura around him remained unchanged.

The pure black custom-made suit accentuated his upright posture.

He exuded an air of noble coldness.

Vivian hadn't expected to see him here.

They had broken up.

Why was he here?

Could he not be here for her?

She forced herself to stay calm and pretended not to see him.

She turned to go around the other way.

Liam frowned at her cold, retreating back.

Seeing her ignore him and walk away, his eyes darkened.

He strode after her.

Vivian hadn't taken more than a few steps when her wrist was suddenly grabbed from behind.

A man's deep voice sounded. "Where are you going?"

Vivian froze.

He was indeed here for her.

She took a deep breath, trying to calm her turbulent emotions.

She turned around, her tone distant and calm. "Mr. Sullivan, is there something you need?"

Liam's face tightened instantly at her unfamiliar address.

The air around him turned icy.

"Is he the reason you rejected me?"

His voice was bitingly cold.

Vivian stared blankly at his stern profile.

She caught a fleeting glimpse of jealousy in his eyes.
###Chapter 188

His gaze pinned her like a blade.

Vivian Bennett froze for several seconds before processing his words.

She gathered her courage and met Liam Sullivan's intense stare.

"Who I date is my freedom," she stated clearly, each word deliberate. "I don't need to report it to you."

She held his gaze without flinching.

Since she had decided to draw a clear line, she would see it through.

"Vivian!" Liam's voice grated through clenched teeth.

He had never felt so powerless toward another person.

Rage burned in his eyes.

Vivian was shaken by his sudden fury.

Steadying herself, she stubbornly insisted, "If there's nothing else, I should go."

She moved to push his hand away.

Liam felt his chest might explode.

He roughly dragged her toward the parking lot.

Vivian, frightened by his menacing aura, began to struggle.

"Let me go!"

Liam ignored her protests, shoving her into the passenger seat.

Vivian desperately pushed against the door, but it was useless.

Only when he slid into the driver's seat with a dark expression did she tremble and ask, "What do you want?"

Liam stared at her lips, then suddenly crushed his mouth against hers in a harsh bite.

Vivian gasped in pain, her eyes instantly welling with tears.

But Liam did not continue the kiss.

He gripped her chin, forcing her to look up.

Seeing the redness at the corners of her eyes, he paused.

His thumb gently brushed her injured lip. His voice softened. "Does it hurt?"

Vivian slapped his hand away, glaring. "Is this fun for you?"

Liam watched her furious expression, then suddenly smirked. "You're fun."

Vivian stared blankly, then scoffed. "Wasn't the agreement terminated?"

Since it was over, why bother disturb?

Liam's eyes turned icy.

He stared at those lips that always spoke cutting words.

"So I never meant anything to you?" His voice was low.

Vivian's lips curved in self-mockery. "What could possibly make someone like you remember me?"

Liam watched her in silence.

His gaze deepened.

Vivian clenched her fingers. "Don't come looking for me anymore."

Her voice was soft. "We're not suited."

A sharp pain stabbed through Liam's chest.

The car fell into dead silence.

Vivian took the black card from her bag.

She held it out to him. "Take this back."

Liam pressed his lips together tightly, not accepting it.

She placed the card on the seat and reached for the central lock.

Liam suddenly grabbed her wrist, pushing her back against the seat.

His tall frame loomed over her.
###Chapter 189

A fierce kiss assaulted her.

Vivian Bennett had no time to react.

Her eyes widened as her lips were brutally sealed.

Liam Sullivan’s kiss carried a punishing edge, forcefully prying her mouth open.

Vivian struggled desperately, but her hands were firmly pinned.

She was forced to tilt her head back, enduring his storm-like assault.

Breathing grew increasingly difficult. She was nearly suffocating.

His arms clamped around her like iron vices, leaving no chance for escape.

He was like a starved beast, savagely plundering her very breath.

The temperature inside the car skyrocketed.

Their bodies pressed tightly together, leaving no space between them.

Liam’s hand slipped inside her blouse, caressing her smooth skin.

Vivian stiffened completely.

A wave of humiliation instantly flooded her heart.

Tears silently traced down her cheeks, dripping onto Liam’s face.

The cold sensation jolted him awake.

He released her, panting heavily, his eyes dark with suppressed desire.

“Why are you crying?” His voice was hoarse, edged with impatience.

Vivian glimpsed her disheveled collar, overwhelmed by shame and anger.

She shoved him away forcefully, her voice trembling. “You bastard! How could you do this to me?”

She immediately slapped him across the face.

The sharp crack of the slap echoed starkly inside the car.

Liam’s expression darkened instantly.

No woman had ever dared to hit him before.

He admitted he had lost control, almost taking her right there in the car.

But her repeated rejections had utterly enraged him.

With no outlet for his fury, he could only slam his fist violently into the car seat.

Vivian hadn’t expected herself to strike him either.

But she felt no regret.

He had started it by bullying her.

Seeing Liam’s ominous expression, fear prickled her heart.

Terrified he might do something even more reckless, she hurriedly pushed at the car door.

This time, Liam did not stop her.

Vivian fled into the building as if escaping a predator.

Liam watched her panicked retreat, his gaze inscrutable.

That night, Vivian tossed and turned, unable to sleep.

She had thought everything was settled after the agreement was terminated.

She never expected Liam to suddenly appear and force a kiss on her.

What did he actually want?

…

She barely slept a wink all night, only managing to doze off as dawn broke.

The doorbell suddenly rang.

Vivian answered the door with her hair a mess.

Sophia Miller stood outside, looking surprised.

“Still not awake?”

“I hardly slept last night.” Vivian rubbed her temples.

A knowing smile played on Sophia’s lips. “Out too late with Zachary?”

Vivian blushed instantly.

“Don’t talk nonsense. Zachary Hudson and I aren’t like that.”

“Then why did he rescue you? And ask you out alone?” Sophia pressed.
###Chapter 190

"It wasn't a date. He just bought me dinner," Vivian Bennett explained instinctively.

Why had Zachary Hudson saved her?

She still couldn't figure it out.

It would make more sense if he wanted to torment her.
Saving her? It felt like a dream.

"That counts as a date. Zachary never buys anyone dinner," Sophia Miller teased with a smile. "And you two had that rumor..."

"It really isn't what you think," Vivian said helplessly.

Sophia stared at her for a moment.
She suddenly raised an eyebrow. "I get it. You have Liam Sullivan now. Naturally, you wouldn't be interested in him."

Vivian's mouth twitched. "What does Liam have to do with this?"

Sophia looked at her with a knowing expression. "Aren't you rejecting Zachary because of Liam?"

Vivian sighed. "Liam and I broke up a while ago."

"What?" Sophia exclaimed in shock.

Vivian's gaze grew distant. "He saw the rumors about me and Zachary. He thought I was two-timing him. He terminated our agreement himself. It's over between us."

Sophia was stunned again.
She hadn't realized her best friend had gone through such a major change.

"Vivian, are you okay?" Sophia's eyes were full of concern.

Vivian's red lips curved slightly. "Why wouldn't I be? I never felt like we truly began in the first place."

Liam had wanted her to be his contractual girlfriend for three months. It had always felt like a burden.
Now that he had ended the agreement, the pressure was gone.
She actually felt relieved.

"Didn't you ever think about holding onto a man like Liam?" Sophia sounded frustrated, as if Vivian was wasting an opportunity.

Vivian sighed. "Let it go. We were never from the same world."
Forcing a relationship might bring temporary happiness, but it would end in separation anyway.
It was better to face reality early.
A quick pain was better than a long one.

The next day, Vivian had plans to visit her cousin, Grace Harper, and keep her company.
Grace's husband, Jason Harper, had been traveling frequently for work.
His last business trip lasted over two weeks. He had just returned, only to leave again.
Grace was often left alone in an empty house.

Vivian took a taxi to her cousin's house and was about to knock.
A dark shadow suddenly lunged at her from behind.
Before she could react, she was struck from behind and knocked unconscious.

Vivian opened her eyes.
She found herself lying in a luxurious hotel suite.
A heavy weight pressed down on her, making it hard to breathe.
In her blurred vision, a tall figure was pinning her down.

Vivian shuddered and screamed.
"Ah... Who is it?"
She was jolted fully awake.
She pushed against the man on top of her with all her strength.

She finally got a clear look at his face.
"Brandon White?"
Vivian cried out in disbelief.
How could it be him?
