She turned immediately to leave.

Liam quickly caught up and grasped her wrist.

"Where do you think you're going?"

His voice held a trace of anger.

Vivian lowered her eyes and spoke distantly.

"Mr. Sullivan, what a coincidence."

His fingers tightened slightly around her wrist.

"So now it's 'Mr. Sullivan'?"

She remembered the times she had intimately called him by his name.

Those memories now felt deeply ironic.

She tried hard to break free from his grip.

But he only held on tighter.

"Speak."

His low voice was icy.

Vivian looked up at his thin, cold lips.

She suddenly recalled something she had read.

Men with thin lips are the most heartless.

Liam's expression grew colder at her silence.

"Have you forgotten everything I said before?"

Vivian's eyelashes fluttered.

What was left to say between them?

"The agreement is terminated. We're done."

She had handled it perfectly.

"I really haven't disturbed you recently."

Except for that time he forcibly took her in his car.

Anger ignited in Liam's eyes.

She made it sound so simple.

"Why was your phone unreachable?"

Vivian was stunned.

He had actually tried to contact her?

Since that day.

She had dragged his number into her block list.

Not bothering each other after a breakup.

Wasn't that the most basic courtesy?
###Chapter 203

Vivian Bennett took a deep breath, trying to calm her emotions.

She forced herself to appear composed as she looked at Liam Sullivan. "Mr. Sullivan, we were never from the same world. Since the agreement has been terminated, please don't bother me anymore."

Liam's gaze turned icy, anger swirling in his eyes.

His jaw tightened, his features sharp and severe.

"Don't bother you?" He let out a cold laugh. "Who was it that approached whom first?"

He would always remember that night.

She was the one who got into his car.

If not for that night, he wouldn't have been unable to forget her.

Vivian froze.

She shook her head in confusion. "When did I ever approach you? I don't remember..."

Could there be some misunderstanding?

She recalled the first time she saw him at the branch company.

Did he think she had ulterior motives just because she was distracted and didn't hear his question clearly?

"Just because you don't remember doesn't mean it didn't happen." Liam's voice was cold and heavy. "Just like us now—we can never go back to the beginning."

"Liam, just let me go." Vivian's voice held a pleading tone. "I just want to be an ordinary employee and live an ordinary life."

She never intended to attach herself to a man like him.

She only wanted simple,ordinary days.

Why did he always have to force his way into her world?

Liam looked at her pale face and finally softened.

He pulled her into his arms. "Do you really not want anything to do with me?"

Vivian panic leaned against his chest, nodding repeatedly.

Liam's eyes darkened, and he suddenly swept her up into his arms.

"Ah!" Vivian cried out in surprise, struggling fiercely. "Let me go! Where are you taking me?"

"Keep shouting if you're not afraid of being seen." His voice was gentle but carried a threat.

Vivian immediately fell silent.

If colleagues saw her entangled with the boss, who knew what rumors would spread.

She couldn't draw attention to herself.

But she didn't want to be carried like this either.

He was the one who said the agreement was void.

"Liam, you bastard!" She was so angry she couldn't think straight. "You only know how to use force with me!"

Liam watched her look of grievance and anger, the corner of his mouth lifting slightly. "If you don't want to be treated like this, then behave."

Vivian's heart sank.

Beyond the Peach Grove was a crowd of people.

If colleagues saw them, how could she continue working at Sullivan Group?

No one would dare to gossip about him as the CEO, but what about her?
###Chapter 204

Vivian Bennett pleaded softly, "Mr. Sullivan, please put me down. I promise I won't..."

Before she could finish, Ryan Johnson appeared at the end of the hallway.

His hurried steps faltered abruptly when he saw Vivian in Liam Sullivan's arms.

A flicker of discomfort crossed Ryan's face before he quickly averted his gaze. "The car is ready, Mr. Sullivan."

Liam gave a slight nod and continued walking forward with Vivian in his arms.

Ryan hurried ahead to open the passenger door.

Liam gently placed Vivian inside before circling around to the driver's seat and starting the engine.

The car slowly pulled away from the company.

Vivian's cheeks burned with embarrassment. She wished she could disappear into the ground.

Ryan had witnessed everything.

No amount of explaining could clear this up now.

She glared angrily at the man beside her. "Liam Sullivan, must you always humiliate me like this?"

Liam kept his eyes on the road, a slight smile playing on his lips. "Ryan knows his place."

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

He had seen right through her concerns.

Even if Ryan remained tight-lipped, she didn't want to be entangled with Liam any longer.

What would people think of her getting into the CEO's car like this?

Vivian began pounding on the window. "Stop the car! Let me out!"

The doors were already locked.

No matter how much she struggled, Liam showed no intention of slowing down.

Soon, the car entered a quiet villa district.

Liam got out smoothly and pulled her from the passenger seat.

"I'm not going in!" Vivian clung desperately to the car door.

Liam's patience finally wore thin.

He threw her over his shoulder and strode into the villa.

Vivian hung upside down, her stomach churning violently.

Liam entered the living room, closed the door behind him, and tossed her onto the sofa.

Vivian scrambled fearfully into the corner of the sofa.

What did he plan to do with her in this place?

She lashed out like a frightened wildcat, scratching and clawing at him. "Liam Sullivan, you bastard! How dare you bully me like this!"

A glint of amusement flashed in Liam's eyes as he watched her furious display.

He easily restrained her flailing hands, his voice low. "No one else dares to act so wild with me except you."

Tears of frustration welled in Vivian's eyes. "I wouldn't be like this if you weren't forcing me!"

"Oh?" He raised an eyebrow. "Then I'd like to hear how exactly I'm forcing you?"

Vivian was about to list his offenses when he spoke again, his tone deliberate. "Was it also me who forced you to block my number?"
###Chapter 205

Liam Sullivan's expression darkened as he recalled the anxious helplessness of repeatedly calling her number only to find it permanently unreachable.

Vivian Bennett's mind flashed back to the moment he had coldly declared, "The three-month agreement is terminated. We're done."

She glared at him indignantly. "You said it yourself. The agreement is void. Why would I keep your number?"

A playful smile touched Liam's lips as he studied her stubborn face. "Are you saying that if the agreement were still valid, you would have saved my number?"

Vivian was caught off guard by his sudden question, momentarily speechless.

Liam leaned down and brushed a light kiss against the corner of her mouth. "So you admit there was something between us before?"

The unexpected kiss made her cheeks flush crimson with shock. "I admitted no such thing!" she refuted in a fluster.

He pulled her slender waist against him, drawing her into his embrace. "Still denying it? You're like a prickly little hedgehog, stinging me at the slightest touch. Everything I said that day was out of anger. Who told you to go on that business trip with Zachary Hudson? And you not only took it seriously, but you had the nerve to block me. Tell me, how should we settle this score?"

He had already uncovered the truth.

The rumors about her and Zachary Hudson were all orchestrated by Hannah Green behind the scenes.

Even the business trip had been arranged by Hannah.

Merely transferring Hannah away from headquarters had been too lenient.

She dared to target his woman repeatedly.

She made Vivian suffer through these baseless scandals.

"Anger?" Vivian's eyelashes trembled slightly as she looked at him in disbelief.

He called it anger?

Did that mean he was going back on his decision to terminate their three-month agreement?

Before Vivian could process this, Liam's handsome face descended upon hers.

Her mind went completely blank, her heartbeat spiraling out of control.

His warm lips covered hers in a domineering, forceful kiss.

Vivian's eyes widened, her hands instinctively pushing against him.

But he only deepened the kiss.

Liam ravaged her mouth relentlessly, making her retreat step by step until her defenses completely crumbled.

The kiss felt like it lasted an entire century.

It wasn't until Vivian whimpered breathlessly from lack of air that Liam reluctantly released her.

He looked down at her flushed face and rapid breathing with a low chuckle. "You still haven't learned how to breathe properly. We need more practice."

Vivian glared at him in embarrassed fury. "I know how! I just don't want to kiss you!"

Those words ignited Liam's anger.

His eyes turned icy cold, as if frozen over.

The unfamiliar environment suddenly felt tense and suffocating.

Liam pressed his thin lips together tightly, his intense gaze locked on her.

The burning intensity of his stare made Vivian's heart flutter nervously.

She panic raised her hands to cover her eyes. "Liam Sullivan, stop looking at me like that!"

His vision suddenly blocked, her soft yet domineering voice ringing in his ears. An involuntary, indulgent smile curved Liam's lips.
###Chapter 206

Liam Sullivan’s warm palm suddenly covered Vivian Bennett’s hand.

He enveloped her slender fingers completely in his grasp.

Vivian’s cheeks instantly flushed pink.

Seeing her shy expression, Liam’s lips curved into a pleased smile.

He deliberately leaned close to her ear and whispered, “I’m afraid if I keep looking at others that way, a certain little jealous one will throw another tantrum.”

Vivian immediately retorted, “Who’s jealous?”

“Not jealous?” Liam narrowed his eyes. “Then why did you block my number? Why have you been ignoring me for days?”

The conversation circled back to its starting point.

Vivian pressed her lips together and stayed silent.

The evidence was right there. She had no defense.

Liam gently stroked her soft hair, as if soothing a bristling cat.

His voice was low. “I admit I acted impulsively.”

“I shouldn’t have said the agreement was terminated.”

“But seeing those rumors about you and someone else… it hurt me here.”

He pressed her hand against his chest.

Vivian could feel his strong, steady heartbeat.

“I’m sorry,” Liam said, gazing into her eyes. “Let me spend a lifetime making it up to you. Okay?”

His words were like a stone dropped into a still lake.

Ripples spread through Vivian’s heart.

A lifetime?

What lifetime could they possibly have together?

Her eyes filled with doubt.

Liam sighed softly and kissed her forehead.

“Don’t worry,” he said with certainty. “Leave everything to me.”

“You just need to trust me.”

His words left her flustered and unsettled.

Staying with him?

The mere thought made her heart race.

But…

The memory of that night, when Brandon White set her up and she slept with Zachary Hudson, suddenly surfaced.

She wasn’t good enough for him anymore.

How could Liam ever accept her like this?

A sour ache rose in her nose.

She shook her head frantically. “Liam, it’s impossible for us.”

“The whole company knows I’m just your employee.”

Liam frowned. “Nothing is impossible.”

“Or…” He moved closer. “Are you really content with just being an employee?”

Vivian was speechless.

With anyone else, she could have admitted it without hesitation.

But in front of Liam, she couldn’t lie to herself.

Some feelings had already quietly changed.

Liam didn’t push her.

He could see her struggling.

Vivian rubbed her temples wearily.

Too much had happened lately. She was reaching her limit.

Coupled with a sleepless night, all she wanted now was to rest.

She had just stood up when Liam grabbed her wrist.

“Where are you going?” His voice was slightly stern.

“My head hurts. I want to go back and rest.”

Liam pulled her firmly back into his arms.

This time, he wouldn’t let her run away.
###Chapter 207

Liam Sullivan’s arm tightened around her waist, pulling her firmly against his chest.

His warm breath brushed her ear. His voice was low and hypnotic. "Sleep here. It's not like we haven't shared a bed before."

Vivian Bennett’s body went rigid. Two bright spots of color bloomed on her cheeks.

She shook her head in a panic. Her lips trembled slightly. "I... I should go back to my own room."

Liam gazed at her flushed face. He suddenly scooped her up into his arms. He carried her swiftly up the stairs.

"Let me go! Put me down!" Vivian squirmed restlessly in his embrace.

Instead of releasing her, he carried her straight into the master bedroom on the second floor.

He pressed her down into the soft bed. He wrapped his arms around her tense body from behind. His tone was both domineering and tender. "You're mine now. Why are you still so shy about sharing a bed?"

His blunt words made her ears burn crimson. She flipped over to glare at him. "Who says I'm yours? You're so shameless!"

Liam knew she still hadn't recognized him as the man from the limousine that night.

He pressed his forehead against hers. His scorching breath enveloped her completely. "Do you hate me? Should I make you mine right now?"

It would be a perfect way to help her recall that night.

Some things could be denied while they remained vague. Once spoken aloud, only embarrassment and palpitations would remain.

Vivian was thin-skinned. She couldn't handle his teasing. She simply turned her back to him, ignoring him.

She never imagined she would have such an entanglement with her big boss, Liam Sullivan.

They were as different as clouds and mud. Yet now they were sharing a bed.

All sorts of indescribable emotions quietly spread through her heart like vines.

Vivian lay still, but her mind was in turmoil.

She didn't turn to look until she heard his even, steady breathing behind her. She studied his sleeping face.

Confusion and hesitation intertwined. She didn't know how much time passed before she finally drifted into a fitful sleep.

Liam watched her closed eyes. Her long lashes rested like butterfly wings against her jade-like skin. She looked like an exquisitely crafted porcelain doll.

He couldn't resist lowering his head to gently kiss her lips. He pulled back carefully, afraid of disturbing her sweet dreams.

Vivian slept deeply. She didn't wake until two in the afternoon.

She frowned slightly at the completely unfamiliar surroundings.

Where was this?

She should have been in the guest room at the Aspen Hot Springs hotel. She should have been with her colleague, Emily Davis.

How did she end up in this luxurious bedroom?

Vivian blinked. Her muddled thoughts suddenly cleared.

She remembered being brought to the private villa by her big boss, Liam Sullivan.

He said his previous announcement about terminating their agreement and cutting ties was all said in anger.

He wanted her to compensate him for a lifetime?

After that, he carried her upstairs. They slept in each other's arms.
###Chapter 208

Vivian Bennett opened her eyes.

The space beside her was empty.

She was still wearing yesterday's clothes.

The room was silent.

Where was Liam Sullivan?

She distinctly remembered him sleeping next to her last night.

Vivian hurried out of bed to wash up.

Her reflection in the mirror looked pale.

She quickly walked downstairs.

The villa was empty.

Sunlight streamed in through the floor-to-ceiling windows.

She approached the main entrance.

The glass door was firmly shut.

It required a code to open.

It didn't budge.

She was trapped here.

He was dressed in casual wear.

He looked refreshed.

Vivian stopped resisting.

She was indeed hungry.

She picked up her fork and knife and began eating.

Her manner was somewhat hurried.

His gaze remained fixed on her.

Vivian put down her utensils after finishing.

Liam spoke suddenly.

"There's an event at the resort tonight. Want to go?"

"I don't want to go with you!"

Liam's eyes darkened.

"What are you afraid of? I won't hurt you."

"Liam, between us—"

Before she could finish.

It held a punishing edge.

The taste of blood filled her mouth.

"Stop saying things you shouldn't."

Her eyes reddened.

This man was always so domineering.
###Chapter 209

She had already made herself perfectly clear.

She wanted no further entanglement with him.

Why couldn't he just let her go?

Liam Sullivan gazed at her small face, filled with grievance and resentment.

The knot in his chest gradually loosened.

Vivian Bennett might dare to throw a tantrum at him when her temper flared.

That overwhelming pressure made her hesitate to act out.

"Let's go. I'll take you back." Liam stood up and grabbed his car keys.

Vivian stiffened her lips. The movement tugged at her wound, sending a sharp pain through her.

His gaze softened unconsciously.

Vivian got in without ceremony, letting him close the door.

Her clear eyes misted over.

She was annoyed and wanted to swat his hand away.

Vivian glared at him angrily. "Why don't you try getting bitten and see for yourself?"

Liam suddenly leaned his face close to hers.

His voice was deep and magnetic. "Want to bite me and find out?"

Vivian's mind buzzed, nearly dizzy.

She did want to pounce and bite him hard, to let him taste the pain.

But seeing the teasing look in his eyes, she instantly came to her senses.

She wouldn't fall for that.

Liam watched her proud, defiant little expression.

He said sternly, "I gave you a chance and you didn't take it. Fine. Next time you say such irresponsible things, see how I deal with you."

Vivian's heart clenched.

When had she ever been irresponsible?

She just didn't want to be with him. She didn't want to climb the social ladder by attaching herself to a wealthy family. Was that wrong?

Isabella Sullivan's warning still rang in her ears.

She just wanted to live her own quiet life in peace.

She really didn't want to get entangled with a man as lofty as Liam Sullivan.

Seeing his serious expression, she dared not voice her anger, afraid he would use harsh methods against her again.

The car sped along the road.

Silence filled the interior.

Neither of them spoke.

She didn't want her colleagues to see her getting out of the big boss's luxury car.

She stole a glance at the man's tightly pressed lips in the rearview mirror.

But her wrist was caught by Liam.

Liam pinned her wrist with one hand.

He lowered his head and kissed her lips.

The cramped car cabin suddenly felt even more crowded.

###Chapter 210

All taken in Liam Sullivan's bed.

Was she supposed to tell Emily that person was Liam Sullivan?
###Chapter 211

Liam Sullivan and his non-blood-related sister already had a love child.

Vivian couldn't muster any enthusiasm.

Ever since Emily said the big boss, Liam Sullivan, only liked Isabella Sullivan.

She had felt completely down.

The resort was decorated with lanterns and streamers, filled with a festive atmosphere everywhere.

The trees along the road were adorned with colorful lights, dazzling and brilliant.

Vivian walked slowly along this road alone, feeling both bored and irritable.

Emily's words kept echoing in her mind.

"Many women at the company have a crush on the boss, but the boss only has eyes for the Vice President."
###Chapter 212

Vivian Bennett’s mind was in turmoil.

Suddenly, her wrist was seized.

She nearly cried out in alarm.

The moonlight outlined his tall, straight figure.

Flickering shadows fell across his face.

“I’m not letting go.”

Gave her an inexplicable sense of peace.

The water was like a mirror, reflecting the moonlight.

###Chapter 213

She dipped her fingertips into the lake.

Warm water enveloped her fingers.

Her eyes widened in surprise.

She couldn't say she went to the hot springs with the big boss.

###Chapter 216

Suddenly having such an intimate relationship with the big boss.

Vivian couldn't believe it. "If Jason really cheated, you wouldn't consider divorce?"

Grace's expression shifted uncertainly.

"I need to understand what happened first... What if that woman seduced him?"

She was instinctively making excuses for her husband.

Vivian was inwardly shocked.

Her cousin clearly wasn't prepared for divorce.

If Jason insisted on ending the marriage, how would Grace cope?

Vivian's expression turned grave.

Any advice would feel hollow right now.

As the saying goes, you can't wake someone who's pretending to sleep.

Jason's infidelity was already a fact.

Yet Grace was only focused on her grief.

She feared her husband would leave her.

There was no resolve to cut ties because of the betrayal.

Vivian truly feared her cousin would suffer deeper harm.

Grace cried until she was completely exhausted.

Seeing her poor condition, Vivian insisted she stay the night.

She dragged her tired body to the bathroom.

After washing up, she went directly to the guest room and fell into a deep sleep.

Vivian could see her cousin was truly exhausted.

Emotionally drained.

She had probably been awake all night since discovering her husband's affair.

She quietly closed the door as she left.

Returning to her own room, she lay in bed but couldn't sleep.

Her mind was filled with concern for her cousin.
###Chapter 217

Sunlight filtered through the blinds, casting patterns across the desk.

This man was always so selfish.
###Chapter 218

He better not follow.

She thought saying this would make a picky nobleman like Zachary give up.

Silence.

Only astonishment remained!

Was he crazy?

He must be joking.

###Chapter 219

Vivian Bennett snapped out of her panic.

In reality, it was just individual small pots for boiling various skewered ingredients.

The "especially delicious" thing Emily mentioned was essentially spicy street food.

The broth base cost five to ten dollars. Vegetable skewers were fifty cents each. Meatballs cost two dollars. Seafood was around four dollars.

With drinks, the per person cost ranged from thirty to eighty dollars.

He leaned in to kiss her.

Vivian pushed him away abruptly.

"What are you doing!"

His plan foiled.

Annoyance flashed in Liam's eyes.

He lowered his voice.

"The couch is too small for my long legs," he said plaintively. "I won't sleep well. Would you really let me suffer?"

Vivian remained unmoved.

She pointed at the floor.

"Then sleep on the floor. It's spacious enough."

Liam tried to say more.

Vivian shut the door decisively.

"Goodnight, Boss Liam!"

The door slammed shut.

Completely cutting off his view.
###Chapter 236

Liam Sullivan stood outside the bedroom door, holding a thin blanket with a dark expression.

He was the CEO of the Sullivan Group, yet he had been locked out by a mere woman.

It was utterly ridiculous.

He had planned to use the stormy night as an opportunity to finally win her over.

But Vivian Bennett remained unmoved by both soft and hard tactics.

He needed to find another way to break through her defenses.

Liam turned off the living room lights and lay down on the sofa with the blanket, closing his eyes to rest.

His mind, however, was racing with various plans.

The next morning.

Vivian opened the curtains and stretched contentedly.

After the storm, the sky was a clear, brilliant blue with bright sunshine.

The flower beds below were lush and green, full of spring vitality.

She stood by the window enjoying a moment of peace when she suddenly remembered something.

There was someone sleeping in the living room!

That's right.

Last night, the big boss Liam Sullivan had stayed at her place.

She had completely forgotten about it.

Vivian patted her cheeks, took a coat from the closet, and gently pushed the door open.

Liam was sitting on the sofa, still drowsy with sleep.

The belt of his bathrobe hung loosely around his waist, revealing his well-defined chest muscles.

He looked weary and exhausted.

"Good morning, Mr. Sullivan. Did you sleep well last night?" Vivian asked softly.

Liam looked up, his eyes full of resentment. "Do I look like I slept well?"

"You couldn't fall asleep?"

Vivian took a step forward and saw the thin blanket crumpled into a ball in the corner of the sofa.

"How could anyone sleep on this sofa?" Liam frowned.

He looked annoyed but held back from lashing out at her, only letting out a low grumble.

"My whole body aches. You're the only one in the world who would dare treat me like this."

Vivian couldn't help but cover her mouth to stifle a laugh.

Was this still the cold and imposing Mr. Sullivan?

Until—

"Still laughing?"

Liam glared at her, his expression darkening further.

Vivian lowered her head, trying hard to suppress her laughter, her shoulders trembling slightly.

After secretly enjoying the moment, she finally composed herself and looked up at the sulking CEO. "Alright, I won't laugh anymore. Go freshen up. We have to go to the company soon."

"What about my breakfast?" Liam shot her a look, his eyes full of expectation.

"I... I'll make it for you." Vivian blinked, reluctantly agreeing.

Hearing her promise, Liam's expression softened slightly.

Just as he was about to speak, Vivian suddenly exclaimed, "Oh no! I forgot you don't have a change of clothes. What will you do for work? Should I go buy some from the store downstairs? But the mall isn't open this early, so maybe you should..."

Vivian trailed off, her eyes hinting that he should leave.

And go home to change.

She didn't have any of his clothes here.

It wasn't that she was kicking him out.

It was just that there was no other way.

He couldn't possibly wear the same outfit.

"No need to buy anything." Liam suddenly spoke with authority.
###Chapter 237

"You can't wear the same clothes for two days straight." Vivian Bennett shot him a glance, her voice dropping low.

Outerwear might be manageable, but undergarments were a different matter.

A man like Liam Sullivan, with his deeply ingrained fastidiousness, would never tolerate wearing the same underwear for two days.

Vivian had counted on this very point, certain it would force him to leave.

"I have a solution."

Liam slowly lifted his gaze, his eyes locking directly with hers.

"What solution?" Her heart skipped a beat, a sense of foreboding washing over her.

A sly glint hid in his eyes, like a hunter who had already set his trap, calmly waiting for his prey to walk right in.

She instantly grew wary.

Liam spoke suddenly. "I can go home and change."

Vivian's expression relaxed.

Perfect. That was exactly what she wanted.

It was rare for them to be on the same page.

"Then what are you waiting for?" Her tone softened, carrying a note of urgency.

She breathed an inward sigh of relief.

She was finally going to get rid of this imposing figure.

Although she had let him stay the night, thankfully nothing had happened.

She had survived this ordeal unscathed.

Liam, however, raised an eyebrow and pointed toward the door. "I live next door to you. 1102."

"1...102?!"

Vivian was completely stunned, her eyes widening into perfect circles.

It took her several seconds to process this. "You live in 1102? That impossibly nitpicky new neighbor who's been making all that renovation noise for days—that's you?!"

"Yes." Liam gave her a brief glance, admitting it freely.

He automatically ignored the "nitpicky" comment.

He watched every subtle change on her face carefully, saying slowly, "From now on, we're neighbors."

"How did you end up next door? Did you buy that unit?!"

Vivian's mind was buzzing, a mix of shock and confusion.

Liam Sullivan had quietly moved in next door, and she was only realizing it now.

What was even more infuriating was that he lived right across the hall. So what right did he have to impose on her place last night?!

He had totally played on her sympathy and tricked her!

How utterly despicable!

Vivian secretly clenched her fists, glaring at him angrily.

Liam certainly wasn't foolish enough to confess now—that he hadn't just bought 1102, but that her own unit, 1101, had long been under his name.

In fact, this entire gated community was a development under the Sullivan Group.

These two units were specially purchased for her.

Seeing Vivian puffing up with anger, her eyes shooting daggers, clearly on the verge of an outburst.

Liam's gaze dimmed, his mood instantly turning somber.

"I had no other choice," he lowered his eyelashes, his voice low and hoarse. "I just wanted to be closer to you."

Vivian's raging anger abruptly stalled.

It was like a clear, pure bell had struck her heart, instantly calming all her agitation.

She stared blankly at the man on the sofa, dressed in an ill-fitting bathrobe, his features weary.
###Chapter 238

Liam Sullivan was born into a top-tier wealthy family.

He was raised in luxury and privilege.

He never had to lift a finger or endure any hardship.

But now, this distinguished man was willing to lower himself for her.

He moved into this ordinary apartment for her.

Vivian Bennett's clenched fingertips trembled slightly.

The defenses she had built in her heart were crumbling inch by inch.

She could no longer maintain her outward composure.

Her eyes revealed an undeniable emotion.

"You really don't have to humble yourself like this," she said, her voice hoarse and choked.

"I'm just an ordinary woman."

"I'm not worth such sacrifice from you."

A man as particular and refined as him was willing to give up his luxurious villa.

Just to be closer to her.

It simply wasn't worth it.

Liam slowly stood up.

He walked to her with steady steps.

He gently took her hand, his gaze intense.

"Any hardship is worth it as long as I can be with you."

"I couldn't sleep all night without you by my side."

His words were simple and plain.

Yet each word struck directly at Vivian's heart.

Her nose tingled with emotion.

Her eyes gradually welled up with tears.

Liam took the opportunity to press her hand against his chest.

He let her feel the strong, powerful heartbeat.

"It beats for you," he said, his deep magnetic voice utterly serious.

"Be my woman. Okay?"

Vivian almost agreed without hesitation.

But at the last moment, she held back.

"Give me a little more time to think..." she murmured, lowering her eyelids.

Her voice was as faint as a mosquito's hum.

Liam lifted her chin, not allowing her to avoid his gaze.

"What more is there to think about?"

Vivian bit her lower lip.

A trace of gloom flashed in her eyes.

"Our statuses are too different."

"How do I know you're not just acting on a whim?"

It wasn't that she didn't want to agree.

She was just afraid of getting hurt.

Liam's eyes darkened.

His tone was resolute.

"Then let's get married directly!"

Vivian's eyes widened in shock.

"Are you joking?"

"I'm serious."

Liam leaned closer, trapping her between his arms.

He gazed at her, his eyes deep like the ocean.

"I pursued you with marriage as the goal."

"If you think dating isn't secure enough, we can go get our marriage license immediately."

"Marriage license?"

Vivian nearly screamed.

They hadn't even dated yet, and he wanted to get married directly?

Liam's expression was solemn.

There was no hint of joking in it.

"I said this on our blind date day."

He indeed had said that.

But Vivian thought he was crazy back then.

Now it seemed he had become even crazier.

She couldn't help but reach out to touch his forehead.

"Do you have a fever? Or did you hit your head?"

Liam's eyes darkened.

He abruptly grabbed her slender wrist.

He pulled her toward him.

Vivian struggled to free her hand.

But he held on even tighter.
###Chapter 239

“What are you doing?” Vivian glared at him angrily.

She hadn’t agreed to anything yet. What did he mean by holding her hand so tightly?

Before she could finish speaking, Liam Sullivan cupped her chin and kissed her.

Vivian’s eyes widened.

She tried to push him away, but he was too strong.

In the end, she had no choice but to endure his domineering kiss.

When it ended, she was left gasping for air.

She shot him an annoyed look.

What should she do?

It seemed she really couldn’t escape this man.

He had moved next door just for her.

Where else could she possibly run?

Vivian lowered her head, not daring to meet his gaze.

She knew his intense stare would make her surrender quickly.

Even without looking up, she could feel his burning, passionate gaze.

“Liam, just let me think, okay?” she said helplessly.

Her mind was in chaos. She needed time to sort through her thoughts.

“How long? An hour?” he pressed, his eyes fixed on her.

“Not today.”

“Tomorrow then?”

“Just give me some space. I can’t give you a specific time right now. It might be today, or it might be tomorrow. But I really need to figure things out between us. I can’t just accept you without thinking clearly.” She ran her fingers through her hair in frustration.

“I can’t wait too long. Think it over today and give me your answer tomorrow!” He released her hand and gripped her chin firmly.

“Don’t do this.” She turned her face away, avoiding his touch.

Looking into his eyes made it impossible for her to think straight.

His gaze was deep and mesmerizing, like a whirlpool that could easily pull her in.

“Let me think. If you really want me to accept you, give me some time. Don’t pressure me.”

Liam stared at her for a long moment, his expression reluctant.

“Three days.”

He had waited too long for her. He had to set a limit. He couldn’t let the waiting become endless torture.

“Three days is my limit,” he said seriously.

“Fine. Three days.” Vivian nodded in compromise.

After finally seeing Liam out, she poured herself a glass of water and sat in the living room to calm down.

The empty, quiet room slowly helped her regain her composure.

But her heart remained tangled and uneasy.

The idea of accepting Liam and being with the powerful CEO felt like a dream to her.

Yet he had moved next door just for her. How had he even come up with that idea?

It was proof of his sincerity.

What should she do?

Vivian ran her hands through her long hair in frustration. She stood up, intending to clear the breakfast dishes.

Her phone on the coffee table chimed twice. A new message had arrived.

She reached for it and opened the message.

It contained a time and a location.

7:30 PM tonight. Sullivan Group branch office.

Vivian’s heart skipped a beat. Alarm bells rang in her mind.

She had been trying to contact her former colleagues at the branch office to inquire about her cousin’s husband, Jason Harper.

But all of them, men and women alike, refused to talk about Jason.

It was as if his name was taboo.

This made Vivian suspicious.

Was this anonymous message from one of those colleagues?

Did they want to tell her something secretly?

Her intuition told her this message was related to Jason’s affair.

After work, she took a taxi to the Sullivan Group branch office.

Night had already fallen outside. The city lights glimmered.

Vivian hid in the dim light of a streetlamp, waiting quietly for Jason’s car to appear.
###Chapter 240

Vivian Bennett immediately recognized her cousin's husband's car.

The sapphire blue Ford.

She clearly remembered that Jason Harper drove home from work every day.

Sure enough, before long.

Jason's car slowly emerged from the underground garage.

It merged into the evening traffic.

Vivian quickly hailed a taxi.

She urged the driver urgently, "Follow that blue Ford closely."

"Don't worry, I'm great at tailing cars," the driver replied cheerfully, stepping on the gas to catch up.

Vivian shrank in the back seat.

She was terrified of being spotted by Jason.

But her eyes remained fixed on that patch of blue ahead.

Jason wasn't heading toward her cousin's home.

The car drove west.

It gradually left the bustling city center behind.

"Miss, is that your man up ahead?" the driver suddenly asked.

Vivian pressed her lips together and didn't answer.

Her silence made the driver give a knowing look.

The scenery outside the window grew increasingly desolate.

But clusters of villas gradually appeared in the distance.

They looked particularly imposing in the twilight.

"This is a famous wealthy neighborhood," the driver remarked with admiration. "Everyone who lives here is rich or powerful."

Vivian's heart sank heavily.

Jason's car drove straight toward the villa's main gate.

He swiped his access card with practiced ease.

The barrier lifted without the slightest pause.

"Seems like a regular visitor," the driver said meaningfully. "This community only recognizes cards, not people."

Cold sweat dampened Vivian's palms.

She finally understood why her colleagues didn't dare reveal Jason's affair.

The other woman lived in a place like this.

"Miss, what now?" the driver turned and asked. "You can't get in."

Vivian stared at the closed gate.

She truly couldn't enter.

Was she supposed to just wait outside helplessly?

Night deepened.

Lights gradually illuminated the villa district.

Each light seemed to mock her powerlessness.
###Chapter 241

Vivian Bennett's mind was a tangled mess of conflicting thoughts.

She truly did not know what to do.

"Driver, let's head back," she finally sighed after a long silence.

The driver nodded understandingly and turned the car toward the city.

Perhaps sensing her low spirits, he offered comfort. "Miss, if you didn't see it, just pretend it never happened."

"Sometimes what you don't see can't hurt you."

"Life is too short to dwell on every little thing. It only brings unnecessary pain."

Vivian closed her eyes and did not respond.

Could she really adopt an out of sight, out of mind approach?

She didn't know if her cousin Grace Harper could do it.

But if it were happening to her, she knew she could never tolerate it.

The warm facade of life had been torn away, revealing a bloody reality.

How much forgiveness would it take to simply ignore all of this?

The car moved steadily forward as Vivian's thoughts drifted further away.

The driver eventually pulled over and turned to her. "Miss, we've already circled half the city."

"Continuing would just waste both time and money. Where would you like to get out?"

His words hit her like a sudden wake-up call.

She immediately took out her phone and called her best friend. "Sophia, I need a drink. Meet me at that bar downtown?"

After hanging up, Vivian paid and got out of the car, heading straight for the bar.

In the car, she had felt an urgent need to release her long-suppressed emotions.

That's why she called Sophia Miller.

Unfortunately, Sophia had already gone abroad again and was currently overseas.

Left with no choice, Vivian decided to go to the bar alone.

To her surprise, she ran into Zachary Hudson right at the entrance.

He wore a camel coat casually left open, leaning lazily against the doorframe.

His elegant posture drew frequent glances and whispered giggles from passing girls.

When he saw Vivian, he immediately waved, a mischievous smile curling his lips.

Vivian initially thought about turning away to avoid him.

But then she reconsidered. Why should she hide?

She had made her position clear and had done nothing wrong.

What did her coming to the bar have to do with him?

Why should she deliberately avoid him?

With that thought, Vivian ignored Zachary completely and strode into the bar.

Unexpectedly, he followed right behind her.

"Didn't expect to see you here looking for fun," he said, a sly glint in his peach blossom eyes.

"I'm not here for fun," Vivian retorted coldly.

"Then what are you here for?" Zachary raised an eyebrow.

"To drink." Vivian felt suffocated and wanted to drown her sorrows.

Zachary's smile widened. "How about I keep you company?"

Just as Vivian was about to refuse, he added lazily, "I was having a great time with a beauty."

"But I dropped everything the moment I saw you. Pretty considerate, right?"

Vivian scoffed dismissively.

Who cared!

Seeing her ignore him, Zachary chuckled again.

The bar's colorful lights reflected in his peach blossom eyes, creating ripples of shimmering color.
###Chapter 242

"That woman was too clingy."

"I had to tell her you were my girlfriend to escape."

"I didn't want to get eaten alive."

"But to repay you for saving me, I'll accompany you all day today."

"Whatever you want to do, I'm all yours."

Vivian Bennett was speechless at Zachary Hudson's audacity.

He used her as a shield?

Was he serious?

But since she had run into this troublemaker, she knew she couldn't shake him off easily.

Vivian kept a straight face and walked straight inside.

She hoped Zachary would get bored and leave on his own.

She chose a quiet booth and sat down.

Zachary slid into the seat beside her.

He glanced around at the blue decor and couldn't help but remark.

"I never knew this city had a place like this."

"It's quite unique."

"I thought you had been to every bar in town chasing women."

Vivian raised an eyebrow.

She poured two glasses from the bottle the waiter brought.

She pushed one toward him and downed hers in one go.

She poured another glass and emptied it again.

Her best friend Sophia Miller had brought her to this bar.

It was quiet despite the city bustle outside, with stylish decor.

She came here for drinks whenever she felt down.

Today was one of those days.

She was almost certain her cousin's husband, Jason Harper, was cheating.

And the other woman wasn't ordinary. She seemed wealthy.

She had probed her cousin Grace Harper before.

Grace knew about her husband's affair but chose to turn a blind eye to save the marriage.

But now?

Knowing the other woman was powerful, how long could the marriage last?

Even if Grace could endure it, Jason might not want her anymore.

Vivian couldn't bear to imagine her cousin being abandoned.

Feeling heavy-hearted, she drank several more glasses.

Zachary watched quietly as she drank.

When she reached for her third glass, his slender fingers stopped her.

"Stop drinking."

"Why can't I drink?"

Vivian pushed his hand away and downed another glass.

She slammed the empty glass on the table.

"Just because I'm honest and play by the rules, I deserve to be betrayed by men like you?"

"Deserve to be sold out?"

"Now I don't even have the right to drink?"

"Not even the right to vent?"

She felt wronged for her cousin and for herself.

Her cousin was stuck in an unhappy marriage with a cheater.

And her?

After that night in the limousine with a stranger, she was left with psychological scars.

She couldn't accept any man now.

She couldn't have a normal relationship.

Nightmares haunted her every night.

She picked up another glass.

Zachary didn't stop her.

He just sat quietly across from her, watching with his captivating eyes.

She drank it all again.

Her cheeks felt hot, and her throat burned, making her cough.

"With how you're drinking, did Liam Sullivan dump you?"

Zachary studied her thoughtfully.

Vivian instinctively denied it.

"Who said I was dumped?"

"We're perfectly fine!"

"We even slept together last night..."

The alcohol loosened her tongue, and she spoke without filter.
###Chapter 243

Zachary Hudson's handsome face instantly darkened.

He abruptly stood up, intending to leave directly.

Vivian Bennett collapsed onto the sofa with a thud, completely unconscious.

She was utterly drunk and passed out.

......

The next morning, Vivian was awakened by a severe headache.

Her throat was dry and sore, almost unable to make a sound.

She habitually reached out to feel for her phone on the nightstand.

After searching for a long time, she found nothing.

Dazed, she propped herself up, trying to locate her phone.

Wait, her bedsheets were supposed to be light purple. Why had they turned cream-colored?

"You're finally awake?" A magnetic and pleasant voice came from above her.

Vivian froze completely.

It was Zachary Hudson's voice!

Oh god, what had she done last night?

Vivian only remembered running into Zachary at the bar while drowning her sorrows.

She had just witnessed her cousin's husband driving to the gated community to meet his mistress, and she was fuming.

She didn't dare tell her cousin this cruel truth.

With nowhere to vent her anger, she could only seek solace in alcohol.

She seemed to have drunk a lot and said a lot...

But when had she passed out? And how had Zachary brought her here?

Frustrated, she grabbed her hair, but her memories remained fragmented and impossible to piece together.

Just as Vivian was desperately trying to recall, Zachary spoke again behind her.

His voice held a hint of teasing. "Since we've already slept together, it's too late for regrets now."

What!

Vivian whipped her head around, glaring fiercely at Zachary behind her.

She then let out a deafening scream. "Zachary Hudson, what did you do to me?"

Zachary was dressed in light gray loungewear, which made him look even more dashing.

A suggestive smile played on his lips as he shrugged innocently. "What could I do? You're usually so quiet and reserved, but last night you were especially passionate. I couldn't resist, so I gave in. But it's fine, I don't mind."

"Zachary Hudson, you bastard! I was drunk yesterday. Couldn't you have stopped me?"

Furious, Vivian jumped out of bed, bending down to search for her shoes while accusing him. "You're a grown man. Don't you feel ashamed using such tactics on a drunk woman?"

Zachary lounged lazily on the edge of the bed, his long fingers supporting his chin, watching her search for her shoes with amusement.

Hearing this, he shook his head helplessly. "I'm terrible at refusing people. You were so desperate last night. How could I stand by and do nothing? Besides, this isn't our first time. Why are you making such a big deal out of it?"

"You..." Vivian was too angry to speak.

How could she not care?

She was practically with Liam Sullivan now.

Vivian suddenly remembered Liam's previous warning. He said he could overlook her past, but if she dared to be with another man, he would never let either of them go.

A shiver ran down Vivian's spine.

As she worried about how to explain this to Liam, she looked down...

Only then did she realize she was still wearing yesterday's clothes. Aside from sleep wrinkles, the bedsheets were neatly made, showing no signs of anyone having slept there.

Which meant Zachary hadn't touched her at all last night!
###Chapter 244

Vivian Bennett breathed a quiet sigh of relief.

She lifted her head and glared at him with displeasure.

"Nothing happened last night at all. Why didn't you say so earlier?"

She was far too trusting of him.

She hadn't even confirmed anything, yet she had assumed they really did...

Zachary Hudson smirked mischievously.

"Where would be the fun in telling you earlier?"

"You!" Vivian's almond-shaped eyes widened with fury.

She nearly lunged at him to bite him.

But since nothing had happened, she could finally relax.

She stood up awkwardly and looked around.

"Zachary, where is this?"

"My home," he answered bluntly.

Vivian was startled and whipped her head to look at him.

"Your... your home? This is your home?"

She scanned the room again.

The luxurious decor featured imported furniture and appliances.

This was clearly no ordinary place.

"Of course it's my home. What did you think it was?"

Her reaction amused him.

"You were completely drunk last night. I wasn't comfortable leaving you at a hotel, so I brought you here."

"Don't worry. Even though you tried very hard to take advantage of me last night, I resisted fiercely. You failed!"

His teasing made Vivian's cheeks burn.

She quickly turned away, pretending to examine her surroundings.

As she expected, Zachary's home was tastefully decorated.

It was full of possessions, yet not cluttered.

One wall displayed several specially treated photographs, exuding a modern artistic feel.

Vivian was drawn to them.

Some pictures showed a younger Zachary, devastatingly handsome with a devilish smile.

She studied each one carefully.

Then she noticed one untreated photo hanging directly on the wall.

It showed two children around ten years old.

The girl beamed radiantly.

The boy stood behind her, also smiling.

The boy was clearly Zachary.

The girl looked familiar, like someone she knew.

Vivian struggled to recall.

Then it hit her.

It was her!

Vivian remembered.

She had seen this girl's photo at Liam Sullivan's home too.

In that picture with Liam and Isabella Sullivan.

Now, here in Zachary's home, was another photo of him with this same girl.

This meant the girl knew both Zachary and Liam.

Who was she?

Why was she connected to both men?

Vivian couldn't figure it out.

Just then, Zachary's voice came from behind her.

"Go freshen up first. I'll get breakfast ready."

Zachary had actually made breakfast!

While washing up, she loosely tied up her hair and checked her reflection.

Traces of the hangover remained on her face.

But after washing, she looked presentable.

Only her eyes held a new worry.

Exiting the bathroom, Vivian found a brand new women's outfit laid out on the bed.

It was probably for her.

She didn't stand on ceremony.

She changed into it before heading downstairs.

Zachary was already in the dining room, setting the table with cutlery.

His eyes lit up when he saw Vivian.

He smiled.

"I just whipped up something simple. Hope it's alright."

Vivian approached the table.

She saw pastries, fried eggs, milk, and even two steamer baskets of translucent little buns.

Each dish looked exquisite and tempting.

She couldn't help but doubt.

Did he really make all this, or was it takeout from a five-star hotel?

She found it hard to believe a young master like him would personally cook breakfast.

Moreover, there wasn't a single servant in this entire villa.

It was very strange.

"Did you really make all this?" Vivian narrowed her eyes, full of suspicion.

"Of course!" Zachary snorted proudly.

Vivian was incredulous.

"I never thought someone as thick-skinned, promiscuous, plagued by bad romances, and unreliable as you could actually have redeeming qualities!"

She sat down opposite him.

She picked up her fork and knife and put a piece of pastry in her mouth.

Zachary raised an eyebrow.

"What? Falling for me now?"
###Chapter 245

Vivian Bennett was taken aback, nearly choking.

She coughed violently.

Zachary Hudson’s lips curved into a faint, mocking smile.
“But I have zero interest in Liam Sullivan’s woman.”

Vivian had been slightly annoyed.
Hearing his words, she felt relieved instead.
It was best that he had no interest in her.

“By the way,” she suddenly remembered something and looked up.
“Who is the girl in that unedited photo in your room?”

Zachary’s peach blossom eyes darkened instantly.
His handsome face became unreadable.
“Why do you ask?”

“I’m curious,” Vivian shrugged.
“What’s there to be curious about?” Zachary narrowed his eyes.

“I saw a photo of her with Liam Sullivan at his house,” Vivian answered casually.
She then lowered her head to continue eating her porridge.
Completely unaware of the change in Zachary’s expression.

The atmosphere suddenly turned tense.
Zachary remained silent for a long time.
Vivian kept eating her breakfast, noticing nothing unusual.

After a while, she looked up again.
“Who exactly is this girl? What’s her relationship with you and Liam Sullivan?”

It was just an offhand question.
But Zachary’s expression changed instantly.
He immediately kicked her out of the villa.

Vivian was utterly shocked.
This man’s mood changed faster than flipping a page.
He just threw her out without hesitation.

Had she offended him?
It didn’t seem so.

She had only asked about the girl in the photo.
Zachary turned hostile immediately.
Clearly, that girl was his taboo.

It was better not to mention her again.
But then Vivian reconsidered.
It would be best if she never had to deal with a scoundrel like Zachary Hudson again.

The three-day deadline agreed upon with Liam Sullivan arrived quickly.
Liam had been waiting for her answer these past three days.
To accept him, or to let him continue waiting.

After work, Liam went to a flower shop first.
Then he drove to Vivian’s apartment complex.

After parking his car, he took the elevator upstairs.
Standing outside unit 1101, he held a specially ordered bouquet of red roses in one hand.
With his other hand, he pressed the doorbell.

The always composed CEO Liam Sullivan felt unusually nervous at this moment.

The doorbell rang while Vivian was preparing dinner in the kitchen.
Puzzled, she walked to the entryway and opened the door.

Under the bright hallway light stood the man in a tailored suit, handsome and arrogant.
Vivian stood by the entryway, momentarily dazed.

“Why are you here?”
Her words instantly cooled Liam’s hopeful mood.
The light in his eyes dimmed slightly.

She didn’t seem to anticipate their three-day agreement as much as he did.
Vivian didn’t notice the disappointment in his eyes.
Her mind was still preoccupied with whether to tell her cousin about the wealthy background of the mistress she had discovered.

“For you,” Liam handed her the red roses.
“Thank you,” Vivian took the flowers, her expression neutral.
“Come in.”

He was already here, and he brought flowers.
She couldn’t just shut him out.
Liam’s mood was clearly less sunny than when he arrived.
###Chapter 246

Liam Sullivan pushed the door open and closed it behind him.

The enticing aroma of a home-cooked meal filled the air.

“You’re cooking?” His deep voice echoed from the entryway.

Vivian Bennett jolted at the sound, suddenly remembering the soup simmering on the stove.

She hastily placed the bouquet of roses in her arms on the coffee table and rushed toward the kitchen.

“My soup!”

Liam followed her into the kitchen.

In the small space, Vivian was frantically adjusting the stove’s heat.

He reached out and pulled her into his embrace.

“What are you doing?” Vivian stiffened, her voice trembling with alarm.

Liam leaned close to her ear.

“I’m waiting for your answer.”

His warm breath brushed against her earlobe. Vivian shivered sensitively and ducked her neck.

“What answer?” she asked instinctively.

“The three-day promise. Don’t tell me you forgot.” His lips nearly touched her ear. “I waited three days. I want your answer now.”

Vivian turned her head away from his touch.

“I remember. Let me go first. The soup is almost ready...”

Liam gave her no chance to escape.

He released her, turned her to face him, and caged her against the counter with his arms.

Their bodies pressed together without a sliver of space between them.

“Liam…” Vivian felt the heat of his body. Her cheeks flushed instantly.

She pushed against his chest.

“Answer me,” he demanded, his gaze intense.

“Don’t pressure me like this…” Vivian bit her lip and avoided his eyes.

His passionate stare always made her lose her rationality.

A flicker of hurt passed through Liam’s eyes.

“We agreed on three days.”

“I’m sorry. I really haven’t decided yet…” Vivian said apologetically.

Too much had happened these past three days.

Her cousin’s husband’s infidelity. The photo at Zachary Hudson’s house. That mysterious girl…

She hadn’t had any time to think about her promise to Liam.

“I understand,” Liam sighed softly.

Vivian looked up at him in surprise.

His expression was neutral, but she didn’t miss the flash of disappointment in his eyes.

He always hid his truest feelings deep inside.

After a moment of silence, Liam straightened up and released her.

Vivian stood still, watching the loss in his eyes. Her heart ached inexplicably.

Liam lowered his gaze and turned to leave the kitchen.

“Liam,” Vivian suddenly called out to him.

He stopped but didn’t turn around. “If it’s not the answer I want to hear, don’t say it.”

The mighty Boss Liam, who had never feared anything, tasted timidity for the first time.

He was afraid of hearing her rejection.

But the word “surrender” didn’t exist in his dictionary.

He closed his eyes briefly, then turned to face Vivian, who still stood by the counter. “I just need one answer. If it’s not the one I want, I’ll keep waiting.”

“What if I can never give you the answer you want?” Vivian lifted her head to meet his gaze.
###Chapter 247

"It's alright, I can wait. One day you'll say yes." Liam Sullivan gazed at her, his eyes shimmering with unwavering determination.

Vivian Bennett looked at his earnest expression and couldn't help but lower her head with a soft laugh. "You haven't even heard my answer yet. How do you know what it is?"

"You agree?" Liam's eyes instantly brightened.

But soon, his expression turned hesitant again.

"Is your answer truly what I want to hear?"

Before Vivian, his usual pride and confidence vanished completely. The formidable CEO who commanded respect in the business world now seemed like an anxious young boy.

"How would I know what you want to hear? I can't read minds." Vivian pressed her lips together, a flicker of mischief in her eyes.

Liam stood at the kitchen doorway, his brow slightly furrowed, looking both troubled and uncertain.

Vivian gestured for him to come closer. "Don't just stand there. Come help me serve the dishes. They'll get cold if we wait any longer."

"Alright." Liam responded immediately, quickly stepping into the kitchen to help arrange the plates.

The atmosphere at the dining table was subtle and quiet.

Vivian never resumed their previous conversation. Liam's emotions rose and fell with her silence.

He finished his last bite of food, put down his bowl and chopsticks, and fixed his gaze on Vivian, who was still eating slowly.

Liam's eyes flickered slightly. He secretly decided to find another opportunity to get a clear answer.

Vivian felt the heat of his stare and looked up uncomfortably, glaring at him. "Stop staring at me. It's weird."

"Am I?" The corner of Liam's mouth lifted, but his gaze didn't waver.

Vivian had no choice but to lower her head and quicken her pace.

Dinner ended in this strange atmosphere. Liam volunteered to do the dishes. The result—a pile of shattered plates and bowls in the sink.

Vivian stared at him, both angry and helpless.

Liam shrugged nonchalantly. "I'll go buy new ones with you."

He started walking towards the door.

Watching his tall, straight back, Vivian bit her lip and suddenly spoke. "How about this? Let's set a one-month limit. We'll use this time to truly get to know each other. If we feel it's not right, we part ways. If it feels right, we stay together."

Liam narrowed his eyes. "What do you mean by 'get to know each other'?"

Vivian's cheeks burned under his gaze.

He was clearly thinking of something else.

Her idea of 'getting to know' wasn't what he assumed.

She glared at him irritably. "Do you agree or not?"

"Are you sure?" Liam walked back to her in a few strides, looking down at her from his height.

Vivian lifted her chin. "As long as you promise..."

Liam's handsome face suddenly moved closer. "Fine, I agree! But..."

Before he could finish his sentence, he leaned in as if to kiss her lips.

Vivian quickly pressed a hand against his chest. "But what?"

Liam's eyes turned dangerous, his voice low and husky. "But getting to know each other as adults shouldn't just be spiritual. It should include physical intimacy too."

Vivian's face instantly flushed crimson. She hadn't expected him to still be angling for benefits at a time like this. She was momentarily speechless.

Watching her flustered expression, Liam sighed helplessly.

He gently stroked her burning cheek with his thumb. "Vivian, I'm a nearly thirty-year-old man with normal desires."

In that aspect, it was impossible for him not to have cravings.

Especially when facing the woman he loved.

He could barely control his urge to have her.

Vivian bit her lower lip, her expression conflicted.

She had unpleasant experiences in that area.

To be honest, she had deep psychological shadows.

But Liam's gaze was intense, as if he wouldn't give up until she agreed.

Vivian had no choice but to brace herself. "Alright, I agree..."
###Chapter 248

Liam Sullivan didn't wait for her to finish speaking before he swept her up into his arms.

He was desperate to become one with her.

He had waited far too long for this day.

Vivian Bennett sensed the danger and quickly pressed her hands against his solid chest. "Didn't you just say you'd go out with me?"

Liam paused.

Had he said that?

Vivian hurriedly reminded him. "You broke my dishes. If you don't buy me new ones, I won't have anything to use for breakfast tomorrow."

Her lips puckered into a pout. Tears shimmered in her eyes.

Liam saw right through her little act.

She was clearly stalling for time.

Even though she had agreed to date him, she was still insisting on that one-month rule.

Vivian thought to herself: she would observe him for a month, avoid intimacy as much as possible, and only take things further if he proved himself worthy.

A dangerous glint flashed in Liam's eyes as the corner of his mouth lifted.

Finally, he released her.

Vivian secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

The next moment, he grabbed her hand and pulled her straight out the door.

His warm palm enveloped her right hand. Vivian stole a glance at him but didn't pull away, allowing him to lead her.

The evening breeze brushed against her face.

She didn't feel cold at all.

Instead, she felt an indescribable sense of contentment.

Was this what love felt like?

Her apartment was located in the heart of the shopping district.

Commercial streets and department stores surrounded the area.

Liam held her hand tightly as they walked forward.

Soon, he led her directly toward a luxury boutique mall.

Vivian hesitated. "Aren't we going to the supermarket to buy dishes? Why are we here?"

"To buy tableware." Liam glanced at her with a smile and entered a Nordic brand store.

He had long disliked her cheap kitchenware. This was the perfect opportunity to replace it with the high-end series he was accustomed to.

Vivian had no choice but to follow him inside.

None of the items on display had price tags.

But it was obvious that anything Liam Sullivan favored would be exorbitantly expensive.

The elegantly poised and impeccably made-up sales associate approached them with trained professionalism.

Liam casually pointed out a few tableware sets before leading her to the vase display.

He gestured toward vases of different materials and asked softly, "Which one do you like?"

Vivian was taken aback. She hadn't expected him to buy a vase.

Was it for the bouquet of roses he had given her?

Her home indeed lacked a suitable vase for such a large arrangement.

She turned to examine the selection carefully.

Under the cone lights, various vases sparkled with exquisite translucence and dazzling beauty.

Vivian looked for a long time, finding each piece utterly captivating.

She looked up at him helplessly. "Do you have any that aren't easily broken?"

Liam raised an eyebrow. "Should we have one custom-made?"

"I was joking." Vivian quickly shook her head. "I just think these vases are so beautiful. It's a shame they break so easily."

Her casual remark made Liam sigh darkly. "You are like my fragile item. The slightest touch might make you leave."

Vivian's expression froze as she stared at him in stunned silence.

Was he serious?
###Chapter 249

Since that night, Liam Sullivan had a legitimate reason to enter Vivian Bennett's home.

They agreed to keep their relationship private at work.

At Sullivan Group, they remained boss and employee.

But Liam appeared at Vivian's apartment whenever he had free time.

Except for sleeping in separate rooms at night, he almost became half-owner of the place.

He often set gentle traps, pulling her into deep kisses.

Vivian repeatedly found herself overwhelmed by his advances, nearly letting him cross the final line.

Tonight, Vivian went to bed early after her shower.

She was startled awake by rumbling thunder.

Heavy rain poured outside, lightning splitting the night sky.

Seattle's rainy season approached.

She curled up in the darkness, her heart racing with each thunderclap.

As she pulled the blankets tighter, the mattress suddenly dipped.

Vivian's eyes flew open, discovering a dark figure sitting on her bed.

Terror seized her, a scream caught in her throat.

A large hand covered her mouth.

The familiar, clean scent of a man filled her senses.

Her eyes widened in disbelief at the intruder.

Liam lifted the covers and pulled her into his arms as he lay down.

Vivian stiffened. "How did you get in?"

His kiss descended.

Between their tangled lips, his voice rumbled low. "It's storming. I thought you might be scared."

His words felt unexpectedly warm in the rainy night.

Almost without thinking, she wrapped her arms around his neck, responding awkwardly.

Encouraged, Liam deepened the kiss.

Only when cool air touched her skin did Vivian realize her nightgown had been pushed to her waist.

Any further would mean losing control.

She snapped back to awareness, pushing hard against him. "Liam Sullivan, you bastard!"

She wasn't ready to give everything yet.

This man constantly looked for opportunities to take advantage.

Liam gazed at her flushed cheeks, deliberately teasing. "Not satisfied? We can continue."

He moved to press down on her.

Vivian struck his chest in frustration. "Who wants to continue with you! Let me go!"

Liam caught her wrists. "Normal men have needs. Weren't you enjoying it too?"

Humiliation and anger warred within her. "Nonsense! Get off me!"

He looked at her furious expression and suddenly laughed softly.

The next moment, his full weight came down upon her.
###Chapter 250

Liam Sullivan pinned Vivian Bennett's hands above her head.

She kicked at him in frustration.

He had never pleaded with a woman so humbly before.

Countless times she had tried to draw boundaries.

Countless times she had refused intimacy.

Yet now she could only let him lie beside her.

She finally fell into a deep sleep within the warmth of his embrace.

Early morning light filtered through the windows.

Vivian woke with a start.

She saw him preparing to rise.

Quickly, she closed her eyes and pretended to sleep.

Liam watched her fluttering eyelashes.

He turned and placed a kiss on her forehead.

"It's still early. Sleep a bit more."

His hoarse voice carried the laziness of just waking.

Vivian secretly opened her eyes.

Finally, she breathed a sigh of relief.
She drifted back to sleep dazed and confused.

They had encountered each other unexpectedly.

Vivian stiffened.

Silently, she walked to the passenger seat.
###Chapter 251

The black sedan glided smoothly onto the main road.

Grace's eyes snapped open, bloodshot.

Her entire body trembled.

"I didn't just see the lipstick mark. I could smell perfume on him too......"

It seemed Jason never ended things with that woman.

Tears streamed down Grace's face.

Her heart felt torn apart, aching terribly.

Since discovering her husband's affair, she had been lying to herself.

Believing that if she tried hard enough, she could save their marriage.

She couldn't figure it out either.

A mediocre man like Jason.

Her cousin had already settled by marrying him.

Vivian promised solemnly.

Grace's eyes suddenly turned cold.

"I refuse to accept being made a cuckold like this."

She balled her fists. "Since he dared to cheat, I'll make sure he leaves with nothing!"

She knew what her cousin was worried about.

She had been too weak all these years, allowing Jason to bully her.

But now, she must learn to protect herself.

She would defend everything that rightfully belonged to her.
###Chapter 253

Her eyelashes fluttered lightly.

Her gaze had nowhere to settle.

Vivian licked her lips, speechless.

Vivian stiffened completely.

Her mind was a turbulent storm, unable to calm down for a long time.

They hadn't known each other for long.

Hatred for her brother grew like wild weeds, choking and relentless.

Since childhood, Brandon had always taken what was hers.

Their father always favored his son. Her stepmother only encouraged the behavior.

She was like a weed, trampled upon yet forced to grow silently.

A wave of powerlessness crashed over her, pulling her under.

Why… couldn't she break free?

She had fought so hard to shake off these chains.

Yet fate kept dragging her back into the abyss.

What would it take to sever this cursed bond for good?

Warm ocean waves gently lapped at her feet.

He bent down to remove his leather dress shoes.

He handed them to her.

She shoved her own shoes into his hands. "You carry mine."

He held both pairs of shoes calmly.

His expression was serene, as if holding priceless treasures instead of ordinary footwear.

A young couple was playing and teasing each other.

Her laughter scattered in the wind.

Liam's frown deepened.

He seemed unable to comprehend such simple happiness.

"Wrong! Because the person they were beating was named Mahjong!"

"I ask a question. If you answer wrong, you carry me. If you answer right, I carry you."

Vivian tilted her head in thought.

"A medium-rare steak meets a well-done steak. Why don't they greet each other?"

Liam answered without expression. "Because they aren't acquainted."

Vivian froze momentarily.

She stamped her foot in frustration. "How did you guess correctly?"

"Was that not the right answer?"

"It is correct, but I can't possibly carry you!"

She felt genuinely annoyed.

Why couldn't he pretend to be wrong occasionally like other men? Just to make her happy.

Liam suddenly crouched down before her.

"Get on."

"Then we'll try another question."
