The bubbles on the water's surface parted and drifted away.

Gathering and scattering, unpredictable.

Her weariness gradually dissolved with the rising steam.

Vivian stepped out of the tub, dressed in her professional attire, and headed to the office.

In the PR department, she brewed a cup of rose tea.

The dried buds slowly unfurled in the hot water.

Layer by layer, the petals bloomed, releasing a subtle fragrance.

She had only taken her first sip when Thomas Carter called an emergency meeting.

A client had sued Sullivan Group.

This project was originally Emily Davis's responsibility.

Rumors said the big boss, Liam Sullivan, already knew.

After the meeting, Emily looked deeply worried.

"Vivian, will I be fired?" she asked, gripping Vivian's hand tightly.

"Don't worry, these things happen," Vivian reassured her softly.

Emily frowned. "But Mr. Sullivan is involved this time, and the client has powerful connections…"

A phone rang abruptly.

Liam Sullivan's name appeared on the screen.

His low voice came through the receiver.

"Come up."

In the past, Vivian might have made an excuse.

But for Emily's sake, she had to go.

She took the private elevator directly to the top floor.

Liam was still in a meeting. His secretary brought her coffee.

She rose from the sofa and walked slowly to the floor-to-ceiling window.

Unease spread through her.

How should she plead for Emily?

Would he even care?

Or would he outright refuse her request?

Outside, the rain began again.

Sunshine was a rare sight during Seattle's rainy season.

The office remained unchanged from her last visit.

Dark clouds gathered, thunder rumbled.

Skyscrapers pierced the cloud layer like black swords aimed at the heavens.

A precarious heaven tilting within the storm.

A perfume bottle on the corner of the desk caught her eye.

She picked up the glass bottle and gently opened the cap.

Familiar footsteps sounded behind her.

Liam Sullivan opened the door and entered.

The rain formed a backdrop, outlining her slender figure.

Her soft profile appeared hazy in the rainy light.

She turned to look at him, her complexion much better than during her illness.

Their gazes met in the air.

Clear eyes meeting profound depth.

He lifted his chin slightly and walked steadily closer.

He placed a file lightly on the desk.

His arm encircled her waist, bracing against the desk edge.

He drew near slowly.
###Chapter 322

His deep eyes locked onto her.

His thin lips curved upward.

His low, husky voice drifted slowly through the air.

"You were very obedient today."

"You came up so quickly."

Vivian Bennett looked up at him.

A sly smile touched her red lips.

She suddenly raised her wrist.

Aiming the perfume bottle at his neck.

Just as she was about to press down—

Liam Sullivan swiftly grabbed her wrist.

He gave it a gentle twist.

Turning her entire body around.

Pinning her down on the sofa.

His chin rested against the hollow of her shoulder.

His arm wrapped around her slender waist.

Warm breath brushed past her ear.

"Is this how you ask for a favor?"

Vivian was slightly stunned.

He obviously knew about Emily Davis.

He also knew they were close.

That was why she was here.

Outside the window, rain poured down heavily.

Leaden-gray curtains of rain mixed with dark clouds.

Like a black river with turbulent undercurrents.

The entire floor-to-ceiling window was clear as a mirror.

Standing here felt like being above the clouds.

Liam held her from behind.

They both gazed at this illusory world below.

In the distance, tides rose and fell.

He leaned against the edge of the desk.

She leaned back into his embrace.

Breathing in his familiar scent.

She suddenly remembered the suit hanging in her apartment this morning.

Smoke-gray with a glossy finish.

She had approached it.

Pressed her cheek gently against the fabric.

As if she could still feel the warmth he left behind.

Like being held tightly by him.

"I told your manager."

"Not to touch Emily Davis for now."

His voice was especially gentle amidst the sound of rain.

Vivian's lips curved slightly.

She let out a soft laugh.

"I knew you would come to me."

Her voice was cold and flippant. "You guessed everything right."

Her fingertips brushed against the watch strap on his wrist.

Caressing the cool metal texture.

Liam rested his chin on top of her head.

A faint dampness drifted from her hair.

Mixing with the familiar subtle scent of roses.

Soft and alluring.

"Here."

"You can ask anything of me."

"Ask you to beg?"

"That might not be impossible."

Vivian laughed without emotion. "But I have nothing to ask for."

"Then why did you come?"

She paused for a moment.

Finally, she spoke softly. "I don't know."

What did it matter if he deliberately forced her to come?

She didn't understand why she had come.

Those two words, "come here," from that phone call.

Were like a giant whirlpool.

Sucking her in.

Falling endlessly.

Plummeting into a bottomless abyss.

Shattering into pieces seemed almost inevitable.

She knew that if she just asked him.

All her problems would be solved.

In front of him, she always got whatever she wanted.

But right now, she didn't need any of that.

She didn't know why she had come.

They had both been busy lately.

She couldn't even remember the last time they met.

He seemed to still care about making her sick that night on the beach.

He raised his hand, pressing the back of it against her forehead.

Both their skins carried the coolness of the rainy day.

No longer burning hot like before.

"Much better." Liam chuckled softly.

"Recovering well."

"You must have taken your medicine obediently."

Vivian remained silent.
###Chapter 323

Liam Sullivan tightened his arms around her.

They both gazed out through the floor-to-ceiling window.

The city lights glittered brilliantly outside.

Inside, the room was utterly silent.

He suddenly cupped her face in his hands.

His voice was low. "You didn't come to beg me for something. You don't need anything from me."

"Then why did you come here today?"

Vivian Bennett met his gaze directly.

A moment later, her eyelids fluttered down.

As if bewitched, her lips parted slightly. "Kiss me."

His fingertip gently brushed the tip of her nose.

His thin lips descended upon hers.

Vivian stumbled backward.

Liam Sullivan steadied her with a firm grip on her waist.

Between the melding of their lips, he chuckled softly. "Remember, you asked me to kiss you."

"Otherwise, I would have made you regret it."

His gaze was dark and detached.

She knew he meant every word.

She didn't understand why she had come either.

Not to beg him.

Not because she happened to be passing by.

And certainly not with some hidden agenda.

Her mind was a complete blank.

She trembled slightly as she returned his kiss.

She closed her eyes.

From the desk to the chair.

She sank deeper step by step.

Plunging into a gentle vortex.

Dark clouds gathered in her mind.

Lightning flashed and thunder rumbled outside.

The rain soaked her heart with dampness.

She suddenly remembered that dream in the bathtub.

In the dream, he wore a pristine white suit.

He rarely wore such bright colors.

He always preferred black and gray.

Elegant and steady, yet hiding a wildness beneath.

But in the dream, he looked astonishingly handsome in white.

A bride in a wedding gown was on his arm.

Her face was unclear.

But it definitely wasn't her.

The rain pattered softly.

Her stockings felt cool against her skin.

Damp strands of hair clung to her temples.

A shiver, like an electric current, ran through her entire body.

She kept falling into a dark whirlpool.

She closed her eyes.

That pure white dream surfaced repeatedly.

The blurry bride in his arms.

She began to struggle.

The dream world was vibrant with color.

Only she was gray.

All the joy had nothing to do with her.

He didn't belong to her either.

She couldn't go on like this.

Outside, the rain poured down in sheets.

Her world was raining too.

She followed his rhythm.

Sometimes urgent, sometimes lingering.

She bit his shoulder.

He was in pain.

She was in even more pain.

It was the only revenge she could give him.

But seeing the bloody teeth marks, she felt no happiness.

Only an endless sense of loss.

Finally, he leaned down.

He gently kissed away her tears.
###Chapter 324

Rain poured heavily outside the window.

Thunder rumbled loudly.

A flash of lightning split the sky.

Vivian Bennett leaned weakly against his shoulder.

A faint smile touched her lips.

Liam Sullivan lit a cigarette.

The ember glowed and faded.

He lounged lazily by the window.

His gaze was tender as he watched her.

His cool knuckles brushed over her swollen lips.

Vivian looked at him through the haze of smoke.

Her eyes were clear and burning bright.

A phone rang abruptly.

Vivian saw the caller ID.

Her heart skipped a beat.

Jason Harper's voice sounded tired and hoarse.

"Vivian, can you come back?"

"Your cousin has been sick for days."

"She won't eat or drink."

Vivian's fingers turned cold.

She answered stiffly.

"I'll come back now."

Jason offered to pick her up.

She quickly refused.

She would take a taxi.

After hanging up, Liam frowned slightly.

"Leaving?"

Vivian explained softly.

"My cousin is sick."

"I need to check on her."

Liam's expression darkened instantly.

"I'll drive you."

"No need."

She lowered her head to open the ride-hailing app.

He snatched her phone away.

"I'm driving you."

Vivian bit her lip hesitantly.

"What if someone sees us?"

"Before, we could say it was a ride."

"Now things are different."

She felt guilty and scared.

Especially during work hours.

Liam returned her phone.

"Don't worry."

"No one will notice at this time."

Vivian nodded reluctantly.

His car would be the fastest.

But she was covered in marks.

She couldn't let anyone see her.

"Wait a moment."

She hurried into the restroom attached to the lounge.

After washing up, she was about to leave.

Then she heard a woman's voice in the office.

"Brother, Grandma and Grandpa want to have dinner."

Isabella Sullivan's voice was coy.

"Don't stand them up this time."

"They'll be angry."

Liam responded coldly.

Isabella chuckled lightly.

Her eyes scanned the gap under the lounge door.

"Grandma and Grandpa have chosen a suitable young lady from an elite family."

"They insist you meet her."

"Not interested."

Liam's tone was icy.

Isabella raised an eyebrow.

"The position of Mrs. Sullivan can't remain empty forever."

"All those vixens out there are eyeing it."

She had sensed something unusual the moment she entered the office.

That vixen Vivian.

She actually dared to seduce her brother in his office.

She had to put her in her place.

Make that woman back off.

The Sullivan family wasn't a place just anyone could enter.
###Chapter 325

Vivian Bennett pushed open the door to her cousin's house.

In the living room, Jason Harper was holding his phone and speaking in a low voice.

Seeing Vivian, he panicked and quickly ended the call.

"Vivian's here?" He forced a smile. "Your sister is in the bedroom."

Vivian glanced at him.

That call must have been to that woman again.

She didn't ask any questions and walked straight to her cousin's bedroom.

She knocked lightly on the door.

Grace Harper's weak voice came from inside. "Come in."

Vivian pushed the door open.

Grace was struggling to sit up in bed. Her face was frighteningly pale.

Vivian hurried forward to support her.

It had only been a few days, but her cousin had lost a noticeable amount of weight.

Her once bright eyes were now dull. Even her breathing seemed labored.

"Grace, where does it hurt?" Vivian asked anxiously.

Grace managed a weak smile. "It's nothing serious. I'm just a little tired."

Vivian frowned. "If you're not feeling well, you should see a doctor. Why are you pushing yourself?"

Grace shook her head. "It's an old problem. Doctors can't fix it."

Vivian understood.

Her cousin's illness was in her heart.

Jason's betrayal was like a thorn, deeply embedded within her.

No one could easily remove it.

"Even so, you should go to the hospital," Vivian insisted.

Grace held her hand. "Really, it's not necessary. A few days of rest will help."

Seeing her stubbornness, Vivian had to give up.

They chatted about recent events.

Grace's eyes suddenly welled up with tears. "Your brother-in-law... he asked for a divorce."

Vivian's heart tightened.

No wonder her cousin had become so ill.

She had always valued her family above all else.

Now Jason was determined to destroy it.

Vivian left her cousin's house with a heavy heart.

The night grew deeper, but she felt no desire to sleep.

Her mind was filled with the image of her cousin's haggard appearance.

Just then, her phone lit up.

She thought it was a message from Grace.

She opened it and froze.

Liam Sullivan's photo was displayed on the screen.

He was having dinner with an unfamiliar woman.

The woman was exquisitely dressed and elegant in her movements. She clearly came from a wealthy family.
###Chapter 326

Vivian Bennett couldn't help but recall the conversation she overheard in Liam Sullivan's office earlier that day.

Isabella Sullivan had gently reminded him to return to the family estate for dinner. His grandparents had specifically arranged a blind date for him.

So the person he was meeting was this elegant and outstanding socialite.

Vivian's heart sank little by little.

She knew there was no future between them. Yet the thought of him meeting someone else still made her chest ache with a fine, piercing pain.

She tossed and turned in bed, only managing to fall asleep late into the night.

Half-asleep, the mattress suddenly dipped.

Vivian groggily opened her eyes. Before she could see who it was, a tall figure loomed over her.

She was about to scream in fright when the man's cool lips pressed down on hers.

His familiar scent instantly soothed her panic.

But why would he suddenly appear in the middle of the night?

Feeling both embarrassed and annoyed, Vivian pushed against him with all her strength.

Liam remained unmoved, instead deepening the kiss.

His kiss carried the urgency of a storm, almost stealing her breath away.

Vivian winced in pain, but gradually softened under his increasingly gentle movements.

After what felt like an eternity, Liam finally released her. He hovered above her, breathing heavily.

The bedside lamp clicked on.

His gaze darkened as he took in her dazed eyes and swollen lips.

He lowered his head to peck the corner of her mouth a few times before lying back down beside her, satisfied.

"What are you doing here?" Vivian thought of the blind date. Her tone was unintentionally sharp.

Liam lazily rested his head on his arm, a slight smile playing on his lips. "I missed you."

Those three short words made her heart tremble. Her anger from moments ago instantly vanished.

She turned her face away and muttered softly, "Who cares..."

The man suddenly flipped over and moved closer. His deep eyes fixed on her flushed cheeks. "Don't care? Then why is your face red?"

Vivian's eyes widened in embarrassment.

She couldn't help but blurt out, "You think everyone falls in love as easily as you do?"

Liam frowned. "Who are you saying falls in love easily?"

"You! Don't think I don't know you went on a blind date today! Facing such a beautiful socialite, dare you say you weren't tempted?" The more Vivian spoke, the more her words dripped with jealousy. Her eyes began to redden.

Liam chuckled lowly and suddenly pressed down on her. "Whether I was tempted or not, aren't you clear? Who was it that cried yesterday saying I was too rough?"

Vivian, frightened, quickly pushed him. "No! I'm still sore..."

Liam, of course, couldn't bear to touch her again.

He pulled her into his arms and gently kissed the top of her head. "I won't touch you tonight. As for the blind date, Grandpa arranged it. I went to save face for the old man, but that woman was too controlling. Not my type."

Vivian still felt sour. "But you still went... What if in the future..."

"It was just a formality." Liam looked down at her, his gaze gentle. "Who I like, don't you know?"

Vivian's face instantly flushed crimson.

Did his words mean... he liked her?
###Chapter 327

Vivian Bennett huffed softly.
"Why haven't you gone back to your own place yet?"

Liam Sullivan's brow furrowed slightly.
"Trying to get rid of me?"

She lifted her clear eyes to meet his deep gaze.
"Your grandfather is already arranging blind dates for you. It's better if we keep some distance."

"If you keep staying here, what if someone finds out?"

The air in the bedroom suddenly grew cold.

Liam silently watched her, his eyes unfathomable.
His gaze carried an intangible pressure.

Being stared at like this made Vivian's heartbeat quicken involuntarily.

She always felt incredibly small before this experienced, profound man.

She bit her lip and insisted,
"Before your family agrees, we shouldn't make our relationship public."

She secretly glanced up to gauge his reaction.

The Sullivan family would undoubtedly struggle to accept such a delicate relationship.

If Liam insisted on being with her, it would cause tremendous turmoil.
She didn't want to put him in a difficult position.

Liam's expression remained unreadable.

Just as Vivian worried he might be angry, he suddenly pulled her into his arms.
He lowered his head and captured her lips in a kiss.

The kiss was lingering and intense, filled with an undeniable dominance.

Only when their breathing became ragged did Liam reluctantly release her.

His voice was low and husky.
"Get some rest."

He got out of bed and left.
The sound of the door closing echoed clearly in the silence.

Vivian lay in bed, her emotions in turmoil.
She tossed and turned, unable to sleep.

The next day happened to be a weekend.

After breakfast, Vivian made plans with Sophia Miller to go shopping.

She touched up her makeup lightly and prepared to head out.

Just as she reached downstairs, her phone vibrated.
A text from Liam appeared: "Wait for me at the corner."

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.
She happily walked toward the corner.

From a distance, she spotted the black limousine parked by the roadside.

She cautiously glanced around before quickly opening the car door and slipping inside.

Gazing at the man's handsome profile, she smiled radiantly.
"Didn't you leave early? Why are you still here?"

Liam gave her a brief glance but didn't respond.

Vivian was already accustomed to his occasional silence.

Sunlight streamed through the window, softening his innate coolness.

He focused on the road ahead, his slender fingers resting lightly on the steering wheel.
It reminded her of the term "warm guy."

Even though it completely contradicted his aloof demeanor.

As the car was about to exit the gated community, Liam finally turned to look at her.
"Where to?"

"Can you drop me off at my cousin's place first?"

There was still plenty of time before her meeting.
She wanted to visit Grace Harper.

The car interior returned to silence, yet a warm atmosphere flowed between them.
###Chapter 328

The black sedan came to a smooth stop by the old residential building.

Liam Sullivan’s long fingers rested on the steering wheel, his profile sharp and cold.

His voice was low. "It's fine to care for your cousin, but you don't need to handle everything personally."

Vivian Bennett froze for a moment, her fingertips tightening unconsciously.

She replied softly, "My cousin is going through a divorce. Right now, I'm the only one who can be there for her."

Liam frowned slightly. "Divorce?"

Vivian lowered her gaze. "Yes. This illness is mostly a matter of the heart."

The atmosphere in the car turned heavy and silent.

Liam's eyes darkened, but he did not press the issue further.

Vivian pushed the car door open and stepped out, her figure looking frail.

She walked quickly into the building unit, her heart feeling heavy.

The door to her cousin's apartment was slightly ajar.

Vivian pushed it open and stopped in her tracks.

The living room was half empty. Pale outlines were all that remained where furniture once stood.

Grace Harper leaned against the bedroom doorframe, her face pale.

Her voice was hoarse. "He moved out."

Vivian's heart sank.

So all those urgent words from her cousin's husband yesterday were just to make a quicker escape.

She suddenly felt a tightness in her chest.

A man's cruelty always exceeded imagination.

"Cousin, are you okay?" Vivian asked gently.

Grace managed a bitter smile. "I saw it coming. I just didn't think it would be this soon."

Vivian sat with her for a long time until Sophia Miller called.

Her best friend's red sports car was parked conspicuously at the entrance of the complex.

Downtown, the commercial street was bustling with people.

Sophia linked arms with Vivian, her eyes scanning the storefronts.

She suddenly leaned closer, lowering her voice. "You've been with Liam Sullivan for a while now. Haven't you thought about giving him a special gift?"

Vivian looked blank. "Like what?"

Sophia grinned mischievously. "How about some lingerie?"

Vivian's ears turned bright red instantly. "What nonsense are you talking about!"

"This isn't nonsense," Sophia raised an eyebrow. "Men love this stuff. Especially a man of Liam's status. Don't you need to use some tactics?"

Vivian glared at her in embarrassment. "Sophia Miller!"

Sophia quickly raised her hands in surrender. "Just a joke. Why so serious?"

Vivian turned her face away, her earlobes still flushed.

Sophia nudged her shoulder, her tone turning serious. "Honestly, a man like Liam Sullivan has countless women eyeing him. You need to have some sense of crisis."

Neon lights fell on Vivian's eyelashes, casting faint shadows.

She stared at the dazzling storefronts for a long time without saying a word.
###Chapter 329

Near the end of the workday, Vivian Bennett's phone screen lit up.

Liam Sullivan's name flashed on the display.

Her heartbeat quickened slightly as her finger swiped the answer button.

His deep voice came through the receiver. "Wait for me after work. We'll have dinner together."

Vivian instinctively glanced around.

After confirming no one was watching, she lowered her voice. "Okay."

Their relationship wasn't public yet. Every date required careful discretion.

She tentatively suggested, "Should I wait at the café across from the office?"

There was a brief silence on the other end.

Liam walked to the floor-to-ceiling window.

His gaze pierced through the glass, focusing on the café across the street.

Though the distance was too great to see anything clearly.

A faint smile touched his lips. "Should I have Ryan pick you up?"

Vivian quickly refused. "No, it wouldn't be good if someone saw."

She paused briefly. "I'll go to the café first after work."

Liam glanced at his wristwatch, his voice softening. "Alright."

After the call ended, he returned to his work.

Vivian packed her things and left the office on time.

She entered the café and chose a secluded corner to sit.

Her fingers absently stirred the coffee in her cup.

Suddenly, a black luxury car pulled up outside the shop.

The familiar license plate caught her eye.

Vivian immediately stood to pay the bill and hurried out of the café.

She opened the passenger door and slid inside.

Liam quickly drove away from the scene.

In a corner of the café, Isabella Sullivan stared intently at the departing car.

Her grip tightened around the coffee cup, knuckles turning white.

So her brother was still secretly seeing this woman.

She had thought it was just a casual fling.

Now it seemed things were far more serious than she imagined.

Liam took Vivian to an upscale French restaurant.

Candlelight flickered, creating a romantic atmosphere.

But Vivian remained distracted throughout the meal.

After dinner, they returned to Emerald Estate villa.

Vivian followed quietly behind Liam, her expression downcast.

Noticing her unusual mood, Liam pulled her into his embrace.

His deep voice murmured near her ear. "What's wrong?"

Vivian leaned against his chest with a soft sigh. "My cousin is getting divorced. I'm worried about her."

Liam raised an eyebrow. "Do you need my help?"

Vivian shook her head. "No, it's her family matter."

"Let me know if you need anything." Liam smoothed her hair. "Don't carry this alone."

Warmth spread through Vivian's heart. She nodded gently. "Mm."

After sharing her concerns, she felt considerably lighter.

Liam watched her gradually brightening expression.

The corner of his mouth lifted. "Feeling better now?"

Vivian looked up at him, her eyes curving into smiles. "Much better."

"Then keep me company while I handle some work in the study?" Liam took her hand.

Vivian felt a twinge of guilt. "Did I delay your overtime work?"

Liam tightened his grip on her hand. "I'm glad you're willing to share your worries with me."

His gaze deepened. "You can always come to me, anytime."

The simple words moved her more than any sweet nothing could.

Warmth flooded Vivian's heart as she actively returned his hand squeeze.
###Chapter 330

Her heart warmed.
Her voice softened.
"Liam Sullivan, don't spoil me too much."
"What if I get used to it?"

"I'll take responsibility if you do."
Liam Sullivan tightened his grip on her hand.
He led her toward the second-floor study.

Vivian Bennett shook her head gently.
"You have work to do. I won't disturb you."

Liam suddenly wrapped an arm around her slender waist.
His fingertips brushed her cheek.
He lowered his head and captured her lips.

The kiss was lingering and tender.
It held endless affection.

Vivian melted into his embrace.
She allowed him to deepen the kiss.
Her body gradually softened into a pool of spring water.

Only when he was satisfied did Liam slowly release her.
He whispered against her ear, "With you here, I work more efficiently."
He then lifted her into his arms and carried her into the study.

Liam sat behind the large desk.
He focused intently on processing documents.

Vivian lounged lazily on the nearby sofa.
She idly flipped through a book.

Two completely different scenes merged strangely into a warm picture.
Neither wanted to break this peaceful and beautiful moment.

When Liam finished his work, he realized the woman on the sofa had fallen asleep at some point.
The corners of his lips lifted.
He bent down and scooped her up.

Vivian woke up groggily.
She yawned and asked, "Finished?"

"Yes." Liam carried her toward the bedroom.

Only when she was tossed onto the soft bed did Vivian fully wake up.
Seeing the fire igniting in Liam's eyes, she panicked and tried to escape.

Liam caught her slender ankle.
His tall body pressed down on her like a mountain.
His voice was low and hoarse. "It's too late to run now."

He gave her no chance to refuse.
He sealed her lips again.

The temperature in the bedroom rose instantly.
The scene became enchanting and moving.

Only when Vivian had melted into a puddle of spring water, emitting kitten-like whimpers, did Liam reluctantly let her go.
He carried her into the bathroom.

Vivian didn't expect Liam to transform into a wolf again in the bathroom.
He tormented her until she nearly passed out.

Half-asleep and half-awake, she secretly swore to herself:
From now on, she would rather keep her worries to herself than confide in this man.
The price was simply too heavy...

Vivian slept deeply this time, completely defenseless.
She woke to find herself in Liam's office again, feeling both anxious and uneasy.

Her whole body ached, making her frown.
She hurriedly washed up and changed clothes.
She gently pushed open the door to the restroom.

The vast office was empty.
Her heart sank.
She knew Liam was busy with work, but she was unbearably hungry.

Just as she hesitated, the office doorknob suddenly turned.
Startled, she turned and ran back toward the restroom.

Liam pushed the door open and saw a figure swiftly disappearing.
His thin lips curved into a captivating smile.

He strode to the restroom door.
He called out softly, "Little thing, why are you hiding after waking up?"
###Chapter 331

Vivian Bennett opened the door.

Liam Sullivan stood outside.

His features were sharp and handsome, radiating vitality.

Vivian let out a soft, sleepy hum.

"Liam Sullivan, I'm hungry."

The man chuckled deeply.

He reached out and pinched the tip of her nose.

"My little piglet sure can eat and sleep."

Vivian's eyes widened instantly.

"Who's your little piglet!"

She protested, her cheeks puffing out in anger.

"It's all your fault that I'm so hungry."

"You kept me up until the middle of the night last night..."

She abruptly cut herself off.

Her cheeks instantly flushed crimson.

Liam's laughter was a low, deep rumble.

"Sorry, my fault."

He leaned close to her ear.

"I'll make sure to feed you properly later."

He pressed the intercom button.

"Grace Evans, bring in the lunch."

A knock soon sounded at the door.

"Come in."

The door was pushed open.

A capable, professional-looking woman entered.

Vivian instinctively tried to retreat.

Liam wrapped an arm around her waist.

Their bodies pressed close together.

He whispered in her ear.

"Don't be afraid. This is my secretary, Grace Evans."

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

She had never heard of this female secretary.

Grace Evans appeared completely composed.

"Hello, Miss Bennett. I'm Grace Evans."

Vivian managed a weak smile.

"Hello."

Grace set out the meal and then left.

Vivian stared at the table full of food.

"When did you get a female secretary?"

Liam raised an eyebrow.

"Jealous?"

He gave a light laugh.

"She's been with me for many years. Primarily handles internal affairs."

Vivian turned her face away.

"Who's jealous? I was just asking."

The man sat down beside her.

His voice was magnetic and alluring.

"My energy is only enough to handle a little temptress like you."

The tips of Vivian's ears burned.

She silently retorted in her mind.

Limited energy?

Who was it that showed no signs of fatigue last night?

Thinking of those images made her cheeks grow even redder.

Like a perfectly ripe tomato.

As they ate quietly, her phone suddenly rang.

It was a call from Jason Harper.

Vivian glanced hesitantly at Liam.

She pressed the answer button.

"Brother-in-law?"

Jason Harper's voice was hoarse.

"Vivian, have you visited your cousin recently?"

Vivian pressed her lips together.

He sounded like he had just woken up.

"Yes."

She replied softly.

A woman's voice suddenly came through the phone.

"Honey, why aren't you coming over yet..."

The call ended abruptly.

Vivian gripped her phone tightly.

That was Lily Evans's voice.

Jason Harper's mistress.

He was still with his mistress.

Yet he had the nerve to pretend to care about her cousin.

How utterly hypocritical.

Liam noticed her change in mood.

"What's wrong?"

Vivian shook her head.

"Nothing."

She put her phone away.

The ringing started again.

Vivian answered it absentmindedly.
###Chapter 332

Her phone rang urgently.

Vivian Bennett answered the call.

The calm voice of a nurse from City Central Hospital spoke from the other end.

She was informed that her cousin, Grace Harper, was being resuscitated in the emergency room.

Vivian’s face instantly turned pale.

Her fingers tightened unconsciously.

She stood up abruptly. "My cousin is in trouble! She's in the emergency room!"

Liam Sullivan immediately set down the documents in his hand.

He steadied her trembling shoulders with a calm demeanor. "Don't panic. I'll take you there."

He quickly arranged for the driver to prepare the car.

He personally escorted her out of the office.

The atmosphere in the car was oppressive.

Vivian clutched the hem of her clothes tightly.

Her mind kept flashing images of her cousin's gentle, smiling face.

She couldn't understand it.

How could someone as strong as her cousin suddenly end up in the emergency room?

When they arrived at the hospital, the light above the emergency room was still on.

Vivian grabbed a passing nurse and urgently asked for information.

Only then did she learn that her cousin had been admitted after a suicide attempt.

A chill ran through her body.

She nearly lost her balance.

Liam promptly supported her waist.

He pulled her into his embrace.

Vivian's voice trembled. "How could she try to kill herself..."

The answer was painfully obvious.

Jason Harper's betrayal and relentless pressure had pushed her cousin to the brink.

The thought of her cousin's husband currently frolicking with his mistress while her cousin lay in the emergency room fighting for her life made Vivian's blood boil with rage.

Her nails dug deeply into her palms.

Liam held her icy cold hand firmly.

He comforted her in a low voice. "Don't be afraid. I've already contacted the hospital director."

His steady voice slightly eased her anxiety.

Vivian leaned against his chest.

She tried hard to steady her breathing.

The emergency room doors finally opened.

The doctor emerged, removing his mask.

Vivian immediately stepped forward. "Doctor, how is my cousin?"

The doctor's expression was grave. "Her life is saved, but..."

He paused. "The patient shows signs of mild depression."

Vivian stood frozen in place.

Depression?

Her cousin had depression?

She never imagined her cheerful cousin could suffer from a psychological illness.

Liam calmly pressed for details. "Can it be confirmed?"

The doctor shook his head. "We need to wait for the patient to wake up for a thorough examination."

After thanking the doctor, Vivian hurried to the hospital room.

Grace Harper lay quietly on the bed.

Her face was as pale as paper.

She looked like a porcelain doll that would shatter at the slightest touch.

Vivian gently held her cousin's cold hand.

Her eyes instantly welled up with tears.
###Chapter 333

The night was thick as ink.

Vivian Bennett kept vigil by her cousin Grace Harper's hospital bed, tears silently tracing her cheeks.

Liam Sullivan returned from his phone call and saw her slender, solitary silhouette.

In his memory, she had always been resilient and decisive.

But now she seemed as fragile as glass, ready to shatter at the slightest touch.

Each teardrop fell like a weight upon his heart, stirring a sharp, piercing pain.

He strode forward and gathered her chilled form into his arms.

Vivian's face pressed against the warmth of his chest, and all her defenses crumbled instantly.

She clung tightly to his solid waist, her tears soaking through his dress shirt.

Liam looked down at the trembling woman in his embrace, his throat tight.

A thousand unspoken words condensed into a firmer, more protective hold.

He only wanted to tell her—Don't be afraid. I'm here.

She wept for a long time before finally lifting her red, swollen eyes.

"Thank you..."

Her voice was hoarse, but filled with genuine emotion.

Without him, she didn't know how she would have survived this nightmare.

"Finished crying?"

Liam knelt down, looking up at her.

His deep eyes held a gentle light, tender enough to drown in.

Vivian stared at him, momentarily spellbound.

"Vivian."

He called her name softly, a slight curve touching his lips.

"How long do you plan to keep staring with those puffy eyes?"

"Huh?" Vivian snapped back to reality, her cheeks flushing.

She had been caught staring again.

She hastily wiped her tear-stained face and mumbled, "Do they really look that bad?"

Liam chuckled softly. "You always focus on the details."

His teasing finally drew a faint smile from her, lightening her heavy heart slightly.

"Get some rest." He glanced at his watch. "It's midnight."

"I can't sleep." Vivian shook her head, her gaze returning to the hospital bed. "My cousin hasn't woken up yet. I won't be able to sleep."

"Alright." Liam didn't press further. He pulled over a chair and sat down. "I'll stay with you."

Vivian was taken aback. "Aren't you going home?"

He was so particular about cleanliness. How could he stand not changing his clothes?

Liam leaned his head back wearily and closed his eyes.

"I'll go later. I'm staying with you for now."
###Chapter 334

Vivian Bennett gently touched his arm with her fingertips.

The man leaned back in his chair with his eyes closed, weariness etched between his brows.

Her voice softened. "Why don't you rest on the bed?"

Liam Sullivan shook his head, his brow furrowing.

After a long moment, his thin lips moved. "Dirty."

Vivian couldn't help but let out a soft laugh.

The man was unbearably particular.

Yet his serious, resistant expression made her heart soften.

The night grew deeper.

Fine snow began to fall outside, dotting the windowsill.

A snowy mist enveloped the entire night sky.

Vivian leaned against the window, her gaze fixed inside the hospital room.

Two figures, one large and one small, filled her entire world.

He was here, keeping watch.

As if time had stopped just for them.

Seven in the morning.

Pale golden dawn light streamed through the glass, filling the hospital room.

Light and shadow cast a warm glow over the two embracing figures.

Tree shadows swayed outside the window as the accumulated snow slowly melted.

The cold winter morning held an unexpected warmth.

Vivian opened her eyes groggily.

She had slept deeply through the night.

Rubbing her throbbing temples, she suddenly froze.

On the opposite hospital bed, Grace Harper was smiling at her.

"Cousin?" Vivian whispered in delighted surprise.

Grace made a quieting gesture.

She glanced behind Vivian and said softly, "He just fell asleep. Don't wake him."

Only then did Vivian realize Liam was lying beside her.

Sunlight fell on his face, softening his sharp features.

His long lashes cast shadows over his deep-set eyes.

Dappled light danced across the bridge of his straight nose.

Vivian's lips curved upward unconsciously.

She quietly drew the curtains to block the harsh sunlight.

That was when she noticed the strong arms wrapped around her waist.

He held her tightly.

Warmth flooded her heart instantly.

Tiny sparks of light shimmered in her eyes.

"Cousin, when did you wake up?"

Grace's voice remained soft. "Last night. I didn't want to disturb you since you were asleep. The doctor came for rounds this morning. He stayed with me the whole time. He just lay down to rest."

Vivian's chest warmed.

She hadn't expected Liam to be so thoughtful.
###Chapter 335

Vivian Bennett turned back and gazed quietly at his sleeping face.

An indescribable emotion welled up in her heart.

She leaned down close to his ear.

Her voice was as light as a feather. "Thank you..."

Her fingertips gently brushed over his handsome brows.

Before she could withdraw her hand, it was suddenly caught by a warm, large hand.

Vivian was slightly startled.

"You're awake?" Her voice carried a trace of panic.

Liam Sullivan opened his deep eyes, a hint of weariness still lingering in them.

"Yeah..." he responded in a low, husky voice.

"Did I wake you?" Vivian felt full of guilt. "Sleep a little longer."

Liam propped himself up and nodded toward Grace Harper on the hospital bed.

He glanced at his wristwatch.

Vivian suddenly remembered and lightly patted her forehead. "Oh no! Don't you have an international meeting today? Starting at nine?"

She looked anxiously at the clock. "There's only an hour and a half left. Will you make it?"

Grace looked apologetic upon hearing this. "It's all my fault for delaying you both. How can you work after staying up all night..."

"Don't say that, Cousin Grace." Liam gave a faint smile, smoothing his slightly disheveled hair. "The doctor just examined you. You're fine now."

He stood up and adjusted his clothing, every movement exuding elegance.

"Stay with your cousin today and rest. No need to come to the company." He turned to Vivian. "I'll handle your leave."

Liam checked his watch again and smiled apologetically at Grace. "Sorry, I have to go now. Contact me anytime if you need anything."

"Go ahead with your work. I'll be fine here." Grace hurriedly urged him.

"I'll walk you down." Vivian threw off the blanket, quickly tidied herself, and followed him.

They walked side by side down the corridor in the faint morning light.

"Thank you so much for yesterday." A gentle breeze brushed through, tousling the strands of hair on her forehead.

Liam narrowed his eyes. "You've said thank you too many times since last night."

Vivian nodded solemnly. "Because I'm truly grateful."

He had been by her side when she needed support the most.

Liam raised an eyebrow. "So you're just going to dismiss me with verbal thanks?"

"What else?" She smiled lightly.

"It's on credit." He smiled meaningfully. "I remember you still owe me compensation."

"You still remember something from so long ago?" Vivian gave him a reproachful look.

"How about we cancel each other out?" she suggested playfully.

"Wishful thinking." Liam flatly refused. "Just see me off here."

They stopped at the hospital's main entrance.

"Do you need a change of clothes?" he suddenly asked.

Vivian was taken aback.

Liam checked the time. "I'll have someone bring clothes over later."

"Thank you..." Warmth spread through her heart.

"The caregiver will be here soon. Get some more rest." He reminded her thoughtfully.

"But you didn't sleep all night. Are you really okay?" Vivian asked with concern.

"I'll find time to rest." He turned and walked into the morning light.
###Chapter 336

Vivian Bennett's heart finally settled somewhat.

"I should go now," Liam Sullivan's deep voice sounded.

"Alright, goodbye," Vivian waved gently.

Liam turned and walked away.

The setting sun stretched his tall figure into a long silhouette.

A golden halo enveloped him, warm and captivating.

For the first time, Vivian noticed how his retreating figure stirred her heart.

The softest part of her heart seemed gently touched.

She unconsciously curved her lips into a smile.

"Liam Sullivan..."

She suddenly called out to him.

She quickly caught up, stopping just a step away.

Her gaze rested on him without concealment, filled with admiration and excitement.

"Has anyone ever told you that you look exceptionally good in a suit?" Vivian said softly, her cheeks slightly flushed.

It was a genuine compliment from her heart.

Liam clearly hadn't expected her to say that. His eyebrows raised slightly, a flicker of amusement in his eyes.

"Vivian Bennett, you certainly know how to talk," he chuckled, his voice laced with pleasure.

He had to admit, her words pleased him greatly.

"But you should know, I look handsome in anything," he added with full confidence.

Vivian couldn't help but laugh out loud.

This guy was ridiculously narcissistic.

After seeing Liam off, Vivian returned to her cousin's hospital room.

The smile instantly vanished from her face.

"Cousin, why were you so foolish?" She tightly grasped Grace Harper's hand, her voice choked with emotion.

Could her cousin truly be suffering from depression?

Otherwise, why would she do something so extreme?

How could she bear to hurt herself like this?

"I already regret it," Grace said with dim eyes, sighing softly. "Fortunately, it's not too late."

Vivian's eyes reddened.

"Cousin, promise me you won't do this again."

Grace smiled self-deprecatingly. "Yes, I was too foolish."

She had nearly paid with her life for a man who didn't love her.

Was there anyone in this world more foolish than her?

Fortunately, heaven had given her a second chance.

"I won't do this again," Grace promised solemnly.

Vivian stared at her blankly. "Cousin..."

Her heart was still filled with worry.

She didn't know if her cousin had truly come to her senses or was just saying this to comfort her.

Grace smiled faintly. "Don't worry, I've figured it out. From now on, I will love myself well and cherish my life. I will never let anyone hurt me again, especially those who aren't worth it."
###Chapter 337

Oliver Winston rose to his feet, a perfectly measured smile gracing his lips.

"Grandmother."

He gave Eleanor Sullivan a slight nod, his tone respectful yet familiar.

During his youth, Oliver had practically grown up at the Sullivan estate.

He and Liam Sullivan had known each other since childhood, their families deeply connected. It was only natural that he came to regard Eleanor as his own grandmother.

Liam's temperament mirrored his mother's.

Outwardly gentle and modest, but profoundly inscrutable beneath the surface. This kind of restrained nature, once unleashed, was all the more startling.

Oliver, however, was innately open and free-spirited, which greatly endeared him to Eleanor.

The elderly lady often summoned him to the old estate for companionship and casual conversation.

Eleanor had been in high spirits lately.

Just a short while ago, she had vacationed overseas with several old friends, returning radiant and refreshed.

She smiled warmly at Oliver. "Yangyang, it's been a while."

Though time had aged her, that smile still held a glimpse of the charm that had once captivated Seattle's high society.

Oliver's eyelashes fluttered slightly as he returned a gentle, bright smile.

Everyone took their seats for the meal.

The atmosphere at the table was initially harmonious and warm, until Liam excused himself to take a phone call.

Eleanor watched her grandson's retreating back, her expression suddenly darkening.

She turned to Oliver. "What are your thoughts on Xiao Xu's marriage?"

Oliver's lips curved into a smile. "Ah Xu has his own plans."

"What plans?" Eleanor's voice turned abruptly cold. "I couldn't be bothered during his years abroad, but don't think for a second I'm unaware."

Oliver lowered his gaze with a light chuckle, offering no further response.

"Yangyang."

"You have my attention."

"You and Xiao Xu are close. You must advise him when the time is right." Eleanor's tone grew heavier. "Casual dalliances are one thing, but if he truly intends to get serious—"

A silver utensil clattered against the fine bone china plate, producing a sharp, clear ring.

Oliver flinched, looking up to find all traces of Eleanor's earlier affection had vanished from her face.

"Absolutely impossible."

Liam Sullivan leaned against the glass corridor of the rooftop restaurant, the wind filling his suit sleeves.

His phone screen glowed with a work update from a subordinate.

He appeared to be handling business, but his mind was troubled by his grandmother's relentless pressure to marry.

Ever since meeting the match his grandparents had arranged, they had been pushing him relentlessly.

He had hinted, ever so subtly, that his heart already belonged to another.

But his grandparents remained adamant. They would accept no one outside their designated social circle.

Ash scattered in the wind, but an invisible hand seemed to slowly tear open a void in his chest.

A throbbing pain between his brows made him realize he had smoked far too much.

Irritated, he reached for his cigarette case, only to find it empty.

The hollow feeling in his chest intensified.

He had no choice but to give up. He dialed a number and spoke in a low voice. "Proceed as planned."

When he returned to the table, the atmosphere remained tense.

Just as Liam was about to sit down, Eleanor slammed her utensils heavily beside her plate.

He was accustomed to such scenes. He cast a cold glance and took his seat calmly.

Across from him, Oliver shot him a look of concern, but Liam ignored it entirely.

He smoothed his napkin and continued his meal.
###Chapter 338

"Who was that?" Eleanor Sullivan set down her napkin.

"The company."

"Which department?"

"The secretary's office."

"Male or female secretary? What's your relationship like? What does her family do?"

Oliver Winston's knuckles turned slightly white.

He had long heard about the Sullivan matriarch's extreme control over Liam's marriage prospects, but today he witnessed it firsthand.

A twenty-nine-year-old man receiving a phone call had to endure such interrogation, as if every woman in the world was eyeing the position of Mrs. Sullivan.

No wonder Liam Sullivan stayed away from home all year.

The silver fork pressed against the grain of the steak, the fissure in his heart tearing wider.

The agitation that had lingered since last night swirled relentlessly.

He preferred quiet, and Eleanor's incessant chatter made him frown deeply.

"Don't get involved with women of questionable character."

"I don't care about your usual fun, but marriage is a serious matter. I only say this because you're not getting any younger."

"What important call had to come during dinner? If you weren't satisfied with the last young lady, I can find you another—"

Clatter—

The knife and fork landed sharply on the bone china plate.

"No need," Liam interrupted, his lips curling into an icy smile. "I simply won't marry."

......

After seeing Eleanor back to the estate, Oliver took Liam to the fencing club.

Over a dozen bouts, every thrust of his blade carried a ruthless force.

Oliver barely managed to parry, finally removing his mask in surrender.

"I yield. You're not holding back at all."

Liam took off his mask, a faint trace of amusement flickering in his eyes.

Seeing his mood slightly improved, Oliver relaxed as they left the venue side by side.

The black sports car sped toward the city center, neon lights flowing past the windows.

Oliver spoke suddenly, "Do you really plan never to marry?"

Liam tapped the steering wheel lightly. "What's the point of marriage?"

"Point?" Oliver scoffed. "There is no point. It's just finding someone to march into the grave with."

He rolled down the window and lit a cigarette, asking softly through the haze, "If you ever marry, would you cheat?"

A low, mocking sound escaped Liam's throat.

As if ridiculing the absurdity of the question.

But Oliver pressed on seriously, "I bet if you marry anyone other than Vivian Bennett, you will cheat."

Liam shot him a cold sidelong glance.

"The only person you'd cheat with would be Vivian."

"You're already following in your father's footsteps. Falling for someone you shouldn't, both of you know it but can't break free."

Oliver took him to their usual bar.

The bartender friend specially mixed them strong drinks.

Liam got completely drunk, while Oliver remained sober throughout.

He finally called the Sullivan family driver to pick them up.

In the car, Liam loosened his tie, his vision blurry.

His head throbbed painfully; the last time he had been this unrestrained was years ago.

He had always exercised extreme restraint with smoking, drinking, and women.

But since yesterday, all three taboos had been shattered.
###Chapter 339

The black limousine descended from the overpass and turned toward Lakeside Manor.

The car window slowly lowered.

Liam Sullivan undid his platinum cufflinks, carelessly rolling his dress shirt sleeves up to his elbows.

The solid lines of his arms were faintly visible in the night.

He lit a cigarette.

The cold wind carried the scent of smoke into the car, cutting through some of his drunken haze.

"Turn right."

The man's voice was deep.

The driver paused, instinctively turning the steering wheel.

The boss was unusually out of character tonight.

"Right again."

"Go straight."

"Enter the complex."

A series of commands left the driver scrambling.

Liam was usually a man of few words, but tonight he was like a human GPS.

The car entered a familiar residential area.

The driver finally understood—this was Miss Vivian's place.

The back seat suddenly fell silent.

Through the rearview mirror, the driver cautiously glanced.

Liam leaned back against the leather seat, his collar disheveled.

The usual air of arrogance and nobility was completely gone.

His eyes were tightly shut, brows furrowed as if he was enduring immense pain.

The car gradually slowed.

It finally stopped beneath a particular building.

"Stop."

The man suddenly spoke.

Then came a barely audible murmur.

"Vivian... I'm not getting married."

Vivian Bennett had a rare free evening.

The afternoon meeting ended early, so she went home ahead of time.

After a simple dinner and finishing her remaining work, she indulged in a comfortable hot bath.

The bathroom was filled with steam.

She stepped out, drying her hair, and noticed several missed calls on her phone.

They were all work-related.

She couldn't be bothered to return them and went straight to her dressing table.

Her fingertips unconsciously brushed over her earlobes.

They were empty.

A man's suit hung in her bedroom.

Her gaze skimmed over it before quickly looking away.

The hair dryer hummed.

As her hair gradually dried, she remembered the clothes still hanging on the balcony.

She pushed open the glass door, and a cold wind hit her face.

Her burgundy silk nightgown clung to her skin, the hem barely covering her thighs.

She hurried to the clothesline, gathered the laundry, and was about to go back inside.

As she turned, her eyes inadvertently glanced down toward the street.

A black limousine slowly drove along the tree-lined road.

The complex's newly installed lighting system was exceptionally bright, the streetlights winding like a river of stars.

That car looked familiar.

She squinted, but by the time she looked again, the car had vanished into the night.

It must have been her imagination.

Vivian withdrew her gaze and gently closed the door.

She curled up on the sofa, her fingers unconsciously rubbing her cold ankles.
###Chapter 340

Vivian Bennett’s gaze fell on the men's suit in the walk-in closet.

The pure black fabric gleamed with an aristocratic sheen under the light.

Her fingers curled unconsciously.

This suit belonged to Liam Sullivan.

He had left it at her place that stormy night when he drove her home.

She never found the right opportunity to return it.

Her eyes remained fixed on it for several seconds.

She shook her head.

She tried to dispel the chaotic thoughts in her mind.

The more she thought, the more agitated she felt.

She simply turned off the light and lay down.

The moonlight outside her window was hazy.

Tree shadows danced below.

A black Maybach was hidden in the night.

Its sleek lines blended into the darkness.

Liam Sullivan leaned against the car door.

The ember of his cigarette glowed between his fingers.

The night breeze brushed against his slightly wrinkled shirt.

He looked up at the window on the eleventh floor.

He waited until the light had been off for a long time.

Then he stubbed out his cigarette and signaled the driver to leave.

The next evening.

Vivian Bennett leaned against the balcony railing, lost in thought.

Twilight descended.

A sudden red glow appeared on the neighboring balcony.

Her heart clenched.

She turned her head sharply.

Liam Sullivan leaned against the glass door.

A cigarette was held between his fingers.

His outline was blurred by the swirling smoke.

"Did I scare you?" He chuckled softly.

Vivian gripped the railing tightly. "When did you get back?"

He had clearly said he would be away for a week.

Yet here he was.

So the car last night wasn't an illusion.

Liam Sullivan extinguished his cigarette.

He vaulted over the balcony railing.

His shadow enveloped her.

Vivian was forced to step back.

Her back pressed against the cold tiles.

One hand gripped her waist.

The other traced the corner of her lips.

His kiss, tasting of tobacco, descended.

It was both gentle and domineering.

Vivian's breathing became ragged.

Her fingers unconsciously clutched his lapel.

"Come to my place?" He murmured against her earlobe.

Without waiting for an answer, he swept her into his arms.

He carried her directly across the balcony into the adjacent apartment.

The deep blue bed dipped under their weight.

Her dark hair spread out like seaweed.

Her pale skin created a stark contrast against the sheets.

Liam's eyes darkened instantly.

He resembled a leopard that had been lying in wait for too long.

The corners of Vivian's eyes turned red.

Her nails dug into his arms.

Why was he never satisfied?

She was the one begging for mercy.

She was the one losing control.

As her consciousness drifted, she heard her own broken whimpers.

Afterward.

Liam Sullivan carried her sleeping form to clean up.

His phone screen lit up.

He frowned at the caller ID and answered.

"Miss Isabella is lingering outside the residential area."

"She might have found out."

His voice was cold as he ordered. "Prepare the car in the basement."

His thumb stroked the sleeping face of the woman in his arms.

His expression gradually darkened.

Isabella Sullivan had overstepped her bounds.

Half an hour later.

A black sedan drove out of the residential area.

Its dark tinted windows blocked all prying eyes.

The night was as dark as ink.
###Chapter 341

Ryan Johnson gripped the steering wheel tightly.

His gaze remained fixed on the road ahead.

His tone carried a hint of hesitation. "Sir, regarding Miss Isabella... Should we really not take any action?"

Liam Sullivan looked down at the small woman sleeping soundly in his arms.

His voice was soft as water. "Leave her be. Starting next week, arrange for discreet protection for Vivian Bennett."

...

Vivian woke to the afternoon sun filling the room.

She was startled to find herself in Liam Sullivan's villa.

She glanced around at the familiar furnishings.

She raised a hand to shield her eyes, her heart stirring with ripples of emotion.

How had she slept so deeply that she didn't even feel him bringing her here?

Her entire body felt sore and weak.

She didn't want to move, but she had to get up.

If this continued, both their bodies would suffer.

She needed to find a way to restrain Liam's insatiable behavior.

She couldn't let him have his way anymore.

After showering and changing clothes, Vivian slowly descended the stairs.

The spacious first floor was empty.

She knew without thinking that the man must be in his study.

She gently pushed open the study door.

Liam sat behind the large desk, his expression focused and serious.

His long fingers flew across the keyboard.

She quietly walked up behind him.

She leaned her soft body against his broad back.

Her voice carried the laziness of someone just awakened. "Liam, when did we get here?"

Liam's hands paused slightly.

He turned his head to look at the fair, delicate face resting on his shoulder.

His voice was deep and magnetic. "What's wrong?"

"How did I not feel anything?" Vivian said, angrily pinching his back.

Her strength was like a tickle to Liam.

He chuckled lowly. "You were sleeping so soundly. How would you know?"

Vivian snorted lightly, her cheeks flushing. "I used to be a light sleeper. The slightest noise would wake me. It's because you exhaust me so much that I sleep so deeply now."

Liam knew the little woman was settling accounts again.

He softened his voice. "How would you like me to make it up to you?"

Vivian was caught off guard by his question.

She was momentarily speechless.

How could such a thing be compensated?

She mumbled softly, her face red. "This is too hard on our bodies... From now on, only once per night."

Liam's face immediately darkened.

Was this little woman trying to kill him?

Why would he only eat half-full when the feast was right before him?

A half-starved existence was simply torture.

He slowed his tone, persuading gently. "Vivian, we only see each other once a week. If I don't deliver the full quota, that little head of yours might start imagining things again. What then?"

Vivian's face turned even redder. "Who's imagining things! Precisely because we only see each other once a week, we must learn restraint. Otherwise... otherwise you can spend next week by yourself!"

This was the first time Liam had been so directly threatened.

And he couldn't even refute it.

He looked at Vivian's stubborn little face.

He decided to retreat in order to advance.

If he really pushed her too far and she stood him up on the weekend, that would be a real problem.

He had no choice but to agree softly. "Alright."

But did this old man truly agree in his heart?

Or was he just feigned compliance?

It wasn't until evening, when Liam pinned Vivian to the bed again, that she realized she had been completely deceived.

This man not only showed no restraint, he intensified his efforts.

This vigorous session of exercise directly caused her to sleep until the next afternoon.

Just as she was preparing to settle accounts with Liam, her cousin Grace Harper suddenly called.
###Chapter 342

Vivian Bennett hurried to the hospital.

She pushed open the door to the hospital room.

Grace Harper was staring out the window, lost in thought.

Vivian walked over quietly.

Her cousin's thin shoulders trembled slightly.

Vivian's heart clenched.

She reached out and held Grace's cold fingers.

"I'm here, Grace."

Grace turned her head.

Her eyes were red, but she forced a weak smile.

"Vivian, you came all this way so late."

Vivian shook her head.

She pulled a chair over and sat by the bed.

"I was worried about you being alone."

Outside the window, the night grew deeper.

Only a small lamp lit the room.

The dim yellow light fell on their clasped hands.

Vivian spoke softly about amusing work stories.

She tried to dispel the heavy atmosphere in the room.

Grace gradually began to laugh.

The worry between her eyebrows lessened.

Vivian secretly breathed a sigh of relief.

......

Liam Sullivan pushed open the door to the Emerald Estate apartment.

The interior was pitch black.

He flipped the switch.

Light flooded the empty living room.

His steps halted.

The air no longer carried the scent of her usual perfume.

He strode quickly into the bedroom.

The closet stood wide open.

It was completely empty.

The vanity was clean.

Even her favorite lipstick was gone.

Liam's chest tightened painfully.

He pulled out his phone to dial.

His fingers trembled slightly.

The cold tone of a powered-off phone echoed in his ear.

His tall, straight figure froze in place.

An unprecedented panic washed over him like a tide.

He stood before the floor-to-ceiling window.

The city lights glittered brilliantly outside.

Yet they could not illuminate the darkness in his heart.

Liam turned abruptly.

He dialed his assistant's number.

"Find Vivian Bennett's current location."

A black car cut through the night.

It stopped at the hospital entrance.

Liam strode down the corridor.

He finally saw that slender figure outside a hospital room.

Vivian was gently closing the door.

She turned and collided directly into his embrace.

His arms tightened around her fiercely.

It felt as if he wanted to crush her into his very bones.
