She needed him to personally apologize and make amends.

Liam understood her intentions perfectly.

But he mercilessly rejected her proposal.

He couldn't be bothered to argue further. He spoke directly to Ryan. "Take Miss Winston to dinner. If that doesn't work, bring Oliver Winston along."

Oliver Winston, who had just reached the corner, silently retreated upon hearing this.

It wasn't that he lacked loyalty.

He just had no desire to wade into these troubled waters.

Liam checked his watch and frowned. "I'm due at my appointment. I have to go."

He strode away with long steps.

Chloe tried to follow, but Ryan blocked her path.

"My apologies, Miss Winston. Mr. Sullivan never brings female companions to business meetings." Ryan said apologetically. "If you require an apology, I can..."

"I don't need you!"

Blocked from pursuing him, Chloe trembled with rage.

She watched Liam's retreating figure, feeling like her heart and lungs would explode.

She shouted at his back, "I will make you take me to dinner and apologize willingly!"

Liam didn't pause. He stepped directly into the elevator.
###Chapter 355

Ryan Johnson glanced sideways at the closed elevator doors.

He wiped the cold sweat from his forehead.

What kind of game was his boss playing today?

He turned to Chloe Winston with an apologetic smile.

His expression was strained.

Chloe's lips were pale with anger.

She shot him a furious glare.

Then she turned and stormed off.

Ryan took a step to follow her.

But seeing her murderous expression, he wisely stopped.

Vivian Bennett had dinner alone.

Ryan appeared punctually at the restaurant entrance.

He said Liam Sullivan had ordered him to pick her up.

She got into the car.

Her phone vibrated in her pocket.

She pulled it out.

A text from Liam.

"Where?" Two brief words.

Vivian set her phone to silent.

She typed quickly, "Just finished eating. Heading back."

Soon, another message popped up. "What did you eat?"

Her eyes sparkled mischievously. "Guess?"

Liam replied bluntly, "No."

"..."

Vivian held her phone, stifling a laugh.

Though separated by screens, she could picture his serious expression.

Afraid Ryan might notice, she quickly composed herself.

Her fingers flew across the screen. "No romance. Aren't you negotiating? So free to text. Won't the client mind?"

Liam didn't skip a beat. "Better than upsetting you."

Vivian felt wronged. "I'm not some tiger wife."

"Quite the opposite," he paused, then added, "Much harder to please. If I upset you, you might punish me tonight. Worried I won't have the energy to keep up."

His bold words hooked her mind.

Images of their intimate nights surfaced instantly.

Her cheeks flushed warm.

Unmentionable scenes filled her thoughts.

Before she could reply, another message arrived. "Naughty girl. Blushing? What are you thinking?"

Her heart jumped.

She nervously glanced around.

Only Ryan was in the car driving.

Liam was nowhere in sight.

How did he know she was blushing?

Did he have x-ray vision?

As if reading her mind, he texted again. "You blush easily."

A two-second pause.

Then another message. "Especially when we're alone at night."
###Chapter 356

Vivian Bennett coughed violently.

Her cheeks burned crimson.

She clutched her phone tightly, unsure how to react.

Ryan Johnson watched her through the rearview mirror.

"Miss Bennett, do you need assistance?"
His voice held genuine concern.

Vivian shook her head in a fluster.

"It's nothing."
Her voice sounded weak, her eyes avoiding his gaze.

Ryan frowned slightly.

"Are you sure?"
He clearly didn't believe her.

Vivian nodded emphatically.

"Really, I'm fine."
She forced herself to appear calm.

Ryan returned his attention to the road.

Vivian looked down at her phone.

She typed quickly.
"Can you be serious for once?"

Liam Sullivan replied instantly.
"When have I not been serious?"
He threw the question back at her.

Vivian fell silent.

She bit her lip as she typed.
"Focus on your business meeting. How will you provide for me if you get distracted during negotiations?"

The message went unanswered.
Liam didn't reply again.

Vivian pouted and put her phone away.

Liam stared at his phone screen.

The text message made his lips curve upward.

He casually set his phone down.

He looked up at the middle-aged man across from him.

He offered an apologetic nod.
"Mr. Marshall, my apologies. My little temptress is being rather demanding today."

Mr. Marshall gave a dry laugh.
"It's rare to see Mr. Sullivan taken with someone. Quite understandable."
His words were flattering, but his eyes held no warmth.

Liam's lips curved slightly.
"Indeed, she shouldn't be neglected."

Mr. Marshall's expression stiffened.

Liam's arrogance was well known.

No one would willingly deal with him unless driven by profit.

But Liam had the capital to be arrogant.

Mr. Marshall swallowed his pride and repeated his earlier pitch.
"Our company just received a shipment of premium building materials. The quality far exceeds industry standards. Your real estate division might have use-"

Liam leaned back lazily in his chair.

He picked up his tea cup, moving deliberately.
"I heard this shipment was originally destined for Hudson Group. The contract was supposed to be signed tomorrow?"
He raised an eyebrow.

Mr. Marshall's heart tightened.

Liam's gaze was too sharp.

It felt like he could see right through everything.

He forced a smile.
"Businessmen naturally seek the best partners."

Liam gave a low chuckle.

The sound was icy, sending chills down the spine.

He set down his tea cup and produced a contract.

He pushed it across the table toward Mr. Marshall.
"Double the price. Sign it."

Mr. Marshall stared, utterly speechless.
###Chapter 357

Mr. Marshall never expected Liam Sullivan to have the contract prepared in advance.

This move completely eliminated any room for further negotiation.

Mr. Marshall had approached Liam with a probing attitude.

He had accidentally learned that Zachary Hudson and Liam Sullivan were once bitter rivals.

They had a complete falling out years ago over a woman.

The business world is a battlefield, and such complicated relationships present profitable opportunities.

He was betting on whether Liam cared more about face or money.

His initial offer was significantly higher than the price given to Hudson Group.

Not only did Liam accept it, he proactively raised the price by two times.

The answer couldn't be more obvious.

Liam paused just as he was about to leave.

He turned back to look at Mr. Marshall, who was gathering the contract documents.

His gaze was sharp as a blade.

"Mr. Marshall, you should know my temper."

"I despise it when people use those I care about to threaten me."

"Seeking profit is acceptable, but one must know when to stop."

Mr. Marshall's face instantly turned pale.

He quickly nodded and bowed in assurance. "It won't happen again. It won't happen again."

A faint smile touched Liam's lips.

"Pleasure doing business."

"Pleasure, pleasure," Mr. Marshall hurriedly replied.

Liam strode out of the private room with long steps.

Mr. Marshall pulled out a handkerchief and wiped the cold sweat from his forehead.

His little scheme had been seen through completely.

When dealing with someone like this, clever tricks only work once.

Mr. Marshall was just about to leave after packing his things.

Zachary Hudson's assistant suddenly approached him.

"Mr. Marshall, we're supposed to sign the contract tomorrow. Why are you here?"

Assistant Gao had heard the rumors and came specifically to salvage the situation.

He had just seen Liam leaving the private room.

Mr. Marshall's smile was strained.

"Assistant Gao, a merchant chases profit. It's only natural."

Gao Jing's expression immediately darkened.

He wasn't ready to give up. "Mr. Marshall, we can discuss the terms again."

"Unnecessary," Mr. Marshall waved his hand in refusal.

Gao Jing pressed further. "Have you already signed a contract with them?"

Mr. Marshall could only offer a bitter smile.

He certainly wouldn't admit he had been outmaneuvered.

Even though he didn't get Hudson Group to raise their price, tripling the original wasn't a loss.

Liam was truly extravagant when it came to that woman.

It was already 7:30 PM when Liam left the tea restaurant.

He was about to retrieve his car and leave when Chloe Winston suddenly rushed out from the side.

This woman had been pestering him relentlessly since a chance encounter.

Liam had completely lost his patience. He simply stepped around her and continued walking.
###Chapter 358

Chloe Winston watched Liam Sullivan completely ignore her.

She was so angry she shouted at his retreating back, "I haven't eaten yet!"

Mr. Marshall had used Vivian Bennett to threaten Liam, putting him in an extremely foul mood.

Chloe Winston had chosen the worst possible time to appear.

A flash of menace crossed Liam's eyes.

He stopped walking and turned to look at her. "Miss Winston, I admire your persistence."

Her relentless pestering reminded him of when he first met Vivian.

Vivian had also done things that displeased him, but she never made him feel this impatient.

This is how people are.

For someone you care about, even their most hateful actions hold a certain flavor when remembered.

For someone you don't care about, no matter how similar their actions, you can never recapture that initial feeling.

Liam pulled a stack of cash from his wallet without looking at it.

He turned back, grabbed Chloe's hand, and shoved the money into her palm.

"This is enough for a meal and a doctor's visit. Don't come looking for me again."

After saying this, he left without a backward glance.

The action might have been insulting, but it was the limit of his tolerance.

Chloe stared at the money in her hand, watching his resolute back as he got into his car and drove away.

She bit her lip hard, turned, got into her own car, slammed the accelerator, and chased after him.

Gao Jing had just exited the tea restaurant and witnessed Liam giving her the money.

He frowned slightly and first reported to Zachary Hudson that the cooperation talks had collapsed.

Hesitating for a moment, he wasn't sure if he should mention Liam's entanglement with another woman.

Zachary's voice came through the phone, "Anything else?"

Gao Jing steeled himself and described what he had just seen, adding at the end, "Maybe it's not what I think..."

Zachary hung up before he could finish.

Vivian finished her meal alone with nothing else to do.

She passed by an event where movie discount coupons were being handed out.

She casually walked into the cinema.

After the movie ended, her phone rang. It was Liam.

His deep voice came through the receiver, "Where are you?"

Vivian told him she had just seen a movie.

"Wait for me outside the cinema." Liam hung up after saying this.

He didn't even give her a chance to say another word.

Knowing he had finished his business and was free, Vivian didn't think much of it.

She put away her phone, walked out of the cinema, and said to Ryan Johnson waiting outside, "You can head back first. Liam is coming right away."

Ryan nodded and left. He knew Liam preferred to pick her up personally whenever he had time.

It wasn't the weekend, and the area wasn't particularly bustling.

The street outside the cinema was sparsely populated, less lively than usual.

Vivian sat down on a nearby bench to wait.

But the person who arrived wasn't Liam...
###Chapter 359

Vivian Bennett’s pupils constricted sharply.

She stared in disbelief at the man who had suddenly appeared before her.

Her heart pounded so hard it felt like it might leap from her chest.

Zachary Hudson stood before her, a faint, mocking smile playing on his lips.

His gaze burned into her.

“Do you love him?”

Vivian froze.

She hadn’t expected him to ask that question so abruptly.

“What did you say?”

Zachary kept his hands in his suit pockets.

His eyes were deep and unreadable.

“I asked if you truly love Liam Sullivan.”

His tone carried an indescribable nuance.

Vivian frowned delicately.

“What exactly are you trying to say?”

“Vivian.” He chuckled softly. “Do you think Liam Sullivan loves you?”

She remained silent.

“You’ve been with him for so long. Haven’t you ever wondered why he chose you in the first place?”

Vivian offered a faint smile.

“I don’t know why he chose me.”

She paused briefly.

“But I know it certainly wasn’t for money or fame.”

“Indeed.” Zachary’s lips curved into a cold smile. “But that doesn’t mean he loves you.”

He looked at her meaningfully.

“Otherwise, why would he still be entangled with other women?”

Vivian’s face paled slightly.

She understood the implication in his words.

“Are you trying to drive a wedge between us?” she said coldly. “I won’t fall for it.”

Zachary let out a light laugh.

“Whether it’s me causing trouble or the truth, you’ll find out soon enough.”

He glanced into the distance.

“Isn’t he on his way to see you right now?”

Vivian pressed her lips together tightly.

Zachary suddenly looked at her deeply.

For a moment, she almost saw a trace of tenderness in his gaze.

“Vivian.” He said quietly. “If I asked you to come back to me, would you reconsider?”

He paused.

“If Liam Sullivan doesn’t love you.”

Vivian didn’t answer.

She had no intention of answering that question.

Soon, a black limousine sped into view.

Liam Sullivan had arrived.

Zachary quietly stepped aside.

A playful smile lingered on his lips.

He was ready to enjoy the show.

To Vivian’s surprise, another car followed closely behind.

Chloe Winston stepped out of the vehicle.

She clearly hadn’t expected Liam to be meeting Vivian.

The moment she saw Vivian, she visibly froze.

Liam got out of his car and walked straight toward Vivian.

He leaned down and placed a kiss on her forehead.

“Have you been waiting long?”

But Vivian’s gaze was fixed on Chloe.

She finally understood the meaning behind Zachary’s earlier words.

This was Liam’s blind date.

The fiance candidate chosen by his grandparents.

Vivian’s heart sank heavily.

She turned to look at Liam, her voice trembling slightly.

“You two…?”

Liam had noticed Chloe following him earlier.

He had intended to lose her.

But then he thought it might be better to let her come.

Chloe walked forward awkwardly.

She stood there, at a loss.
###Chapter 360

She forced a faint smile. "Miss Winston? Please don't misunderstand, I was just..."

Chloe Winston's words trailed off, unspoken.

She couldn't very well say, in front of Vivian Bennett, that she insisted Liam Sullivan take her to dinner as an apology.

Vivian, however, smiled gently at her. "There's been some misunderstanding between you two, hasn't there?"

Chloe froze, staring at her in surprise.

Vivian's smile remained soft. "I'm aware of that little misunderstanding you had with Liam Sullivan before."

Trouble in front, prying eyes behind.

One woman scheming for Liam Sullivan, one man watching her with predatory interest.

The situation was utterly suffocating.

Vivian laughed coldly inside. Was Zachary Hudson hiding in the shadows, waiting for them to lose their composure?

Or was he pitying her right now?

Sensing the woman beside him fall silent, Liam Sullivan frowned and pinched her waist.

He nibbled her earlobe, completely ignoring their audience. "What are you thinking about?"

A sharp pain shot through her waist, but Vivian's expression didn't change.

She tilted her head back to look up at the tall man beside her. "Liam Sullivan, when are we getting married?"

"Hmm?" Liam's eyebrows rose.

Across from them, Chloe's eyes widened. "Married?"

The question came out of nowhere.

No one expected Vivian to bring up marriage at that moment.

Vivian smiled brightly, actively linking her arm through Liam's and leaning against him.

She gazed up at him with a charming smile, her eyes reflecting only him.

"Shall we get married?"

He had always been the one to bring up marriage, while she consistently avoided it.

This time, she was the one initiating it, her eyes filled with anticipation.

Liam looked at her deeply.

Suddenly, the corner of his mouth lifted. He gave only one word in reply.

"Okay."

Chloe's face turned deathly pale. Watching their interaction, her expression grew extremely uncomfortable.

Vivian tightened her hold on Liam's arm, openly displaying their intimacy.

Sometimes, a small action could eliminate a great deal of trouble.

Chloe took it all in, her face growing increasingly unpleasant.

Vivian chose that moment to look at her, asking with a light smile, "Miss Winston, regarding that previous misunderstanding between you and him, as his fiancée, I apologize on his behalf. I'm truly sorry."

Truthfully, given Vivian's nature, she preferred to coldly observe the women swarming around Liam Sullivan.

But this time was different.

She didn't want to give the satisfaction to the person watching from the shadows.

She disliked even more having a woman pestering Liam Sullivan.

Chloe's mind went blank, completely unable to process the situation.

She had known all along that Vivian and Liam were a couple. She understood her own place.

But hearing them discuss their wedding date still left her with an indescribable ache in her heart.
###Chapter 361

Chloe Winston forced a smile, her cheeks flushing slightly.

She waved her hands hastily. "It's fine, really. Just a small matter. Don't worry about it."

For some reason, the earlier insistence that Liam Sullivan apologize and treat her to a meal had completely vanished.

She suddenly felt terribly out of place standing there.

Chloe gave an awkward laugh. "Sorry to disturb you. I just remembered I have something to do. I should go."

She nodded quickly and turned to leave in a hurry.

Standing before Vivian Bennett and Liam Sullivan, she felt nothing but nervousness and confusion. She couldn't even form coherent sentences and didn't want to stay a moment longer.

Watching Chloe hurriedly get into her car and drive away, Vivian sighed inwardly.

She suddenly realized Chloe might have feelings for Liam.

But other people's feelings for Liam were none of her concern.

She believed that after hearing their marriage news, Chloe wouldn't bother Liam over their previous minor conflict anymore.

The feeling of crushing someone's budding affection was indescribably complex.

The moment Chloe left, Liam immediately dropped his cooperative demeanor.

He lifted Vivian's chin, raising an eyebrow with a light laugh. "Care to explain? When did you decide to marry me?"

She had never been this straightforward before.

Vivian met his gaze, her eyes curving into smiles. "Just now."

Liam's eyebrows rose even higher. "What made you suddenly decide?"

Vivian feigned mystery. "Guess?"

Liam didn't hesitate. "I won't."

Even without guessing, he knew the reason.

Perhaps he should thank Chloe and that man over there?

Liam glanced at Zachary Hudson's parked car, a faint smile touching his lips.

Without their pressure, she probably wouldn't have agreed to marry him so easily.

Knowing Zachary was still nearby, Vivian didn't linger. She soon left with Liam.

After they departed, Zachary slowly emerged.

His hands were clenched tightly, his eyes shadowed with gloom.

She was actually going to marry Liam Sullivan...

He never expected she would choose him, just like Wanwan had.

Zachary turned abruptly and slammed his fist hard against the wall.

His eyes were filled with dark fury, his face tense, with no outlet for the frustration coiling in his heart.

Returning to Emerald Estate, Liam changed his shoes and entered the house, his mood noticeably good.

His expression was normal, but the slight curve at the corner of his mouth betrayed his true feelings.

Vivian went to the kitchen and made a bowl of noodles for Liam, who hadn't eaten dinner, then called him to the living room.

Watching Liam elegantly eat his noodles while flipping through a magazine, Vivian couldn't help asking, "What are you looking at?"

Liam looked up at her. After a few seconds, he pushed the magazine toward her. "Which style do you like?"

Vivian looked down and saw it was a bridal magazine.

He was actually looking at this?
###Chapter 362

Vivian Bennett rested her chin in her hands, her gaze playful as she glanced at him.
"Mr. Sullivan, it hasn't even been two hours since you proposed.
Is there really such a rush?"

For once, Liam Sullivan didn't ignore her question.
He set down his fork and knife, his expression serious.
"This matter requires careful planning."

Vivian laughed at his solemn demeanor.
"What kind of planning?"

"At the very least, we must visit your parents first."

A matter like marriage required both families to meet.

Vivian's smile tightened slightly.
"They live elsewhere.
I haven't been in contact with them for years.
I'm not even sure if their address has changed..."

Liam frowned.

Vivian deliberately lightened the mood, changing the subject.
"Shouldn't we consider your family's opinion first?
After all, marriage isn't just about the two of us."

Liam was unconcerned.
His parents would no longer object.
The matter was already settled.

Still, they needed to be notified.
It was better to let Vivian handle the troublesome details.

"Oh," Vivian suddenly remembered something.
"Should we personally tell your sister, Isabella Sullivan?"

She was unsure.

Liam responded lazily.
"Yes."

"But I'm not close to her.
What if she..."

Vivian worried that anyone in the Sullivan family might not accept her.

Since they were getting married, she hoped to get along well with everyone.

Liam chuckled at her anxious expression.
"You're worrying so much before even entering the family.
What will you do later?"

"..."

"Don't worry.
She won't have time to bother with you.
Just focus your attention on me."

"..."

Vivian was left speechless.

Late at night.

After his shower, Liam picked up his phone and made a call.

Oliver Winston's voice came through.
"What is it?"

Liam was brief and to the point.
"I'm getting married."

Oliver was silent for a few seconds.
He asked tentatively，"What does that have to do with me?"

"I just felt like telling you."

"..."

The call ended abruptly.
Liam didn't mind.
He tossed his phone aside casually.

When Vivian emerged from her shower, Liam was leaning against the headboard.
A laptop rested on his legs, his slender fingers typing on the keyboard.

Hearing her footsteps, he quickly sent an email and closed the computer.

Vivian approached.
"Still working?"

"No," Liam brushed it off lightly.
"Just some personal matters."

Vivian didn't press further.
After applying her skincare products and drying her hair, she slipped into bed.

Liam turned off the bedside lamp, plunging the room into darkness.
###Chapter 363

Liam Sullivan picked Vivian Bennett up from work.

The atmosphere in the car was heavy.

He remained silent the entire drive, his brow deeply furrowed.

Vivian turned to look at him.

His jaw was tight, his expression grave.

They arrived at Emerald Estate.

Vivian got out of the car and turned to face him.

Liam walked toward her, his face unreadable.

She asked softly, "Is something wrong at the company?"

His voice was flat. "Why do you ask?"

"You've been frowning this whole time."

She reached out, her fingertips gently smoothing his forehead.

"Don't frown so much."

She smiled. "You'll get wrinkles."

Liam's eyes darkened.

He stared at her face, his gaze complex.

Vivian was about to speak.

But he said, "Let's go inside first."

His tone was distant.

Vivian frowned. "What's really going on with you?"

Liam leaned against the car.

He pulled out a cigarette case and lit one.

Vivian reached over and took it from him.

"Stop smoking."

She said seriously, "Smoking won't solve anything."

Liam watched her.

He finally put the cigarette case away.

"You win." He pinched her cheek.

But his eyes remained deep and troubled.

He turned and walked toward the villa.

Vivian followed, linking her arm with his.

"What happened?"

Liam remained silent.

That night.

The bedroom was dimly lit.

Liam suddenly grasped her hand.

"We're going to the family estate tomorrow."

Vivian was startled.

"To see your family?"

"Yes."

She did not object.

The next day.

The car sped along the highway.

Patriarch Sullivan called.

Liam gave short, clipped answers.

Vivian looked out the window.

She said softly, "Is it too late to back out now?"

Liam's fingers stilled.

He turned to look at her, his gaze intense.

"You're on my ship now. There's no escaping."
###Chapter 364

Liam Sullivan's lips curved slightly.

He turned his head to gaze forward, his eyes deep like ancient pools.

The car entered the Sullivan Family Estate.

The air grew heavy.

Liam led Vivian Bennett into the living room.

His intention was clear.

The elders maintained a polite distance from Vivian.

They neither accepted nor rejected her outright.

Patriarch Sullivan saw his long-absent grandson.

His face darkened immediately.

"Do you still remember this family?"

Liam's expression remained cool.

"I've been busy with work lately."

The Patriarch's chest tightened at his detached demeanor.

"How busy could you be? I once held that position myself."

"How many subsidiaries did Sullivan Group have when you retired?"

The Patriarch's face turned ashen.

Isabella Sullivan rushed back to the estate.

She found her grandfather clutching his chest and gasping for air.

She hurried to support him.

"Grandfather, what's wrong?"

Seeing his granddaughter, his expression softened.

"Just my old condition. It's nothing."

She spoke gently while helping him sit.

"Your blood pressure is high. You mustn't get so upset."

The Patriarch finally smiled at her obedient demeanor.

Dinner was about to begin.

Albert Sullivan entered slowly.

He lived at the estate but kept to himself.

Liam introduced Vivian to his uncle.

Albert gave her a cold look.

"Which prominent family does Miss Bennett come from?"

Silence filled the room.

Vivian met his gaze directly.

"I'm not from a prominent family."

Albert's face hardened instantly.

"If you're not from a good family, how are you worthy of Liam?"

Vivian's fingers trembled slightly.

Albert's tone turned mocking.

"Liam's previous matches were all society daughters."

"Now he chooses a woman with no background."

The words were brutally harsh.

Vivian paled.

Liam's eyes turned icy.

The atmosphere around him dropped sharply.

"Vivian is the woman I've chosen as my wife."

His voice was low and resolute.

It held undeniable certainty.

Isabella lowered her eyes to hide her jealousy.

Her brother was completely bewitched by this woman.

Now he had actually brought her to the Sullivan home.
###Chapter 365

After lunch, Patriarch Sullivan called Liam into the study for a private talk.

Vivian Bennett sat alone in the living room, idly flipping through a magazine.

Isabella Sullivan approached with a bright smile, taking Vivian's hand affectionately.

"Vivian, let me show you something wonderful."

Vivian instinctively tried to pull her hand back, but Isabella held it tightly.

"My uncle just bought a little black puppy. It's absolutely adorable, out in the back garden."

Vivian frowned slightly, sensing something unusual about Isabella's enthusiasm.

But being at the Sullivan Estate, she felt it was impolite to refuse directly.

She reluctantly nodded. "Alright."

They walked side by side toward the back garden.

Passing through lush greenery, a massive glass conservatory suddenly came into view.

Inside, a hundred flowers bloomed in vibrant competition, full of spring vitality that starkly contrasted with the season outside.

"Wow, when did Grandfather build such a beautiful conservatory?"

Isabella exclaimed with delight, running inside. Vivian followed at a slower pace.

A rich floral fragrance washed over them. Vivian bent down to gently smell a rose.

Suddenly, a dark shadow burst from the flower beds, lunging directly at her face.

Vivian screamed, stumbling and falling to the ground in a panic.

It was a half-person-high black Tibetan Mastiff, baring its fangs as it prepared to attack again.

She fought desperately against the vicious dog, her sleeves tearing under its sharp teeth.

Isabella stood far away by the conservatory entrance, a cold smirk curling her lips.

Vivian struggled frantically, her hand brushing against a ceramic flowerpot nearby.

Summoning all her strength, she smashed the pot hard against the mastiff's head.

The dog whimpered twice, then went limp and stopped moving.

Isabella's expression changed abruptly. She suddenly screamed at the top of her lungs. "Help!"

The sound of hurried footsteps approached as servants rushed to the scene.

Albert Sullivan and his wife, along with Eleanor Sullivan, also arrived quickly.

Eleanor gasped sharply at the sight of Vivian's disheveled state.

The girl's sleeves were shredded. Blood streaked her face and hands. Her whole body trembled uncontrollably.

"Get up quickly, are you badly hurt?" The elderly woman hurriedly supported her.

Vivian shook her head weakly, all her energy drained from the struggle.

Albert noticed his beloved dog lying motionless on the ground. Rage filled his eyes.

"You killed my Shadow!" He lunged at Vivian like a madman.

Patriarch Sullivan arrived just in time, striking his brother across the back with his cane.

Albert seemed not to feel it. His hands closed tightly around Vivian's neck.

Vivian instantly choked, her vision blurring and darkening.
###Chapter 366

Albert Sullivan's eyes were blood red.

He choked Vivian Bennett's neck with a death grip.

Sylvia Sullivan and Eleanor Sullivan desperately pulled at his arms.

Their efforts could not loosen his hold.

Vivian's face turned blue.

Her breathing grew faint.

Patriarch Sullivan raised his cane.

He struck Albert hard on the shoulder.

Albert cried out in pain and released his grip.

Vivian collapsed to the floor like a fallen leaf.

He lunged forward again, screaming wildly.

"You killed Shadow! I want your life!"

His fingers brushed against the bloodstained jade pendant at her neck.

He froze abruptly.

Albert knelt on the ground, dazed.

His eyes cleared, regaining their sanity.

He looked at Vivian, who was barely alive.

His hands trembled as he withdrew them.

Everyone breathed a sigh of relief.

Yet confusion filled their hearts.

"Call an ambulance!" Eleanor shouted urgently.

She knelt down and pressed hard on Vivian's philtrum.

Vivian's eyes slowly opened.

Her gaze was unfocused.

The ambulance was taking too long.

Eleanor ordered the driver to prepare the car.

Servants carefully carried Vivian into the vehicle.

Eleanor followed closely behind.

The car sped away.

Sylvia understood what the two dogs meant to her husband.

Over the years, they had filled the void of not having children.

Albert treated Shadow and Snowball like his own sons.

Six years of devoted care had forged a bond as strong as blood.

Shadow's death had completely shattered him.

Sylvia understood his loss of control.

But why had the gentle Shadow suddenly attacked Vivian?

A dog that had never harmed anyone in six years, how could it suddenly turn vicious?

She looked around the devastated greenhouse.

She imagined the horror of what had happened.

Patriarch Sullivan instructed the servants to clean up the scene.

He noticed Isabella Sullivan trembling in the corner.

"It's alright now," the old man comforted gently.

Isabella suddenly burst into tears.

"It's all my fault for being too cowardly..."

"If I had been braver, Miss Vivian wouldn't have been hurt..."

"But Shadow was so terrifying then, I didn't dare to step forward..."
###Chapter 367

Isabella Sullivan’s eyes welled with tears.
Her voice trembled softly.
Her slender shoulders shook slightly.
She looked like a frightened doe.

“Grandpa, I was so scared…”
She sobbed and lowered her head.
Her fingers unconsciously twisted the hem of her dress.

Patriarch Sullivan patted her back with a heavy heart.
“Silly child, how could this be your fault?”
His tone was gentle, but a flicker of relief passed through his eyes.
He was grateful his precious granddaughter wasn’t the one injured.

Sylvia Sullivan quickly handed her a tissue.
She looked at Isabella’s pale face and spoke softly.
“Anyone would be terrified in that situation.”

Isabella took the tissue and dabbed her eyes.
She lowered her gaze to hide her inner satisfaction.
The dog’s frenzy had been frightening.
But she wished Vivian had been bitten more severely.
This outcome felt too lenient.

The crowd gradually dispersed.
Albert Sullivan remained kneeling on the ground.
He stared blankly at Shadow.
He didn’t understand why he had suddenly dropped to his knees.
When the blood-stained jade flashed through his mind, he felt possessed.
A splitting headache overwhelmed him.
His thoughts were in chaos.

The hospital smelled of disinfectant.
Vivian Bennett lay on the hospital bed.
A sharp pain throbbed in her neck.
Nausea washed over her, leaving her face pale.

The doctor carefully treated her wounds.
Her right hand required six stitches.
Each stitch sent a jolt of pain through her nerves.

Eleanor Sullivan insisted on imported vaccines.
She looked at Vivian’s pale face with deep guilt.
This was the first girl Liam had brought home.
And it had ended in disaster.
The memory of Albert’s madness still haunted her.
This matter would not be easily resolved.

She sighed softly and noticed a familiar figure outside the door.
“Vivian, I need to buy something.”
Eleanor hurriedly left the room.

Two minutes later, the door swung open violently.
Liam Sullivan stood at the entrance.
His expression was dark and terrifying.

He had been discussing business with his grandfather.
An urgent call forced him to leave abruptly.
He had specifically asked his grandmother to watch over Vivian.
He never expected such an accident to occur.

When the call came from the family estate, he nearly crushed his phone.
He ran every red light rushing to the hospital.
Seeing his grandmother guarding the room brought him some relief.
But the sight of Vivian’s pale face shattered his heart.
###Chapter 368

Liam Sullivan watched Vivian Bennett's carefree smile.

Rage instantly surged within his chest.

This woman had nearly been disfigured, yet she could still laugh.

He wanted to strangle her.

His hands clenched into fists.

He turned to find the doctor.

The doctor said her right wrist was bitten by the dog, requiring six stitches. The rest were scratches.

Fortunately, her winter clothes were thick.

If it had been summer, the consequences would have been unthinkable.

He couldn't imagine how his delicate and soft wife found the strength to fight a mad dog.

If she had been too scared to fight back...

If she hadn't picked up that flowerpot and hit the dog's head...

A wave of fear washed over him, leaving his entire body cold.

He stood still, glaring coldly at Vivian.

His pupils contracted tightly.

His lips were pressed into a thin line.

After a long silence, he finally spoke through gritted teeth. "Vivian Bennett, you're something else."

His voice was filled with fury.

Vivian trembled in fear.

She extended her uninjured hand, pitifully tugging at the hem of his shirt.

Liam's face was dark and stormy.

He ruthlessly pushed her hand away.

Vivian knew he was truly angry.

She pouted in grievance.

Her eyes darted around.

She whispered in a coaxing tone, "Liam, it hurts so much."

Liam immediately grew tense.

But seeing her cunning expression, he said angrily, "Vivian Bennett, I hope it hurts you to death."

"Otherwise, don't expect me to forgive you."

Vivian's nose stung, and she began to cry.

"Liam, I thought I was going to die back then."

"But then I thought of you."

"I couldn't bear to leave you."

"So I fought with it desperately..."

"I worked so hard to survive."

"And you treat me like this..."

Remembering that heart-stopping scene, she still felt lingering fear.

If she hadn't found that flowerpot, she might have been killed by that black dog resembling a Tibetan Mastiff.

Listening to her sobbing account, Liam's heart wrenched painfully.

He secured her wrist receiving the IV drip.

He lifted the blanket.

He delivered several hard slaps to her bottom.

His voice was stern and icy. "Tell me, what did you promise me when we returned to the family estate?"

Vivian was shocked by his actions.

Recalling her promise that night, she knew she was in the wrong.

She bit her lip and remained silent.

Seeing her stubbornness, Liam slapped her again, even harder.

The pain and grievance overwhelmed Vivian.

Her parents had never spanked her like this since she was a child.

She immediately burst into loud sobs.
###Chapter 369

Liam Sullivan's slap wasn't actually that hard.

Compared to the excruciating pain from the dog bite, it was insignificant.

But Vivian Bennett couldn't stop herself from crying.

Tears fell like broken strings of pearls, flowing more and more fiercely.

Liam watched her with a cold gaze, but his tone softened unconsciously. "Vivian Bennett, if your face gets ruined, I won't even look at you again."

Vivian sobbed, indignant. "Liam Sullivan, are you really that shallow? Besides this face, is there nothing inside me that attracts you?"

Liam stared at her reddened eyes, tears still streaking her cheeks.

He hardened his heart and sneered coldly. "Besides your passable looks, all you do is eat and sleep. You're foolish and disobedient. What inner appeal could you possibly have?"

Vivian's fragile heart took a heavy blow.

Was she really that worthless?

The more she thought about it, the more wronged she felt.

Liam let her sulk, neither comforting nor acknowledging her.

Vivian lay alone, fuming, and eventually fell asleep without realizing it.

She only woke up, groaning softly, when the nurse came to remove the IV.

In her groggy state, she saw Liam holding her hand tightly, his gaze intense.

He looked as tense as if he were the one having the needle removed.

She couldn't help but smile faintly.

Concerned about the injury on her wrist, Liam insisted she stay in the hospital for observation.

Eleanor Sullivan never returned after leaving.

Vivian asked, puzzled, "Didn't Grandma say she was going to buy something? Why is it taking so long?"

Liam's gaze was profound as he spoke lightly. "She had something to attend to and went back first."

Vivian was startled. "She saw you come in?"

"No, she spoke with the nurses and asked them to take good care of you." Liam said it so seriously it was hard not to believe him.

Vivian was half-convinced, feeling that something was off.

But she didn't have the energy to investigate further now.

Seeing her in pain, Liam felt both angry and frustrated.

Before returning to the estate, he had repeatedly warned her not to trust anyone, especially to stay away from Isabella Sullivan.

Yet she had forgotten everything and ended up hurting herself like this.

Seeing his still-dark expression, Vivian didn't dare make a fuss no matter how much it hurt.

Liam had his subordinate order takeout.

Suppressing his anger, he fed her bite by bite.

At first, Vivian was a bit shy and ate reservedly.

Perhaps she was truly hungry, as she ended up eating more than Liam.

Watching her carefree demeanor, Liam was furious but helpless.

Worried that today's events would leave her traumatized about the Sullivan Estate, he decided to stay with her in the hospital overnight for observation.
###Chapter 370

The next morning, Liam Sullivan brought Vivian Bennett back to the villa.

He cleared his entire schedule to personally care for her.

His movements were meticulous and gentle, even testing the temperature of her medicine before feeding it to her.

Vivian watched his focused profile, warmth flooding her heart.

This thoughtful care was more considerate than even family could provide.

Isabella Sullivan learned about her discharge and came to visit.

She carried a fruit basket with a concerned expression on her face.

Her voice choked up when she mentioned not being able to save her in time that day.

"Vivian, I regret being so terrified in that moment..."

Tears streamed down her face as she spoke.

Vivian comforted her softly. "Anyone would be scared in that situation."

Though she said this, doubts lingered in her heart.

The incident that day was too strange.

The black dog had clearly been charging toward Isabella. Why did it suddenly turn toward her?

These questions pricked at her heart like thorns, but she kept them to herself.

Late at night, Vivian was sound asleep when she felt the mattress dip.

She opened her eyes alertly, meeting Liam's intense gaze.

The bedside lamp cast a warm glow around him, softening his sharp features.

She blinked in surprise. "When did you get back?"

Liam smiled at her sleepy appearance.

Even with her injuries, she looked utterly adorable to him.

He carefully pulled her into his arms, avoiding all her wounds.

He kissed her lips, gently yet possessively deepening the kiss.

The kiss lasted until they were both breathless.

Liam pressed his forehead against hers, his voice husky. "When you're better, you'll compensate me double."

He suppressed his urges, simply holding her tightly.

Vivian understood his implication, her cheeks burning instantly.

Liam got up to wash his hands, returning with a tube of ointment.

He applied it gently to her wounds with a cotton swab.

Vivian winced in pain, but he continued without pause.

"What is this medicine?"

"Oliver Winston's special scar removal ointment. It's very effective."

Vivian asked quietly, "If I have scars, will you dislike me?"

Liam paused, amusement flashing in his eyes.

So she had been worrying about this all along.

He ruffled her hair. "I like you just the way you are. Even if you were ugly, I'd still want you."

His blunt words were more moving than any sweet nothing.

Vivian's heart felt sweet, as if soaked in honey.

She leaned into his embrace, savoring his comforting warmth.

The night deepened as they fell asleep in each other's arms.
###Chapter 371

The first light of dawn filtered through the windows.

Vivian Bennett awoke slowly.

The space beside her in the bed was already empty.

Her fingertips brushed against the pillow, still warm.

Only then was she certain last night had not been a dream.

Liam Sullivan's scent still lingered in the air.

But he was gone.

She pushed herself up to sit.

She squeezed toothpaste onto her brush with her clumsy left hand.

The mirror reflected the scabbed wound on her face.

The doctor had warned her to keep the injury dry.

She puffed her cheeks in frustration.

She made a funny face at her reflection.

A week later, the day to remove the stitches finally arrived.

Grace Harper somehow found out.

She rushed over in a flurry.

The moment she saw the wound on Vivian's face, her eyes welled up.

"How did you get hurt so badly?" Grace's voice trembled.

Vivian forced a light laugh. "It's fine now. Really."

Grace's expression suddenly turned serious. "Where is Liam Sullivan? Why did he hide this from me?"

"I asked him not to tell you," Vivian explained softly. "I didn't want you to worry."

Grace looked deeply concerned. "Tell me the truth. Are you unhappy with him?"

Vivian linked her arm through Grace's. "He's been personally taking care of me all week. Please don't worry."

Grace seemed slightly reassured.

She held Vivian's hand. "Come stay with me for a few days once you're healed."

"Okay," Vivian agreed with a smile. "I'll definitely come stay with you."

After seeing her cousin out, the Sullivan Family Estate suddenly descended into chaos.

Isabella Sullivan went for a walk in the garden after work.

An unexpected incident occurred.

Albert Sullivan's white dog suddenly went mad.

It lunged at her and began biting.

Servants rushed over at the sounds.

They were frozen in horror by the scene.

Albert Sullivan hurried forward to intervene.

The dog even bit its own master.

Patriarch Sullivan made a swift decision.

He ordered the mad dog to be put down.

Isabella lay collapsed on the ground, her face covered in blood.

She stared blankly into space.

Albert's arms were covered in deep wounds.

Blood soaked through his sleeves.

The wail of an ambulance shattered the estate's peace.

Eleanor Sullivan spoke to Liam with a fearful sigh. "A dog we raised for six years just went mad. It bit Vivian first, and now this…"

An uneasy atmosphere filled the estate.

Even though the mad dog had been dealt with, fear continued to spread through everyone's hearts.
###Chapter 372

Patriarch Sullivan entered the hospital room leaning on his cane.

His gaze was heavy as he looked at Albert Sullivan lying in bed.

Without a word, he raised his cane and struck Albert.

"You useless thing! Raising two mad dogs that threw the whole family into chaos!"

Albert's arms and hands were wrapped in bandages.

He couldn't block the old man's cane.

He jumped off the bed abruptly.

His face darkened with fury.

"My dogs never caused any trouble in six or seven years! Someone must have tampered with them this time!"

The Patriarch's eyes sharpened instantly.

"Are you telling the truth?"

Albert winced in pain.

His voice turned icy cold.

"I swear on my life! When I find out who did this, I'll make them pay with their life!"

Thinking of his two beloved dogs dying so tragically.

His eyes reddened instantly.

Seeing him like this, the Patriarch felt both angry and helpless.

"Stay in the hospital quietly! If you cause more trouble, don't bother coming back to the Sullivan family!"

He left trembling, leaning on his cane.

Sylvia Sullivan spoke softly from the couch.

"I also think the dogs' deaths were suspicious. But who in the Sullivan family would do such a thing?"

Albert's expression suddenly turned sinister.

After a moment of silence, he stood up abruptly.

"Honey, we're checking out right now!"

Sylvia quickly grabbed his arm.

"Are you crazy? How can you leave the hospital when you're still injured?"

Albert felt as if his heart were being torn apart.

His beloved dogs of many years had died under mysterious circumstances.

How could he lie quietly in the hospital?

In the end, Sylvia couldn't persuade him otherwise.

She accompanied Albert back to the Sullivan Family Estate.

As soon as they entered, they saw Animal Control workers preparing to remove the dogs' bodies.

Albert rushed over frantically.

"Who gave you permission to touch my dogs!"

The workers were startled by his outburst.

They quickly explained, "We're under orders to take them back for autopsy."

Albert stood frozen for a moment.

Then he blocked their path.

He absolutely refused to let them take the dogs away.

Hearing the commotion, the Patriarch walked out from the main hall.

He watched Albert's outburst with a deep frown.

"Haven't you embarrassed us enough? Do you need to make this known to everyone?"

He gave Sylvia a meaningful glance.

Sylvia understood and went to hold her husband's arm.

"Albert, don't you want to know how the dogs really died?"

She watched her husband's expression carefully.

She persuaded gently, "Only an autopsy can clear their names. Do you want them to be remembered as mad dogs for nothing?"

Albert's eyes showed struggle.

His gaze swept over the Animal Control workers.

One of them quickly assured him.

"Mr. Sullivan, don't worry. We'll return them after the examination."
###Chapter 373

Albert Sullivan froze in place.

Sylvia Sullivan had to pull him hard before he stumbled backward.

His gaze remained fixed on the two small dogs.

Staff placed the dogs into white bags.

The sound of zippers was piercing.

Every member of the Sullivan family was under suspicion.

The Patriarch was paranoid.

He would rather kill by mistake than let anyone go.

The autopsy report came out the next day.

Both dogs had been injected with a strange drug.

A specific scent triggered its effects.

When activated, they would viciously attack their target.

Albert trembled all over.

He wanted to skin the culprit with his own hands.

But there was a puzzling point.

Shadow only attacked Vivian Bennett.

Yet Snowball even bit its owner.

He suddenly remembered grabbing at it.

That was why it went mad and attacked.

Who was really behind this?

The Sullivan family was thrown into panic.

Steward found syringes and needles in Isabella Sullivan's room.

The Patriarch did not publicize it.

He instructed the steward to send them for testing quietly.

Animal Control confirmed the drug.

The investigation revealed Isabella had purchased the medication.

The evidence was laid bare.

The Patriarch locked himself in his study for a long time.

Snowball had been injected with an excessive dose.

Isabella's hands and arms were severely mauled.

It would be difficult for them to fully recover.

The scar on her mouth would require plastic surgery.

Albert knew Vivian had set it up.

He wished he could go to the hospital and kill her.

But she had already been sent abroad.

He was furious but powerless.

He moped around all day.

He suddenly fell seriously ill.

Vivian's face did not bear any scars.

She had returned to work long ago.

She had lived at Liam Sullivan's villa for a month.

She never expected Isabella would harm others and end up harming herself.

Isabella was clever.

She might have discovered her relationship with Liam.

But self-sabotage was too extreme.

So cruel and bloody.

Who was secretly helping her get revenge?

Liam noticed Vivian had not returned to the villa.

He felt somewhat annoyed.

But he had social engagements and did not say much.

Late on a cold winter night, the north wind howled.

After his social event ended, he told his driver to go to the gated community where Vivian rented.

A faint light glowed in one of the rooms.

He imagined her lying lazily on the bed.

The corner of his mouth curved upward.

He dialed the number memorized in his heart.

Vivian was sleeping groggily.

The phone by her bed rang.

She fumbled for her phone without looking.

Her voice was muffled. "Hello? Who is this?"
###Chapter 374

Liam Sullivan chuckled softly at the hoarse, drowsy voice on the phone. "Were you asleep?"

His voice was rich like aged red wine, magnetic and laced with unconcealed affection.

Vivian Bennett snapped awake instantly, a smile tugging at her lips. "Is your business dinner over?"

He gazed up at her window, his tone even. "Not yet."

"What kind of dinner goes this late?" Her soft voice felt like a feather brushing against his heart.

Liam's Adam's apple bobbed. He suddenly wanted to crush her into his embrace. "I'm downstairs. Come out."

Her heart skipped a beat. She ran barefoot to the window.

The black Maybach stood out under the streetlights. She hastily ended the call, grabbed a coat, and slipped downstairs quietly.

Liam stood in the night breeze in his black suit, tall and imposing yet radiating loneliness.

Seeing her running figure, he opened his arms and caught her firmly.

Vivian shivered slightly from the chill he carried, catching the strong scent of alcohol. "Why didn't you call me? It's so cold out here."

Liam stared at her clean, makeup-free face, his breathing suddenly growing heavy.

He pulled the car door open, his voice low. "Get in first."

The driver tactfully stepped out for a smoke.

The interior fell silent instantly.

Liam pressed her against the car door. One hand gripped her waist, the other cupped her cheek. He lowered his head and captured her soft lips.

Since she moved back, he had been swamped, barely finding time to even look at her properly.

All his longing now poured into this fervent kiss.

The temperature inside the cramped space soared suddenly.

Liam's hand slipped inside her pajamas, desperate to meld her into his very bones.

"Come home with me," he rasped, his voice thick with desire.

Before Vivian could process it, the driver returned to his seat.

Half an hour later, the car glided into Emerald Estate.

Vivian looked at the familiar villa, her heart rippling with emotion.

Liam led her inside. The late-night community was empty.

He suddenly pinned her against the wall and kissed her again.

Vivian pushed against him in panic. "There are cameras... don't..."

Liam buried his face in the crook of her neck, his breath scalding. "I can't help it."

He swept her up into his arms and strode toward the villa.

The electronic lock beeped open. He carried her across the threshold.

Their passionate entanglement spread from the entrance all the way to the bedroom.

Though he didn't stay here often, the place was always kept immaculate.

The thirty-minute drive from Lakeside Manor to Emerald Estate had unleashed all his pent-up longing in this moment.
###Chapter 375

The night was deep, emotions surged.

They hadn't been this intimate for a long time, and both were completely immersed in the moment.

Liam Sullivan was like a starving wolf, unleashing himself freely in the silent night.

He was relentless and tireless.

Vivian Bennett felt as if she was about to fall apart.

No matter how she pleaded, he refused to stop.

Finally, exhausted, she fell into a deep sleep.

Liam, satisfied, carried her to the bathroom.

He carefully cleaned her and changed the bedsheets.

They fell asleep in each other's arms, sleeping soundly.

The peace was broken by a ringing phone.

Liam opened his eyes and glanced at Vivian in his arms.

He got up quietly, put on his robe, and walked out of the bedroom.

"President, the routine meeting is already half an hour late." Ryan Johnson's voice was anxious.

"Postpone it to ten-thirty." Liam hung up decisively.

Ryan waited anxiously in the underground parking lot.

When he saw Liam carrying Vivian, his jaw nearly dropped.

Although it wasn't the first time the President had brought Miss Bennett to the company, this time was particularly conspicuous.

Ryan muttered to himself: Is it really okay to show off like this?

But he was happy for the President.

Vivian curled up in Liam's arms, sleeping soundly.

Her porcelain face, with fluttering eyelashes, looked like a delicate doll.

Ryan was mesmerized for a moment.

Liam kicked him displeased.

Ryan snapped back to reality and hurriedly pressed the elevator button.

The atmosphere in the small elevator was oppressive.

Liam's face was dark, his gaze sharp as a knife.

Ryan shrank in the corner, not daring to breathe.

He had only looked a little longer. Was it necessary to be so possessive?

...

When Vivian woke up, she found herself in the President's restroom.

She was stunned for a few seconds before remembering last night's events.

She felt both anxious and angry.

She quickly washed up, changed clothes, and prepared to call home.

Just as she picked up the landline receiver, she heard movement outside the door.

Startled, she quickly put down the phone and hid back in the restroom.

This sneaking around feeling drove her crazy.

Holding her breath, she pressed her ear against the door.

"Does your President often stay here?" A deep male voice faintly came through.

Vivian's heart skipped a beat.

Why did this voice sound so familiar?

Just as she was puzzled, the footsteps grew closer.

She hurriedly hid in the bathroom.

Just as she closed the door, someone outside tried to open the restroom door.

Ryan was terrified, breaking out in a cold sweat.

"Second Master, the President is very particular. He doesn't like others entering his restroom."

Albert Sullivan glanced suspiciously at the locked door, but ultimately let go.
###Chapter 376

Vivian Bennett nearly collapsed onto the cold tiles of the restroom.

Her heart was pounding wildly.

She clamped a hand tightly over her mouth to stifle a scream.

Thankfully, she had locked the door earlier.

If her uncle had discovered her in Liam Sullivan's private lounge…

She didn't dare imagine the consequences.

Why was Albert Sullivan suddenly at the company?

Just recently, he had been lying critically ill in a hospital bed.

Had he recovered so quickly?

She tiptoed back to the bed and lay down again.

She pulled the covers over her head.

Her mind was filled with dread about her relationship with Liam being exposed.

Just as she was drifting into a drowsy state, the mattress beside her dipped.

Vivian jolted awake.

Liam was sitting on the edge of the bed, impeccably dressed in his suit.

She gasped in fright. "Why don't you make any sound when you walk?"

The man chuckled, his voice deep and rich. "Scared by my grandfather and uncle?"

He pulled her, blankets and all, into his arms.

Vivian let out a muffled groan.

She had been thoroughly frightened.

She looked up at him. "What were they doing here?"

Liam smoothed her hair. "Uncle's health is failing. Grandfather plans to have someone else take over the resort. He wants him to return to Seattle to recuperate."

Vivian's fingers twitched.

Albert Sullivan had always been marginalized within the Sullivan family.

Having finally gained some real authority, how could he possibly let it go so easily?

Was his condition truly that severe?

Liam scooped her up into his arms. "Go freshen up first. The food will get cold."

Vivian struggled out of his hold and padded into the bathroom, rubbing her eyes.

When she emerged, a set of exquisite meal containers were arranged on the coffee table.

A sudden thought made her face pale. "I haven't asked Manager Thomas for leave yet!"

Liam continued serving food without pausing. "I requested it for you this morning."

Vivian's eyes widened. "What did you tell him?"

"The truth." The man raised an eyebrow. "Told him you were with me. Taking the day off."

She felt like she might faint.

She had taken an excessive number of leaves recently.

It wasn't surprising Thomas Carter would call to verify.

But Liam's answer—

"Are you insane?" She pinched his arm in panic. "How could you say something like that directly!"

Liam caught her wrist, his gaze darkening. "Vivian, we are getting married."

A hint of disappointment was hidden in his voice.

"Why do you always want to keep us a secret?"

Vivian's heart ached.

She knew what was bothering him.

She leaned over and gently kissed the corner of his mouth. "Just give me a little more time… I need my family to accept this gradually."

After the meal, Ryan Johnson appeared punctually at the office door.

Vivian adjusted her scarf and hurried out of the Sullivan Group building with her head down.
###Chapter 377

Liam Sullivan brought Vivian Bennett to the Sullivan Family Estate for dinner once again.

Vivian had just set down her fork and knife when she looked up and saw Sylvia Sullivan supporting Albert Sullivan as they strolled slowly through the garden.

Since they had crossed paths, she offered a warm smile and greeted them. "Grandma Sylvia, has Grandpa Albert not fully recovered yet?"

Sylvia shook her head with a worried expression. "His illness keeps recurring because of his heartache. Those two dogs meant everything to him. He just can't move on yet. Perhaps with more time, he'll gradually improve."

Vivian studied Albert carefully. Aside from being somewhat thinner, his complexion didn't appear too poor.

She bid them farewell gently. "Grandma Sylvia, please continue your walk. I'll head back inside now."

Sylvia nodded with a smile. But as Vivian turned to leave, Albert suddenly spoke. "Vivian, could you let me see your jade pendant again? I've been dreaming about it lately."

Vivian paused mid-step, her expression troubled.

Sylvia quickly intervened. "That jade is Vivian's keepsake from her mother. She can't just show it to anyone. You've been obsessing over it simply because the jade quality caught your collector's eye."

She then signaled to Vivian with a look. "Hurry back inside. He's been talking nonsense recently. Don't take it to heart."

Vivian left with a smile, but tension coiled inside her.

Albert had always been narrow-minded and far from kind.

Keeping her distance remained the wisest choice.

As the year drew to a close, Liam grew increasingly busy. He often left before dawn and returned late at night.

Some days, Vivian didn't even catch a glimpse of him.

Tonight, she had just settled into bed when her phone vibrated.

Seeing the caller ID, she immediately answered.

"What are you doing? Not asleep yet?" His deep voice traveled through the receiver, making her ears burn.

She hummed lazily. "I've finished all the important tasks. Just some trivial matters remain."

Hearing her soft voice, Liam's lips curved upward. "Working so hard? Do you need my help?"

"What can you even do?" she teased, suddenly feeling sad. "Liam, it feels like forever since I've seen you. I miss you. When will you be done with work?"

A low chuckle came through the phone. "I asked you to come keep me company at the office this morning, but you refused. Now you say you miss me?"

She rolled over in her blankets. "I really do miss you. Should I come find you right now?"

Liam's laughter grew more delighted. "Sure. I'll be waiting."

After hanging up, she threw on her coat, grabbed her bag, and rushed out.

But as she pushed open the front door, she saw a familiar tall figure standing beside his car.

Her heart skipped a beat. She nearly gasped in surprise.

Liam leaned against the car hood in his black overcoat, watching her with smiling eyes.
###Chapter 378

The night was deep and dark.

Streetlights cast a soft glow, outlining a tall, slender figure of a man.

He leaned casually against his car, a cigarette held between his fingers.

In the haze of smoke, his sharply defined features appeared exceptionally handsome.

He was a man of utterly bewitching allure.

Vivian Bennett’s heart skipped a beat.

She rushed toward him without a second thought, throwing herself heavily into his arms.

Liam Sullivan caught her soft form and chuckled deeply. "In such a hurry to throw yourself at me?"

"Who's throwing themselves at you?" Vivian pinched his waist in flustered annoyance. "Weren't you supposed to wait for me? Why are you at my doorstep?"

This man was too infuriating.

He was right downstairs, yet he deliberately kept it from her.

A captivating smile curled at the corner of Liam's lips.

He bent his head close to her ear, his warm breath brushing her cheek. "I missed you."

His deep, husky voice sent shivers through Vivian's entire body.

She leaned into his embrace, her strength to resist completely gone.

Liam simply carried her into the car.

The car sped toward Emerald Estate.

Cool breezes whispered outside the window, but inside the car, it was as warm as spring.

Liam held the steering wheel with one hand and gripped her hand tightly with the other.

Vivian could feel the scorching heat of his palm.

Blushing, she tried to pull away. "Let go first. Focus on driving."

Liam watched her shy, flustered expression, his eyes darkening.

He accelerated, heading straight for their destination.

The moment the car entered the garage, Liam unbuckled his seatbelt and pressed her into the seat.

The temperature inside the car suddenly soared.

Two people who had longed for each other became tightly entangled.

Liam's kisses fell like rain.

The interior was brightly lit.

The vintage Persian rug her mother had loved was gone.

It was replaced by a cold, hard European geometric pattern.

All the furniture had been replaced too.

The rich colors her mother had preferred were now covered in a pale, milky white.

Even the smell in the air had changed.

This house was no longer the home from her memories.

"Please go in. The master must be getting impatient." The servant urged in a low voice.

Vivian took a deep breath.

She finally stepped inside.
###Chapter 381

High ceilings created an airy, open atmosphere.

No arguments tonight.

Just a peaceful meal.

Ever since being cast out of the White family.

Closing the distance between them.

Suppressed sorrow washed over her.

To pave the way for Brandon.

That he wanted to mend their father-daughter relationship.

###Chapter 383

Grace Harper's reply made his expression turn to ice.

"Vivian went on a trip with Sophia Miller.
She should be back in about a week."

The dial tone buzzed in his ear.
The night outside the car window pressed down heavily.

He sat silently in the driver's seat.
Neon lights flickered across his sharply defined profile.

His phone suddenly lit up.

"Tell Pinnacle." His voice was laced with ice.
"I will arrive in half an hour."

Ryan let out a long breath of relief into the phone.

He didn't understand why the CEO had suddenly changed his mind.

But at least he wouldn't have to face that smiling tiger Mr. Jiang alone.
###Chapter 390

Liam Sullivan appeared in the lobby of the five-star hotel shortly after.

His expression was stern, radiating a chilling coldness.

Ryan Johnson quickly approached.

Even from a distance, he could feel the overwhelming low pressure.

Ryan's heart sank.

Could it be because of Miss Vivian again?

No one but Vivian Bennett could cause such emotional turbulence in the CEO.

Liam strode into the private dining room.

The president of Pinnacle Corp immediately rose to greet him. "Mr. Sullivan, your presence is a great honor."

Liam gave a slight nod. "You're too kind, Mr. Johnson."

After the pleasantries, Liam took his seat with ease.

He raised his glass, his gaze sweeping across the room. "I'll drink first. Please, enjoy yourselves at your own pace."

His slender fingers wrapped around the crystal glass, and he drained it in one go.

The strong liquor slid down his throat.

"Mr. Sullivan, you are most generous." Mr. Johnson followed suit, downing his own drink.

Everyone stood up to join the toast.

"To Mr. Sullivan. May our cooperation be pleasant." Mr. Johnson raised his glass again.

Executives from Pinnacle Corp took turns proposing toasts.

Liam drank one glass after another.

The burning liquor scorched his stomach, yet his face remained composed.

A faint, almost imperceptible smile played on his thin lips.

He appeared humble, yet carried an air of detachment.

His innate noble demeanor was intimidating.

Lily Ashcroft tried several times to drink on his behalf, but was refused each time.

She worried his stomach couldn't handle it.

After several rounds, Liam signaled for Ryan to take over.

This subtle gesture warmed Lily's heart.

She gazed obsessively at the man beside her.

The light outlined his sharp, defined profile.

His high nose bridge and thin lips.

Every feature exuded mature charm.

He sat quietly in the halo of light, like a deity.

By the end of the social engagement, Lily's heartbeat had yet to calm.

She timidly stepped forward to support him. "Sir, shall I take you back?"

Liam frowned and shook off her hand. "Unnecessary."

His voice was low and hoarse.

He walked straight to the hotel's executive office.

Oliver Winston raised an eyebrow and smiled upon seeing him. "Meeting with that old stubborn from Pinnacle Corp?"

Liam lazily sank into the sofa, grunting in affirmation.

"Did you have a fight with Vivian?" Oliver probed.

Liam's expression darkened further.

He wished he could drag her back immediately.

In less than five minutes, he stood up to leave.

The door slammed shut with a loud bang.

Oliver shook his head helplessly at the closed door.

Liam called for a designated driver and returned directly to the company.

The next morning, Ryan pushed open the office door.

Liam was sitting at the executive desk, reviewing documents.

The man's face was stern, still wearing the same suit from yesterday.

Before Ryan could speak, a low voice sounded. "What's the schedule for the next few days?"
###Chapter 391

Ryan’s fingers trembled slightly.

He quickly picked up the iPad to report the schedule.

Liam Sullivan’s brows furrowed slightly.

“Postpone all non-essential matters.”

“Book a flight to Zurich for tomorrow evening.”

Ryan was stunned.

“You’re going on a business trip, sir?”

“I haven’t prepared any contingency plans yet…”

Liam stood up and walked toward the lounge.

His suit jacket was casually draped over his arm.

“It’s a private matter.”

“I’ll be going alone.”

Ryan’s mind raced.

Zurich had no ongoing projects or old friends.

Why would the CEO suddenly go there?

He immediately booked the flight.

When Ryan returned to the CEO’s office, Liam had already changed into a fresh shirt.

His cufflinks gleamed with a cold light.

He was reviewing documents with his head lowered.

A metal pen twirled effortlessly between his fingers.

The sharp lines of his profile looked like they were carved from stone.

Ryan quietly retreated and closed the door.

Had the CEO been working relentlessly these past few days just to free up time for Zurich?

What secret was hidden there?

Vivian Bennett and Sophia Miller arrived in Zurich as night fell.

The hotel lobby glittered under crystal chandeliers.

They dropped their luggage and headed straight to the restaurant.

They had just settled by the window when a deep male voice cut through the noise.

“Vivian Bennett?”

She looked up abruptly.

Julian Klein stood at the edge of light and shadow.

His black suit accentuated his tall, straight posture.

His snow-white shirt collar was impeccably neat.

His pale complexion stood out in the crowd.

His noble demeanor drew discreet glances from all around.

Every encounter with him felt like opening a mystery box.

Vivian curved her lips into a smile.

“What a coincidence.”

“Are you here in Zurich too?”

“Just vacationing to clear my head.”

His gaze drifted to the suitcase beside her.

“What about you?”

“The same…”

Her fingers curled unconsciously.

After a brief exchange, Julian led five or six elite-looking attendants toward the private dining area.

Sophia gripped Vivian’s sleeve excitedly.

“That man is pure walking testosterone!”

“Where did you meet him?”

Vivian watched the group’s efficient retreating figures.

“A client.”

“I’ve only met him twice.”

But did a vacation require bringing an entire team?

The next morning, mountain mist still lingered.

Rhododendron blooms covered the entire hillside.

Layers of crimson and violet spread like a spilled palette.

Sophia darted through the flowers with her camera.

Vivian bent down to gently smell the dew-kissed petals.

Tiny droplets clung to her hair.

They didn’t return to the hotel until the sun began to set.

Their legs were sore and weary.

The revolving door slowly turned.

They came face-to-face with Julian Klein.

Petal fragments dusted the shoulders of his trench coat.

It was as if he had just walked out of the same sea of flowers.
###Chapter 392

Julian Klein wore a blue casual tracksuit with a large backpack slung over his shoulder.

His demeanor was completely different from yesterday's efficient and steady presence.
Now he appeared relaxed and free-spirited.

Vivian Bennett's heart skipped a beat when she saw him.
She slowly curved her lips into a smile. "Mr. Klein, what a coincidence to see you again?"

Julian's gaze swept over both women, his lips quirking slightly. "Did you two just come down from the mountain?"

Sophia Miller raised her eyebrows in surprise. "How did you know?"

"Just a guess," Julian said, his deep eyes settling on Vivian.

She wore a purple knitted batwing top with distressed jeans today.
She looked playful and lively.
Her cheeks were flushed from exercise, her lips naturally pink.
Her long hair cascaded loosely, giving her a lazy beauty.

This version of her made Julian's heart inexplicably stir.
That familiar thrill surfaced again, yet he couldn't remember where he had seen her before.

The setting sun's glow illuminated Vivian's delicate profile.
In the interplay of light and shadow, it quietly overlapped with a certain silhouette in his memory.

Julian's voice was low and ethereal. "Vivian Bennett, have we met before?"

A breeze brushed past, lifting the strands of hair by her cheeks.
Vivian gently tucked the stray hairs behind her ear and smiled. "The hotel, the company, and here—that makes three times, right?"

Julian suddenly snapped back to reality.
Suppressing the strange feeling in his heart, he made an awkward invitation. "Since we're so fated, would you like to have dinner together?"

"Thank you for the offer, Mr. Klein," Vivian politely declined. "We're too tired from hiking today and want to rest at the hotel."

She pulled Sophia along as they quickly entered the hotel lobby.

Julian watched their retreating figures and couldn't help shaking his head.
What had been wrong with him lately?
Why did he always feel an indescribable sense of familiarity every time he saw this Vivian Bennett?

Sophia muttered as they walked. "That Mr. Klein was looking at you strangely. Even his pickup line was so cliché. Could he be interested in you?"

Vivian chuckled lightly. "Don't guess wildly. He was just being polite."

Sophia was too tired to argue and merely snorted. "Just be careful anyway."

"Got it," Vivian said, collapsing onto the sofa to massage her aching legs.

Both were too exhausted to move.
They ordered room service for dinner and went to bed early.

They slept straight through until noon the next day.
Due to overexertion, they decided to spend the entire day resting at the hotel.

They curled up in bed watching paid movies until nightfall when they finally regained their energy.

Full of enthusiasm, they headed to Zurich's food street to sample local specialty snacks and browse the specialty exhibition.

......
Liam Sullivan had someone waiting for him as soon as he got off the plane.

The black car sped through the night, blending into the dark sky.

Tree shadows danced along the roadside, dappled light flickering inside the car.
In the shifting light, the man's sharply defined profile appeared even colder.

Liam Sullivan closed his eyes to rest, but an image of Vivian Bennett's smiling face surfaced in his mind.
###Chapter 393

Liam Sullivan's eyes snapped open.

His gaze was dark as ink, calm yet detached.
At least on the surface.

The car sped through the streets.
It took only half an hour from the airport to the hotel.

The car door opened.
Liam stepped out.
He was about to move forward when his peripheral vision caught two women walking and laughing.

His gaze sharpened instantly.
His brow furrowed slightly as he watched Vivian Bennett and Sophia Miller chatting cheerfully.
She wore a light blue polo shirt and faded jeans.
The wind tousled her long hair, and she casually tucked a few stray strands behind her ear.
Revealing her delicate and elegant profile.

He stood rigidly in place.
Watching her radiant smile, all the suppressed torment inside him churned violently.
No amount of restraint could quell it.
As she drew closer, the urge to act nearly overwhelmed him.

His expression remained calm, but a storm was brewing beneath the surface.
His thin lips parted, and he called out in a low voice, "Vivian."

Vivian's smile froze on her face.
She looked up at the man now standing mere steps away.
Liam Sullivan?
Her heart gave a violent jolt.

Why was he here?
Had he come for her?
Vivian's heartbeat accelerated wildly.

Seeing his tall, imposing figure, his innate nobility and oppressive aura.
She almost turned to flee.
She knew that expression all too well.
The calmer he seemed, the more furious he truly was.

Remembering his past actions, Vivian schooled her features and spoke with feigned composure. "What are you doing here?"

Liam's suppressed emotions had long since crumbled.
He couldn't tolerate her cold, distant attitude.
He was instantly provoked.

He strode forward abruptly, wrapping an arm around her waist.
Without allowing any resistance, he hoisted Vivian over his shoulder.

"Ah! You brute! Put me down!" Vivian struggled uncomfortably.

Sophia watched their tense standoff, but ultimately did not intervene.
She watched them disappear from view and shook her head gently.
Liam's possessiveness was too strong.
He had tracked them down after only two days.

Thinking of a certain oblivious person, a faint sense of loss washed over her.

......

Liam carried Vivian straight into the hotel.
The manager, recognizing the guest, approached respectfully. "Mr. Sullivan, your room key."
His expression turned awkward and puzzled upon seeing the woman slung over Liam's shoulder.

Liam ignored the look, took the key card, and entered the elevator.
Vivian burned with humiliation.
Even though it was late, several sets of eyes in the lobby had turned their way.

She fumed and shouted, "Liam Sullivan, put me down, you jerk!"

The man angrily swatted her backside.
His voice was low and stern. "Vivian, shut up. Or I'll do something even more jerkish."

"......"
Vivian's heart sank. She pressed her lips together and fell silent.
###Chapter 394

The elevator doors slid open.

Liam Sullivan hoisted Vivian Bennett over his shoulder.

He strode into the suite.

He tossed her onto the plush bed without a hint of gentleness.

Vivian landed with a dizzying thud.

She struggled to push herself up.

His heavy frame pinned her down instantly.

"Liam Sullivan, let me go!"

She writhed in a mix of fury and fear.

She couldn't break free.

Liam's expression was terrifyingly dark.
