Her fingers trembled, and she nearly dropped her phone.

Was this man actually using social media now?

She hesitated, staring at the screen.
Her phone suddenly rang loudly.

Liam's deep voice pierced her eardrum. "Why haven't you accepted my request?"

Vivian laughed nervously. "I just think you look really handsome when you cook..."

"So you decided to mock me?" The man's voice was icy.

She immediately surrendered. "I'll delete it right now!"

The moment the call ended, she swiftly deleted that post.

The news of White Corporation's acquisition came abruptly.

All employees kept their positions, but the entire senior management was replaced.

Vivian had seen it coming.

White Corporation had been internally corrupt and poorly managed for years.
It was already on the verge of collapse.

Being acquired was the best possible outcome.

It just went against William White's final wishes.

If he knew in the afterlife, he would probably resent her.

Julian Klein completed the acquisition as his father instructed.
His mood, however, was unusually heavy.

He made a special trip to Seattle.
He waited downstairs at Vivian's company.

The moment she walked through the glass doors, his breath caught.

Her face was almost identical to the photo his father treasured in his study.

Could it really just be a coincidence?

He stood there, utterly distraught.
He watched Vivian get into a black Rolls-Royce and drive away.

Heavy rain poured down late at night.

The Sullivan Family Estate was suddenly in chaos.

Albert Sullivan had gone alone to the back garden in the rain.
He had accidentally fallen.

He wasn't discovered by the servants until morning.
He was rushed to the hospital.

Sylvia Sullivan was panicked and terrified.

Her husband's condition had been stable lately.
Why would he go out into the garden in the middle of a storm?
###Chapter 420

She looked at the unconscious man on the hospital bed.
Tears streamed down her face uncontrollably.

Albert Sullivan had been admitted to the hospital with a high fever from being caught in the rain.
During the examination, doctors discovered impact wounds on his head.

Sylvia Sullivan kept silent vigil by his bedside.
She cared for him meticulously without a single word of complaint.
She remained silent even after his fever broke.

One day, while Sylvia was away, Albert slipped out of his hospital room.
He went to the Sullivan Group headquarters wearing his patient gown.

He stood quietly in a corner, watching Vivian Bennett's busy figure.
He suddenly remembered the jade pendant she wore.
"This is a keepsake from my mother." Her gentle voice echoed in his ears.

Albert's mind exploded.
He could no longer maintain his composure.

Returning to his hospital room, he quickly changed clothes.
He sent someone to find the White family's address in the city and immediately drove there.

Standing before the White family villa, Albert's emotions churned.
He took a deep breath and stepped forward to knock on the main door.

A servant opened the door and looked him over. "Who are you looking for, sir?"
Albert smiled nervously. "Is Vivian Bennett home?"

The servant paused. "The young mistress moved out long ago."
"Only the young master and his wife live here now."

"May I see them?" Albert asked.
After a moment's hesitation, the servant let him in.

Albert observed the courtyard layout as he walked.
"This garden is quite distinctive. Who designed it?"

The servant said proudly, "The late mistress designed it."
"It's been kept exactly as she left it."

Albert suddenly froze in place.
The familiar scene awakened long-buried memories.
An identical courtyard appeared in his mind, along with a slender figure...

Brandon White didn't expect to see Albert Sullivan.
He only remembered him as Liam Sullivan's uncle.

"Mr. Sullivan, what brings you here unexpectedly?" Brandon asked.
Albert quickly collected himself, realizing his abruptness.

The moment he saw Vivian, this idea had taken root.
He needed to come to the White residence to confirm certain things.

The courtyard's layout confirmed his suspicions.
Everything was Sophia White's work.

Why had she married Brandon White?
Why had she never looked for him...

Countless questions swirled in his mind.
Albert smiled awkwardly. "I was just passing by and admired your courtyard."
"My apologies for the intrusion."

Brandon saw through it but didn't comment. "Since you're here, join me for tea."
He had a servant brew a pot of excellent tea.
###Chapter 421

Albert Sullivan's gaze drifted around the room.

A flicker of surprise passed through his eyes.

"The design of this house is quite unique."

Brandon White felt a slight stir in his heart.

Was Albert truly drawn here by the design?

A faint curve touched the corner of his lips.

"It was the masterpiece of my sister's mother."

"She was a very talented designer."

"It's a pity she passed away too early."

Albert's memories had only just returned.

He hadn't delved deeply into Sophia White's affairs yet.

Hearing that name felt like a heavy blow to his heart.

Even though he already knew she was gone.

Hearing it firsthand was still hard to bear.

A sour bitterness rose in his throat.

He was momentarily speechless.

Albert stood up abruptly.

"I've disturbed you for today."

"I'll pay another visit another day."

His hurried departure left Brandon deep in thought.

Something felt off about the whole situation.

He immediately ordered his subordinates to investigate.

Early the next morning.

Albert returned to the hospital.

Sylvia Sullivan had been anxiously searching for him.

Seeing him dressed in a sharp suit, she froze.

She reached out to touch his forehead.

"Albert, are you alright?"

Her voice was full of worry.

Albert gently pushed her hand away.

"I'm fine."

"I just went out to take care of some business."

His voice was calm and even.

Facing the wife who had been by his side for over twenty years.

His heart was filled with mixed emotions.

All these years, he had lived absurdly.

Yet she never left his side.

If his memories hadn't returned.

He would have been willing to continue living like that.

But fate played a cruel joke.

Making him remember long-buried past events in his twilight years.

That youthful love from his prime.

Now only brought heart-wrenching pain.

Under his father's interference.

Everything ended in separation between life and death.

The moment his memories returned.

He finally understood so many doubts.

Why his elder brother tried so hard to cover things up.

Why all the hospital diagnoses claimed nothing was wrong.

It was all an elaborately set trap.

Sylvia watched him.

Feeling like he was a completely different person.

Her voice trembled.

"Don't scare me like that again."

"If anything happens to you..."

"How will I go on living?"

Albert showed a gentle smile.

"Don't worry."

"I will spend the rest of my life properly by your side."

But he had to uncover the truth first.

Those vivid memories.

Tormented his heart day and night.

Like a bone stuck in his throat.

He secretly contacted his old associates.

Ordering them to investigate the White family background thoroughly.

Not a single detail could be overlooked.

The operation had to be discreet.

They absolutely must not alert the enemy.
###Chapter 422

The evening sun cast its fading light through the floor-to-ceiling windows.

It filled the CEO's office with a warm glow.

Liam Sullivan sat behind his large desk.

He was focused on reviewing documents.

His sharp profile looked especially deep in the soft light.

Vivian Bennett gently pushed the door open.

She stepped inside quietly.

She softened her footsteps, careful not to disturb him.

She responded to his kiss.

Men were always so domineering.

He settled beside her.

She nestled against his chest, her leg draped over him, breathing in his unique, comforting scent.

Ryan Johnson smiled as he handed over a file, noting the CEO's refreshed appearance.
"The planning department just sent this proposal."

Liam took the file and spoke calmly.
"You can head home for the day if there's nothing else."

Ryan's eyes widened in surprise.
Normally, he wouldn't dare leave before the CEO.

Today was truly unusual.

Ryan hummed a cheerful tune as he walked out.

Upon reaching the secretarial office, he saw Lily Ashcroft sitting there with red-rimmed eyes.

He sighed softly.
"Ms. Ashcroft, why are you still here?"

Lily hurriedly composed herself and forced a smile.
"I was just about to leave."

The elevator was silent except for the two of them.

After a moment of thought, Ryan spoke gently.
"Ms. Ashcroft, if you want to stay by the CEO's side long-term, you must know your place. You've been with him for years. You understand his temperament."

Lily, being clever, smiled awkwardly.
"Thank you for the reminder, Mr. Johnson."

The elevator doors opened.
Ryan whistled cheerfully as he strode away.

If anything were to happen to Ms. Bennett, the CEO would undoubtedly reassign Lily immediately.

Liam was urgently summoned by the elder Sullivans that day.

He thought something serious had happened.

Instead, they had brought Isabella Sullivan back home.

They wanted him to forgive Isabella and drop the matter of her framing Vivian.

Isabella, knowing her position, proactively apologized to Liam.
###Chapter 424

The entire Sullivan family had clearly conspired together.

He was the only one kept in the dark.

Liam was about to marry Vivian Bennett.

XX Club.

Oliver Winston and Ethan Roscente sat in a private room playing cards and chatting.

###Chapter 426

###Chapter 427

"You think you're invincible just because you killed a few ants, Lin Feng?"

Tian Chen's eyes flashed with cold light.

He immediately struck back with a palm aimed at Tian Chen.

Su Wenxiang and Su Yuqing both gasped sharply.

###Chapter 428

###Chapter 429

###Chapter 430

###Chapter 431

Country M was surrounded by ocean.

Vivian Bennett arrived here, her body still weak from recent illness.

She had cut off all contact back home.

She healed alone.

She sought solace by herself.

She no longer thought about Liam Sullivan.

Since he had chosen Isabella Sullivan.

Why should she cling to him?

Prolonged pain was worse than short, sharp agony.

She chose to withdraw voluntarily.

Her phone suddenly rang.

Vivian glanced at the screen.

It was a call from her mentor, Professor Tony Morrison.

Professor Morrison had always held her in high regard.

He had advised her to stay in Country M for her career years ago.

But she had insisted on returning home.

Now, looking back, she truly regretted it.

If she had known this would happen, she should have listened to her professor.

"Professor, what made you think of calling me?" she answered.

The professor sounded relieved. "Hearing your relaxed voice puts my mind at ease. I was worried you wouldn't be able to move on."

Warmth spread through Vivian's heart.

So her teacher had been thinking of her all along.

When she first arrived in Country M, she was both sick and despondent.

Only now did she realize so many people around her still cared.

She had to live well for them.

"Teacher, I'm fine now. I will look forward and not be defeated by difficulties," she said softly.

Yes, it was all in the past.

Her relationship with Liam Sullivan was just a dream.

She would forget it gradually.

Just like the wound on the back of her head, it would eventually heal.

Even the deepest emotional scars would fade with time.

"Since you're in better spirits, why not attend a conference in Europe for me? I simply can't stand these endless meetings anymore. They give me a headache," the professor suddenly changed the subject.

Vivian was surprised. "You have so many students. Why specifically ask me? But I have been quite free lately. A fully-funded trip to Europe sounds nice."

It had been a long time since she visited Europe.

She loved the quiet towns there.

The leisurely atmosphere helped one forget their troubles.

After everything that happened, she needed to relax.

"I knew you'd like it. The institute covers all expenses. Feel free to travel after the conference. But there's a reason I asked you. Your profile says you speak the language of Country K, right? This conference is in Country K, and there's a hospital visit involved. Knowing the local language would be helpful," the professor explained.

Vivian's smile instantly froze.

Hearing the words "Country K" made her entire body stiffen.

That place was all too familiar to her.

Familiar like a recurring nightmare.

It was where she ended up after being cast out by the White family.

"Vivian? Why are you silent?" The professor noticed her sudden quiet and asked curiously.

Vivian snapped back to reality.

She realized she was covered in a cold sweat.

She couldn't help but smile bitterly.

So even after all this time.

She still couldn't let it go.
###Chapter 432

Running away could not erase the past.

Those shadows would forever haunt her life.

Vivian Bennett took a deep breath.

She finally made up her mind.

She would return to that place.

She would resolve the turmoil that had plagued her for years.

"Professor, I'll go."

She packed her luggage efficiently.

Her flight was booked quickly.

Vivian headed straight to the airport.

After more than ten hours in the air.

The plane landed at the capital airport of Country K.

The conference organizers had been waiting for some time.

They picked her up and drove her directly to the hotel.

After a light lunch.

Vivian rested for two hours.

She changed into a vacation-style floral maxi dress.

She put on a woven sun hat.

Picking up her camera, she left the hotel.

The capital city was not large.

But it possessed profound historical depth.

Free from industrial pollution.

The air was fresh and pleasant.

Flowers and green trees were everywhere.

People strolled leisurely along the streets.

Children played and laughed.

Adults chatted in cafes.

Vivian was infected by this atmosphere.

Her mood gradually relaxed.

She walked with her camera, taking pictures.

She captured interesting street scenes and portraits.

She bought snacks from food stalls to try.

She sat under flowering trees, enjoying them slowly.

She never expected returning to this place.

Could feel so peaceful and free.

She looked up at the azure sky.

Watched flocks of pigeons fly past.

A long-lost sense of ease rose in her heart.

Vivian wandered to the toy street.

Shop windows were filled with exquisite puppets.

She suddenly remembered her tenth birthday.

That year she was homeless on the streets.

She couldn't even afford a doll.

She could only hide in a corner and cry.

The memories brought a faint sadness.

Those years with the White family.

She had suffered too much.

With her father gone.

The past seemed to have vanished like smoke.

Today, memories kept surfacing.

Perhaps because this was where it all began.

She continued walking along the street.

She arrived at a beautiful lakeside square.

A giant angel sculpture stood in the center.

Fountain spray glittered under the sunlight.

Refracting brilliant rainbows.

People played and relaxed on the square.

Children flew kites and fed pigeons.

Laughter and joyful sounds filled the air.

Vivian felt happy.

She decided to rest on a bench near the fountain.

She smiled and walked toward an ornate iron bench.

Her gaze casually swept across the fountain.

She suddenly stopped walking.

Her pupils constricted sharply.

She gasped.

A man stood on the other side of the fountain.

He was tall and well-built.

His Eastern features stood out strikingly.

His handsome face resembled a sculpture.

Even in casual clothes.

He could not conceal his innate commanding presence.

The man was walking indifferently past the fountain.

He seemed to sense something.

He suddenly turned his head and looked over.

He also froze on the spot.

Vivian's entire body stiffened.

She never expected to encounter Liam Sullivan here.
###Chapter 433

After leaving the country, Vivian Bennett completely severed all ties with Liam Sullivan.

She no longer inquired about any news regarding him.

She had always believed he would eventually end up with Isabella Sullivan.

She never imagined she would see him again one day.

Fate seemed to be playing an absurd joke on her.

At this moment, Liam Sullivan was equally stunned by this unexpected encounter.

He stopped in his tracks, his gaze fixed on her.

The fountain's spray scattered, refracting brilliant colors under the sunlight.

A light mist drifted, gently enveloping the figures by the pool.

The two of them remained completely unaware.

They stared at each other silently through the curtain of water.

The swirling mist blurred each other's faces.

Yet Vivian Bennett clearly felt the icy coldness in his gaze.

Even though she couldn't see clearly, that chill penetrated the mist and went straight to her heart.

She couldn't bear such a gaze.

Pulling down her baseball cap, she turned and hurried away.

She did not look back.

She did not dare to look back.

It was just a chance encounter. It shouldn't disturb her peace of mind.

Like two intersecting straight lines, they briefly met before inevitably drifting further apart.

Vivian Bennett quickly walked down the square and hailed a taxi.

Once inside the car, she instinctively looked back.

His figure was long gone from the square.

Only after returning to her hotel did her heart begin to settle slightly.

Why was he here?

He should have been back in the country.

Perhaps he was in France on a business trip?

But she immediately shook her head.

The probability of such a coincidence was minuscule.

A man as busy as him wouldn't have the leisure to linger here.

She refused to believe they would meet again.

It was absolutely impossible.

Vivian paced around her room, trying to convince herself.

If she treated this chance encounter as a dream, it wouldn't trouble her.

Her mood gradually brightened.

She took a long, relaxing hot shower.

She planned to try the local specialty cuisine tonight and then get a good night's sleep.

She was here on vacation. Nothing should affect her mood.

Just after she finished blow-drying her hair, the local guide girl called.

She invited Vivian to a masquerade ball.

It was the city's anniversary celebration.

There would be a fireworks display and a masquerade ball at the lakeside square.

The most dazzling couple at the ball would be chosen as the King and Queen.

They would receive the honor of starting the fireworks show.

Vivian was immediately interested.

The most enchanting part of traveling was experiencing these rich, folk-style activities.

Though she was somewhat tired, she didn't want to miss such a fun experience.

She happily agreed.

The girl said she would pick her up in an hour.

They would go choose ball gowns and masks together.

Vivian quickly touched up her makeup.

She applied an exquisite party makeup look.

She adorned the corners of her eyes with a few rhinestones.

She put on jade-green colored contact lenses.

She decided to play the part of a medieval European noble lady.

It was a complete transformation.

Soon, the guide girl arrived right on time.

The two of them went to a shop specializing in renting ball costumes.

Vivian chose a gorgeous mask.

It had a pale gold base glaze with meandering jade patterns.

A vine rose adorned the right side.

It was both mysterious and elegant.
###Chapter 434

Vivian Bennett chose a ball gown with a voluminous skirt.

Intricate lace adorned the hem.

Gold thread embroidery shimmered under the lights.

It exuded the noble grace of an aristocratic young lady.

The receptionist's eyes sparkled.

"You look stunning, Miss Bennett."

"Like a princess stepping out of an oil painting."

"It would be perfect with golden curls."

Vivian readily agreed.

She put on a long, wavy blonde wig.

The reflection in the mirror became instantly unfamiliar.

The mask covered most of her face.

Only her delicate chin and red lips were visible.

She couldn't even recognize herself in the mirror.

Perfect.

This was exactly the effect she wanted.

The receptionist finished changing too.

They drove to the central square together.

Night had fallen.

Lights began to twinkle.

The square had transformed into a sea of revelry.

Masked young men and women moved through the crowd.

Classical formalwear and eccentric costumes created a dazzling spectacle.

Laughter rose and fell all around.

Their appearance immediately drew many gazes.

Masked gentlemen approached one after another to ask for dances.

Vivian smiled and politely declined.

She hadn't seen a partner who caught her eye yet.

As they strolled, a burst of screams erupted from the northeast corner.

Vivian turned to look.

Several masked men entered the square.

Their faces were hidden.

But their entire demeanor set them apart.

The one in the center was particularly striking.

A portly older gentleman accompanied a blond man.

The receptionist gripped her arm excitedly.

"It's the Mayor!"

"Even a mask can't hide his build."

Vivian raised an eyebrow.

"You get this excited about seeing the Mayor?"

"Anyone would think a superstar had arrived."

"It's not for him!"

The girl stamped her foot.

"It's the blond gentleman beside him!"

"The Mayor is blocking my view of the handsome man."

Vivian studied the blond man.

The courtly attire emphasized his tall, straight posture.

But...

"What if he has an ugly face under the mask?"

She teased her on purpose.

The girl immediately retorted.

"He's definitely handsome!"

"We've all guessed who he is."

"He can't be a prince, can he?"

Vivian joked casually.

The girl's eyes shone.

"You guessed it!"

"It's really Prince Dacia of S country!"

"He's been visiting our country these past few days."

"The Mayor is personally accompanying him."

Vivian was startled.

It really was a prince.

A prince attending a commoners' masquerade.

How interesting.

"Who do you think the prince will ask to dance?"

The girl clenched her fists nervously.

"Who will be tonight's Cinderella?"

Vivian chuckled softly.

She lightly tapped the girl's mask with her fingertip.

"You, of course."

"My Miss Cinderella."
###Chapter 435

They walked to a corner, laughing together.

That world felt too distant for them.

It was fun to imagine.

But reality was, the prince already had a designated dance partner.

At exactly eight o'clock.

The portly mayor waddled onto the podium.

Reporters raised their cameras, snapping pictures frantically.

The mayor began with a passionate speech.

He spoke of the city's history and his love for the land.

Finally, he got to the main point.

"The masquerade ball is a tradition of our celebration." He smiled at the crowd below. "I'm deeply moved to see so many masked citizens."

His tone shifted abruptly.

"But while we celebrate, we must not forget those who are suffering. The northern floods have left many homeless."

He waved his plump hand and announced, "This year, we're changing the rules. Ladies will take the stage, and gentlemen will bid for a dance partner. All proceeds will go to the disaster area!"

His voice echoed across the square through the microphone.

This exciting game instantly ignited the crowd.

The girls were eager to participate.

The men found it both charitable and entertaining.

Deafening applause and cheers erupted.

"Let's go too!" The receptionist girl excitedly pulled Vivian Bennett. "Helping the disaster area is our duty."

Vivian let herself be dragged along.

She knew the girl was just in it for the fun.

Staff members arranged them into a line.

Each was given a rose.

"Do your best to show your charm. Give the rose to the gentleman who wins your bid."

Vivian inexplicably thought of ancient courtesan scenes.

They were near the front of the line.

Soon, it was the receptionist girl's turn.

She cheerfully ran onto the stage.

A man wearing a wolf king mask won her bid for 500 euros.

The two immediately chatted animatedly, completely forgetting about Vivian.

Vivian felt both annoyed and amused.

A staff member urged her onto the stage.

She struck a casual pose.

She was just participating anyway.

She secretly guessed her own worth.

Seeing the previous girls go for between 300 and 500 euros.

She should be around the same.

"50 euros." Someone in the crowd shouted a bid.

Vivian nearly choked.

Starting that low?

The price slowly increased to 400 euros.

She breathed a sigh of relief.

At least it wasn't too embarrassing.
###Chapter 436

Vivian Bennett felt a flicker of hope when someone bid four hundred euros.

At least someone was willing to bid for her.

She wouldn't have the lowest price of the night.

"I bid one hundred thousand euros for a dance with this lady."

A clear, pleasant male voice rang out from below the stage.

His tone was calm and collected, brimming with confidence.

Vivian's lips parted beneath her mask.

One hundred thousand euros?

Had she heard correctly?

The number left her feeling dazed.

The entire hall fell silent.

All eyes turned to the man who had made the bid.

Vivian followed their gaze and froze.

It was the man dressed as the Prince Charming.

She never expected this prince to choose her.

For a moment, she felt dizzy with luck.

Because of his bid, she instantly became the center of attention.

Vivian even began to fantasize about becoming the ball's queen tonight.

After all, dancing with a prince was an honor in itself.

But her heart remained surprisingly calm.

She simply found the situation quite amusing.

The mayor beamed as he spoke. "Thank you for your generous donation, sir. The people in the disaster area will remember your kindness."

He was clearly pleased with the price.

He was even happier to have this chance to please such an important figure.

"Please, this beautiful lady, step down and share a dance with this gentleman." The mayor gestured for Vivian to come forward.

Vivian gladly prepared to descend the stage.

The jump from three hundred euros to one hundred thousand was astonishing.

She lifted her gown's hem, a smile playing on her lips.

Just as she was about to step forward—

A voice she knew to her very core cut through the air from a distance.

"Five hundred thousand euros."

The voice was low and clear, leaving no room for doubt.

Vivian's steps halted instantly.

Her smile froze on her face.

Beneath her mask, her pupils constricted sharply.

Under the brilliant starlight, she stood as if spellbound.

Time seemed to stand still.

Even her breathing became cautious.

It was him…

Liam Sullivan.

Vivian stared in disbelief at the figure approaching.

He wore an exquisite black mask trimmed with gold.

His eyes were as deep as the night.

His perfectly tailored black suit accentuated his tall, straight posture.

Even without lavish attire, his presence was overwhelming.
###Chapter 437

The night was as dark as ink.

A tall, slender figure approached under the moonlight.

His steps were unhurried.

He moved like a fallen angel descending through the darkness.

An aura of opulent gloom swept through the entire gathering.

His noble presence was overwhelming.

The surrounding crowd instinctively stepped back.

They cleared a path for him.

Vivian Bennett's breath caught.

Her heart tightened abruptly.

She watched as Liam Sullivan drew closer.

Her fingernails dug deeply into her palms.

Had he recognized her?

Or did he simply want to be the center of attention?

Whatever the reason, she did not want to dance with him.

Not even a little bit.

She clutched her gown tightly.

Her pleading gaze turned toward the white knight.

Please.

Save me.

Prince Dacia received her beseeching look.

His protective instincts flared instantly.

"I bid one hundred thousand," he announced clearly.

His sunny disposition contrasted sharply with Liam's dark intensity.

Liam's eyes turned icy cold.

Invisible sparks crackled in the air.

The tension was palpable.

"Two hundred thousand," Liam's voice was lazy.

Yet it carried an undeniable pressure.

Vivian's heart clenched.

She knew this man too well.

The more someone competed for something, the more determined he became to possess it.

How long could Prince Dacia hold out?

She dreaded to think.

She could only fix the prince with an even more vulnerable look.

Prince Dacia was thoroughly provoked.

As royalty, he could not lose to some unknown man.

Especially when a beauty was counting on his protection.

"Three hundred thousand," he raised his chin.

He displayed the haughty demeanor unique to royalty.

Liam's gaze swept over the pale woman on the stage.

Then he looked at the preening prince.

A mocking smile touched his lips.

"Four hundred thousand," his voice was low as the night.

The entire square fell silent.

Everyone held their breath.

This sudden bidding war left them all stunned.
###Chapter 438

Vivian Bennett’s heart leaped violently.

She could hardly believe her ears.

This was insane.

It was just a charity ball.

They were only bidding for a single dance.

The highest bid in the past had been a few thousand dollars.

But now the number had skyrocketed to an astronomical figure.

This wasn't a bid for a dance partner.

It was a battle for ownership over her.

Vivian's fingertips turned cold.

A chill ran down her spine.

Tonight would not be peaceful.

Even the mayor, who had been excited earlier, now looked uneasy.

The crazier the bids became, the paler his face turned.

Prince Dacia had a special status.

The man who suddenly appeared had a powerful aura.

He was clearly dangerous.

These two were not bidding.

They were fighting for pride.

If this continued, someone would get hurt.

The mayor was sweating nervously.

His gaze suddenly fell on Vivian.

His eyes lit up.

He raised his hand abruptly.

"Gentlemen, please stop!"

The auction hall fell silent instantly.

"I know you both have big hearts and want to contribute more to the disaster relief."

"Although this is an auction, shouldn't we also consider the lady's own wishes?"

Two icy glares shot toward him.

They carried undisguised coldness and warning.

The mayor shuddered in fear.

Wasn't he trying to prevent bloodshed?

Couldn't these two madmen understand his good intentions?

If he weren't about to retire, he wouldn't bother with this mess.

He forced himself to stay calm.

He spoke quickly.

"Since you can't agree, why not let this beautiful lady choose her own dance partner?"

"Ladies first."

"I'm sure both gentlemen will respect that."

He didn't give anyone a chance to object.

He finished his words in one breath.

The audience exchanged glances.

The two men's eyes seemed ready to slice him apart.

Vivian, at the center of the storm, glared fiercely at the mayor behind her mask.

That fat fool had thrown the problem to her.

He was pushing her into a fire pit.

Of course she wanted to choose Prince Dacia.

But Liam Sullivan's gloomy gaze almost devoured her.

It seemed that if she dared not choose him, there would be no good outcome.

Cold sweat gathered in Vivian's palms.

All eyes in the room were fixed on her.

They waited for her choice.

She glanced helplessly at Liam.

Her heart felt icy cold.

This was the second time she had encountered him today.

She didn't believe it was fate.

Every meeting brought only hurt and pain.

It was like an unavoidable calamity.

No matter how many times they met, she didn't want this kind of cursed fate.

Vivian unconsciously clenched her fists.

Her nails dug deeply into her palms.

She raised her eyes.

Her gaze landed gently on Prince Dacia.

She deliberately softened her voice.

It carried shyness and joy.

"I choose this gentleman."

Her emerald eyes shimmered with watery light.

She focused intently on Dacia.

It was as if he alone in the world deserved her devotion.

Everyone else was completely ignored by her.
###Chapter 439

Liam Sullivan stood alone in the crowd, like a forgotten island.

The mayor wiped his sweat and forced a smile. "Congratulations to this gentleman for winning the beauty's favor."

Thunderous applause filled the air.

Prince Dacia lifted his chin and cast a contemptuous glance at Liam.

He strode onto the stage and performed a perfect gentleman's bow to Vivian Bennett.

Vivian placed her hand in his palm.

Their classical gowns shimmered under the lights.

They looked like royalty stepping out from the Middle Ages.

"What a perfect match."

"A fairy tale prince and princess."

No one looked at Liam anymore.

Losers didn't deserve attention.

He stood alone in the bustling crowd.

A mocking smile touched his lips.

But his gaze remained locked on the two figures.

Dark fire burned in his eyes.

His knuckles turned white from clenching.

Did she think changing her outfit, altering her voice, and wearing a mask would make him fail to recognize her?

Ridiculous.

Others might be fooled.

But not him.

Her essence had been carved into his bones.

Melted into his soul.

Even if she turned to ashes, he would recognize her at a glance.

This was fate.

Entangled until death.

When they met again in the square this afternoon, his calm heart had stirred once more.

Why was she here?

Was heaven not finished with them?

He couldn't find her anywhere back home, yet they easily met in a foreign country.

Liam sneered coldly.

His gaze fell upon the two chatting and laughing happily.

Look, without him, she could still climb to great heights.

Effortlessly.

Perhaps she truly had no heart.

A heart made of stone that could never be warmed.

But why did she get to be so happy?

As if the past meant nothing.

What did his pain and struggle mean in her eyes?

Liam retreated into the shadows under a tree.

Leaning against the trunk, his gaze sharp as ice blades.

The auction ended.

The ball began.

Vivian and the prince conversed happily.

The prince was arrogant yet amusing.

Like a boy who never grew up.

He made her laugh softly.

The mask hid her face.

But it couldn't conceal the light in her eyes.

A dull ache spread through Liam's chest.

He picked up a glass of wine and drained it in one gulp.

The alcohol burned his throat.

But it couldn't extinguish the fire in his heart.

Music flowed through the hall.

The prince extended his hand to her.

"A dance?"

Vivian nodded.

They glided onto the dance floor.

Her skirt swirled around her.

Liam's eyes darkened instantly.

He set down his glass and strode forward.
###Chapter 440

Vivian Bennett couldn't help but ask Prince Dacia why he had chosen her.

After all, they were complete strangers.

He felt she was just the right height to dance with him.

It was just because of her height.

A bit silly.

"That gentleman from earlier, you know him, don't you?"

Vivian felt a heaviness in her chest.

Love and hate intertwined?

Hate, perhaps.

Love?

A man who could casually sleep with any woman.

How could he possibly have genuine feelings?

Every time she remembered that scene of him and Isabella Sullivan in the bathroom.

She felt suffocated with pain.

She had actually once believed he had fallen for her.

His persistence.

Was simply because he couldn't have her.

Without warning, he cupped her face.

His lips landed on her cheek.

Both were wearing half-masks.

A kiss wasn't difficult.

But the position.

From behind, it looked like a passionate kiss.

Especially since Vivian had her back to Liam Sullivan.

She couldn't see the front at all.

She only felt the gaze behind her suddenly intensify.

It felt like it could pierce right through her.

Vivian's eyes widened.

Annoyed, she tried to push him away. "How can you just kiss someone like that?"

Prince Dacia looked innocent. "In Europe, cheek kissing is very common. A kiss doesn't mean anything. Your strong reaction—are you afraid he'll get jealous? Heh, you can't see his expression, but I'll tell you—his eyes are frighteningly dark, like he wants to devour me."

"Who's afraid of him getting jealous?" Vivian snapped. "His reaction has nothing to do with me. I'm worried about you."

This naive prince had no idea how terrifying Liam Sullivan could be.

Offending him.

Even for a prince.

Would not end well.
###Chapter 441

She felt quite fond of this prince and didn't want him to get into trouble.

But this naive classmate clearly led too dull a life and always sought excitement.

The more Vivian Bennett tried to dissuade him, the more determined Prince Dacia became.

"What can he do to me? Would he dare kill me? Since you say there's nothing between you, you shouldn't stop me. Why care about his gaze? If you truly want to break free, you should act completely indifferent and make him give up completely—unless you still love him." Prince Dacia's words stung like needles.

Vivian felt her most denied emotions being exposed.

Yes, why should she care about his gaze at this point.

Since they led separate lives, her actions didn't need his interference.

She didn't need to restrain her freedom because of his watchful eyes, or she would forever live in his shadow.

"I don't love him, so I am free to enjoy this night that belongs to me." Vivian took a deep breath, ignoring the burning gaze behind her.

"Then dance wildly with me, lovely lady."

Prince Dacia laughed heartily, pulling her onto the stage where spotlights instantly enveloped them.

He led her in a cheerful tap dance. Being passionate and unrestrained, he played with abandon, laughing continuously.

The festival atmosphere was electric. Everyone danced with joy, and happy laughter filled the air. Vivian was infected by this pure happiness, throwing herself wholeheartedly into the feast.

As she danced, she felt her melancholy gradually dissipate, her spirit feeling liberated and elevated.

Even with Liam Sullivan's icy gaze from the corner, she was no longer affected by his mood.

Tonight, she would be her true self, bound by no one.

Vivian smiled, taking Prince Dacia's hand, then reaching for nearby strangers. Everyone joined hands quietly, cheering and forming a circle to dance freely.

The square echoed with celebratory songs and laughter. Strangers danced together happily, enjoying this simple joy.

The dance lasted over two hours, leaving everyone exhilarated yet exhausted.

For the final King and Queen of the Dance contest, the mayor honored Prince Dacia and Vivian, having them press the button to start the fireworks show.

Vivian felt incredibly happy. The people here, even the prince she danced with, were strangers, yet they gave her a relaxation she never experienced back home.

Amid the crowd's countdown, she and Prince Dacia pressed the electronic button to launch the fireworks.

Boom—vibrant fireworks blossomed overhead, beautiful and dreamlike.

Many couples embraced and kissed excitedly, watching the brilliant sparks continuously blooming in the night sky.

"How about it? Such a wonderful night, beautiful girl from the East." Prince Dacia held her waist, standing at the lake's edge, admiring the view while laughing cheerfully.

"Yes." Vivian took a deep breath, the lake breeze refreshing her mind. "Thank you, Dacia. I was very unhappy earlier, but now I'm completely relaxed."
###Chapter 442

Prince Dacia laughed heartily.

Happiness belongs to oneself.

Since you've decided to let go, you should do it completely.

I can see you don't really want to be with that man.

Dare to accept my invitation?

We'll go have some fun later.

My good friend is hosting a midnight barbecue at his place.

He loves having lots of friends gathered together.

Vivian Bennett raised an eyebrow.

Why wouldn't I dare?

I've been dancing for so long, I'm hungry now.

When does your friend's party start?

She knew Prince Dacia meant no harm.

Someone of his status wouldn't dare cause trouble recklessly.

Besides, she thought making such a friend was nice.

He had no romantic interest in her.

It made her feel relaxed.

Then let's go.

We'll go right now.

We're wearing masks anyway.

I don't have to bother with all those royal formalities.

That's why I particularly love these masquerade balls.

Prince Dacia stopped watching the fireworks.

He didn't even bid farewell to the mayor.

He pulled Vivian Bennett through the crowd.

They weaved left and right through the people.

It felt quite amusing.

Once outside, they hailed a taxi.

They both looked back at the mayor hopping anxiously in the distance.

They burst into laughter.

Prince Dacia's friend lived nearby.

It was already lively when they arrived.

There were many energetic young people.

Not nobility at all.

They were all free-spirited individuals.

Prince Dacia preferred making friends with all kinds of ordinary people.

Joy was the main theme here.

There were all sorts of eccentric artists.

Musicians too.

All were people with fascinating personalities.

Vivian found them incredibly fun.

No matter who she chatted with, she felt lighthearted happiness.

Everyone ate barbecue together in the garden.

They watched their friends' entertaining little performances.

And drank rum.

Though Vivian didn't know them, she laughed heartily.

She was in high spirits and drank quite a bit.

Her face turned completely red.

The barbecue party lasted all night.

Finally, Prince Dacia saw she really couldn't handle more.

He gallantly personally escorted her back to the hotel.
