A faint smile touched Liam's pale lips.

"If you won't help, I'll have to do it myself." He toyed carelessly with the edge of the gauze. "Would you stand by while a patient struggles alone?"

The doctor was angered by his indifferent attitude.

But professional ethics prevented him from doing nothing.

"Prepare the dressing change kit." The doctor instructed the nurse. He turned back to Liam. "I hope you understand your body isn't capital to squander. How heartbroken your family would be to see this."

A flicker of mockery passed through Liam's eyes.

"You're not family. How would you know they'd be heartbroken?" He chuckled softly. "Perhaps they'd be delighted."

The doctor paused.

"A woman who watched my finger get severed without blinking." Liam looked down at his hand. "Would she care about a minor injury like this?"

Silence fell in the hospital room.

The doctor said no more. He carefully redressed the wound.

This time, following Liam's request, the bandaging was much more refined.

"Done." The doctor tied the final knot. "But you must still be careful. Keep it dry."

Liam examined his hand with satisfaction.

"Thank you. It looks much better now." He looked up. "Also, I want to be discharged."

The doctor opened his mouth, then swallowed his objections.

After completing the paperwork, Liam changed back into his suit.

The doctor watched his tall, solitary figure and spoke impulsively. "Young man, don't be so pessimistic. Someone will love you. Have faith in life."

Liam didn't stop walking. A self-mocking curve lifted his lips.

How could there be despair when hope never existed?

His world had long sunk into the abyss.

This was just falling deeper.

Since shattering was inevitable, what remained to fear?

Vivian set down her phone and struggled to rise from the bed.

Every muscle ached fiercely.

Though he hadn't been rough last night, his demands were urgent and relentless.

As if deliberately tormenting her.

A dull pain throbbed in her lower abdomen. Her back ached too much to straighten.

If not for the afternoon meeting, she would have stayed in bed all day.

She changed into her professional suit, pulling her hair into a meticulous updo.

The reflection showed a capable, efficient woman.

Except...

Vivian stared in frustration at the skin revealed by her collar.

Faint, mottled love marks peeked through.

She gritted her teeth in anger.

If colleagues saw this, she would die of embarrassment.

It was all that bastard's fault.
###Chapter 455

Vivian Bennett quickly took out her foundation and concealer.

She carefully applied them while looking in the mirror.

Those red marks were truly embarrassing.

It took her over ten minutes to completely cover them up.

Her phone suddenly rang.

It was a call from Prince Dacia.

This new friend always wanted to take her out.

But she didn't dare to involve him now.

If Liam Sullivan found out.

He wouldn't care about Prince Dacia's status.

That man became completely irrational when jealous.

She decisively ended the call.

She ordered room service.

Originally wanting breakfast.

The server reminded her it was already lunch time.

She had to change her order to lunch.

After finishing lunch.

It was still early before the meeting.

She felt too tired to go out.

She could only stay in her room watching TV.

The TV was showing a travel program about islands.

The blue sea and sky made her dazed.

The familiar scenery stirred memories.

She couldn't help falling into deep thought.

"What are you thinking about?"

A deep voice came from behind her.

Liam Sullivan's arms wrapped around her waist.

He pulled her entire body into his embrace.

His chin rested on top of her head.

His warm breath enveloped her.

Vivian's body stiffened slightly.

This intimacy felt completely out of place during their cold war.

"Nonsense, I wasn't thinking about anyone."

She looked out at the ancient streets.

Her voice carried a distant quality.

Liam snorted lightly.

He used his uninjured hand to stroke her face.

His tone filled with jealousy: "Which man has you so nostalgic? It's not Zachary Hudson, is it?"

He tightened his arms.

His face pressed against her cheek.

He demanded arrogantly: "I want to know who it is."

Vivian struggled to breathe from his tight hold.

"I already said I wasn't thinking about anyone!"

She struggled angrily.

Amusement flashed in Liam's eyes.

He loosened his grip slightly.

He lazily snorted: "Better not be. Otherwise I won't let him off."

Vivian had no response.

His palm pressed against her chest.

Feeling the rhythm of her heartbeat.

His voice carried a childish obsession: "This can only belong to me. You can only think about me."

Vivian turned her head and looked at him sarcastically: "What if I demand your heart belongs only to me? Could you do that?"

Liam gazed at her.

Suddenly curving his lips.

He replied softly: "Yes, I could do that."
###Chapter 456

Vivian Bennett felt a tightness in her chest as she watched his indifferent expression.

She forced a bitter smile. "You agreed so easily because you don't actually mean it, do you? It's just a hypothetical after all. Hypotheticals don't require any real commitment. The woman who could truly make you humble yourself to keep a promise probably doesn't even exist in this world."

Liam Sullivan remained silent, his deep gaze fixed on her face.

He neither confirmed nor denied her words.

Unfathomable emotions swirled in those dark eyes, elusive as mist and wind.

Vivian grew uncomfortable under his intense stare.

The air thickened with suffocating tension.

She averted her eyes and hastily picked up the documents on the table. "It's time. The car from the conference organizers should be here."

Liam released his hold on her waist and slowly adjusted his cuffs.

He strode toward the door with effortless grace.

Vivian stared blankly at his retreating figure. "Where are you going?"

He glanced back with a raised brow, a teasing smirk playing on his lips. "I'm accompanying you to the meeting."

Vivian's expression immediately darkened.

She had finally found a moment alone. Was he really going to deprive her of even these few hours of freedom?

Her chest constricted, making it difficult to breathe.

"What identity will you use to accompany me? The conference organizers didn't invite you. Your sudden appearance would be highly inappropriate. If this is about monitoring me, it's completely unnecessary. You're holding my passport. I have nowhere to run."

Anger flushed her eyes red as she glared at his arrogantly smiling face.

Liam shrugged dismissively. "Unless you plan to immigrate illegally, forget about leaving Country K. This is essentially my territory. The immigration office has already been notified. Without my permission, you can't even set foot in the airport."

Vivian clenched her fists, nails digging deep into her palms.

He had truly restricted her freedom.

"What exactly do you want?"

"Simple." Liam stepped closer, lifting her chin with his fingertip. "Preventing my woman from straying. You attracted a prince with just one dance. How could I possibly feel at ease? To avoid being cuckolded, I naturally have to keep a close watch."

His tone turned icy when he mentioned the word "prince."

Vivian laughed in exasperation. "Liam Sullivan, since when do you have so much free time? You came all the way abroad just to do something this childish?"

He leaned in close, his warm breath brushing her ear. "I enjoy it. If I want to do something, I'll make it happen. So you'd better behave yourself. If I catch you making eyes at another man..."

His unfinished sentence hung heavy with threat.

Vivian snatched her handbag and walked out without looking back.

Liam followed at a leisurely pace, a pleased smile gracing his lips.

They reached the hotel lobby where staff immediately approached. "Miss Bennett, your car is waiting."

Vivian nodded politely. "Thank you. Please book a dinner for one for tonight."

Liam's expression instantly darkened.

He gripped her wrist and calmly told the staff, "That won't be necessary. We'll handle dinner ourselves."

Turning back to Vivian, he adopted a gentle smile. "Darling, I'll take you to the finest restaurant."

Vivian wanted to refuse, but meeting his warning gaze, she swallowed her words.
###Chapter 457

"Alright."

"By the way, Miss Bennett, a gentleman was waiting for you outside earlier," the staff member added.

Liam Sullivan's expression darkened instantly.
He shot Vivian Bennett a chilling sidelong glance.
His look was accusatory, like he had just caught her in an affair.

Vivian's heart sank.
Who could be looking for her?
It definitely wasn't staff.
Could it be that ridiculous Prince Dacia?
Highly likely.
His thought process was bizarre, simple yet unpredictable.
Hanging up on him might have only made him more persistent.

This was bad.
Last night's incident had already put Liam on edge regarding Dacia.
If he showed up now, even though she knew he only saw her as a friend...
Liam would absolutely suspect ulterior motives.

A headache.
A real headache.

"Hmph, feeling guilty?" Liam's displeasure grew as he watched her shifting expressions.
"Let's go see."

He wrapped his arm possessively around her waist.
The gesture was intimate, yet underscored clear ownership.
They walked out to the lobby.
Prince Dacia, disguised with sunglasses, was indeed waiting there.

Dacia's eyes lit up the moment he saw Vivian.
He spread his arms wide for an enthusiastic hug.
"Vivian Bennett! Why wasn't your phone working? I thought something happened to you. I came to check."

Liam stepped directly in front of Vivian.
His gaze was icy and full of warning.
"Talk if you must. Keep your hands off my woman."

Dacia froze.
He studied the hostile man before him, and suddenly understood.
"Ah! You're the man who was bidding against me last night!"

Liam remembered the scene vividly.
Vivian had publicly chosen this man over him.

A deep chill surged in his eyes.
Unrestrained killing intent radiated from him.
"It was me. What gives you the right to compete for my woman?" His voice was frigid.

Dacia's temper flared.
"Who I am doesn't matter. What matters is you lost last night. You lost to me!"

"Dacia, stop it!" Vivian urgently hissed under her breath.
Couldn't he sense the murderous aura around Liam?
He was already enraged, and Dacia was just provoking him further.
It was suicidal.

She quickly stepped forward, linking her arm intimately with Liam's.
She pressed her soft body against his chest and looked up at him.
Her expression was a mix of annoyance and coquettish pleading.
"Don't do anything rash. I told you, he's just a friend. He has no interest in me like that. He already has a fiancée."

She clutched his hand nervously.
Her eyes were pleading, her voice soft and dripping with sweetness.

Liam looked down at her beseeching face, and his heart softened slightly.
But his anger remained.
She was actually pleading for this pretty boy.

Hmph.

Dacia looked utterly confused.
"What fiancée? I don't have..."

Vivian shot him a fierce glare.
He had no idea how terrifying Liam could be.
He had no concept of the intensity of Liam's possessiveness.

Dacia received her warning look.
Though displeased, he obediently shut his mouth.

Liam gave him a cold once-over.
His pleasant voice held a subtle threat.
"If he's just a friend, then fine. But if I find out you have any improper designs on my woman..."

He paused, his voice dropping even colder.
"Be careful the car you're in suddenly explodes. Or the food you eat is mysteriously poisoned. Dying in such an ugly way... that would make you the real loser."

Dacia's eyes widened in shock.
He looked at Vivian in disbelief.
"Is he joking? Does he think he's a terrorist?"
###Chapter 458

Vivian Bennett looked at Prince Dacia with a grave expression.

She spoke each word clearly. "He is more terrifying than a terrorist."

Prince Dacia's face instantly paled.

He stumbled back several steps, eyeing Liam Sullivan with deep wariness.

Vivian sighed softly.

"Prince Dacia, we merely crossed paths by chance."

"I know you enjoy making friends, but a friend like me will only bring you trouble."

"Please don't contact me again."

"After we leave this country, we won't see each other again."

"But I will remember you as a friend."

Prince Dacia looked at her with genuine regret.

He was naturally open-minded and respected others' choices.

"You are a remarkable woman. I will remember you too."

He turned to Liam, his tone firm. "Treat her well."

"I've never seen a woman who carries such sorrow even in her moments of joy."

"She must have endured immense pain. You shouldn't cause her more."

Liam arched an arrogant brow. "I don't need you to teach me how to act."

Prince Dacia shrugged and walked away.

Liam turned his gaze to Vivian.

She was watching Prince Dacia's retreating figure.

"Protecting him so fiercely?" He sneered. "Drawing such a clear line so quickly. Afraid I'll harm him?"

Vivian met his eyes directly. "He is a good man."

"I don't want to drag him into this abyss."

Liam let out a dismissive snort. "In your eyes, everyone else is good. Only I am the villain?"

"Isn't that true?"

"It doesn't matter." Liam lowered his gaze. "At least I am the most special one."

"A man who isn't ruthless enough could never keep you."

Vivian glared at him. "You're so good at justifying yourself."

She shook off his hand and walked straight out.

A car was already waiting outside.

They got in and headed towards the downtown Government Complex.

This meeting was an officially organized event.

After getting out of the car, Vivian couldn't help but smile.

"Goodbye." She waved her work pass. "You can't get in without this."

"You'll have to wait outside."

She finally managed to one-up him, and it felt incredibly satisfying.

Liam saw right through her.

He got out of the car, closed the distance, and captured her lips in a fierce kiss.

"Goodbye." He released her, a teasing glint in his eyes.

Vivian watched him walk towards a café and couldn't resist calling out. "Will you wait until the meeting is over?"
The meeting would last several hours.

Liam glanced back with a smile. "What do you think?"

Vivian bit her lip. "Hmph, suit yourself."

"If I don't see you when I come out, I'll just go back to the hotel and eat by myself."

"I won't let you have dinner alone." Liam waved his hand and walked away.

Vivian stared at his retreating back, momentarily lost in thought.

She shook her head and entered the building.

The meeting was held on the sixth floor.

The spacious conference room was filled with experts from around the world.
###Chapter 459

Vivian Bennett was not Professor Tony Morrison.

She naturally could not occupy the front row's chief seat.

The organizers placed her in the third row's section for young scholars.

She did not mind this.

Such academic exchanges had always been a stage for established authorities to showcase their achievements.

Young scholars rarely had opportunities to stand out.

If Professor Tony Morrison had attended, he would undoubtedly have been the center of attention.

Since her substitution, the organizers' enthusiasm had noticeably cooled.

A young man from the East sat to her left.

He wore gold-rimmed glasses and carried himself with a calm, scholarly demeanor.

His professional and rigorous manner was impressive.

Their similar Eastern features led to a natural conversation.

To her right was a blond young man from Country A.

Upon hearing her mentor was Tony Morrison, he eagerly joined their discussion.

The three young peers conversed pleasantly.

The conference had not yet begun, but they were already deeply engaged in professional topics.

Vivian could not help but smile.

If Liam Sullivan saw this scene, he would probably be consumed by jealousy.

The thought inexplicably lifted her spirits.

The more he tried to restrain her, the more she would resist.

Even if it was just a trivial resistance.

The venue gradually filled.

Except for those absent due to unforeseen circumstances, almost every seat was occupied.

Only the two chief seats in the front row remained empty.

It was unclear who could be so important as to warrant such a grand entrance.

"Perhaps it's the conference chairman, Dr. Bernard," the blond young man shrugged. "That old authority has always been arrogant. If not for his academic family background, he wouldn't have gotten the chairman position. In my opinion, your mentor should be sitting there."

Vivian smiled slightly. "My mentor is more focused on academic research. He doesn't care much for these empty titles."

The Eastern man adjusted his glasses. "Who is the other seat for? Is there an expert in the field more heavyweight than Professor Tony Morrison?"

They discussed it for a long time but could not guess who deserved that seat.

Like the other attendees, they were full of curiosity about the person who was about to appear.

The conference chairman finally appeared, beaming with joy.

He quickly walked up to the podium and first welcomed the experts from various countries.

He then spoke about the future of the industry and the significance of this exchange.

Finally, he cleared his throat, his voice trembling slightly with excitement.

"This conference aims to promote international academic exchange. We plan to establish a free medical charity for disadvantaged groups." The chairman's face glowed with pride. "Although the ideal is beautiful, funding has always been the biggest challenge. Such institutions usually rely on social donations and corporate sponsorships..."

He suddenly raised his voice. "But today, a socially responsible entrepreneur heard about this and decided to fully fund our institution! He will donate a huge sum annually to ensure its smooth operation!"

The blond young man raised an eyebrow and smiled. "No wonder the chairman is so excited. He's faced constant controversy since taking office. This finally proves his capability. I just wonder who this benefactor really is?"
###Chapter 460

Vivian Bennett sighed softly.

No wonder this seat was so important.

The cash cow had arrived.

"Such a momentous occasion deserves to be shared with everyone."

"Let us now give our warmest applause to welcome this generous gentleman, full of kindness and a strong sense of justice!"

The chairman led the applause.

He smiled broadly and gestured toward the conference room door.

Thunderous applause immediately filled the room.

Everyone looked toward the door with curiosity.

They all wanted to see this legendary figure with their own eyes.

Vivian also looked over with anticipation.

She was secretly calculating.

Perhaps she could invite this philanthropist to sponsor her own medical organization in the future.

The automatic doors slid open slowly.

A tall, imposing figure appeared in the doorway.

The man's strikingly handsome face wore a benevolent smile.

It was like an angel descending to the mortal world.

Vivian's eyes widened instantly.

She could hardly believe what she was seeing.

This was as absurd as meeting a demon in heaven.

The man's smile was dazzling.

A holy light seemed to radiate from him.

He had practically become the spokesperson for the Holy Father.

He was surrounded by a halo.

The sight was hard for her to process.

But compared to her grim expression, the room erupted into even more enthusiastic applause.

Many women were captivated by Liam Sullivan's beauty and compassion.

She even heard someone behind her whisper in admiration.

They called him the reincarnation of the Archangel Michael.

Vivian helplessly held her forehead.

Was something wrong with everyone's eyes?

Calling Liam Sullivan an angel.

If they knew his true identity, countless glasses would shatter.

Even if he were an angel, he would absolutely be Lucifer after his fall.

"Your expression is strange," the blond man asked curiously. "Do you know him?"

"No," Vivian denied quickly.

She did not want to be associated with that flashy man.

But she couldn't help suspecting Liam Sullivan's purpose for being here.

For a moment, she even thought he had come for her.

Upon second thought, that seemed impossible.

She didn't have that much influence.

It was normal for the Sullivan Group to do charity work.

This was probably just a coincidence.

But Liam Sullivan personally doing good deeds felt like a big bad wolf delivering money.

He definitely had ulterior motives.

She hurriedly lowered her head.

She was afraid he would notice two men sitting beside her.

These two would be unlucky.

She always felt like a plague nowadays.

Wherever she went, she brought trouble to others.

The man in the front row was tall and broad.

He blocked her from view.

She carefully hid in his shadow.

She prayed the man wouldn't notice her.
###Chapter 461

Liam Sullivan stepped onto the stage with effortless composure.

Spotlights illuminated him, making his tall figure appear even more dazzling.

He took the microphone, his voice deep and pleasing.

Vivian Bennett rolled her eyes from her seat.

This man played the philanthropist with flawless perfection.

His words overflowed with compassion, every syllable radiating saintly brilliance.

Vivian felt she wasn't listening to a speech, but attending a revival meeting.

This man had blood on his hands, yet he acted like someone who wouldn't even step on an ant.

Brilliant. Absolutely brilliant.

She was utterly impressed.

The speech ended, and thunderous applause erupted throughout the venue.

Everyone praised his noble soul.

Yet Liam humbly stated he had only done what was expected.

This modesty won even greater admiration.

He descended the stage but did not return to the VIP seats in the first row.

Instead, he walked gracefully toward the third row.

The event organizers looked confused, trying to guide him back to the main seating.

Liam waved dismissively. "I'm not an expert. It wouldn't be appropriate to sit in the experts' section."

His words drew another wave of admiration.

Only Vivian covered her face in despair.

What kind of eyes did this man have to spot her here?

This had nothing to do with not wanting the expert seat. He clearly wanted to drive away the person next to her.

Sure enough, Liam stopped before the Eastern gentleman.

He flashed an angelic smile and politely requested to switch seats.

The Eastern gentleman, feeling honored, quickly stood up and yielded his seat.

Liam stood before Vivian, smiling down at her lowered head.

Knowing escape was impossible, she looked up and glared. "What do you want?"

Liam pointed to the vacated seat. "Move over there."

That spot was next to a young woman.

Vivian instantly understood his intention.

He not only wanted to remove the Eastern gentleman but also to separate her from the blond man.

She absolutely refused to comply.

But Liam stood there with an innocent smile.

Curious glances from the crowd intensified.

Reluctantly, she moved to the other seat.

Liam sat down in her original spot, looking satisfied.

Ambiguous looks immediately surrounded them.

Vivian lowered her voice. "Didn't you say you'd wait outside?"

"Outside was boring." Liam raised an eyebrow. "This is more interesting."

His tone instantly turned arrogant.

All traces of his earlier humility vanished.

"Then go find somewhere else to amuse yourself. Why pretend to be a philanthropist?"

Liam's lips curved with pleasure. "It's fun to play the good guy occasionally."

He closed his eyes contentedly. "Being worshipped feels rather nice."
###Chapter 462

Vivian Bennett's eyes widened in disbelief.

She simply could not comprehend such extravagant spending.

"You donated a fortune without blinking, just to experience some fleeting feeling?"

Her understanding of Liam Sullivan's capricious nature was once again shattered.

This man acted solely on his whims.

Perhaps it was precisely because he lacked genuine intent that he could act so recklessly.

Liam chuckled softly, his eyes shimmering with mesmerizing ripples.

He leaned close to her ear, his warm breath brushing against her sensitive lobe.

"Who said I donated to play the saint?"

His voice was low like the night wind, laced with amusement.

"That money was donated for you."

Vivian's heart trembled.

She turned her face away, avoiding his overly intimate proximity.

"I don't have that much influence over you."

Her voice carried detachment, her lashes lowered to conceal her emotions.

Liam spread his hands helplessly, a lazy smile curling his lips.

"So unromantic. I'm trying so hard to please you, and you don't even show a hint of appreciation."

He sighed deliberately. "Other women would have thrown themselves into my arms by now."

Vivian lifted her indifferent face against the backlight.

"Appreciation requires sincerity. Fake performances aren't worth emotional investment."

Liam's eyes darkened slightly, finally dropping his playful demeanor.

"Fine, I admit it. I donated to get in and keep an eye on you."

His tone turned cold abruptly, carrying a dangerous edge.

"And sure enough, I caught you fooling around."

His slender fingers tapped lightly on the armrest.

"I only looked away for a moment, and you're already flirting with two different men."

He narrowed his eyes, his voice icy. "Your appetite is quite large, isn't it?"

Vivian gritted her teeth in anger.

"This seating was arranged by the conference, not me!"

She glared at him fiercely, flames of fury burning in her eyes.

Liam raised an eyebrow.

"You didn't have to talk to them."

"I'm not mute!" Vivian nearly laughed in exasperation. "You want to forbid normal social interaction? Why don't you just lock me up!"

"All affairs start with 'normal social interaction'," Liam sneered, his gaze sharp as a blade.

Vivian turned away, her chest heaving violently.

"Unreasonable!"

She no longer wanted to argue with this obsessive man.

The old Liam was domineering, but at least he gave her some breathing room.

Now he was like a complete madman, wanting to tie her to his belt.

This suffocating possessiveness was driving her insane.

Liam sensed her nearing breaking point and finally relented.

For the entire three-hour meeting, he didn't disturb her again.

He simply lowered his head to fiddle with his phone, handling some unknown business.

As the meeting ended, the crowd began to stir.

The blond man glanced at Liam, then gave Vivian a regretful smile.

"I wanted to invite you to dinner, but it seems you're already taken."

Vivian was taken aback.

Liam had actually been right.

Her romantic luck had been absurdly strong lately.

Liam smirked and wrapped his arm around her waist, his fingertips gently stroking her side.

"I suggest you don't get any ideas about her." He looked at the blond man, a dangerous curve on his lips. "I get very scary when I'm jealous."

The blond man felt a chill down his spine under his gaze, but couldn't help asking, "How scary?"

A dark glint flashed in Liam's eyes.
###Chapter 463

"I might... kill someone." The amusement vanished from Liam Sullivan's eyes.

A cruel smile touched his lips.

The blond man's face turned deathly pale.

He stumbled backward several steps.

Then he turned and fled without looking back.

Liam raised an eyebrow disdainfully.

"Such courage dares to pursue you? Even this morning's fool was better than that."

Vivian Bennett massaged her temples in resignation.

"One moment you're playing the philanthropist, the next you reveal your true nature. If you're going to act, could you at least show some restraint?"

Liam shrugged carelessly.

"Pretending to be kind is tedious. Inspiring fear is far more satisfying."

Vivian sighed in acceptance.

Expecting a demon to restrain its nature was futile.

The fact this young master had held back this long was already a miracle.

"Let's go. No one will dare ask you out now." Liam's lips curved in triumph.

"My dear, it's time for our dinner."

His reserved revolving restaurant crowned the city's tallest skyscraper.

For over a decade, it had been the fairy-tale destination of countless young women's dreams.

Exorbitant prices and strict reservation requirements made it a symbol of status and wealth.

Crystal chandeliers hung from the domed ceiling.

Vines and roses twisted into floral walls.

The diamond-like floor reflected a sky of glittering stars.

Vivian stood frozen, as if she had stepped into Alice's wonderland.

The distant dream of her girlhood was now coming true in the most unexpected way.

She instinctively turned and met Liam's bottomless gaze.

Roses bloomed behind him, framing him like a prince from a fairy tale.

Handsome.

Elegant.

Powerful enough to control the entire world.

Vivian hurriedly closed her eyes.

When she opened them again, clarity had returned.

A prince should have a pure heart.

But black blood flowed through his veins.

"Do you like it?" Liam approached.

He tucked a white rose behind her ear.

Its fragrance enveloped them.

He leaned down and pressed a kiss to her forehead.

Vivian recoiled as if electrocuted.

The moment her dream came true filled her with panic.

Since witnessing that unbearable scene, she had vowed never to let her heart sway for him again.

The rose slipped from her hair.

It shattered on the starry floor.
###Chapter 464

His occasional tenderness always made her heart race uncontrollably.

Liam Sullivan was like a dangerous flame.

She knew his hands were stained with blood and he cared for nothing.

Yet there was a deadly attraction about him.

It drew her in despite her fear and resentment.

She wasn't as strong-willed as she had imagined.

Though her words refused him firmly, her heart had already wavered.

Reason screamed at her: falling for him would lead to eternal damnation.

Only by staying sober could she avoid complete ruin.

"I don't like white roses." She coldly plucked the flower from her hair and tossed it aside before turning toward the dining table.

Liam gazed at the abandoned rose.

Its delicate petals scattered on the floor, mirroring his heart at that moment.

He forced a smile, bent down to pick up the flower, and carefully tucked it into his pocket.

Then he followed her as if nothing had happened, pulling out her chair before taking his seat opposite.

Vivian Bennett avoided his eyes, looking out through the floor-to-ceiling windows.

The city's nightscape unfolded beneath them, its lights sparkling like a river of stars.

The noise of the world was shut out, leaving only the sound of their breathing in the quiet.

It felt as though they were standing above the clouds, looking down on all of creation.

"You can see the entire ancient city from here." Liam's soft voice broke the silence. "Though we can't live forever, isn't it romantic to witness the traces of time together?"

This was the most genuine form of togetherness he could imagine.

No flowery words needed—just standing side by side watching time pass.

Vivian's tone was flat. "I'm long past the age of dreaming about romance. Sentimental scenes like this don't work on me. Let's order. Coming all this way to eat is a pure waste of time."

Liam's chest tightened, disappointment flashing in his eyes. "I think it's worth it."

He raised his hand and clapped three times.

The restaurant doors swung open.

Waiters in traditional attire pushed rose-adorned carts into the room in a procession.

The table was soon covered with an array of exquisite dishes.

Vivian stared in surprise—every single one was prepared to her taste.

She lowered her head and silently picked up her fork and knife.

"The head chef here is quite famous." Liam cut into a tender lamb chop, his eyes hopeful. "Would you like to try it?"

"It's alright." She speared some vegetables. "Why the mix of Eastern and Western dishes?"

"I had a feeling you'd like it." He smiled like a fox who'd gotten his way.

"I'm not picky about food. As long as it's edible, it's fine."

She finished the food on her plate quickly and set her napkin down.

"I'm full."

Liam looked stunned at the table full of untouched dishes.

His carefully prepared efforts were met with such indifference, stirring waves of emotion in his eyes.

"Try a few more dishes. This lamb chop is perfectly grilled." He placed the cut meat on her plate and carefully drizzled sauce over it.

Watching his actions, Vivian felt a sharp pain in her heart.

It shouldn't be like this.

Why did the man she was supposed to hate have to show such tenderness?

This torturous conflict was driving her mad.
###Chapter 465

Vivian Bennett pushed her plate away.

The silverware clinked sharply.

"I'm finished." She stood, her voice icy. "You can have the lamb chops."

Liam Sullivan's fingers tightened around his wine glass.

Vivian left the dining room without looking back.

Her heels struck the marble floor with finality.

Beyond the revolving restaurant's glass walls, the city glittered like stars.

Liam sat alone at the table.

A romantic piano piece echoed through the empty dining room.

He slowly cut into his lamb chop.

His movements were elegant but lonely.

Vivian returned to her hotel room.

Hot water cascaded from the showerhead.

She stood eyes closed beneath the spray.

Drops trailed down her cheeks.

She couldn't tell if it was water or tears.

The mirror reflected a pale face.

Her robe hung loosely at her waist.

She remembered Liam bending to pick up the white rose.

The image unsettled her.

She brushed powder across her cheeks.

She chose a simple black gown.

She left every piece of jewelry in the case.

Liam was waiting in the hotel lobby when she descended.

His black tuxedo accentuated his tall frame.

He took her hand and pressed a kiss to it.

"Very plain." He eyed her attire. "You're learning."

Vivian withdrew her hand.

"I just don't want any trouble."

The ballroom blazed with light.

Socialites and elites mingled with champagne flutes.

The conference chairman hurried forward enthusiastically.

"Mr. Sullivan, your presence honors us."

Vivian was forced to stay at Liam's side.

She discreetly observed the industry leaders.

These were people she rarely had access to.

Liam seemed disinterested.

He swirled his champagne glass absently.

But his gaze never left Vivian.

Vivian was enjoying a conversation with a renowned scholar.

She didn't notice Liam's darkening expression.

Until his arm suddenly circled her waist.

"Excuse us."

He guided her toward the terrace.

Night air brushed her face.

"Having fun?" Liam's tone was unreadable.

Vivian pulled away from his arm.

"More fun than being with you."

Liam's eyes darkened.

He stepped closer.

"Remember who you belong to."

Vivian lifted her chin.

"I belong to myself."

The ballroom music shifted.

A waltz began to play.

Liam extended his hand.

"Shall we dance?"

Vivian looked at his offered hand.

She did not respond.

The atmosphere on the terrace grew tense.

In the distance, city lights flickered.

Their reflections gleamed in each other's eyes.
###Chapter 466

Vivian Bennett moved through the party crowd with bright enthusiasm.

She held Liam Sullivan's hand, introducing him to several renowned industry veterans.

Liam appeared indifferent, a flicker of impatience in his eyes.

But seeing Vivian's sparkling gaze, he maintained basic courtesy.

He politely shook hands, a perfectly measured smile on his lips.

After the introductions, Vivian chatted happily with a silver-haired older woman.

Liam had no interest in such socializing.

Seeing she was an elderly lady, he relaxed slightly.

He took a cocktail and turned toward the garden.

"Is that handsome gentleman your boyfriend?" The older woman smiled, studying Vivian.

Vivian paused, her smile strained.
"It's not what you think."

"The way he looks at you is special." The woman said meaningfully. "Cold on the surface, but his eyes never leave you."
Vivian pressed her lips together, silent.

"If you like him, you should hold on tight." The woman lowered her voice. "Several young ladies were asking about him earlier. Western girls are much more forward than Eastern ones."

A strange tightness gripped Vivian's chest.

She instinctively scanned the room, but Liam was gone.

She picked up her glass and walked into the garden. The night breeze carried a chill.

A faint sound came from deep within the garden.

Vivian's steps halted.

Among the swaying shadows of flowers, a man and woman embraced intimately.

A blonde woman kissed the man before her passionately.

Vivian meant to retreat, but then she clearly saw the man's profile.

Her pupils contracted sharply. Her fingertips turned icy.

She retreated silently back to the hall, her face pale as paper.

The glass in her hand nearly shattered from her grip.

So this tender affection was nothing but an elaborate deception.

She set down her glass and bid farewell to the host.

The host asked in surprise, "Aren't you leaving with Mr. Sullivan?"

Vivian forced a bitter smile and disappeared through the doorway.

She didn't hear the sudden exclamation behind her.
"Someone's fallen into the water—"
###Chapter 467

Vivian Bennett rushed out of the banquet hall.

She stood in the night, looking around in confusion.

Her chest felt tight and suffocating.

The oppressive feeling grew heavier and heavier.

It almost choked her.

She couldn't tell if it was anger or jealousy.

Her head throbbed with pain.

Her entire body trembled uncontrollably.

Her mind was filled with the entangled figures in the garden.

Liam Sullivan and that woman pressed tightly together.

The image refused to fade.

She shook her head vigorously.

But she couldn't shake off that glaring scene.

She knew his true nature.

Why get angry?

He could even touch his own sister.

Other women were no exception.

Vivian pressed her throbbing temple.

A sharp pain pierced her heart.

She had been deceiving herself all along.

She claimed to have moved on.

But her heart had never truly let go.

Only after witnessing his betrayal firsthand.

Did she realize how ridiculous she had been.

Liam Sullivan's tenderness was all an illusion.

Perhaps what he said the night he took her was his true feelings.

He said he only desired her body.

It was purely physical attraction.

Since there was no love.

Betrayal came naturally and easily.

Just like that chaotic night with Isabella Sullivan.

Now it was happening all over again.

Yet she always fell under his spell.

She softened time and time again.

It was utterly foolish.

Vivian was exhausted.

Not physically tired.

But heartbroken and disillusioned.

She kicked off her high heels.

Her bare feet stepped on the cold gravel path.

She didn't know how long she had walked.

Raindrops suddenly began to fall from the sky.

The cold wind brushed against her skin.

She couldn't help but shiver.

Her eyes suddenly felt warm.

Why did her tragedy never end?

Fate never favored her.

Her phone rang abruptly.

Vivian answered it blankly.

Liam Sullivan's name flashed on the screen.

Her heart constricted violently.

She took a deep breath and answered.

Liam asked where she was.

Her voice was calm and steady.

She said she was tired and had left first.

She wanted to be alone.

She would return to the hotel later.

He should do as he pleased.

After hanging up, she turned off her phone directly.

She was surprised by her own composure.

No crying, no questioning.

What right did she have?

She wasn't even his mistress.

Her cheek suddenly felt wet.

She raised her hand and touched cold tears.

She looked at the moisture on her fingertips and smiled bitterly.

It was an outcome she had foreseen.

There was nothing to cry about.

Vivian hailed a taxi.

She packed her luggage before Liam returned.

She bought a ticket and flew back to the United States overnight.

Only by staying far away from him.

Could this entanglement finally end.

After returning, she immediately changed her number.

She found a new place to live.

That night in the rain planted the seeds of illness.

After moving, she finally couldn't hold on any longer.

A high fever persisted, and she was admitted to the hospital.

Vivian lay on the hospital bed.

She truly experienced what it meant for illness to drain one's strength.

###Chapter 468

Liam Sullivan hadn't contacted her these past few days.

She hadn't reached out to him either.

This clean break was for the best.

It saved her from witnessing him entangled with other women again.

The memory of that scene had turned her stomach for days.

She had lost her appetite completely.

Her entire frame had grown noticeably thinner.

Vivian Bennett slept in her hospital room.

Upon waking, she sensed an unusual presence.

Her lashes fluttered as she lifted her gaze toward the doorway.

Liam Sullivan stood there.

He held a bouquet of flowers in one hand and a fruit basket in the other.

His face was icy, his eyes dark and fixed on her.

A terrifying chill radiated from him.

Vivian met his gaze for several seconds.

She didn't understand why he was looking at her with such a predatory glare.

He was the one who had done wrong.

Messing around with his sister, then stealing kisses with another woman at the banquet.

How dare he show up here with that attitude?

Vivian coolly averted her eyes.

She refused to engage with him.

But she could feel Liam moving closer.

He stopped at the foot of her hospital bed.

He stared down at her from his height.

His presence became impossible to ignore.

Liam watched the woman on the bed treating him like air.

A savage rage surged within him.

His hands clenched into fists at his sides.

The quiet room echoed with the sound of his knuckles cracking.

"What are you doing here?" Vivian finally spoke first.

Liam glared at her coldly. "I should be asking you that. Disappearing again? Vanishing for days whenever you're upset?"

His voice was heavy with suppressed anger.

His burning gaze fixed on her unrepentant face.

He had returned to the banquet days ago to find her gone.

He had been so angry he smashed his phone on the spot.

When he called her, she told him to manage his own schedule.

She said she needed space.

By the time he rushed back to the hotel, she had already checked out and left.

He didn't know what had set her off this time.

She just left without a word.

She had disappeared from his life once again, silently.

Liam had his people search for a long time.

They finally tracked her down.

It turned out she had already settled in the U.S.

No wonder he couldn't find her back home.

He immediately flew over.

He had intended to give her a stern lesson.

But then he saw her lying in a hospital bed.

Her face was pale, her entire being looked haggard.

He instantly couldn't bring himself to scold her.

But the anger in his heart refused to be suppressed.

Liam's expression was extremely grim.

"What's it to you?"

Vivian's words were like a knife.

They plunged straight into Liam's heart.

He stared at her in disbelief.

He couldn't believe she would say something so hurtful.

His clenched fists trembled slightly.

He thought of everything he had done for her these past days.

All of it was coldly dismissed by her.

A heart-wrenching pain surged in his chest.

Why did he try so hard to be good to her?

Yet she always ignored his sincerity.

She struck him down cruelly, time and again.
###Chapter 469

Liam Sullivan's chest heaved violently.

Days of accumulated depression and agony erupted at this moment.

His eyes burned crimson, fixed intensely on the figure lying with her back to him on the hospital bed.

"Vivian Bennett, what madness possessed you tonight?"

His voice was hoarse, each word gritted out between his teeth.

"I gave you roses, and you threw them on the ground."

"I cooked dinner for you myself, and you left without taking a single bite."

"At the gala, I patiently socialized for your sake, dealing with people I had no interest in. The moment I turned around, you were gone."

"I searched the entire city to find you at the hospital, and you coldly tell me it's none of my business."

Liam slammed his fist against the wall.

"Vivian, is your heart made of iron? Or do you simply not have one?"

The figure on the bed finally stirred.

Vivian slowly pushed herself up.

She turned her face toward him. Her pale features looked almost translucent under the lights.

Those always affectionate eyes were now cold as ice.

"Who asked you to do any of that?"

Her voice was soft, yet sharp as a blade.

"Who wanted you to do those things?"

Liam's face instantly lost all color.

He looked at her icy expression, feeling as if his heart were being brutally squeezed.

"You think putting on a gentle, devoted act would move me?" Vivian's lips curled into a mocking smile. "What game are you playing this time? A new way to torment me?"

She remembered the blonde woman pressed against him.

She remembered how he leaned down to kiss her.

Her stomach churned violently.

"If it's not sincere, why put on this hypocritical performance?"

His entire body trembled from her words.

He stepped closer to the bed, pain and fury swirling in his eyes.

"Vivian, you are truly an idiot."

His voice dropped to a terrifying low.

"If I, Liam Sullivan, wanted to play with someone, a snap of my fingers could make their life hell."

"Would I need to humble myself like this? Endure being wounded by you over and over, still shamelessly clinging to you?"

He grabbed her shoulders, his grip so forceful it nearly crushed her bones.

"I love you so pathetically, offering my heart for you to trample. What more do you want from me?"

Vivian lifted her face, tears streaming down silently.

She looked at the man before her.

The man she loved to the depths of her soul, and hated to the marrow of her bones.

"You love me?"

She suddenly laughed, tears flowing even harder.

"You love me, so you kiss that blonde woman at the gala?"

"You love me, so you take me by force against my will?"

"You love me, so you sleep with your so-called sister while being with me?"

Her voice shook so badly she could barely continue.

"Liam, do you find this particularly thrilling? Particularly amusing?"

Liam's pupils constricted violently.

He stared at her as if seeing her for the first time.

"What did you say?"
###Chapter 470

Vivian Bennett's heart had shattered into a million pieces.

Each fragment carried a raw, bleeding pain.

She could only wrap herself in layers of protection.

Building high walls with coldness and distance.

Rejecting all his kindness and tenderness.

Because she could no longer bear that kind of hurt.

When you truly love someone.

Even the slightest betrayal.

Is enough to shatter your heart completely.

Her heart-wrenching sobs echoed through the air.

Each accusation stabbed toward Liam Sullivan like a knife.

Liam stood completely stunned.

He stared blankly at her agonized expression.

Suddenly, as if remembering something.

His face turned deathly pale.

But a terrifying light ignited in his eyes.

He shouted at her, "What do you mean by that? I told you long ago, Isabella and I are only siblings in name. There's no blood relation between us. We're nothing like the forbidden lovers rumors claim..."

Vivian's tears fell uncontrollably.

Rain and tears mixed on her face.

She was pale, without a trace of color.

Liam jolted.

His eyes grew more confused. "What could Isabella and I have done? That night at Emerald Estate, we didn't..."

Vivian's heart felt pierced by a sharp thorn.

A detailed pain spread from her chest throughout her entire body.

Remembering what she saw at Emerald Estate that night.

Her eyes gradually reddened.

The words stuck in her chest finally poured out. "Don't rush to deny it. That night you drank most of a bottle of aphrodisiac wine. You carried the drugged Isabella Sullivan away."

That kind of scene.

She couldn't describe it in detail.

Every time she recalled it.

Her chest felt suffocated, making it hard to breathe.

Liam sharply cut off her increasingly harsh words.

His voice was piercingly cold. "What nonsense are you spouting?"

He quickly processed what she had just said.

His brows furrowed tighter and tighter.

His gaze toward her was sharp and cold.

As if he wanted to bore two holes through her.

"Isabella and I are nothing as filthy as you imagine."

Vivian's face was full of disbelief.

"At Emerald Estate, you saw me and Isabella doing it with your own eyes?" Liam suddenly questioned in a deep voice.

Vivian's pupils constricted violently.

The suffocating pain in her chest struck again.

Though she hadn't pushed open the bathroom door.

Those intimately overlapping bodies.

And Isabella's occasional screams and heavy panting.

Could all that have been her imagination?

"Isabella was indeed drugged that day, but I didn't touch her. What you saw was probably me standing behind her, holding her head under the cold water in the bathtub."

Vivian's eyes widened suddenly.

She stared intently at the man for a long moment.

As if judging the truth of his words.

"That night I did drink most of a bottle of aphrodisiac wine. But later," Liam suddenly grabbed her hand, "I kept thinking about us being together. I used my own hand..."

A strange tingling sensation spread through the hand he held.

She tried to break free but couldn't.

"I imagined my hand was you."
###Chapter 471

Vivian Bennett’s delicate brows furrowed tightly as she glared fiercely at the shameless man before her.

“Who wants to hear your nonsense?”

She bit her lip, her gaze piercing directly into his deep eyes.

“Did you really not touch her?”

Liam Sullivan’s jawline tightened abruptly.

“You thought I touched her, so you left without a word for so long?”

A sharp pain twisted in Vivian’s heart.

“If you really touched another woman, and that woman is your nominal sister, how much would that hurt me? Shouldn’t I have left?”

Liam felt as if his heart was being brutally squeezed.

All suspicion, resentment, and distrust vanished in an instant.

Only overwhelming heartache and regret remained.

He pulled her forcefully into his arms, refusing to let go no matter how she struggled.

“It’s my fault, all my fault.”

His voice was hoarse and filled with endless tenderness.

“I didn’t explain things to you in time, and I made you suffer so much grievance.”

All his pride crumbled at this moment.

Completely defeated by her scalding tears.

Right now, he only wanted to hold her tightly and soothe her pain.

“Let me go.”

Vivian struggled fiercely a few times but couldn’t break free from his restraint.

Her grievances turned into fists, pounding against his solid chest.

“I won’t let go.”

He tightened his arms instead, pressing her deeper into his embrace.

He lowered his head and gently kissed her damp hair.

Vivian was both angry and heartbroken, her fists falling heavier.

“Let go! We haven’t settled our accounts yet!”

Her voice was tearful but stubborn.

“Don’t think I’ll forgive you just like this! Even if you didn’t touch Isabella Sullivan, you still kissed other women before, forced me time and again… You bastard! I hate you! I hate you to death! Why did you have to make me feel so awful?”

The thought of him kissing someone else made bitterness surge wildly.

This kind of betrayal was unbearable to her.

Just imagining him being intimate with others was enough to shatter her heart.

Liam endured her pounding, his chest aching from the blows.

This woman really showed no mercy.

But he deserved it.

In love, a man should protect the woman he loves and shelter her from storms.

Yet he had made her suffer endless grievances.

“It’s all my fault, alright?”

He softened his voice, his palm gently stroking her trembling back.

Trying to calm her agitated emotions.

Vivian clutched the fabric of his shirt, tears flooding out.

“It was your fault all along! Making me so sad… Forcing me, threatening me, hurting me… The most despicable thing is, when I finally started to like you, you actually cheated…”

The more she spoke, the sadder she became.

This relationship had brought her nothing but pain and grievance.

Liam sighed helplessly, a bitter smile touching his lips.

“I admit to all the previous mistakes, but this last one, I can’t admit.”

“You did it and still won’t admit it!” Vivian punched him again.

“The facts are what they are, how can I admit it?” Liam looked at her helplessly. “I clearly didn’t cheat, what do you want me to admit?”

Vivian’s eyes widened in anger.

“You still dare to quibble? That time in the garden at the banquet, I saw you holding a woman and kissing her! How long had you known her before you started kissing? Do you even have any bottom line? You can’t deny this, I saw it with my own eyes!”

She accused him righteously, growing angrier the more she thought about it.

She danced with a prince, spoke with a man, and he watched her like a hawk.

But then he turned around and casually kissed another woman.

Just thinking about it made her suffocate with pain.

At least she wouldn’t casually kiss someone.

Liam’s temples glistened with fine sweat as he rubbed them helplessly.

“This matter… there’s actually a misunderstanding.”
###Chapter 472

Feeling guilty?

I saw it with my own eyes.

How could there be any misunderstanding?

Vivian Bennett felt a tightness in her chest.

She turned her face away, refusing to look at him.

Perhaps in his eyes, a kiss meant nothing.

A man like him was used to women throwing themselves at him.

But she was different.

From the moment she fell for him, she could no longer tolerate any ambiguity.

The jealousy is strong.

His fingertips gently brushed the corner of her eye.

You ran away so fast.

Did you not hear someone fall into the water?

Fall into the water?

Vivian was stunned for a moment.

Then she glared at him.

Don't try to fool me.

We must clear this up today.

I was on a phone call in the garden.

I didn't notice that drunk blonde woman lunging at me.

His tone was aggrieved, but his eyes were serious.

Her makeup was terrifyingly thick.

Her perfume gave me a headache.

How could I possibly be interested in that taste?

I directly picked her up and threw her into the pool.

Who knew you would appear right at that moment?

Was it really such a coincidence?

If you don't believe me, go ask the guests from that night.

I'm not afraid of a confrontation.

His gaze was frank.

Hmph, of course I will ask.

The stone stuck her heart loosened slightly.

Such matters were easy to verify.

He had no reason to lie.

Liam Sullivan pulled her into his arms.

His fingertips gently caressed her pale face, still weak from illness.

Can you forgive this fool now?

So it was all a misunderstanding.

Yet it caused them both such torment and heartache.

Fortunately, they finally tore down their disguises.

They laid their true feelings bare under the sunlight.

Vivian lifted her head.

Tears glistened in her eyes.

The scars of the past still ached.

Like a thorn embedded deep in her heart.

Liam Sullivan, the way you treated me back then...

Trampling on my dignity.

I said I would never forgive you.

His heart felt as if it were being squeezed tightly.

He knew his sins were unforgivable.

Back then, he was blinded by hatred.

He thought her escape was because she didn't love him.

So he retaliated in the cruelest way possible.

So... you still won't forgive me?

I shouldn't forgive you.

Just like I shouldn't have fallen in love with you.

If I could control my heart, I wouldn't be in such pain.

Liam shuddered.

A sudden light ignited in his eyes.

You...

I love you.

She finally spoke the words buried deep in her heart.
###Chapter 473

Vivian Bennett gazed into Liam Sullivan's deep eyes, her heart filled with complex emotions.

The past hurts were still vivid in her mind.

Yet she couldn't truly bring herself to hate him.

Perhaps this relationship had been a mistake from the very beginning.

But reason could no longer control her heart.

She had already fallen deeply without even realizing it.

Now that her emotional knots were untied, her body would naturally heal.
###Chapter 474

Liam Sullivan stayed by Vivian Bennett's side at the hospital without leaving for three days.

He remained until she had fully recovered and was discharged.

"Should I use actions instead?"

Liam's hearty laughter echoed from outside the door.

Listening to his fading footsteps, Vivian finally let out a sigh of relief.

She ran the hot water, removed her clothes, and slowly sank into the tub.

The warm water enveloped her body, every pore relaxing.

More importantly, her mood felt incredibly light at this moment.

But this happiness had come too suddenly, making her feel vaguely uneasy.

"Can I really be this happy forever?" She murmured to herself, staring at the ceiling.

She didn't know why she had such worries.

Perhaps it was because she had experienced too much hurt, making her so apprehensive about gains and losses.

Vivian shook her head, dismissing those negative thoughts.

Since she had already resolved her inner conflicts, she should trust Liam.

She should believe they could walk this path together forever.
###Chapter 475

Hope flickered back to life in Vivian Bennett's heart.

She dried her hair and stepped out of the bathroom.

The room was empty.

Liam Sullivan was gone.

Her heart suddenly felt hollow.

That awful man.

He stirred her up and then just vanished.

He didn't even say goodbye.

She sat on the edge of the bed, sulking.

Ten minutes later, the lock clicked softly.

Her eyes instantly lit up.

Liam Sullivan walked in carrying a paper box.

A teasing smirk played on his lips.

"I was only gone for a moment."

"Did you miss me this much already?"

Vivian shot him an annoyed glare.

"Don't flatter yourself."

"I wasn't missing you at all."

She denied it stubbornly.

But deep down, she had felt uneasy.

After all, he had just confessed his deep feelings.

Then he disappeared in the blink of an eye.

Anyone would start overthinking.

Liam brought over a small table.

He placed the paper box on it.

Vivian peeked over curiously.

"What's this?"

"Some pastries and hot soup."

He carefully unwrapped the packaging.

Delicate pastries steamed warmly.

The creamy soup smelled rich and inviting.

"You haven't been eating well these past few days."

"Drink some soup to warm your stomach first."

Vivian's eyes grew warm.

So he had gone out to buy food.

He noticed she hadn't been eating much.

He prepared this specially, worried she might be hungry.

His thoughtfulness moved her.

She remembered that night in Country K.

She had left him alone and walked away.

The dinner he had meticulously prepared.

Was left neglected and cold.

He must have been so hurt then.

Liam ladled out the soup and handed it to her.

He then arranged the pastries for her.

His movements were surprisingly skilled.

Vivian held the soup bowl and sighed.

"I didn't expect you to be so proficient."

"I thought a young master like you couldn't do anything."

He was born with a silver spoon in his mouth.

He also had a hedonistic personality.

He avoided lifting a finger if he could.

Yet he handled things with such efficiency.

Liam raised an eyebrow and chuckled lightly.

"You thought I was useless?"

"Being lazy doesn't mean I'm incapable."

A shadow passed through his eyes.

His voice suddenly turned colder.

"No one can be lucky forever."

"Survival skills are fundamental."

"Even at my lowest, I wouldn't starve."

Vivian noticed the shift in his tone.

The hint of sarcasm was startling.
###Chapter 476

Vivian Bennett suddenly realized she didn't know Liam Sullivan as well as she thought.

His sudden mood shifts left her completely bewildered.

"Liam, what's wrong?" She looked at him with unease.

Liam quickly composed himself, a faint smile returning to his lips.

"Nothing, just feeling sentimental." He paused. "But I'll allow you to live a life of luxury."

Vivian couldn't help but laugh. "Wouldn't that make me useless? If I ever fell on hard times, it would be terrible."

She knew that feeling all too well.

After her mother passed away, she was no longer the young mistress of the White family.

She had endured her stepmother's abuse, her brother's bullying, and days of hunger and cold.

Only by mastering survival skills could one truly live in this world.

But she didn't understand how Liam, a young master raised in privilege, could comprehend such things.

Could he truly understand her suffering?

Liam's lips curved with confidence. "With me here, how could I ever let you fall to such depths? Just be my live-in lover. I'll take good care of you. No matter what happens, I won't let you suffer."

His voice was deep and gentle. "Just stay in my arms and be happy forever."

Vivian's heart warmed.

This man's sweet words always made her heartbeat quicken.

She had always been independent and self-reliant, accustomed to caring for others.

Suddenly having someone pamper her like this left her both touched and flustered.

It turned out she was also a woman who needed to rely on someone.

She scooped up a spoonful of soup and picked up a pastry. After hesitating, she brought it to his lips.

"Would you like some?" she asked softly.

Amusement sparkled in Liam's eyes as he took a bite from her hand.

His tongue accidentally brushed against her fingertip.

Vivian's hand trembled, and she almost reflexively slapped him.

She quickly withdrew her hand and lowered her head to eat quietly.

Although their relationship had grown much closer, such intimate flirtation still made her uncomfortable.

After finishing their late-night snack, Vivian felt drowsy and urged Liam to take a shower.

When Liam emerged excitedly from his shower, he found her already asleep on the bed.

Her sleeping face was peaceful yet weary.

Liam smiled helplessly and climbed into bed quietly.

He carefully wrapped his arm around her waist and pressed his face against her neck.

Inhaling the faint fragrance of her hair, he closed his eyes contentedly.

Deep in the night.

Vivian suddenly began to tremble.

"No... don't touch me... let go..." she murmured unconsciously, her voice choked with tears.

Liam immediately woke up.

He gently held her shoulders and whispered in her ear, "Vivian, wake up. You're having a nightmare."

Vivian didn't wake up but sank deeper into the nightmare.

She suddenly raised her hand and slapped him hard across the face.

The sharp sound of the slap echoed piercingly in the silent night.
###Chapter 477

"Get away... don't touch me..." Vivian Bennett screamed, her voice breaking as she flailed her arms wildly.

Her consciousness was blurred.

She struggled like a frightened child, striking Liam Sullivan several times in her panic.

A sharp, stinging pain bloomed across Liam's left cheek from the unexpected slap.

Hearing her cry out with such revulsion, he almost thought she was cursing him directly.

Coming to his senses, he realized she was still struggling unconsciously.

He quickly seized her wrists, pinning her arms firmly against her sides.

Vivian twisted with her eyes closed for a few moments before her struggles gradually ceased.

She grew quiet.

A single, clear tear traced silently from the corner of her eye.

Her body trembled slightly.

Liam's heart clenched.

He sensed something was terribly wrong with her, as if she was trapped in immense sorrow.

He could only hold her tightly, repeating her name over and over.

Perhaps his calls reached her.

Vivian's eyelashes fluttered.

She slowly opened her eyes.

She stared at the ceiling, her gaze hollow and dazed, utterly terrified.

"Vivian, it was just a nightmare. Don't be afraid. I'm here," Liam murmured, gathering her into his embrace.

He gently stroked her rigid back.

He kissed her forehead, using his own warmth to comfort her.

Vivian gradually came back to herself.

She turned her head to look at him, her focus still scattered but slowly sharpening.

Feeling his soothing pats, her emotions gradually steadied.

Yet her face remained deathly pale.

She seemed burdened and distracted.

"What nightmare did you have? You're drenched in sweat. Were you being chased by a monster?" Liam asked lightly, wiping the cold sweat from her temple, trying to lighten the mood.

Vivian shuddered violently.

Her face turned even paler, all color draining from her features.

Liam noticed her drastic change immediately.

He held her hand. "Don't be afraid. Dreams aren't real. Tell me what you dreamed about?"

In all their nights sharing a bed, this was the first time he had seen her have a nightmare.

She was so terrified and helpless, it made his heart ache.

She wasn't a timid person by nature.

Whatever dream could frighten her this much must have touched her deepest, most hidden fears.

Vivian's fingers trembled slightly.

She looked up at him, her lips parting as if to speak, but no words came out.

Because this was less a nightmare and more a return to over a decade ago.

Back when her mother had just passed away.

Back when her father had remarried.

She suffered abuse and beatings from her stepmother.

She was bullied and tormented by her brother.

Those long-buried memories, ones she rarely recalled anymore, had blurred with time.
###Chapter 478

The night was dark as ink.

Vivian Bennett jolted upright in bed.

Fine beads of cold sweat covered her forehead.

Her chest heaved violently.

She gasped for air.

Her fingernails dug deep into her palms.

The dream had felt too real.

It was as if she had returned to that fiery night over a decade ago.

Thick smoke choked her, making it impossible to open her eyes.

Scorching heat waves blasted against her face.

She desperately tried to run.

But her legs felt as heavy as lead.

Helplessness and fear completely consumed her.

That long-forgotten scene.

Tonight, it reappeared with startling clarity.

Every detail was vividly precise.

Even the palpitations felt exactly the same.

She could hardly distinguish between dream and reality.

Until urgent calls pierced through the nightmare.

Pulling her back from the abyss.

"Vivian?"

Liam Sullivan's deep voice sounded beside her.

His warm palm stroked her back.

Only then did Vivian fully awaken.

She turned her head blankly.

Meeting Liam's concerned gaze.

"Nightmare?" His brow furrowed deeply.

Vivian nodded gently.

Her lips still pale.

"What did you dream about?" Liam pulled her into his arms.

He felt her trembling body.

Vivian remained silent for a moment.

Finally, she understated it. "I dreamed of a fire. I couldn't escape."

She lowered her eyelids.

Hiding the complex emotions in her eyes.

Those sealed past things.

She didn't know how to bring them up.

Nor did she want to mention them again.

Liam gazed at her pale profile.

Sensing her evasion keenly.

But he didn't press further.

He only held her tighter.

"You've been too tired lately," he said softly. "Between work and illness, the psychological pressure is too great."

Vivian leaned against his chest.

She murmured a soft "Mm."

Perhaps that was really the case.

Otherwise, why would she suddenly dream of something so distant?

"Since you're tired, rest properly." Liam's tone brooked no argument. "Don't go to work these next few days. Recuperate at home."

Vivian immediately lifted her head. "How can that work? Professor Morrison still has projects…"

"I'll help you ask for leave." Liam cut her off. "Your health comes first."

His gaze was firm and commanding.

Vivian wanted to argue further.

But he stopped her with a look.

"Be good." He caressed her cheek. "You're not in any state to work right now."

Vivian looked into his concerned eyes.

Finally, she nodded gently.

Perhaps she really needed a good rest.

Otherwise, even her dreams would remind her.

Those past events she deliberately tried to forget.

Had never truly disappeared.
###Chapter 479

She had just been discharged from the hospital.

Nightmares haunted her every night.

She hardly ever slept peacefully.

The dark circles under her eyes were so obvious.

How could her body handle it?

He was truly afraid she would collapse from exhaustion.

Vivian Bennett’s small face scrunched up.

Her watery eyes were full of pleading.

“The professor rarely gives me opportunities like this.”

“It’s not heavy work anyway.”

“If I suddenly don’t show up,”

“people will think I’m acting like a diva.”

Liam Sullivan pinched the tip of her nose.

“My woman certainly has the right to.”

“Be good and listen to me.”

“You’re too overbearing.”

Vivian glared at him, puffing her cheeks.

Since this man confessed his feelings,

he had become even stricter than before.

So falling in love meant losing freedom.

How pitiful.

“I should be overbearing with you.”

Liam snorted lightly.

His arm tightened around her.

His palm stroked her soft hair.

He slowly soothed the bristling kitten.

Vivian relaxed under his touch.

Her temper gradually faded.

She leaned comfortably against his chest.

Muttering softly,

“When am I ever unruly?”

“I’m obviously the most well-behaved.”

Liam pinched her cheek.

“If you were truly well-behaved,”

“why would you attract so much attention?”

“Let me count how many there are.”

Vivian rescued her face from his grip.

Her eyes sparkled with mischief.

“You’re just narrow-minded.”

“Jealous and overbearing for no reason.”

“I never knew you were like this.”

Liam narrowed his eyes.

In an instant, he pressed her into the sofa.

“I’m narrow-minded?”

His tone rose dangerously at the end.

“It seems I need to properly train you.”

“Then you’ll understand how good I am.”

His handsome face drew closer.

Their breaths intertwined.

Vivian’s heart raced.

She had a feeling she was about to suffer.

This man really shouldn’t be provoked.

Otherwise, she would be punished again.

She quickly softened her voice and begged for mercy.

“I was just joking.”

“You’re the most generous and the best.”

“The best boyfriend ever.”

Liam chuckled lowly.

“You surrender pretty fast.”

“Otherwise, I really would have punished you.”

His smile turned more wicked.

A mischievous light flickered in his eyes.

His thin lips gently nibbled her chin.

His voice was hoarse and tempting.

“Actually, I wanted to punish you more.”

“Why did you surrender so quickly?”

His warm hand slipped under her clothes.

It glided over her smooth skin.

Vivian’s cheeks flushed.

She grabbed his wrist in annoyance.

“No sneaky attacks.”

Liam kissed her flushed face.

Unapologetic.

“This is obviously a direct attack.”

“Darling.”

“It’s so soft here.”

“What is it?”

He laughed wickedly.

Vivian pinched his arm.

But she couldn’t break free from those wolfish claws.
###Chapter 480

Vivian Bennett felt a knot of frustration in her chest.

She glared at the man pinning her down.

This man was rotten to the core.

She surrendered completely.

His warm breath brushed against her earlobe.

It was infuriating.

His scorching skin radiated intense heat.

His eyes looked misty and blurred.

Wasn't she already letting him do anything he wanted?

Why did he still seem so tormented?

His gaze drifted over her like flowing water.

He captured her delicate hand and guided it slowly downward...

Vivian instinctively tried to pull back.

But he held her firmly in place.

This was so humiliating.

So frustrating.

The way he glanced at her lightly.

Dear God.

To encounter such an utterly wicked man.

Her bright eyes stared at him speechlessly.

Why didn't he just take her.

Instead of making her do this shameful thing.

This had nothing to do with her.

She refused to do such an embarrassing act.
###Chapter 481

Liam Sullivan's low, husky voice held an undeniable force.
"I want you to help me."

The space beside her was empty.
He had been disappearing mysteriously a lot lately.

She arranged the flowers in a vase by the window.

"Who dares to chase my girlfriend? I'll have to treat him to some bullets."

"Extremely jealous. My heart aches."

"Did you enjoy the lunch?"
###Chapter 482

"The food is absolutely incredible. Where did you find this chef?" Vivian Bennett couldn't contain her amazement. "This tastes even more authentic than back home. It's a shame you're not here. It's truly delicious."

Her puzzled voice traveled through the phone.

There were very few authentic Chinese restaurants in the area. Most had adapted their flavors to suit local tastes, losing their original essence.

She could hardly believe she was tasting such genuine hometown cuisine here.

"As long as it tastes good, there's no need to question its origin," Liam Sullivan chuckled softly, his tone filled with affection.

Of course, no chef of this caliber could be found locally.

He had specially arranged a private jet to bring the chef over from their homeland overnight.

"Where did you run off to again?" Vivian's voice sounded muffled. "I woke up and you were already gone."

Other new couples woke up together.

Even though they weren't married yet, they were at least in the passionate throes of a new relationship.

It never used to bother her before, but now she felt an inexplicable sense of loss.

"Your test results are back. There's a slight displacement in your phalanx, and the wound is severely infected."

The doctor frowned at him. "I warned you to be careful with that finger. How did it get worse in just one day? Did you get it wet?"

"Withholding information from your doctor won't help. I need to know what you did yesterday," the doctor said, exasperated by his uncooperative patient.

"I got caught in the rain. And I threw someone into a pool. I might have injured it then," Liam stated indifferently.

The doctor inhaled sharply. "I've never seen a patient so reckless with their own health. No wonder the wound is infected and you're running a fever. Even if no one else cares about you, you should at least value yourself."

Liam smiled genuinely this time. "Who says no one cares? I'm being very careful with this finger now, which is why I came for treatment immediately. Doctor, you must use your best skills to heal my hand quickly."

The doctor was taken aback.

This proud man, who had seemed so desolate and cold just days ago, now appeared positive and lively.

Clearly, he had found happiness that reignited his hope.

The doctor wondered what kind of woman possessed such magic to warm even the coldest heart.

"If you want proper recovery, you should be hospitalized. That's the only way your hand can rest completely and avoid further damage," the doctor advised.

Liam shook his head with a smile. "I can't. I'll finish this IV drip, but I must return tonight."

"May I ask why?" the doctor asked helplessly, looking at his stubborn patient.

"She has nightmares at night. I need to be there for her," Liam said softly.

The doctor suddenly recalled his words from the other day.

He said he had twisted his own finger because the person he liked said she'd rather his finger was broken.

Understanding dawned on the doctor. "You don't want her to know because you're afraid she'll blame herself and feel guilty, right?"
###Chapter 483

Liam Sullivan froze for a moment, then chuckled softly.

"Doctor, being too perceptive isn't always a good thing."

"Since you understand my intentions, please help me properly."

"Encountering a patient like you, I truly don't know if it's a blessing or a curse." The doctor shook his head and turned to process the reports.

......

Vivian Bennett woke up with nothing to do.

Liam wasn't by her side.

She decided to go out for a stroll.

Eight thousand euros, equivalent to tens of thousands of dollars.

###Chapter 486

A few days later, Vivian Bennett returned to the country with Liam Sullivan.

But even back in familiar surroundings, the terrifying dreams still haunted her.

###Chapter 487

Liam Sullivan’s fingertips gently brushed her swollen cheek.

His voice was low and tender. “Does it hurt?”
